eng: When God began to create the heavens and the earth.
local: Le gɔmedzedzea me Mawu wɔ dziƒo kple anyigba.

eng: the earth was without shape or form, it was dark over the deep sea, and Gods wind swept over the waters.
local: Ke anyigba le nyama, nɔnɔme meli nɛ o, ele ƒuƒlu, eye viviti do ɖe tsi gbana la ŋku me, eye Mawu ƒe Gbɔgbɔ le agba sam le tsia dzi.

eng: A record of the ancestors of Jesus Christ, son of David, son of Abraham.
local: Yesu Kristo, si nye Dawid vi, amesi nye Abraham vi la, ƒe dzidzimegbalẽ.

eng: Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
local: Abraham dzi Isak, Isak dzi Yakob, eye Yakob dzi Yuda kple nɔviawo;.

eng: God remembered Noah, all those alive, and all the animals with him in the ark. God sent a wind over the earth so that the waters receded.
local: Eye Mawu ɖo ŋku Noa kple gbemelã kple aƒemelã, siwo katã le egbɔ le aɖakaʋua me la dzi, eye Mawu na ya ƒo ɖe anyigba dzi, ale bena tsia de asi miemie me.

eng: The snake was the most intelligent of all the wild animals that the LORD God had made. He said to the woman, Did God really say that you shouldnt eat from any tree in the garden.
local: Ke da dze aye wu gbemelã, siwo katã Yehowa Mawu wɔ la, eye wògblɔ na nyɔnu la bena: Vavãe Mawu gblɔ bena, migaɖu abɔmetiawo katã ƒe tsetsea ɖeke o mahã?.

eng: This is the record of Adams descendants. On the day God created humanity, he made them to resemble God.
local: Esia nye Adam ƒe dzidzimeviwo ŋutinyagbalẽ. Esi Mawu wɔ Adam la, ewɔe ɖe eya ŋutɔ ƒe nɔnɔme.

eng: The heavens and the earth and all who live in them were completed.
local: Ale wowu dziƒo kple anyigba kple wo me nu gbogboawo katã nu enye si.

eng: When the number of people started to increase throughout the fertile land, daughters were born to them.
local: Ke esi amewo de asi agbɔsɔsɔ me le anyigba dzi, eye wodzi nyɔnuwo la.

eng: The man Adam knew his wife Eve intimately. She became pregnant and gave birth to Cain, and said, I have given life to a man with the LORDs help.
local: Eye Adam ɖo kple srɔ̃a Xawa, eye wòfɔ fu dzi Kain, eye wògblɔ bena: Yehowa ɖi nam be, medzi ŋutsu.

eng: The LORD said to Noah, Go into the ark with your whole household, because among this generation Ive seen that you are a moral man.
local: Tete Yehowa gblɔ na Noa be na: Yi ɖe aɖakaʋu la me, wò ŋutɔ kple aƒewò me katã, elabena mekpɔ bena, ame dzɔdzɔe nènye le dzidzime sia dome.

eng: The springs of the deep sea and the skies closed up. The skies held back the rain.
local: Eye anyigbatu me tsiwo nu tso, eye dziƒo nu tu; ale wɔ tsi la ke.

eng: God said, Let there be light. And so light appeared.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ be: Kekeli nedo! Eye kekeli do.

eng: The woman said to the snake, We may eat the fruit of the gardens trees.
local: Nyɔnua ɖo ŋu na da la be: Míeɖua abɔmetiawo ƒe tsetse faa;.

eng: and created them male and female. He blessed them and called them humanity on the day they were created.
local: Ewɔ wo wonye ŋutsu kple nyɔnu, eye wòyra wo: eye wòna ŋkɔ wo bena „ame“ le gbesigbe wòwɔ wo.

eng: She gave birth a second time to Cains brother Abel. Abel cared for the flocks, and Cain farmed the fertile land.
local: Emegbe la edzi enɔvia Habel, eye Habel va zu lãnyila, ke Kain ya zu agbledela.

eng: The divine beings saw how beautiful these human women were, so they married the ones they chose.
local: mawuviwo kpɔ bena, amegbetɔviwo ƒe nyɔnuwo dze tugbe ŋutɔ, eye woɖe wo dometɔ, siwo katã dze wo ŋu.

eng: On the sixth day God completed all the work that he had done, and on the seventh day God rested from all the work that he had done.
local: Eye Mawu wu eƒe ɖo, si wòwɔ la nu le ŋkeke adreagbe, eye wòdzudzɔ le ŋkeke adreagbe tso eƒe do, siwo katã wòwɔ la me.

eng: The waters receded gradually from the earth. After one hundred fifty days, the waters decreased.
local: Eye tsia le miemiem le anyigba la dzi ʋeʋeʋe. Edze miemie gɔme le ŋkeke alafa ɖeka kple blaatɔ̃ megbe.

eng: From every clean animal, take seven pairs, a male and his mate; and from every unclean animal, take one pair, a male and his mate.
local: Ɖe lã matsrimatsriwo katã atsu kple asi adreadre, kple lã tsitsriwo atsu kple asi eveveve.

eng: God saw how good the light was. God separated the light from the darkness.
local: Eye Mawu kpɔ kekelia bena enyo. Eye Mawu ma kekeli la kple viviti la dome.

eng: but not the fruit of the tree in the middle of the garden. God said, Dont eat from it, and dont touch it, or you will die.
local: ke Mawu gblɔ le ati, si le abɔa titina la ƒe tsetse ŋuti bena: Migaɖu eƒe ɖeke o, eye migaka asi eŋu hã o, be miagaku o!.

eng: When Adam was years old, he became the father of a son in his image, resembling him, and named him Seth.
local: Eye esi Adam xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blaetɔ̃ la, edzi ŋutsuvia ɖe, si ɖii, eye wòyɔe bena Set.

eng: The LORD said, My breath will not remain in humans forever, because they are flesh. They will live one hundred twenty years.
local: Eye Yehowa gblɔ bena: Nye gbɔgbɔ maganɔ amewo me boo o, elabena ŋutilã wonye le woƒe vodada me, eye woƒe agbe me ŋkekewo nanye ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blave!.

eng: Some time later, Cain presented an offering to the LORD from the lands crops.
local: Esia megbe la eteƒe medidi o, Kain tsɔ agblemenua ɖewo wɔ vɔsae na Yehowa.

eng: and from the birds in the sky as well, take seven pairs, male and female, so that their offspring will survive throughout the earth.
local: Nenema ke naɖe dziƒoxe dzodzoewo hã atsu kple asi adreadre, bena woƒe ƒome natsi agbe le anyigba dzi.

eng: God blessed the seventh day and made it holy, because on it God rested from all the work of creation.
local: Eye Mawu yra ŋkeke adrelia, eye wòkɔ eŋuti, elabena eya dzi wòdzudzɔ eɖokui le tso eƒe dɔwɔwɔ, siwo katã Mawu wɔ, eye wòna wodo la me.

eng: and in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day, the ark came to rest on the Ararat mountains.
local: Le ɣleti adrelia ƒe ŋkeke wuiadrelia dzi aɖakaʋu la va xa ɖe Ararat-towo dometɔ ɖeka ta me.

eng: God named the light Day and the darkness Night. There was evening and there was morning: the first day.
local: Mawu yɔ kekeli la be ŋkeke; ke viviti la eyɔe be zã. Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke gbãtɔ.

eng: The snake said to the woman, You wont die!.
local: Eye da la gblɔ na nyɔnua bena: Miele kuku ge akpɔ o;.

eng: After Seths birth, Adam lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Esi Adam dzi Set megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa enyi, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: In those days, giants lived on the earth and also afterward, when divine beings and human daughters had sexual relations and gave birth to children. These were the ancient heroes, famous men.
local: Le ŋkeke mawo me la ame dzɔtsuwo le anyigba dzi; eye esi Mawu viŋutsuwo ɖe amegbetɔwo ƒe vinyɔnuwo, eye wodzi vi na wo megbe la hã wogale; woawo nye ame kako ŋkɔxɔla, siwo nɔ agbe le blema.

eng: God said, Let there be a dome in the middle of the waters to separate the waters from each other.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ be: Alili nenɔ tsia titina, be wòanye dometsonu anɔ tsi kple tsi dome!.

eng: In seven days from now I will send rain on the earth for forty days and forty nights. I will wipe off from the fertile land every living thing that I have made.
local: Elabena esusɔ ŋkeke adre mana tsi nadza ɖe anyigba dzi ŋkeke blane kple zã hã blane, eye matsrɔ̃ nusianu, si mewɔ ɖe anyigba dzi.

eng: This is the account of the heavens and the earth when they were created. On the day the LORD God made earth and sky.
local: Esia nye dziƒo kple anyigba ƒe dzɔdzɔmenyawo le wo wɔwɔ me le ɣeyiɣi, si me Yehowa Mawu wo anyigba kple dziƒo.

eng: while Abel presented his flocks oldest offspring with their fat. The LORD looked favorably on Abel and his sacrifice.
local: Habel hã tsɔ eƒe lãwo ƒe ŋgɔgbevi, siwo dami la ƒe ɖewo vɛ. Eye Habel kple eƒe nunana dze Yehowa ŋu;.

eng: The waters decreased gradually until the tenth month, and on the first day of the tenth month the mountain peaks appeared.
local: Eye tsia le miemiem ʋeʋeʋe vaseɖe ɣleti ewolia dzi; le ɣleti ewolia ƒe ŋkeke gbãtɔ dzi towo ƒe tagbɔ dze.

eng: In all, Adam lived years, and he died.
local: Eye Adam ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo katã nye ƒe alafa asieke kple blaetɔ̃, eye woku.

eng: God knows that on the day you eat from it, you will see clearly and you will be like God, knowing good and evil.
local: ke boŋ Mawu nyae bena, gbesigbe ke miaɖu eƒe ɖe la, miaƒe ŋkuwo laʋu, eye mianɔ abe Mawu ene, anya nyui kple vɔ̃.

eng: Noah did everything the LORD commanded him.
local: Eye Noa wɔe, abe alesi Mawu de se nɛ ene.

eng: The LORD saw that humanity had become thoroughly evil on the earth and that every idea their minds thought up was always completely evil.
local: Eye esi Yehowa kpɔ bena, amegbetɔwo ƒe vɔɖivɔ̃ɖi va sɔ gbɔ le anyigba dzi, eye woƒe tamesusuwo kple dzimedidiwo nye vɔ̃ ko ɖaa la.

eng: but didnt look favorably on Cain and his sacrifice. Cain became very angry and looked resentful.
local: ke Kain kple eƒe nunana ya medze eŋu o. Eye Kain bi dzi ŋutɔ, eye wòɖo adã.

eng: God made the dome and separated the waters under the dome from the waters above the dome. And it happened in that way.
local: Eye Mawu wo alili la, eye wòmã tsi, si le alilia te, kple tsi, si le alilia ta me la dome. Eye wòva me nenema.

eng: After forty days, Noah opened the window of the ark that he had made.
local: Le ŋkeke blane megbe la Noa ʋu aɖakaʋua ƒe fesre, si wòwɔ la.

eng: before any wild plants appeared on the earth, and before any field crops grew, because the LORD God hadnt yet sent rain on the earth and there was still no human being to farm the fertile land.
local: Ke gbemetia ɖeke mele anyigba dzi haɖe o, eye nu miemiea ɖeke medo ɖe gbe me haɖe o; elabena Yehowa Mawu mena tsi dza ɖe anyigba dzi haɖe o, eye ame meli ade anyigba la o.

eng: When Seth was years old, he became the father of Enosh.
local: Eye esi Set xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka kple atɔ̃ la, edzi Enos.

eng: The LORD regretted making human beings on the earth, and he was heartbroken.
local: eve Yehowa bena, wòwɔ ame ɖe anyigba dzi, eye woɖu dzi nɛ.

eng: Noah was years old when the floodwaters arrived on earth.
local: Noa xɔ ƒe alafa ade, esime tsiɖɔɖɔ ɖe anyigba.

eng: The woman saw that the tree was beautiful with delicious food and that the tree would provide wisdom, so she took some of its fruit and ate it, and also gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate it.
local: Esi nyɔnu la kpɔ bena, ati la nyo na ɖuɖu, ebiã ŋu na ame, eye wòdzroa ame, eye wònana wodzea nunya la, egbe eƒe tsetsea ɖe heɖu, eye wòna srɔ̃a, si le egbɔ hã woɖu.

eng: The LORD said to Cain, Why are you angry, and why do you look so resentful?.
local: Tete Yehowa gblɔ na Kain bena: Nukaŋuti nèle dzi bim, eye nukaŋuti nèle adã ɖom?.

eng: God named the dome Sky. There was evening and there was morning: the second day.
local: Eye Mawu yɔ alilia be dziƒo. Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke evelia.

eng: though a stream rose from the earth and watered all of the fertile land.
local: Eye afu do tso anyigba dzi, eye wònyɔ ɖe anyigba la katã ƒe ŋku me.

eng: He sent out a raven, and it flew back and forth until the waters over the entire earth had dried up.
local: Eye wòʋu nu le akpaviãa ɖe nu ɖo ɖa, eye wòle dzodzom tso afi yi afi, vaseɖe esime tsia mie le anyigba dzi vo.

eng: After the birth of Enosh, Seth lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Esi Set dzi Enos megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa enyi kple adre, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: So the LORD said, I will wipe off of the land the human race that Ive created: from human beings to livestock to the crawling things to the birds in the skies, because I regret I ever made them.
local: Eye Yehowa gblɔ bena: Matsrɔ̃ ame, siwo mewɔ la ɖa le anyigba blibo la katã dzi, tso amewo kple lãwo dzi vaseɖe nu tatawo kple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo dzi, elabena evem bena, mewɔ wo.

eng: Noah, his sons, his wife, and his sons wives with him entered the ark to escape the floodwaters.
local: Eye Noa kple viaŋutsuwo kple srɔ̃a hekpe ɖe viaŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo ŋuti, woyi ɖe aɖakaʋu la me, hafi tsi va ɖe anyigba.

eng: God said, Let the waters under the sky come together into one place so that the dry land can appear. And thats what happened.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ be: Tsi, si le dziƒoa te la, neƒo ƒu ɖe teƒe ɖeka, bena ƒuƒuiƒe nadze! Eye wòva me nenema.

eng: the LORD God formed the human from the topsoil of the fertile land and blew lifes breath into his nostrils. The human came to life.
local: Azã Yehowa Mawu tsɔ anyi mè ame, eye wògbɔ agbegbɔgbɔ ɖe eƒe ŋɔti me; ale ame la zu luʋɔ gbagbe.

eng: Then they both saw clearly and knew that they were naked. So they sewed fig leaves together and made garments for themselves.
local: Tete wo kpakple eve ƒe ŋkuwo ʋu, eye wodze sii bena, yewole amama, eye wòtɔ amagbawo hesa ɖe ali.

eng: If you do the right thing, wont you be accepted? But if you dont do the right thing, sin will be waiting at the door ready to strike! It will entice you, but you must rule over it.
local: Alo menye ale wòle oa? Ne èwɔ nyui la, wò ŋku me akɔ, ke ne mèwɔ nyui o la, ekema nuvɔ̃ de xa ɖe ʋɔtru nu, eye wòɖo tamea ɖe ɖe ŋuwò; ke wò la ɖu edzi!.

eng: Then he sent out a dove to see if the waters on all of the fertile land had subsided.
local: Tete wòʋu nu le ahɔnea ɖe nu ɖo ɖa, ne yeakpɔ bena, tsia mie vo le anyigba blibo la katã dzi hã?.

eng: But as for Noah, the LORD approved of him.
local: Ke Yehowa ve Noa nu.

eng: In all, Seth lived years, and he died.
local: Eye Set ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo katã nye ƒe alafa asieke kple wuieve, eye wòku.

eng: From the clean and unclean animals, from the birds and everything crawling on the ground.
local: Lã matsrimatsriwo kple lã tsitsriwo kple xe dzodzoewo hekpe ɖe nusiwo katã tana le anyigba dzi la ŋuti.

eng: The LORD God planted a garden in Eden in the east and put there the human he had formed.
local: Tete Yehowa Mawu tso abɔ ɖe Eden le ɣedzeƒe, eye wotsɔ ame, si wòmè la, ɖo afima.

eng: During that days cool evening breeze, they heard the sound of the LORD God walking in the garden; and the man and his wife hid themselves from the LORD God in the middle of the gardens trees.
local: Eye esi wose Yehowa Mawu ŋkɔ le anyinufaɣi le abɔa me la, amea kple srɔ̃a woɣla wo ɖokui ɖe Yehowa Mawu ɖe abɔmetia ɖe te.

eng: God named the dry land Earth, and he named the gathered waters Seas. God saw how good it was.
local: Mawu yɔ ƒuƒuiƒe la be anyigba, ke tsi ƒoƒua la eyɔe be atsiaƒu. Eye Mawu kpo bena enyo.

eng: but the dove found no place to set its foot. It returned to him in the ark since waters still covered the entire earth. Noah stretched out his hand, took it, and brought it back into the ark.
local: Ke esi ahɔne la mekpɔ afisi wòadze ɖo o la, egatrɔ va egbɔ le aɖakaʋu la me, elabena tsi le anyigba blibo la katã dzi, eye wòdo asi ɖa lée de aɖakaʋu la me.

eng: Cain said to his brother Abel, Lets go out to the field. When they were in the field, Cain attacked his brother Abel and killed him.
local: Esia megbe la Kain gadɔ nu kple nɔvia Habel. Ke eva ve me bena, esime wole gbe me la, Kain tso ɖe nɔvia Habel ŋuti, eye wòwui.

eng: These are Noahs descendants. In his generation, Noah was a moral and exemplary man; he walked with God.
local: Esia enye Noa ƒe dzidzimeviwo ŋutinya Noa enye ame dzɔdzɔe kple ame maɖifɔ le eŋɔlimetɔwo dome, eye Noa zɔ kple Mawu.

eng: When Enosh was years old, he became the father of Kenan.
local: Eye esi Enos xɔ ƒe blaasieke la, edzi Kenan.

eng: two of each, male and female, went into the ark with Noah, just as God commanded Noah.
local: woyi ɖe Noa gbɔ atsu kple asi eveveve le aɖakaʋu la me, abe alesi Mawu de se na Noa ene.

eng: The LORD said to Cain, Where is your brother Abel? Cain said, I dont know. Am I my brothers guardian.
local: Tete Yehowa gblɔ na Kain bena: Afika noviwò Habel le? Eye wòɖo ŋu nɛ be: Nyemenyae o! Alo nɔvinye dzikpɔla menyea?.

eng: He waited seven more days and sent the dove out from the ark again.
local: Emegbe wòlala ŋkeke adre, eye wòʋu nu le ahɔnea nu le aɖakaʋu la me.

eng: God said, Let the earth grow plant life: plants yielding seeds and fruit trees bearing fruit with seeds inside it, each according to its kind throughout the earth. And thats what happened.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ be: Anyigba nemie gbe fẽwo, nu miemie, siwo tsea ku, kple ati tseku, siwo tsena wo tɔ wo tɔwoe, esiwo me ku le, le anyigba la dzi. Eye wòva me nenema.

eng: In the fertile land, the LORD God grew every beautiful tree with edible fruit, and also he grew the tree of life in the middle of the garden and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
local: Eye Yehowa Mawu na ati vovovo, siwo nya kpɔna, siwo ƒe tsetse nyo na ɖuɖu la, mie tso anyigba me; eye agbeti la kpakple sidzedze nyui kple vɔ̃ ƒe ati la le abɔ la titina.

eng: The LORD God called to the man and said to him, Where are you.
local: Tete Yehowa Mawu yɔ amea gblɔ nɛ bena: Afika nèle?.

eng: Noah had three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
local: Eye Noa dzi ŋutsuvi etɔ̃: Sem, Xam kple Yafet.

eng: After Kenans birth, Enosh lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Esi Enos dzi Kenan megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa enyi kple wuiatɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: After seven days, the floodwaters arrived on the earth.
local: Eye wòva me le ŋkeke adreagbe bena, tsi dza ɖe anyigba la dzi.

eng: The LORD said, What did you do? The voice of your brothers blood is crying to me from the ground.
local: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Nuka enye esi nèwɔ? Kpɔ ɖa, nɔviwò ƒe ʋu, si nèkɔ ɖe anyi la ƒe gbe le ɖiɖim tso anyigba dzi mese!.

eng: The earth produced plant life: plants yielding seeds, each according to its kind, and trees bearing fruit with seeds inside it, each according to its kind. God saw how good it was.
local: Anyigba mie gbe fẽwo, nu miemie, siwo tsea ku wo tɔ wo tɔwoe, kple ati, siwo tsena, siwo me ku le, wo tɔ wo tɔwoe. Eye Mawu kpo bena enyo.

eng: The dove came back to him in the evening, grasping a torn olive leaf in its beak. Then Noah knew that the waters were subsiding from the earth.
local: Eye ahɔne la va egbɔ le fiẽ me, eye kpɔ ɖa, amitiɖɔmee le enu. Azɔ Noa dze sii bena, tsia mie le anyigba dzi.

eng: A river flows from Eden to water the garden, and from there it divides into four headwaters.
local: Eye tɔsisia ɖe do tso Eden, be woade tsi abɔ la; eye tso afima wòdze ɖe alɔ ene me.

eng: The man replied, I heard your sound in the garden; I was afraid because I was naked, and I hid myself.
local: Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Mese ŋkɔwò le abɔa me, eye meva; elabena mele amama, eye meɣla ɖokuinye.

eng: In Gods sight, the earth had become corrupt and was filled with violence.
local: Eye xexeame gblẽ le Mawu ƒe ŋku me, eye nu vlo wɔwɔ xɔ anyigba la dzi.

eng: In all, Enosh lived years, and he died.
local: Eye Enos ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo katã nye ƒe alafa asieke kple atɔ̃, eye woku.

eng: In the six hundredth year of Noahs life, in the second month, on the seventeenth day—on that day all the springs of the deep sea erupted, and the windows in the skies opened.
local: Esi Noa xɔ ƒe alafa ade, le ɣleti evelia ƒe ŋkeke wuiadrelia dzi tututu la, anyigbatu me tsiwo katã ŋɔ, eye dziƒo nu ʋu.

eng: You are now cursed from the ground that opened its mouth to take your brothers blood from your hand.
local: Azã la woaƒo fi ade wò, adzo le anyigba, si ke eƒe nu xɔ nɔviwò ƒe ʋu le asiwò no la dzi.

eng: He waited seven more days and sent out the dove, but it didnt come back to him again.
local: Eye wògalala ŋkeke adre bubu, eye wògaʋu nu le ahɔnea nu, ke megado va egbɔ o.

eng: There was evening and there was morning: the third day.
local: Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke etɔ̃lia.

eng: The name of the first river is the Pishon. It flows around the entire land of Havilah, where there is gold.
local: Gbãtɔ ŋkɔ nye Pison; eƒo xlã Xawila-nyigba blibo la katã; afima sika le;.

eng: He said, Who told you that you were naked? Did you eat from the tree, which I commanded you not to eat.
local: Egblɔ be: Ameka toe na wò be, amamae nèle? Alo ɖe nèɖu ati, si ŋuti meɖe se na wò be, megaɖu eƒe ɖeke o la ƒe ɖe mahã?.

eng: It rained on the earth forty days and forty nights.
local: Eye tsi dzi ɖe anyigba dzi ŋkeke blane kple zã blane sɔŋ.

eng: When Kenan was years old, he became the father of Mahalalel.
local: Eye esi Kenan xɔ ƒe blaadre la, edzi Mahalalel.

eng: God saw that the earth was corrupt, because all creatures behaved corruptly on the earth.
local: Eye Mawu kpɔ bena, xexeame gblẽ keŋ; elabena amegbetɔwo katã ƒe zɔzɔme gblẽ le anyigba dzi.

eng: When you farm the fertile land, it will no longer grow anything for you, and you will become a roving nomad on the earth.
local: Ne eŋlɔ anyigba la, megale nu ʋẽ ge na wò o; tsatsala kple godzela nanye le anyigba dzi!.

eng: In Noahs six hundred first year, on the first day of the first month, the waters dried up from the earth. Noah removed the arks hatch and saw that the surface of the fertile land had dried up.
local: Le ƒe alafa ade kple ɖeka me le ɣleti gbãtɔ ƒe ŋkeke gbãtɔ dzi tsia mie le anyigba dzi. Eye Noa fo aɖakaʋu la tame ɖa, eye wotsa ŋku, eye kpɔ ɖa, anyigba dzi ƒu.

eng: God said, Let there be lights in the dome of the sky to separate the day from the night. They will mark events, sacred seasons, days, and years.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ be: Nu keklẽwo nado ɖe dziƒolilia ŋu, bena woade vovo ŋkeke kple zã dome, eye woanɔ anyi anye dzesiwo, afia azãgbewo, ŋkekewo kple ƒewo.

eng: That lands gold is pure, and the land also has sweet-smelling resins and gemstones.
local: eye anyigba sia ƒe sika nyo ŋutɔ; afima bedolax- kple soham-kpe hã le.

eng: The man said, The woman you gave me, she gave me some fruit from the tree, and I ate.
local: Ame la ɖo ŋu bena: Nyɔnu, si nètsɔ nam la, eyae na atia ɖem meɖu.

eng: That same day Noah, with his sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth, Noahs wife, and his sons three wives, went into the ark.
local: Le ŋkeke ma dzi tututu Noa kple viaŋutsuwo: Sem, Xam kple Yafet kple Noa srɔ̃ hekpe ɖe viaŋutsuawo srɔ̃ etɔ̃awo ŋuti, woyi ɖe aɖakaʋu la me;.

eng: After the birth of Mahalalel, Kenan lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Esi Kenan dzi Mahalalel megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa enyi kple blane, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: God said to Noah, The end has come for all creatures, since they have filled the earth with violence. I am now about to destroy them along with the earth.
local: Tete Mawu gblɔ na Noa bena: Ŋutilãwo katã ƒe nuwuwu ɖo azɔ, elabena woƒe nu vlo wɔwɔ xɔ anyigba la dzi; kpɔ ɖa, matsrɔ̃ wo kple anyigba siaa.

eng: Cain said to the LORD, My punishment is more than I can bear.
local: Eye Kain gblɔ na Yehowa bena: Nye vodada la kpe wu nye tsɔtsɔ.

eng: In the second month, on the twenty-seventh day, the earth was dry.
local: Le ɣleti evelia ƒe ŋkeke blave vɔ adrelia dzi anyigba dzi ƒu keŋkeŋ.

eng: The name of the second river is the Gihon. It flows around the entire land of Cush.
local: Tɔsisi evelia ŋkɔ nye Gixon; eƒo xlã Kus-nyigba blibo la katã.

eng: They will be lights in the dome of the sky to shine on the earth. And thats what happened.
local: eye woanye nu keklẽwo anɔ dziƒolilia ŋu, bene woaklẽ ɖe anyigba dzi! Eye wòva me nenem.

eng: The LORD God said to the woman, What have you done?! And the woman said, The snake tricked me, and I ate.
local: Eye Yehowa Mawu gblɔ na nyɔnua bena: Nukaŋuti nèwɔ alea? Nyɔnu la ɖo ŋu ne be: Da lae blem, eye meɖui.

eng: In all, Kenan lived years, and he died.
local: Eye Kenan ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo katã nye ƒe alafa asieke kple ewo, eye woku.

eng: They and every kind of animal—every kind of livestock, every kind that crawls on the ground, every kind of bird.
local: woawo kple gbemelãwo katã le woƒe ƒomeviwo nu kple aƒemelãwo katã le woƒe ƒomeviwo nu kple nu tata, siwo katã tana le anyigba dzi, le woƒe ƒomeviwo nu hekpe ɖe xewo kple nusiwo katã dzona la ŋuti le woƒe ƒomeviwo nu.

eng: so make a wooden ark. Make the ark with nesting places and cover it inside and out with tar.
local: Tsɔ dzatiwo kpa aɖakaʋu na ɖokuiwò, eye natso eme keŋ, atsɔ aŋɔ atre eme kple ekɔgo.

eng: Now that youve driven me away from the fertile land and I am hidden from your presence, Im about to become a roving nomad on the earth, and anyone who finds me will kill me.
local: Wòe nyãm egbe tso agbledenyigba la dzi; madzo le wò ŋkuta anye tsatsala kple godzela le anyigba dzi, eye ava me bena, amesiame, si akpɔm la, awum.

eng: God spoke to Noah.
local: Eye Mawu ƒo nu kple Noa gblɔ bena:.

eng: God made the stars and two great lights: the larger light to rule over the day and the smaller light to rule over the night.
local: Tete Mawu wɔ nu keklẽ gã eveawo. Ewɔ gãtɔ, bena woaɖu ŋkeke dzi, kple suetɔ, bena woaɖu zã dzi, hekpe ɖe ɣletiviwo ŋu.

eng: The name of the third river is the Tigris, flowing east of Assyria; and the name of the fourth river is the Euphrates.
local: Tɔsisi etɔ̃lia ŋkɔ nye Xidekel; esi to Asur ƒe ɣedzeƒe gome. Eye tɔsisi enelia nye Frat.

eng: The LORD God said to the snake, Because you did this, you are the one cursed out of all the farm animals, out of all the wild animals. On your belly you will crawl, and dust you will eat every day of your life.
local: Eye Yehowa Mawu gblɔ na da la bena: Esi nèwɔ alea la, eyata woaƒo fi ade wò le aƒemelãwo kple gbemelãwo katã dome: Wò ƒodo dzi nazɔ, eye ʋuʋudedie naɖu le wò agbe me ŋkekewo katã me!.

eng: When Mahalalel was years old, he became the father of Jared.
local: Eye esi Mahalalel xɔ ƒe blaade vɔ atɔ̃ la, edzi Yered.

eng: they came to Noah and entered the ark, two of every creature that breathes.
local: Eye lãwo tsyo ƒomevi, siwo me agbegbɔgbɔ le la, woyi de Noa gbɔ eveveve le aɖakaʋu la me.

eng: This is how you should make it: four hundred fifty feet long, seventy-five feet wide, and forty-five feet high.
local: Eye ale nawɔe enye si; Aɖakaʋu la ƒe didime nanye abɔklugui alafa etɔ̃, eƒe kekeme nanye abɔklugui blaatɔ̃, eye eƒe kɔkɔme nanye abɔklugui blaetɔ̃.

eng: The LORD said to him, It wont happen; anyone who kills Cain will be paid back seven times. The LORD put a sign on Cain so that no one who found him would assault him.
local: Eye Yehowa gblɔ nɛ bena: Eyata amesiame, si awu Kain la, woabia hlɔ̃e zi gbɔ zi adre. Eye Yehowa de dzesi Kain, bena amesiame, si ado goe la, nagawui o.

eng: Go out of the ark, you and your wife, your sons, and your sons wives with you.
local: Do go le aɖakaʋu la me, wò ŋutɔ kple srɔ̃wo kple viwòŋutsuwo hekpe ɖe viwòŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo ŋuti!.

eng: The LORD God took the human and settled him in the garden of Eden to farm it and to take care of it.
local: Eye Yehowa Mawu tsɔ ame la ɖo Eɖen-bɔ me, be woaŋlɔ eme, eye wòakpɔ edzi.

eng: God put them in the dome of the sky to shine on the earth.
local: Eye Mawu tsɔ wo ɖo dziƒolilia ŋu, bena woaklẽ ɖe anyigba dzi.

eng: After Jareds birth, Mahalalel lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Esi Mahalalel dzi Yered megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa enyi kple blaetɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: I will put contempt between you and the woman, between your offspring and hers. They will strike your head, but you will strike at their heels.
local: Made adikã mia kple nyɔnu la dome, wò dzidzime kple nyɔnu la ƒe dzidzime dome, eya la agbã ta na wò, eye wò la àɖu afɔkpodzi nɛ.

eng: Male and female of every creature went in, just as God had commanded him. Then the LORD closed the door behind them.
local: Eye lã, siwo yi ɖe egbɔ la, wonye atsu kple asi; lã vovovowo yi ɖe egbɔ, abe alesi Mawu ɖe se nɛ ene. Eye Yehowa do ʋɔ ɖe enu.

eng: Make a roof for the ark and complete it one foot from the top. Put a door in its side. In the hold below, make the second and third decks.
local: Eye naɖe fesre, siwo akɔ abɔklugui ɖeka la, ɖe aɖakaʋu la ƒe gliwo dzi aƒo xlãe godoo va kpe; eye naɖe ʋɔtru aɖakaʋu la ƒe axa dzi, eye nawɔ dzisasrã etɔ̃, etetɔ, titinatɔ kple elametɔ.

eng: Cain left the LORDs presence, and he settled down in the land of Nod, east of Eden.
local: Eye Kain dzo le Yehowa ƒe ŋku me, eye wòva nɔ Nod-nyigba dzi le Eden ƒe ɣedzeƒe gome.

eng: Bring out with you all the animals of every kind—birds, livestock, everything crawling on the ground—so that they may populate the earth, be fertile, and multiply on the earth.
local: Lã vovovo, siwo katã le gbɔwò; xe dzodzoewo kple aƒemelãwo kpakple nu tata, siwo katã tana le anyigba dzi la, nado go kpakpli wò, ne woaʋã wo ɖokui le anyigba dzi, eye wòadzi asɔ gbɔ le anyigba dzi!.

eng: The LORD God commanded the human, Eat your fill from all of the gardens trees.
local: Eye Yehowa Mawu de se na ame gblɔ bena: Ɖu abɔmetiawo katã ƒe tsetse faa;.

eng: to rule over the day and over the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. God saw how good it was.
local: woaɖu ŋkeke kple zã dzi, eye woamã kekeli kple viviti dome. Eye Mawu kpɔ bena enyo.

eng: In all, Mahalalel lived years, and he died.
local: Eye Mahalalel ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo katã nye ƒe alafa enyi kple blaasieke vɔ atɔ̃, eye woku.

eng: The flood remained on the earth for forty days. The waters rose, lifted the ark, and it rode high above the earth.
local: Eye tsia le anyigba la ɖem ŋkeke blane sɔŋ, eye tsia le agbɔ sɔm hekɔ aɖakaʋu la ɖe dzi, ale bena, aɖakaʋu la le tsia ŋgɔ.

eng: To the woman he said, I will make your pregnancy very painful; in pain you will bear children. You will desire your husband, but he will rule over you.
local: Eye wògblɔ na nyɔnua bena: Mana nakpe fu geɖe le fufɔfɔ me, alé ku hafi àdzi vi, ke wò tame anɔ srɔ̃wò ŋu, eye eya anye gã ɖe dziwò.

eng: I am now bringing the floodwaters over the earth to destroy everything under the sky that breathes. Everything on earth is about to take its last breath.
local: Elabena nye la mana tsi naɖe anyigba la, bena ŋutilã, siwo katã le dziƒoa te, esiwo me agbegbɔgbɔ le la, natsrɔ̃, eye anyigba dzi nuwo katã naku.

eng: Cain knew his wife intimately. She became pregnant and gave birth to Enoch. Cain built a city and named the city after his son Enoch.
local: Eye Kain da kple srɔ̃a, eye wòfɔ fu dzi Henox. Eye wòtso du, tso via ƒe ŋkɔ na du la bena Henox.

eng: So Noah went out of the ark with his sons, his wife, and his sons wives.
local: Tete Noa do go, eye viaŋutsuwo kple srɔ̃a kpakple viaŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo hã kpe ɖe eŋuti.

eng: but dont eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, because on the day you eat from it, you will die.
local: ke ati, si na wodzea si nyui kple vɔ la, megaɖu eya ƒe tsetsea ɖeke o; gbesigbe naɖu eƒe ɖe, ku ke naku.

eng: To the man he said, Because you listened to your wifes voice and you ate from the tree that I commanded, Dont eat from it, cursed is the fertile land because of you; in pain you will eat from it every day of your life.
local: Eye wògblɔ na Adam bena: Esi nèse srɔ̃wo ƒe gbe, eye nèɖu ati, si ŋuti mede se na wò be, megaɖu eƒe ɖekeɖeke o la ƒe ɖe la, eyata woaƒo fi ade anyigba le tawò, àkpe fu hafi akpɔ nu àɖu tso me le wò agbe me ŋkekewo katã me.

eng: There was evening and there was morning: the fourth day.
local: Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke enelia.

eng: When Jared was years old, he became the father of Enoch.
local: Eye esi Yered xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blaade vɔ eve la, edzi Henox.

eng: The waters rose and spread out over the earth. The ark floated on the surface of the waters.
local: Eye tsia le ɖɔɖɔm ɖe dzi hesɔ gbɔ ɖe anyigba la dzi, eye aɖakaʋu la le tsatsam le tsia ŋgɔ.

eng: But I will set up my covenant with you. You will go into the ark together with your sons, your wife, and your sons wives.
local: Ke mabia nu kpli wò bena, nayi ɖe aɖakaʋu la me, wò ŋutɔ, viwòŋutsuwo kple srɔ̃wò hekpe ɖe viwòŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo ŋu.

eng: Irad was born to Enoch. Irad fathered Mehujael, Mehujael fathered Methushael, and Methushael fathered Lamech.
local: Eye wodzi Irad na Henox, eye Irad dzi Mehuyael, eye Mehuyael dzi Metusael, eye Metusael dzi Lamex.

eng: All the animals, all the livestock, all the birds, and everything crawling on the ground, came out of the ark by their families.
local: Lãwo katã, nu tata wo katã kple xe dzodzoewo katã kpakple nusi wo katã zɔna le anyigba dzi la, wodo go le aɖakaʋu la me hamehame.

eng: Then the LORD God said, Its not good that the human is alone. I will make him a helper that is perfect for him.
local: Eye Yehowa Mawu gblɔ bena: Menyo bena ame la natsi akogo o; mawɔ kpeɖeŋutɔ, si asɔ ɖe eŋu la nɛ.

eng: Weeds and thistles will grow for you, even as you eat the fields plants.
local: Ŋu kple aŋɔka amie na wò, eye naɖu anyigba dzi amagbewo!.

eng: God said, Let the waters swarm with living things, and let birds fly above the earth up in the dome of the sky.
local: Tete Mawu gblɔ bena: Nu gbagbewo neƒu tsi le tsia me, eye xe dzodzoewo nedzo le ya me le dziƒolilia ƒe ŋku me.

eng: The waters rose even higher over the earth; they covered all of the highest mountains under the sky.
local: Eye tsia do agbogbo de anyigba dzi ŋutɔŋutɔ hede to kɔkɔ, siwo katã le dziƒoa te la.

eng: After Enochs birth, Jared lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Esi Yered dzi Henox megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa enyi, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: From all living things—from all creatures—you are to bring a pair, male and female, into the ark with you to keep them alive.
local: Ɖe nu gbagbewo katã kple ŋutilãwo katã eveveve yi ɖe aɖakaʋu la me, ne woanɔ agbe kpli wò; woanye atsu kple asi.

eng: Lamech took two wives, the first named Adah and the second Zillah.
local: Ke Lamex ɖe srɔ̃ eve; ɖeka ŋkɔ enye Ada, eye evelia ŋkɔ enye Zila.

eng: Noah built an altar to the LORD. He took some of the clean large animals and some of the clean birds, and placed entirely burned offerings on the altar.
local: Tete Noa ɖi vɔsamlekpui, eye wòtsɔ lã matsrimatsriwo kple xe matsrimatsriwo ƒe ɖewo na Yehowa hemè wo le vɔsamlekpui la dzi.

eng: by the sweat of your face you will eat bread— until you return to the fertile land, since from it you were taken; you are soil, to the soil you will return.
local: Fifia ate wò, hafi nakpɔ abolo aɖu, vaseɖe esime nagatrɔ azu anyi, elabena eya mee woɖe wò tsoe. Anyi nènye, eye anyi ke nagatrɔ azu!.

eng: So the LORD God formed from the fertile land all the wild animals and all the birds in the sky and brought them to the human to see what he would name them. The human gave each living being its name.
local: Tete Yehowa Mawu tsɔ anyi mè gbemelãwo katã kple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo katã, eye wòkplɔ wo va amea gbɔe, be yeakpɔ alesi wòayɔ woe; eye alesi ame la yɔ nu gbagbeawoe la, nenema anye woƒe ŋkɔ.

eng: God created the great sea animals and all the tiny living things that swarm in the waters, each according to its kind, and all the winged birds, each according to its kind. God saw how good it was.
local: Eye Mawu wo ƒumelã klitsuwo kple nu gbagbe, siwo katã tana, eye woʋãna le tsia me, hamehame, kple xe, siwo katã dzona. Eye Mawu kpo bena enyo.

eng: The waters rose twenty-three feet high, covering the mountains.
local: Eye tsia dɔ gbɔ to kɔkɔwo ta me abɔklugui wuiatɔ̃; ale wɔ, eye towo katã bu de tsia me.

eng: In all, Jared lived years, and he died.
local: Eye Yered ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo katã nye ƒe alafa asieke kple blaade vɔ eve, eye woku.

eng: From each kind of bird, from each kind of livestock, and from each kind of everything that crawls on the ground—a pair from each will go in with you to stay alive.
local: Dziƒoxe dzodzoewo le woƒe ƒomeviwo nu, aƒemelãwo le woƒe ƒomeviwo nu kple nu, siwo katã tana le anyigba la dzi, le woƒe ƒomeviwo nu, wo dome eveveve woava gbɔwò, bena woanɔ agbe.

eng: Adah gave birth to Jabal; he was the ancestor of those who live in tents and own livestock.
local: Eye Ada dzi Yabal, amesi nye agbadɔ me nɔlawo kple lãnyilawo fofo.

eng: The LORD smelled the pleasing scent, and the LORD thought to himself, I will not curse the fertile land anymore because of human beings since the ideas of the human mind are evil from their youth. I will never again destroy every living thing as I have done.
local: Ke esi Yehowa se ʋeʋẽ lililia la, egblɔ le eƒe dzi me bena: Nyemagaƒo fi ade anyigba akpɔ le amegbetɔ ta o. Elabena ame ƒe dzimesusu enye vɔ̃ tso ɖevime ke, eye nyemagatsrɔ̃ nu gbagbewo katã, abe alesi mewo ene o.

eng: The human named all the livestock, all the birds in the sky, and all the wild animals. But a helper perfect for him was nowhere to be found.
local: Eye ame la tsɔ ŋkɔ na aƒemelãwo katã kple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo kpakple gbemelãwo katã; ke amea ya mekpɔ kpeɖeŋutɔ, si asɔ nɛ o.

eng: The man named his wife Eve because she is the mother of everyone who lives.
local: Eye ame la tsɔ ŋkɔ na srɔ̃a bena Xawa, elabena eya ava nye ame gbagbewo katã dada.

eng: Then God blessed them: Be fertile and multiply and fill the waters in the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth.
local: Eye Mawu yra wo gblɔ bena: Midzi, ne miasɔ gbɔ, eye miayɔ atsiaƒutsia me, eye xe dzodzoeawo nasɔ gbã le anyigba dzi!.

eng: When Enoch was years old, he became the father of Methuselah.
local: Eye esi Henox xa ƒe blaade vo atɔ̃ la, edzi Metusala.

eng: Every creature took its last breath: the things crawling on the ground, birds, livestock, wild animals, everything swarming on the ground, and every human being.
local: Eye nu gbagbe, siwo ʋãa wo ɖokui le anyigba dzi; xe dzodzoewo, aƒemelãwo, gbemelãwo kple nusiwo katã tana le anyigba dzi, hekpe ɖe amewo ŋu, wo katã wotsi tɔ la me.

eng: Take some from every kind of food and stow it as food for you and for the animals.
local: Ke wò la tsɔ nuɖuɖu ƒomevi vovovo, siwo katã woɖuna la, dzra ɖo ɖe gbɔwò, bena woanye nuɖuɖu na mia kpli wo siaa.

eng: His brothers name was Jubal; he was the ancestor of those who play stringed and wind instruments.
local: Enɔvia ŋkɔ nye Yubal, amesi nye saŋkuƒolawo kple dzekulawo fofo.

eng: As long as the earth exists, seedtime and harvest, cold and hot, summer and autumn, day and night will not cease.
local: Tso esia dzi, ŋkeke alesi anyigba li la, nuƒaƒã kple nuŋeŋe, viwo kple fifia, dzomeŋɔli kple pepi, ŋkeke kple zã nu matsi o.

eng: So the LORD God put the human into a deep and heavy sleep, and took one of his ribs and closed up the flesh over it.
local: Eye Yehowa Mawu na ame dɔ alɔ̃ yi me ʋĩ, eye wòɖe eƒe agbaƒutiwo dometɔ ɖeka, eye wotsɔ lã ɖo eteƒe.

eng: The LORD God made the man and his wife leather clothes and dressed them.
local: Eye Yehowa Mawu tsɔ lãgbalẽwu do na Adam kple srɔ̃a.

eng: There was evening and there was morning: the fifth day.
local: Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke atɔ̃lia.

eng: Noah did everything exactly as God commanded him.
local: Eye Noa wɔe; alesi Mawu de se nɛ la, nenema pɛ wòwɔ.

eng: Enoch walked with God. After Methuselahs birth, Enoch lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Esi wòdzi Metusala megbe la, Henox zɔ kple Mawu ƒe alafa etɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: Everything on dry land with lifes breath in its nostrils died.
local: Nusianu, si agbegbɔgbɔ le eme, si gbɔna toa ŋɔti me, eye wonɔa agbe le anyigba ƒuƒui dzi la ku.

eng: Zillah also gave birth to Tubal-cain, the ancestor of blacksmiths and all artisans of bronze and iron. Tubal-cains sister was Naamah.
local: Eye Zila hã dzi Tubalkain, amesi nye akɔbli- kple gaŋudowolawo fofo; eye Tubalkain nɔvinyɔnu ŋkɔ nye Naama.

eng: With the rib taken from the human, the LORD God fashioned a woman and brought her to the human being.
local: Tete Yehowa Mawu tsɔ agbaƒuti, si wòɖe le amea me la, wɔ nyɔnui, eye wòkplɔe va ame la gbɔe.

eng: The LORD God said, The human being has now become like one of us, knowing good and evil. Now, so he doesnt stretch out his hand and take also from the tree of life and eat and live forever.
local: Eye Yehowa Mawu gblɔ be: Kpɔ ɖa, ame zu sigbe mía dometɔ ɖeka ene, eye wònya nyui kple vɔ. Ke be wòagaɖo asi ɖa agbe agbetia hã ƒe ɖe aɖu, eye wòanɔ agbe ɖoɖoɖo o la.

eng: God said, Let the earth produce every kind of living thing: livestock, crawling things, and wildlife. And thats what happened.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ bena: Nu gbagbewo nedo tso anyigba me hamehame, aƒemelãwo kple nu tatawo kple gbemelãwo hamehame!.

eng: God wiped away every living thing that was on the fertile land—from human beings to livestock to crawling things to birds in the sky. They were wiped off the earth. Only Noah and those with him in the ark were left.
local: Nusianu, si nɔ anyigba dzi, tso amewo dzi vaseɖe aƒemelãwo kple nu tatawo kpakple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo dzi, wotsrɔ̃ wo ɖa le anyigba dzi; negbe Noa kple nusiwo le egbɔ le aɖakaʋua me koe tsi agbe.

eng: In all, Enoch lived years.
local: Eye Henox ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo katã nye ƒe alafa etɔ̃ kple blaade vɔ atɔ̃.

eng: Lamech said to his wives, Adah and Zillah, listen to my voice; wives of Lamech, pay attention to my words: I killed a man for wounding me, a boy for striking me.
local: Eye Lamex gblɔ na srɔ̃wo bena: Ada kple Zila, miɖo to nye gbe; mi Lamex srɔ̃wo, mise ye nyawo! Vavã, mewu ŋutsu ɖe nye abi teƒe kple ɖekakpui ɖe nye dodro teƒe.

eng: The human said, This one finally is bone from my bones and flesh from my flesh. She will be called a woman because from a man she was taken.
local: Eye ame la gblɔ be: Azɔ ko! Esia enye ƒu tso nye ƒu me kple lã tso nye lã me; woayɔ ame sia bena nyɔnu, elabena ŋutsu mee woɖee tsoe.

eng: the LORD God sent him out of the garden of Eden to farm the fertile land from which he was taken.
local: Yehowa Mawu ɖe ame ɖa tso Eden-bɔ la me, bene woaŋlɔ anyigba, si me woɖee tsoe.

eng: God made every kind of wildlife, every kind of livestock, and every kind of creature that crawls on the ground. God saw how good it was.
local: Ale Mawu wɔ gbemelãwo hamehame, aƒemelãwo hamehame kple lã, siwo katã tana le anyigba dzi hamehame. Eye Mawu kpɔ bena enyo.

eng: Enoch walked with God and disappeared because God took him.
local: Esi Henox zɔ kple Mawu ta la, ebu vĩ, elabena Mawu kplɔe dzoe.

eng: The waters rose over the earth for one hundred fifty days.
local: Eye tsia le ɖɔɖɔm ɖe anyigba dzi ŋkeke alafa ɖeka kple blaatɔ̃ sɔŋ.

eng: so Cain will be paid back seven times and Lamech seventy-seven times.
local: Elabena wòbia hlɔ̃ Kain zi adre, ke Lamex la zi blaadre vɔ adre.

eng: This is the reason that a man leaves his father and mother and embraces his wife, and they become one flesh.
local: Eya ŋuti ŋutsu agblẽ fofoa kple dadaa ɖi ahaku ɖe srɔ̃a ŋuti, eye woanye ŋutilã ɖeka.

eng: Then God said, Let us make humanity in our image to resemble us so that they may take charge of the fish of the sea, the birds in the sky, the livestock, all the earth, and all the crawling things on earth.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ be: Mine míawɔ ame ɖe míaƒe nɔnɔme, woaɖi mí, eye wòaɖu ƒumelãwo, dziƒoxe dzodzoewo, aƒemelãwo kple gbemelãwo katã kpakple lã, siwo katã tana le anyigba dzi la dzi!.

eng: When Methuselah was years old, he became the father of Lamech.
local: Eye esi Metusala xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blanyi vɔ adre la, edzi Lamex.

eng: He drove out the human. To the east of the garden of Eden, he stationed winged creatures wielding flaming swords to guard the way to the tree of life.
local: Tete wònyã ame la, eye wòna Kerubiwo tsɔ yi, si trɔa eɖokui, eye wòklẽna abe dzo ƒe aɖe ene la, ɖanɔ Eden-bɔ la ƒe ɣedzeƒe gome, bena woadzɔ agbeti la mɔ nu.

eng: Adam knew his wife intimately again, and she gave birth to a son. She named him Seth because God has given me another child in place of Abel, whom Cain killed.
local: Eye Adam gadɔ kple srɔ̃a, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Set; elabena egblɔ be: Mawu ɖo dzidzime bubu Habel, amesi Kain wu la teƒe nam.

eng: The two of them were naked, the man and his wife, but they werent embarrassed.
local: Eye wo kpakple eve la wole ƒeƒle, ame la kple srɔ̃a, eye ŋu mekpea wo o.

eng: After Lamechs birth, Methuselah lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Esi Metusala dzi Lamex megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa adre kple blanyi vɔ eve, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: God created humanity in Gods own image, in the divine image God created them, male and female God created them.
local: Eye Mawu wɔ ame ɖe ye ŋutɔ ƒe nɔnɔme, Mawu ƒe nɔnɔme nue wòwɔe ɖo; ŋutsu kple nyɔnu wòwɔ.

eng: Seth also fathered a son and named him Enosh. At that time, people began to worship in the LORDs name.
local: Eye wodzi ŋutsuvi na Set hã, eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Enos. Le ɣemaɣi me la wode asi Yehowa ŋkɔ yɔyɔ me.

eng: In all, Methuselah lived years, and he died.
local: Eye Metusala ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo katã nye ƒe alafa asieke kple blaade vɔ asieke, eye wòku.

eng: God blessed them and said to them, Be fertile and multiply; fill the earth and master it. Take charge of the fish of the sea, the birds in the sky, and everything crawling on the ground.
local: Eye Mawu yra wo, eye Mawu gblɔ na wo bena: Midzi, ne miasɔ gbɔ, eye miayɔ anyigba la dzi, eye mianye agbo ɖe edzi, miaɖu ƒumelãwo kple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo kple lã, siwo katã zɔna le anyigba dzi la dzi!.

eng: Then God said, I now give to you all the plants on the earth that yield seeds and all the trees whose fruit produces its seeds within it. These will be your food.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ be: Kpɔ ɖa, metsɔ nu miemie tseku, siwo katã le anyigba blibo la dzi, kple ati, siwo katã tsena, siwo me ku le la, na mi, bena woanye nuɖuɖu na mi;.

eng: When Lamech was years old, he became the father of a son.
local: Eye esi Lamex xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blanyi vɔ eve la, edzi ŋutsuvia ɖe.

eng: To all wildlife, to all the birds in the sky, and to everything crawling on the ground—to everything that breathes—I give all the green grasses for food. And thats what happened.
local: ke gbewo kple amagbewo katã nanye nuɖuɖu na anyigba dzi lãwo katã kple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo katã kple nusiwo katã tana, siwo me agbe le, le anyigba dzi. Eye wòva me nenema.

eng: and named him Noah, saying, This one will give us relief from our hard work, from the pain in our hands, because of the fertile land that the LORD cursed.
local: Eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Noa, elabena egblɔ be: Ame sia ana gbɔdzɔe mí tso míaƒe dɔwɔwɔ kple fuɖeɖewo me le anyigba, si Yehowa ƒo fi de la dzi.

eng: After Noahs birth, Lamech lived years; and he had other sons and daughters.
local: Esi Lamex dzi Noa megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa atɔ̃ kple blaasieke vɔ atɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: God saw everything he had made: it was supremely good. There was evening and there was morning: the sixth day.
local: Eye Mawu kpo nusiwo katã wòwɔ la, eye kpɔ ɖa, wonye ŋutɔ. Ale zã do, eye ŋu ke, eya nye ŋkeke adelia.

eng: In all, Lamech lived years, and he died.
local: Eye Lamex ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo katã nye ƒe alafa adre kple blaadre vɔ adre, eye wòku.

eng: When Noah was years old, Noah became the father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
local: Esi Noa xɔ ƒe alafa atɔ̃ la, edzi Sem, Xam kple Yafet.

eng: God blessed Noah and his sons and said to them, Be fertile, multiply, and fill the earth.
local: Eye Mawu yra Noa kple viaŋutsuawo, eye wògblɔ na wo bena: Midzi, ne miasɔ gbe, eye miayɔ anyigba la dzi!.

eng: These are the descendants of Noahs sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth, to whom children were born after the flood.
local: Esia nye Noa viŋutsuwo, Sem, Xam kple Yafet ƒe dzidzimeviwo ŋutinya: Le tsiɖɔɖɔ la megbe la wodzi ŋutsuviwo na wo.

eng: All of the animals on the earth will fear you and dread you—all the birds in the skies, everything crawling on the ground, and all of the seas fish. They are in your power.
local: Metsɔ anyigba dzi lãwo katã kple dziƒoxe dzodzoewo katã kple nusiwo katã tana le anyigba dzi, kple tɔmelãwo katã de asi na mi, eye mianye vɔvɔ̃ kple ŋɔdzi na wo.

eng: Japheths sons: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
local: Yaƒet viŋutsuwo nye: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Yawan, Tubal, Mesex kple Tiras.

eng: Everything that lives and moves will be your food. Just as I gave you the green grasses, I now give you everything.
local: Nu gbagbe, siwo katã ʋãna, nenema nu siawo katã metsɔ na mi, bena woanye miaƒe nuɖuɖu abe amagbewo ke ene.

eng: Gomers sons: Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
local: Gomer viŋutsuwo nye: Askenaz, Rifat kple Togarma.

eng: However, you must not eat meat with its life, its blood, in it.
local: Hafi lã, si ʋu tsi lã me na la, migaɖui o!.

eng: All people on the earth had one language and the same words.
local: Ke gbe ɖeka kple nuƒoƒo ɖeka ŋugbe le amegbetɔwo katã nu.

eng: Javans sons: Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Rodanim.
local: Yawan viŋutsu wo nye: Elisa, Tarsis, Kititowo kple Dodanitɔwo.

eng: I will surely demand your blood for a human life, from every living thing I will demand it. From humans, from a man for his brother, I will demand something for a human life.
local: Ke mia ŋutɔwo miaƒe ʋu la, mabiae le lãwo kple amewo siaa si, mabia amesiame ƒe agbe le nɔvia si.

eng: The LORD said to Abram, Leave your land, your family, and your fathers household for the land that I will show you.
local: Eye Yehowa gblɔ na Abram bena: Ʋu le wò anyigba dzi kple ƒowòmeawo gbɔ kple fofowò ƒe me, eye nayi anyigba, si mafia wò la dzi!.

eng: When they traveled east, they found a valley in the land of Shinar and settled there.
local: Eye wòva me bena, esi wodze mo ɖo ta ɣedzeƒe gome la, wokpɔ gbadzaƒea ɖe le Sinear-nyigba dzi, eye wonɔ afima.

eng: From these the island-nations were divided into their own countries, each according to their languages and their clans within their nations.
local: Amesiawo me dukɔ, siwo nɔa ƒukpowo dzi la, dzɔ tso. Esiawo nye Yafet viŋutsuwo le woƒe anyigbawo, woƒe gbe vovovowo, woƒe ƒomewo kple woƒe dukɔwo nu.

eng: Abram went up from Egypt toward the arid southern plain with his wife, with everything he had, and with Lot.
local: Ale Abram ʋu le Egipte kple srɔ̃ kple nusiwo katã le esi la, hekpe ɖe Lot ŋuti, gatrɔ va dziehe gome.

eng: While Amraphel was king of Shinar, Ellasars King Arioch, Elams King Chedorlaomer, and Goiims King Tidal.
local: Eye wòva me le ŋkeke mawo me bena, Amrafel, Sinear-fia, Ariok, Elasar-fia, Kedorlaomer, Elam-fia kple Tideal, Goyim-fia.

eng: Whoever sheds human blood, by a human his blood will be shed; for in the divine image God made human beings.
local: Amesi kɔ ameʋu ɖi la, amewo akɔ eya hã ƒe ʋu ɖi; elabena Mawu ƒe nɔnɔme nue wowɔ ame ɖo.

eng: I will make of you a great nation and will bless you. I will make your name respected, and you will be a blessing.
local: Mawɔ wò dukɔ gã, eye mayra wò, mana wò ŋkɔ nanyɔ, eye nazu yayra.

eng: They said to each other, Come, lets make bricks and bake them hard. They used bricks for stones and asphalt for mortar.
local: Eye wogblɔ na wo nɔewo bena: Mina míamè anyikpe, eye míaƒo dzoe! Ale anyikpe zu xɔtukpe na wo, eye aŋɔ le akaloba teƒe na wo.

eng: Hams sons: Cush, Egypt, Put, and Canaan.
local: Xam viŋutsuwo nye: Kus, Mizrayim, Put kple Kanaan.

eng: Abram was very wealthy in livestock, silver, and gold.
local: Eye lãwo, klosalo kple sika geɖe le Abram si taŋ.

eng: declared war on Sodoms King Bera, Gomorrahs King Birsha, Admahs King Shinab, Zeboiims King Shemeber, and the king of Bela, that is, Zoar.
local: wova wɔa aʋa kple Bera, Sodom-fia, Birsa, Gomora-fia, Sineab, Adma-fia, Semeber, Zeboyim-fia, kple Bela- alo Zoar-fia.

eng: As for you, be fertile and multiply. Populate the earth and multiply in it.
local: Ke miawo la midzi, ne miasɔ gbɔ, miʋã mia ɖokui le anyigba dzi, eye mikeke ta ɖe edzi!.

eng: I will bless those who bless you, those who curse you I will curse; all the families of the earth will be blessed because of you.
local: Mayra amesiwo yra wò, ke amesiwo doa vlo wò la, maƒo fi ade wo, eye le wò me woayra anyigba dzi meawo katã le.

eng: They said, Come, lets build for ourselves a city and a tower with its top in the sky, and lets make a name for ourselves so that we wont be dispersed over all the earth.
local: Eye wogblɔ bena: Mina míatso du, eye miatu mɔ̃ kɔkɔa ɖe, si tame nato dziƒo, ne míaƒe ŋkɔ nanyɔ, bena míagakaka ɖe anyigba blibo la katã dzi o!.

eng: Cushs sons: Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabteca. Raamahs sons: Sheba and Dedan.
local: Kus viŋutsuwo nye: Seba, Xawila, Sabta, Rama kple Sabtexa; Raema viŋutsuwo nye Seba kple Dedan.

eng: Abram traveled, making and breaking camp, from the arid southern plain to Bethel and to the sacred place there, where he had first pitched his tent between Bethel and Ai.
local: Eye wòzɔ tso dziehe gome vaseɖe Betel, vaseɖe teƒe, si wòtu eƒe agbadɔ ɖo tsã, le Betel kple Ai dome.

eng: These latter kings formed an alliance in the Siddim Valley .
local: Nenem me siawo katã bla nu va Sidim-gbadzaƒe, afisi Dzeƒu le azɔ.

eng: God said to Noah and to his sons with him.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ na Noa kple viaŋutsuawo bena:.

eng: Abram left just as the LORD told him, and Lot went with him. Now Abram was years old when he left Haran.
local: Tete Abram ʋu, abe alesi Yehowa gblɔ nɛ ene, eye Lot hã yi kplii. Abram xɔ ƒe blaadre vo atɔ̃, esime wòʋu tso Xaran.

eng: Then the LORD came down to see the city and the tower that the humans built.
local: Tete Yehowa ɖi va anyi, be yeakpɔ du kple mɔ̃ kɔkɔ, si amegbetɔviwo le tutum la ɖa.

eng: Cush fathered Nimrod, the first great warrior on earth.
local: Kus dzi Nimrod; ame sia de asi gãɖuɖu me le anyigba dzi.

eng: that is, to the place at which he had earlier built the altar. There he worshipped in the LORDs name.
local: afisi wòɖi vɔsamlekpui ɖo gbã. Eye Abram yɔ Yehowa ƒe ŋkɔ le afima.

eng: For twelve years they had served Chedorlaomer, and in the thirteenth year they revolted.
local: Fe wuieve sɔŋ wosubɔ Kedorlaomer; ke le ƒe wuietɔ̃lia me la wodze aglã.

eng: Abram took his wife Sarai, his nephew Lot, all of their possessions, and those who became members of their household in Haran; and they set out for the land of Canaan. When they arrived in Canaan.
local: Eye Abram kplɔ srɔ̃a Sarai, kple tɔgãyɔvia Lot, eye wotsɔ woƒe kesinɔnuwo katã kple amesiwo katã wokpɔ le Xaran la, eye woʋu, be wòayi Kanaan-nyigba dzi, eye wova Kanaan-nyigba la dzi.

eng: I am now setting up my covenant with you, with your descendants.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, mebia nu kpli mi kple míaƒe dzidzimeviwo.

eng: And the LORD said, There is now one people and they all have one language. This is what they have begun to do, and now all that they plan to do will be possible for them.
local: Eye Yehowa gblɔ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, dukɔ ɖeka wonye, eye gbe ɖeka le wo katã nu, eye esia nye woƒe nuwɔwɔ ƒe gɔmedzedze ko; azɔ nusianu, si dim wole be, woawɔ la, womado kpoe o.

eng: The LORD saw him as a great hunter, and so it is said, Like Nimrod, whom the LORD saw as a great hunter.
local: Ame sia enye adela kalẽtɔ le Yehowa ƒe ŋku me, eyata wogblɔna bena: Adela kalẽtɔ le Yehowa ƒe ŋku me abe Nimrod ene.

eng: Now Lot, who traveled with Abram, also had flocks, cattle, and tents.
local: Ke Lot, amesi ʋu kple Abram hã, kpɔ alẽwo, nyiwo kple agbadɔwo.

eng: In the fourteenth year, Chedorlaomer and the kings of his alliance came and attacked the Rephaim in Ashteroth-karnaim, the Zuzim in Ham, the Emim in Shaveh-kiriathaim.
local: Le ƒe wuienelia me Kedorlaomer kple fia, siwo kpe ɖe eŋuti la, va si Refaitɔwo le Astarot-Kamayim, Zuzitɔwo le Ham, Emitɔwo le Kiryatayim-gbadzaƒe.

eng: Abram traveled through the land as far as the sacred place at Shechem, at the oak of Moreh. The Canaanites lived in the land at that time.
local: Eye Abram tsa le anyigba la dzi vaseɖe Sixem kple Moreli gã la gbɔ; ke ɣemaɣi la Kanaantɔwo le anyigba la dzi.

eng: and with every living being with you—with the birds, with the large animals, and with all the animals of the earth, leaving the ark with you.
local: kpakple nu gbagbe, siwo katã le mia gbɔ, xe dzodzoewo, aƒemelãwo kple gbemelãwo katã, esiwo le mia gbɔ, kple anyigba dzi lãwo tsyo ƒomevi, siwo do go le aɖakaʋu la me.

eng: Come, lets go down and mix up their language there so they wont understand each others language.
local: Mina míaɖi ayi afima, ne míatɔtɔ woƒe gbe, bena amea ɖeke nagase nɔvia ƒe gbe o!.

eng: The most important cities in his kingdom were Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh in the land of Shinar.
local: Eƒe fiaɖuƒe ƒe gɔmeɖoɖo enye Babel, Erek, Akad kple Kalne le Sinear-nyigba dzi.

eng: They had so many possessions between them that the land couldnt support both of them. They could no longer live together.
local: Eye anyigba mede wo nu bena, woanɔ wo nɔewo gbɔ o; elabena woƒe kesinɔnuwo sɔ gbɔ akpa, eyata womete ŋu nɔ wo nɔewo gbɔ o.

eng: and the Horites in the mountains of Seir as far as El-paran near the desert.
local: kple Xoritɔwo le woƒe to Seir dzi vaseɖe El-Paran, si te ɖe gbedzi lɔƒo.

eng: I will set up my covenant with you so that never again will all life be cut off by floodwaters. There will never again be a flood to destroy the earth.
local: Mebia nu kpli mi bena, nyemagatsrɔ̃ ŋutilã sia ŋutilã kple tsiɖɔɖɔ o; tsi magaɖe anyigba agblẽ nu le edzi akpɔ o.

eng: The LORD appeared to Abram and said, I give this land to your descendants, so Abram built an altar there to the LORD who appeared to him.
local: Eye Yehowa ɖe eɖokui fia Abram gblɔ nɛ bena: Wò dzidzimeviwo matsɔ anyigba sia ana. Eye wòɖi vɔsamlekpui ɖe afima na Yehowa, amesi ɖe eɖokui fiae.

eng: After these events, the LORDs word came to Abram in a vision, Dont be afraid, Abram. I am your protector. Your reward will be very great.
local: Le nya siawo megbe la Abram se Yehowa ƒe nya le ŋutega me bena Abram, megavɔ̃ o; nye enye wò akpoxɔnu; wò fetu sɔ gbɔ ŋutɔ.

eng: Then the LORD dispersed them from there over all of the earth, and they stopped building the city.
local: Eye Yehowa na bena, wokaka tso afima ɖe anyigba blibo la katã dzi, eye wodzudzɔ ɖu la tsotso.

eng: Asshur left that land and built Nineveh, Rehoboth City, Calah.
local: Eye wòʋu le anyigba sia dzi yi ɖe Asur, eye wotso Niniwe, Rexobot-Ir, Kalax.

eng: Conflicts broke out between those herding Abrams livestock and those herding Lots livestock. At that time the Canaanites and the Perizzites lived in the land.
local: Eye dzre dzɔ ɖe Abram ƒe lãnyilawo kple Lot ƒe lãnyilawo dome. Ke Kanaanitɔwo kple Perizitɔwo hã nɔ anyigba la dzi le ɣemaɣi me.

eng: Then they turned back, came to En-mishpat , and attacked the territory of the Amalekites, as well as the Amorites who lived in Hazazon-tamar.
local: Emegbe wòtrɔ va En-Mispat, si enye Kades, eye wòsi Amalekitɔwo katã kple Amoritɔ, siwo nɔ Xazazon-Tamar la hã.

eng: From there he traveled toward the mountains east of Bethel, and pitched his tent with Bethel on the west and Ai on the east. There he built an altar to the LORD and worshipped in the LORDs name.
local: Eye wòʋu tso afima yi ɖe to dzi le Betel ƒe ɣedzeƒe, eye wòtu eƒe agbadɔ ɖi; eɖe Betel ɖe ɣetoɖoƒe kple Ai ɖe ɣedzeƒe, eye wòɖi vɔsamlekpui na Yehowa heyɔ Yehowa ŋkɔ le afima.

eng: God said, This is the symbol of the covenant that I am drawing up between me and you and every living thing with you, on behalf of every future generation.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ bena: Esia enye nubabla, si meɖo anyi le mía kple miawo kple nu gbagbe, siwo katã le mia gbɔ la dome hena dzidzimeawo katã tegbetegbe la ƒe dzesi.

eng: But Abram said, LORD God, what can you possibly give me, since I still have no children? The head of my household is Eliezer, a man from Damascus.
local: Tete Abram gblɔ bena: Aƒetɔ Yehowa, esi vikoe le wɔyem, eye ye nuwo domenyila enye Damaskotɔ Eliezer la, nuka gɔ̃ natsɔ nama?.

eng: Therefore, it is named Babel, because there the LORD mixed up the language of all the earth; and from there the LORD dispersed them over all the earth.
local: Eyata woyɔnɛ bena Babel, le esi Yehowa tɔtɔ amegbetɔwo katã ƒe gbe le afima, eye Yehowa na wokaka tso afima ɖe anyigba blibo la katã dzi ta.

eng: and Resen, the great city between Nineveh and Calah.
local: kple Resen le Niniwe kple Kalax dome, esia enye du gãtɔ.

eng: Sarai, Abrams wife, had not been able to have children. Since she had an Egyptian servant named Hagar.
local: Eye Abram srɔ̃ Sarai medzi vi nɛ kpɔ o. Ke Egipte-nyɔnua ɖe, si woyɔna be Hagar la, nye eƒe kosi.

eng: Abram said to Lot, Lets not have disputes between me and you and between our herders since we are relatives.
local: Tete Abram gblɔ na Lot bena: Megana dzre nanɔ mía kpli wò, nye lãnyilawo kple wò lãnyilawo dome o, elabena nɔviwo míenye.

eng: Then the kings of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, Zeboiim, and Bera took up battle positions in the Siddim Valley.
local: Tete Sodom-fia, Gomora-fia, Adma-fia, Zeboyim-fia kple Bela-alo Zoar-fia woho, eye wodze aʋa ɖe Sidim-gbadzaƒe.

eng: Then Abram set out toward the arid southern plain, making and breaking camp as he went.
local: Eye Abram le ʋuʋum le dziehe gome yim ʋeʋeʋe.

eng: I have placed my bow in the clouds; it will be the symbol of the covenant between me and the earth.
local: Meɖo nye anyieʋɔ ɖe alilikpowo ŋu, bena wòanye nubabla, si le mía kple anyigba dome la ƒe dzesi.

eng: He continued, Since you havent given me any children, the head of my household will be my heir.
local: Eye Abram gblɔ bena: Mèna vim o, eye kpɔ ɖa, nye aƒedzikpɔla anyi ye dome.

eng: These are Shems descendants. When Shem was years old, he became the father of Arpachshad, two years after the flood.
local: Esia nye Sem ƒe dzidzimeviwo ŋutinya. Esime Sem xa ƒe alafa ɖeka la, edzi Arpaxsad, le ƒe evelia me le tsiɖɔɖɔ la megbe.

eng: Egypt fathered Ludim, Anamim, Lehabim, Naphtuhim.
local: Eye Mizrayim dzi Luditɔwo, Anamitɔwo, Lehabitɔwo, Naftuxitɔwo.

eng: Sarai said to Abram, The LORD has kept me from giving birth, so go to my servant. Maybe she will provide me with children. Abram did just as Sarai said.
local: Eye Sarai gblɔ na Abram bena: Kpɔ ɖa, Yehowa xe nye vidzidɔ, eyata dɔ kple nye kosi la, ɖewohĩ maxɔ mamayɔvi mahĩ? Eye Abram ɖo to Sarai ƒe gbe.

eng: against King Chedorlaomer of Elam, King Tidal of Goiim, King Amraphel of Shinar, and King Arioch of Ellasar, four kings against five.
local: ɖe Kedorlaomer, Elam-fia, Tideal, Goyim-fia, Amrafel, Sinear-fia, kple Ariok, Elasar-fia ŋuti; fia ene tsitre ɖe fia atɔ̃ ŋuti.

eng: When a famine struck the land, Abram went down toward Egypt to live as an immigrant since the famine was so severe in the land.
local: Eye do to le anyigba la dzi. Eye Abram yi ɖe Egipte, be wòa nye amedzro le afima; elabena dɔa nu sẽ ŋutɔ le anyigba la dzi?.

eng: When I bring clouds over the earth and the bow appears in the clouds.
local: Eye ava me bena, ne mele ƒu ƒom alilikpowo le dziƒo, eye anyieʋɔ ɖi ɖe alilikpoawo ŋu lã.

eng: After Arpachshad was born, Shem lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Esi Sem dzi Arpaxsad megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa atɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: The LORDs word came immediately to him, This man will not be your heir. Your heir will definitely be your very own biological child.
local: Ke Yehowa gblɔ nɛ bena: Menye eya anyi wò dome o; boŋ amesi nava dzi la, eya anyi wò dome.

eng: Pathrusim, Casluhim, and Caphtorim, from which the Philistines came.
local: Patrusitɔwo, Kasluxitɔwo, amesiwo dome Filistitɔwo dzɔ tso, kple Kaftoritɔwo.

eng: After Abram had lived ten years in the land of Canaan, Abrams wife Sarai took her Egyptian servant Hagar and gave her to her husband Abram as his wife.
local: Le Abram ƒe Kanaan-nyigba dzi nɔnɔ ƒe ewo megbe la, Sarai, Abram srɔ̃, tsɔ eƒe kosi, Egipte-nyɔnu Hagar, na srɔ̃a Abram wòɖe.

eng: Isnt the whole land in front of you? Lets separate. If you go north, I will go south; and if you go south, I will go north.
local: Alo menye anyigba blibo la nye esi le ŋkuwò me oa? De megbe tso gbɔnye dzro! Ne ètia mia me la, matsɔ ɖusi me, eye ne ètia ɖusi me la, matsɔ mia me.

eng: Now the Siddim Valley was filled with tar pits. When the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah retreated, they fell into them; and the rest fled to the mountains.
local: Ke aŋɔʋe geɖewo le Sidim-gbadzaƒe la. Esi Sodom-kple Gomora-fia wòsi la, woge ɖe wo me; ke ame mamleawo si yi to dzi.

eng: I will remember the covenant between me and you and every living being among all the creatures. Floodwaters will never again destroy all creatures.
local: ekema maɖo ŋku nye nubabla, si mebia kpli mi kpakple ŋutilã sia ŋutilã, siwo me agbegbɔgbɔ le la dzi, eye tsi magaɖɔ aɖe anyigba la bena, nu gbagbewo katã natsrɔ̃ o.

eng: Just before he arrived in Egypt, he said to his wife Sarai, I know you are a good-looking woman.
local: Ke esi wògogo Egipte la, egblɔ na srɔ̃a Sarai bena: Kpɔ ɖa, menya bena, nyɔnu dzetugbe nènye.

eng: When Arpachshad was years old, he became the father of Shelah.
local: Esi Arpaxsad xɔ ƒe blaetɔ̃ vɔ atɔ̃ la, edzi Selax.

eng: Then he brought Abram outside and said, Look up at the sky and count the stars if you think you can count them. He continued, This is how many children you will have.
local: Eye wòkplɔe yi ɖe xexe gblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ dziƒo ɖa, eye naxlẽ ɣletiviwo, ne ate ŋu axlẽ wo la. Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Nenema wò ƒome anɔ.

eng: Canaan fathered Sidon his oldest son, and Heth.
local: Eye Kanaan dzi Zidon, eƒe ŋgɔgbevi, kple Xet.

eng: He slept with Hagar, and she became pregnant. But when she realized that she was pregnant, she no longer respected her mistress.
local: Eye wòdɔ kple Hagar, eye wòtɔ fu. Ke esi wòkpɔ bena, yefɔ fu la, eva eƒe aƒenɔ kpɔ ge ɖe ƒomevii.

eng: Lot looked up and saw the entire Jordan Valley. All of it was well irrigated, like the garden of the LORD, like the land of Egypt, as far as Zoar .
local: Tete Lot tsa ŋku Yordan-nutowo katã me, eye wòkpɔ bena, tsi li ŋutɔ, elabena Yehowa metsrɔ̃ Sodom kple Gomora haɖe o, ele abe Yehowa ƒe abɔ me ene, eɖi Egipte-nyigba vaseɖe Zoar ke.

eng: They took everything from Sodom and Gomorrah, including its food supplies, and left.
local: Eye woha Sodom kple Gomoratɔwo ƒe kesinɔnuwo kple nuɖuɖuwo katã hedzoe.

eng: The bow will be in the clouds, and upon seeing it I will remember the enduring covenant between God and every living being of all the earths creatures.
local: Eyata ne anyieʋɔ do ɖe alilikpowo ŋu la, makpɔe ahaɖo ŋku nubabla, si le Mawu kple ŋutilã, siwo katã me agbegbɔgbɔ le le anyigba dzi dome la dzi tegbee.

eng: When the Egyptians see you, they will say, This is his wife, and they will kill me but let you live.
local: Ne Egiptetɔwo akpɔ wò la, ekema woagblɔ bena, srɔ̃e sia, eye woawum; ke woana wòa natsi agbe.

eng: After Shelah was born, Arpachshad lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eye esi Arpaxsad dzi Selax megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa ene kple etɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites.
local: Yebusitɔwo, Amoritɔwo kple Girgasitɔwo.

eng: Abram trusted the LORD, and the LORD recognized Abrams high moral character.
local: Eye wòxɔ Yehowa dzi se, eye wòbui nɛ dzɔdzɔenyenyee.

eng: Sarai said to Abram, This harassment is your fault. I allowed you to embrace my servant, but when she realized she was pregnant, I lost her respect. Let the LORD decide who is right, you or me.
local: Eye Sarai gblɔ na Abram bena: Nu manyomanyo, si teƒe kpɔm mele la, etso gbɔwò. Nye ŋutɔ metsɔ nye kosi na wò; ke azɔ esi wòkpɔ bena, yefɔ fu ko la, eva kpɔ ye ge ɖe ƒomevii. Yehowa nadrɔ̃ ʋɔnu le mía kpli wò dome!.

eng: They also took Lot, Abrams nephew who lived in Sodom, and everything he owned, and took off.
local: Eye wole Lot, Abram ƒe tɔgãyovi, amesi nɔ Sodom hã, eye woha eƒe kesinɔnuwo dzoe.

eng: So Lot chose for himself the entire Jordan Valley. Lot set out toward the east, and they separated from each other.
local: Eye Lot tia Yordan-nutowo me katã na eɖokui, eye Lot ʋu yi ɖe ɣedzeƒe gome; ale nɔvi kple nɔvi dome klã.

eng: God said to Noah, This is the symbol of the covenant that I have set up between me and all creatures on earth.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ na Noa bena: Esia enye nubabla, si meɖo anyi le mia kple ŋutilã, siwo katã le anyigba dzi dome la ƒe dzesi.

eng: When Shelah was years old, he became the father of Eber.
local: Esi Selax xɔ ƒe blaetɔ̃ la, edzi Eber.

eng: So tell them you are my sister so that they will treat me well for your sake, and I will survive because of you.
local: Gblɔ ko dzro bena, nɔvinyenyɔnu nènye, ne manɔ dedie le tawò, eye matsi agbe le tawò!.

eng: the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites.
local: Xiwitɔwo, Arkitɔwo kple Sinitɔwo.

eng: He said to Abram, I am the LORD, who brought you out of Ur of the Chaldeans to give you this land as your possession.
local: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Nye enye Yehowa, si kplɔ wò tso Ur le Kaldea, bena matsɔ anyigba sia na wò, ne wòanye wò domenyinu.

eng: Abram said to Sarai, Since shes your servant, do whatever you wish to her. So Sarai treated her harshly, and she ran away from Sarai.
local: Tete Abram gblɔ na Sarai bena: Kpɔ ɖa, wò kosi la le asiwò me, wɔ nusianu, si nyo ŋuwò la, ɖo eŋu faa! Tete Sarai bɔbɔe ɖe anyi; eyata wòsi le egbɔ.

eng: When a survivor arrived, he told Abram the Hebrew, who lived near the oaks of the Amorite Mamre, who was the brother of Eshcol and Aner, Abrams treaty partners.
local: Tete amea ɖe si va gblɔe na Hebritɔ Abram, si le Amoritɔ Mamre, Eskol kple Aner nɔvi ƒe ali gãwo te la. Ame siawoe bia nu kple Abram.

eng: Abram settled in the land of Canaan, and Lot settled near the cities of the valley and pitched his tent close to Sodom.
local: Abram nɔ Kanaan-nyigba dzi; ke Lot nɔ nuto ma ƒe duwo me le eƒe agbadɔ hom le tutum, vaseɖe esime wòɖo Sodom.

eng: After Eber was born, Shelah lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eye esi Selax dzi Eber megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa ene kple etɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: Noahs sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth came out of the ark. Now Ham was Canaans father.
local: Noa ƒe viŋutsu, siwo do go le aɖakaʋua me la, woawo nye: Sem, Xam kple Yafet; Xam sia enye Kanaan fofo.

eng: When Abram entered Egypt, the Egyptians saw how beautiful his wife was.
local: Eye esi Abram va Egipte la, Egiptetɔwo kpɔ nyɔnu la bena, edze tugbe ŋutɔ.

eng: But Abram said, LORD God, how do I know that I will actually possess it.
local: Ke egblɔ nɛ bena: Aƒetɔ Yehowa, nuka me madze sii le bena, màtsɔe anyi domee mahã?.

eng: the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites. After this the Canaanite clans were dispersed.
local: Arwatitɔwo, Zemaritɔwo kple Xamatitɔwo; emegbe Kanaan ƒe ƒome va keke ta ɖe dzi.

eng: The LORDs messenger found Hagar at a spring in the desert, the spring on the road to Shur.
local: Ke Yehowa ƒe dɔla kpɔe le tsivudoa ɖe, si le gbe dzi le Sur-mɔ dzi la to.

eng: When Abram heard that his relative had been captured, he took all of the loyal men born in his household, three hundred eighteen, and went after them as far as Dan.
local: Azɔ esi Abram se bena, woɖe aboyo nɔvia la, eɖe eƒemevi alafa etɔ̃ kple wuienyi, amesiwo nya aʋawɔwɔ la, eye wòti wo yome vaseɖe Dan.

eng: The citizens of Sodom were very evil and sinful against the LORD.
local: Ke Sodomtɔwo nye ame baɖawo, eye woda vo geɖe ɖe Yehowa ŋuti.

eng: When Eber was years old, he became the father of Peleg.
local: Esi Eber xɔ ƒe blaetɔ̃ vɔ ene la, edzi Peleg.

eng: These were Noahs three sons, and from them the whole earth was populated.
local: Noa viŋutsu etɔ̃ siawo me amegbetɔwo katã dzo tso hekaka ɖe anyigba blibo la katã dzi.

eng: The Canaanite boundary extends from Sidon by way of Gerar to Gaza and by way of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim to Lasha.
local: Eye Kanaan ƒe anyigba keke tso Zidon ɖo ta Gerar kple Gaza, ɖo ta Sodom, Gomora, Adma kple Zeboyim vaseɖe Lasa.

eng: He said, Bring me a three-year-old female calf, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.
local: Eye wòɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Lé nyinɔ, si xɔ ƒe etɔ̃, gbɔnɔ, si xɔ ƒe etɔ, agbo, si xɔ ƒe etɔ̃, akpakpa kple ahɔne ve nam!.

eng: When Pharaohs princes saw her, they praised her to Pharaoh; and the woman was taken into Pharaohs household.
local: Eye esi Farao ŋumewo kpɔe la, wokafui na Farao, eye wokplɔ nyɔnu la va Farao ƒe me.

eng: and said, Hagar! Sarais servant! Where did you come from and where are you going? She said, From Sarai my mistress. Im running away.
local: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena; Hagar, Sarai ƒe kosi, afika nètso, eye afika néyina? Eɖo ŋu bena: Ɖe mesi le nye aƒenɔ Sarai gbɔ.

eng: After Lot separated from him, the LORD said to Abram, From the place where you are standing, look up and gaze to the north, south, east, and west.
local: Esi Lot klã le Abram gbɔ megbe la, Yehowa gblɔ na Abram bena: Tsa ŋku tso afisi nele, eye nakpɔ anyiehe, dziehe, ɣedzeƒe kple ɣetoɖoƒe ɖa.

eng: During the night, he and his servants divided themselves up against them, attacked, and chased them to Hobah, north of Damascus.
local: Eye eya ŋutɔ kple eƒe ameawo dze aʋa va fiti wo le zã me hesi wo, eye wonyã wo vaseɖe Xoba le Damasko ƒe anyiehe.

eng: After Peleg was born, Eber lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eye esi Eber dzi Peleg megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa ene kple blaetɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: These are Hams sons according to their clans, their languages, their lands, and their nations.
local: Esiawo nye Xam-viwo le woƒe ƒomewo, gbewo, anyigbawo kple woƒe dukɔwo nu.

eng: Noah, a farmer, made a new start and planted a vineyard.
local: Eye Noa de asi agbledede me, eye wòde weingblea ɖe.

eng: He took all of these animals, split them in half, and laid the halves facing each other, but he didnt split the birds.
local: Tete wotsɔ nu siawo katã vɛ nɛ hefe wo me, eye wòɖo akpa afafãawo ɖe agbaka eve me ɖewo nɔewo gbɔ; ke mefe xeawo ya me o.

eng: Things went well for Abram because of her: he acquired flocks, cattle, male donkeys, men servants, women servants, female donkeys, and camels.
local: Eye wòwɔ Abram nyuie le eta, eye wotso alẽwo, nyiwo, tedzitsuwo, kluviwo, kosiwo, tedzinɔwo kple kposɔwo nɛ.

eng: The LORDs messenger said to her, Go back to your mistress. Put up with her harsh treatment of you.
local: Eye Yehowa ƒe ɖɔla la gblɔ nɛ bena: Trɔ yi wò aƒenɔ gbɔ, eye nabɔbɔ ɖokuiwò ɖe ete!.

eng: He brought back all of the looted property, together with his relative Lot and Lots property, wives, and people.
local: Eye wògaxɔ kesinɔnuawo katã kple nɔvia Lot hã le wo si hekpe ɖe eƒe kesinɔnuawo, nyɔnuawo kple ameha la ŋuti.

eng: because all the land that you see I give you and your descendants forever.
local: Elabena anyigba blibo la katã, si kpam nèle la, matsɔe ana wò kple wò dzidzimeviwo tegbetegbe.

eng: When Peleg was years old, he became the father of Reu.
local: Esi Peleg xɔ ƒe blaetɔ̃ la, edzi Regu.

eng: Children were also born to Shem the father of all Ebers children and Japheths older brother.
local: Eye wodzi ŋutsuwo na Sem, amesi nye Eber-viwo katã tɔgbui kple Yafet nɔvi xoxoetɔ.

eng: He drank some of the wine, became drunk, and took off his clothes in his tent.
local: Ke esi wòno wein mu la, eɖe ama ɖe eƒe agbadɔ me.

eng: When vultures swooped down on the carcasses, Abram waved them off.
local: Tete xe ƒonuwo va dze ɖe lãwo dzi, ke Abram nyã wo ɖa.

eng: Then the LORD struck Pharaoh and his household with severe plagues because of Abrams wife Sarai.
local: Ke Yehowa ɖe fu geɖe na Farao kple eƒe aƒe katã le Sarai, Abram srɔ̃ ŋuti.

eng: The LORDs messenger also said to her, I will give you many children, so many they cant be counted.
local: Eye Yehowa ƒe dɔla la gblɔ nɛ bena: Mawɔ wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ ŋutɔ, ale bena le woƒe agbɔsɔsɔ ta womate ŋu axlẽ wo o.

eng: After Abram returned from his attack on Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, the king of Sodom came out to the Shaveh Valley to meet him.
local: Azɔ esi woɖu Kedorlaomer kple fia, siwo kpe ɖe eŋuti dzi hetrɔ gbɔna va aƒe la, Sodom-fia va kpee le Sawebali me, si woyɔna be fiabali me la me.

eng: I will make your descendants like the dust of the earth. If someone could count the bits of dust on the earth, then they could also count your descendants.
local: Eye mana wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ abe ʋuʋudedi ene ale be, ne amea ɖe ate ŋu axlẽ ʋuʋudedi la, eya koe ate ŋu axlẽ wo dzidzimeviwo hã.

eng: After Reu was born, Peleg lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eye esi Peleg dzi Regu megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa eve kple asieke, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: Shems sons: Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud, and Aram.
local: Sem viŋutsuwo nye: Elam, Asur, Arpaxsad, Lud kple Aram.

eng: After the sun set, Abram slept deeply. A terrifying and deep darkness settled over him.
local: Esi ɣe le to ɖom la, Abram dɔ alɔ̃ yi me ʋĩ, eye kpɔ ɖa, vɔvɔ̃ lée, eye tsizi do ɖee.

eng: Ham, Canaans father, saw his father naked and told his two brothers who were outside.
local: Eye Xam, Kanaan fofo, kpɔ fofoa ƒe ama, eye wòɖagblɔe na nɔvia eveawo le xexe.

eng: So Pharaoh summoned Abram and said, Whats this youve done to me? Why didnt you tell me she was your wife?.
local: Eye Farao yɔ Abram ve gblɔ ne bena; Nuka enye si nèwɔ ɖe ŋunye; nukaŋuti megblɔe nam bena, ye srɔ̃e wònye o mahã?.

eng: The LORDs messenger said to her, You are now pregnant and will give birth to a son. You will name him Ishmael because the LORD has heard about your harsh treatment.
local: Yehowa ƒe dɔla la gagblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, èfɔ fu, eye àdzi ŋutsuvi, eye nayɔ eƒe ŋkɔ bena Ismael; elabena Yehowa se wò konyi, si nèfa le wò fukpekpe la ŋuti.

eng: Now Melchizedek the king of Salem and the priest of El Elyon had brought bread and wine.
local: Ke Melxizedek, Salem-fia, tsɔ abolo kple wein vɛ; ame sia enye Mawu dziƒoʋitɔ ƒe nunɔla.

eng: Stand up and walk around through the length and breadth of the land because I am giving it to you.
local: Tsi tre natsa, eye nakpɔ anyigba la ƒe didime kple eƒe kekeme, elabena wòe màtsɔe ana!.

eng: When Reu was years old, he became the father of Serug.
local: Esi Regu xɔ ƒe blaetɔ̃ vɔ eve la, edzi Serug.

eng: Arams sons: Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.
local: Aram viŋutsuwo nye: Uz, Xul, Geter kple Mas.

eng: Then the LORD said to Abram, Have no doubt that your descendants will live as immigrants in a land that isnt their own, where they will be oppressed slaves for four hundred years.
local: Eye wògblɔ na Abram bena: Nyae, naganyae bena, wò dzidzimeviwo anye amedzrowo le anyigbaa ɖe, si menye wo tɔ o la dzi, eye woasubɔ anyigba ma dzi tɔwo, eye woamawo ate wo ɖe anyi ƒe alafa ene sɔŋ.

eng: Shem and Japheth took a robe, threw it over their shoulders, walked backward, and covered their naked father without looking at him because they turned away.
local: Tete Sem kple Yafet tsɔ avɔ lé ɖe abɔta hezɔ megbemegbee, eye wotsyɔe na wo fofo, eye wometrɔ kɔ kpɔ wo fofo ƒe ama o.

eng: Why did you say, Shes my sister, so that I made her my wife? Now, heres your wife. Take her and go.
local: Nukaŋuti nègblɔ bena, ye nɔvinyɔnue, eye mena wòzu srɔ̃nye mahã? Ke azɔ kpɔ ɖa, meɖe asi le srɔ̃wò ŋuti na wò, kplɔe yii!.

eng: He will be a wild mule of a man; he will fight everyone, and they will fight him. He will live at odds with all his relatives.
local: Awɔ nu abe lu ene, akɔ asi dzi ɖe amesiame ŋu, eye amesiame akɔ asi dzi ɖe eya hã ŋu, eye wòatigi nɔvia wo katã ɖo.

eng: and he blessed him, Bless Abram by El Elyon, creator of heaven and earth.
local: Eye wòyrae gblɔ bena Mawu dziƒoʋitɔ, si wɔ dziƒo kple anyigba, nayra Abram!.

eng: After Serug was born, Reu lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eye esi Regu dzi Serug megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa eve kple adre, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: So Abram packed his tent and went and settled by the oaks of Mamre in Hebron. There he built an altar to the LORD.
local: Eye Abram le eƒe agbadɔ hom le tutum, le ŋgɔgbe yimii, eye wòva nɔ Mamre ƒe ati gã gbɔ le Xebron-nutowo me, eye wòɖi vɔsamlekpui ɖe afima na Yehowa.

eng: Arpachshad fathered Shelah, and Shelah fathered Eber.
local: Arpaxsad dzi Selax, eye Selax dzi Eber.

eng: But after I punish the nation they serve, they will leave it with great wealth.
local: Gake hã mava wɔ fu dukɔ, si subɔm wole la, eye le esia megbe la woatsɔ kesinɔnu geɖewo adzoe.

eng: When Noah woke up from his wine, he discovered what his youngest son had done to him.
local: Ke esi aha la kɔ le mo na Noa, eye wòse nusi via ɖevitɔ wɔ ɖe eŋu la.

eng: Pharaoh gave his men orders concerning Abram, and they expelled him with his wife and everything he had.
local: Eye Farao do amewo, bena woakplɔ eya kple srɔ̃a kple etɔwo katã aɖo ɖa.
