eng: When we were binding stalks of grain in the field, my stalk got up and stood upright, while your stalks gathered around it and bowed down to my stalk.
local: Miele mɔli biam le agble me, tete nye mɔli babla tsi tre, eye wòdzɔ kã; kasiaa mia tɔwo va ƒo xlãe, eye wode la agu nɛ.

eng: Eliphazs sons were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz.
local: Elifaz viŋutsuwo nye Teman, Omar, Zefo, Gaetam kple Kenas.

eng: Jacob set up a pillar on her grave. Its the pillar on Rachels tomb thats still there today.
local: Eye Yakob tu dzesikpe ɖe eƒe yɔdo dzi, esia nye ŋkuɖodzikpe le Raxel ƒe yɔdo dzi vaseɖe egbegbe.

eng: They killed Hamor and his son Shechem with their swords, took Dinah from Shechems household, and left.
local: Wowu Xamor kple via Sixem hã kple yi, eye wokplɔ Dina tso Sixem ƒe me hedzoe.

eng: His brothers said to him, Will you really be our king and rule over us? So they hated him even more because of the dreams he told them.
local: Tete nɔviawo gblɔ nɛ bena: Ɖe nèle didim be, naɖu fia ɖe mía nu, eye naɖu mía dzia? Tso ekema dzi wogalé fui ɖe dzi wu le eƒe drɔ̃ekuku kple eƒe nyawo ta.

eng: At that time, Judah moved away from his brothers and settled near an Adullamite named Hirah.
local: Le ɣemaɣi me la Yuda ʋu le nɔviawo gbɔ heyi ɖe Xira. Adulamtɔ la gbɔ.

eng: Timna was the secondary wife of Eliphaz, Esaus son, and she gave birth to Amalek for Eliphaz. These are the sons of Esaus wife Adah.
local: Eye Esau viŋutsu Elifas srɔ̃ evelia ŋkɔ enye Timna, eya dzi Amalek na Elifaz. Esiawo nye Esau srɔ̃ Ada ƒe viŋutsuwo.

eng: Israel continued his trip and pitched his tent farther on near the tower of Eder.
local: Emegbe la Israel gadze mɔ, eye wòtu eƒe agbadɔ ɖe Migdal-Edel godo.

eng: When Jacobs other sons discovered the dead, they looted the city that had defiled their sister.
local: Ke Yakob vi mamleawo va ɖe nu le ame kukuawo ŋu, eye woha du la, elabena wogblẽ wo nɔvinyɔnu.

eng: Then Joseph had another dream and described it to his brothers: Ive just dreamed again, and this time the sun and the moon and eleven stars were bowing down to me.
local: Gbe ɖeka egaku drɔ̃e bubu, eye woli eya hã na nɔviawo bena: Megaku drɔ̃e be, ɣe, ɣleti kple ɣletivi wuiɖekɛ wode ta agu nam.

eng: There Judah saw the daughter of a Canaanite whose name was Shua, and he married her. After he slept with her.
local: Afima Yuda kpo Kanaantɔ Sua ƒe vinyɔnu le, eye woɖee, eye wòdo kplii.

eng: These are Reuels sons: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah. These are the sons of Esaus wife Basemath.
local: Reguel viŋutsuwo nye: Naxat, Serax, Sama kple Misa. Woawo nye Esau srɔ̃ Basmat ƒe viŋutsuwo.

eng: While Israel stayed in that place, Reuben went and slept with Bilhah his fathers secondary wife, and Israel heard about it. Jacob had twelve sons.
local: Ke esi Israel le nuto sia me la, Ruben de asi fofoa ƒe kosi Bilha ŋu, eye Israel see.

eng: They took their flocks, their cattle, and their donkeys, whether in the city or in the fields nearby.
local: Eye wokplɔ woƒe alẽwo, nyiwo kple tedziwo, eve wotso nusiwo wokpɔ le dua me kple agble me la, hedzoe.

eng: When he described it to his father and brothers, his father scolded him and said to him, What kind of dreams have you dreamed? Am I and your mother and your brothers supposed to come and bow down to the ground in front of you.
local: Eye esi wòlii na fofoa kple nɔviawo la, fofoa ka mo nɛ, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Drɔ̃e ka enye si nèku? Alo ɖe mia kple dawo kple nɔviwòwo míava dze klo ade ta agu na wò mahã?.

eng: These are the sons of Esaus wife Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah, Zibeons son: she gave birth to Esau, Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
local: Esiawo nye Esau srɔ̃ Oholibama, Ana ƒe vinyɔnu, si enye Zibeon ƒe tɔgbuiyɔvi ƒe viŋutsuwo, eya dzi Yehus, Yaelam kple Korax na Esau.

eng: The sons of Leah were Reuben, Jacobs oldest son, and Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun.
local: Eye viŋutsu wuieve le Yakob si. Lea viŋutsuwo nye Ruben, Yakob ƒe ŋgɔgbevi, eyome Simeon, Lewi, Yuda, Isaxar kple Zebulon.

eng: she became pregnant and gave birth to a son, whom she named Er.
local: Eye wòfa fu hedzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Ger.

eng: They carried off their property, their children, and their wives. They looted the entire place.
local: Woha woƒe kesinɔnuwo katã, eye woɖe aboyo ɖeviwo kple nyɔnuwo siaa, eye woha woƒe xɔme nuwo hã.

eng: His brothers were jealous of him, but his father took careful note of the matter.
local: Tso esia dzi la nɔviawo ʋã ŋui; ke fofoa dzra nya la ɖo.

eng: The sons of Rachel were Joseph and Benjamin.
local: Raxel viŋutsu wo nye Yosef kple Benyamin.

eng: She became pregnant again, gave birth to a son, and named him Onan.
local: Eyome la efɔ fu gadzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòna ŋkɔe be Onan.

eng: These are the tribal chiefs from Esaus sons. The sons of Eliphaz, Esaus oldest son: Chief Teman, Chief Omar, Chief Zepho, Chief Kenaz.
local: Ame siawo nye amegãwo le Esau viwo dome. Esau ƒe ŋgɔgbevi Elifaz viwo nye, amegã Teman, amegã Omar, amegã Zefo, amegã Kenas.

eng: Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, Youve put me in danger by making me offensive to those who live here in the land, to the Canaanites and the Perizzites. I have only a few men. They may join forces, attack me, and destroy me, me and my household.
local: Tete Yakob gblɔ na Simeon kple Lewi bena: Miehe dzɔgbevɔ̃e va dzinye, eye anyi gbadzinɔla, siwo nye Kanaanitɔwo kple Perizitɔwo la, woalem ɖe fu me: kpɔ ɖa, ame ʋeea ɖewo koe míenye, eye ne woaƒo ƒu ɖe mia ŋu la, ekema woaɖu mía dzi, eye woatsrɔ̃m kple tɔnyetɔwo katã.

eng: Josephs brothers went to tend their fathers flocks near Shechem.
local: Gbe ɖeka esi nɔviawo yi wo fofo ƒe alẽwo kplɔ ge le Sixem la.

eng: The sons of Bilhah, Rachels servant, were Dan and Naphtali.
local: Raxel ƒe kosi Bilha ƒe viŋutsuwo nye Dan kple Naftali.

eng: Chief Korah, Chief Gatam, and Chief Amalek. These are the tribal chiefs of Eliphaz in the land of Edom; they are Adahs sons.
local: amegã Korax, amegã Gaetan kple amegã Amalek. Amegã siawo dzɔ tso Elifaz me le Edom-nyigba dzi. Woawo nye Ada viwo.

eng: Then she gave birth to one more son and named him Shelah. She was in Chezib when she gave birth to him.
local: Emegbe la egadzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Sela. Enɔ Kesib hafi dzii.

eng: Israel said to Joseph, Arent your brothers tending the sheep near Shechem? Come, Ill send you to them. And he said, Im ready.
local: Israel gblɔ na Yosef bena: Nɔviwòwo le alẽwo kplɔm le Sixem, va madɔ wò ɖe wo gbɔ. Eɖo ŋu bena: Nye enye si!.

eng: They said, But didnt he treat our sister like a prostitute.
local: Woɖo ŋu ne bena: Ɖe eya kpɔ mɔ abu mia nɔvi gboloe mahã?.

eng: The sons of Zilpah, Leahs servant, were Gad and Asher. These were Jacobs sons born to him in Paddan-aram.
local: Eye Lea ƒe kosi Silpa ƒe viŋutsuwo nye Gad kple Aser. Esiawo nye Yakob ƒe viŋutsu, siwo wodzi nɛ le Mesopotamia.

eng: These are the sons of Reuel, Esaus son: Chief Nahath, Chief Zerah, Chief Shammah, and Chief Mizzah. These are the tribal chiefs of Reuel in the land of Edom; they are the sons of Esaus wife Basemath.
local: Esiawo nye Esau vi Reguel ƒe viŋutsuwo: Amegã Naxat, amegã Serax, amegã Sama kple amegã Misa. Woawo nye amegã, siwo dzɔ tso Reguel me le Edom-nyigba dzi, eye wonye Esau srɔ̃ Basmat ƒe viwo.

eng: Judah married his oldest son Er to a woman named Tamar.
local: Eye Yuda ɖe srɔ̃ na eƒe ŋgɔgbevi Ger, eŋku nye Tamar.

eng: Jacob said to him, Go! Find out how your brothers are and how the flock is, and report back to me. So Jacob sent him from the Hebron Valley. When he approached Shechem.
local: Eye wògblɔ ne bena: Yi ɖakpɔ bena, nɔviwòwo kple lãhawo li sesĩe mahã? Emegbe la nava gblɔe nam. Ale wòdɔe tso Xebron-bali me, eye wòva Sixem.

eng: Jacob came to his father Isaac at Mamre, that is, Kiriath-arba. This is Hebron, where Abraham and Isaac lived as immigrants.
local: Eye Yakob va fofoa Isak gbɔ le Mamre le Kiryat-Arba, esia nye Xebron, afisi Abraham kple Isak wonye amedzrowo le.

eng: These are the sons of Esaus wife Oholibamah: Chief Jeush, Chief Jalam, and Chief Korah. They are the tribal chiefs of Esaus wife Oholibamah the daughter of Anah.
local: Esiawo nye Esau srɔ̃ Oholibama ƒe viwo. Amegã Yehus, amegã Yaelam kple amegã Korax. Amegã siawo dzo tso Esau srɔ̃ Oholibama, si enye Ana ƒe vinyɔnu la me.

eng: But the LORD considered Judahs oldest son Er immoral, and the LORD put him to death.
local: Ke Yuda ƒe ŋgɔgbevi Ger enye ame vɔ̃ɖi le Yehowa ƒe ŋku me, eyata Yehowa wui.

eng: a man found him wandering in the field and asked him, What are you looking for.
local: Ke esi wòtra mɔ le gbedzi la, ŋutsua ɖe kpɔe, eye wòbiae bena: Nuka dim nèle?.

eng: At the age of years.
local: Eye Isak ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo nye ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blanyi.

eng: These are the sons of Esau, who is Edom, and these are their tribal chiefs.
local: Esiawo nye Esau ƒe viŋutsuwo, eye woawo nye woƒe amegãwo Eya nye Edom.

eng: Judah said to Onan, Go to your brothers wife, do your duty as her brother-in-law, and provide children for your brother.
local: Tete Yuda gblɔ na Onan bena: Ɖe nɔviwò srɔ̃ ahoe, bena nado ƒome ɖi na nɔviwò.

eng: Joseph said, Im looking for my brothers. Tell me, where are they tending the sheep.
local: Eɖo ŋu bena: Nɔvinyewo dim mele: mede kuku, gblɔ afisi wole woƒe lãhawo nyim le la nam.

eng: Isaac took his last breath and died. He was buried with his ancestors after a long, satisfying life. His sons Esau and Jacob buried him.
local: Eye Isak ku amegã ɖeɖi, eye agbenɔnɔ tii, hafi woku, eye wòyi ɖe tɔgbuiawo gbɔ Eye via Esau kple Yakob woɖu.

eng: These are the sons of Seir, the Horite, who live in the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah.
local: Esiawo nye Xoritɔ Seir, amesi nɔ anyigba la dzi tsã la, ƒe viŋutsuwo Lotan, Sobal, Zibeon, Ana.

eng: Onan knew the children wouldnt be his so when he slept with his brothers wife, he wasted his semen on the ground, so he wouldnt give his brother children.
local: Ke esi Onan nya bena, ɖeviawo manye ye tɔ o la, ɖaa ne edɔ nɔvia srɔ̃ gbɔ la, enana tsi la duduna ɖe anyigba, bena yemagado ƒome ɖi na nɔvia o.

eng: The man said, They left here. I heard them saying, Lets go to Dothan. So Joseph went after his brothers and found them in Dothan.
local: Ŋutsu la gblɔ bena: Mese wogblɔ bena: Mina míaʋu le afisia ayi Dotan! Tete Yosef li nɔviawo, eye wòva tu wo le Dotan.

eng: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan. These are the Horite tribal chiefs, Seirs sons, in the land of Edom.
local: Dison, Ezer kple Disan. Woawo nye amegãwo le Xoritɔ Seir, amesi nɔ Edom-nyigba dzi la, ƒe viwo dome.

eng: They saw Joseph in the distance before he got close to them, and they plotted to kill him.
local: Ke esi wokpɔe le adzɔge, eye mekpɔ ɖo wo gbɔ haɖe o la, wode adaŋu le eŋu be, yewoawui le aye me.

eng: The LORD considered what he did as wrong and put him to death too.
local: Nuwɔwɔ sia vɔ̃ɖi le Yehowa ƒe ŋku me, eyata wowu eya hã.

eng: Lotans sons are Hori and Heman, and Lotans sister was Timna.
local: Lotan ƒe viŋutsuwo nye: Xori kple Hemam; eye Lotan nɔvinyɔnu enye Timna.

eng: The brothers said to each other, Here comes the big dreamer.
local: Eye wogblɔ na wo nɔewo bena: Mikpɔ drɔ̃ekula enye kem gbɔna ɖa!.

eng: Judah said to Tamar his daughter-in-law, Stay as a widow in your fathers household until my son Shelah grows up. He thought Shelah would die like his brothers had. So Tamar went and lived in her fathers household.
local: Eye Yuda gblɔ na lɔ̃xoa Tamar bena: Yi fofowò ƒe me, eye nanye ahosi, vaseɖe esime vinye Sela natsi. Elabena ele vɔvɔ̃m bena, eya hã ava ku abe nɔviawo ke ene. Tete Tamar yi ɖanɔ fofoa ƒe me.

eng: These are Shobals sons: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
local: Esiawo nye Sobal ƒe viŋutsuwo: Alwan, Manaxat, Ebal, Sefo kple Onam.

eng: After a long time, Judahs wife the daughter of Shua died. Then, after a period of mourning, he and his neighbor Hirah the Adullamite went up to Timnah, to those who were shearing his sheep.
local: Le ŋkeke geɖewo megbe la Sua vinyɔnu, si nye Yuda srɔ̃ la ku. Eye esi Yuda fa nɛ vɔ la, eya kple xɔlɔ̃a Xira, Adulamtɔ la woyi ɖe Timnat, be woakpɔ amesiwo le fu sẽm na eƒe alẽwo la ɖa.

eng: Come on now, lets kill him and throw him into one of the cisterns, and well say a wild animal devoured him. Then we will see what becomes of his dreams.
local: Mina miawui, eye miatsɔe aƒu gbe ɖe ɖoa ɖe me, eye míagblɔ bena, lã wodaa ɖe vuvui, ekema miakpɔ nusi eƒe drɔ̃e la ava zu.

eng: These are Zibeons sons: Aiah and Anah. Anah is the one who found water in the desert while pasturing his father Zibeons donkeys.
local: Esiawo nye Zibeon ƒe viŋutsuwo: Aya kple Ana; eya nye Ana ma, si tɔ tsi dzodzoe vudo le gbedzi, esime wòle fofoa Zibeon ƒe tedziwo kplɔm la.

eng: When Reuben heard what they said, he saved him from them, telling them, Lets not take his life.
local: Esi Ruben see la, exɔ nya ɖe enu gblɔ bena: Gbeɖe, migana miawui o!.

eng: Tamar was told, Your father-in-law is now on his way up to Timnah to shear his sheep.
local: Ke esi wogblɔ na Tamar bena, toa gbɔna va Timnat fu sẽ ge na eƒe alẽwo la.

eng: These are Anahs children: Dishon and Anahs daughter Oholibamah.
local: Esiawo nye Ana ƒe viwo: Dison kple Ana ƒe vinyɔnu Oholibama.

eng: So Tamar took off the clothing she wore as a widow, covered herself with a veil, put on makeup, and sat down at the entrance to Enaim on the road to Timnah, since she realized that although Shelah had already grown up, she hadnt been given to him as a wife.
local: eɖe eƒe ahovɔwo ɖi, tsɔ motsyɔnu tsyɔ ŋku me, eye wòtsyɔ avɔ blukɔɔ heva bɔbɔ nɔ Enaim-gbo, si le Titmnat-mɔ dzi la nu; elabena ekpɔ be, Sela tsi, gake wometsɔ ye nɛ, be wòaɖe o.

eng: Reuben said to them, Dont spill his blood! Throw him into this desert cistern, but dont lay a hand on him. He intended to save Joseph from them and take him back to his father.
local: Tete Ruben gblɔ na wo bena: Migakɔ ʋua ɖeke ɖi o: ke boŋ mitsɔe de do kema, si le gbedzi la me, eye migade asi eŋu o! Elabena ele didim be, yeaxɔe le wo si, eye yeaɖoe ɖe fofoa.

eng: These are Dishons sons: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
local: Esiawo nye Dison ƒe viŋutsuwo: Xemdan, Esban, Yitran kple Keran.

eng: When Joseph had been taken down to Egypt, Potiphar, Pharaohs chief officer, the commander of the royal guard and an Egyptian, purchased him from the Ishmaelites who had brought him down there.
local: Eye esi wokplɔ Yosef va Egipte la, Egiptetɔ Potifar, Farao ƒe aƒedzikpɔla kple eŋuti mewo ƒe amegã la ƒlee le Ismaeltɔ, siwo kplɔe yi afima la si.

eng: Judah saw her and thought she was a prostitute because she had covered her face.
local: Ke esi Yuda kpɔe la, ebu be, gboloa ɖee; elabena etsyɔ nu ŋku me.

eng: When Joseph reached his brothers, they stripped off Josephs long robe.
local: Esi Yosef va nɔviawo gbɔ la, woɖe eƒe awu le eŋu, eƒe awu ʋlaya, si wòɖo.

eng: These are Ezers sons: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
local: Ezer viŋutsuwo nye Bilhan, Sawan kple Akan.

eng: The LORD was with Joseph, and he became a successful man and served in his Egyptian masters household.
local: Ke Yehowa nɔ kple Yosef, eye nusianu dze edzi nɛ nyuie, eye wònɔ eƒe aƒetɔ, Egiptetɔ la, ƒe me.

eng: He turned to her beside the road and said, Let me sleep with you, because he didnt know she was his daughter-in-law. She said, What will you give me for sleeping with you.
local: Tete wòdze yi ɖe egbɔ gblɔ bena: Na mamlɔ gbɔwò! Elabena menyae bena, lɔ̃xoa wònye o. Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Nuka nanam hafi amlɔ gbɔnye?.

eng: took him, and threw him into the cistern, an empty cistern with no water in it.
local: Eye wolée tsɔe ƒu gbe ɖe do la me; ke doa le ƒuƒlu, eye tsia ɖeke mele eme o.

eng: These are Dishans sons: Uz and Aran.
local: Disan viŋutsuwo nye: Uz kple Aran.

eng: His master saw that the LORD was with him and that the LORD made everything he did successful.
local: Esi eƒe aƒetɔ kpɔ bena, Yehowa li kplii, eye Yehowa na nusianu, si wòwɔna, dzea edzi nɛ la.

eng: He said, I will give you a kid goat from my flock. She said, Only if you give me some deposit, as security to guarantee that you will send it.
local: Egblɔ bena: Malé gbɔ̃tsu ɖeka le lãhawo me aɖo ɖe wò. Eɖo ŋu bena: Ekema tsɔ nane ɖo awɔba nam, vaseɖe esime naɖoe ɖem.

eng: When they sat down to eat, they looked up and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead, with camels carrying sweet resin, medicinal resin, and fragrant resin on their way down to Egypt.
local: Emegbe wobɔbɔ na anyi, be yewoaɖu nu, kasiaa wokpɔ Ismael-sihawo tso Gilead gbɔna heyina ɖe Egipte, eye woƒe kposɔwo tsɔ aŋe, lifi kple kotoklobo.

eng: These are the Horite tribal chiefs: Chiefs Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah.
local: Esiawo nye Xoritɔwo ƒe amegãwo: Amegã Lotan, amegã Sobal, amegã Zibeon, amegã Ana.

eng: Potiphar thought highly of Joseph, and Joseph became his assistant; he appointed Joseph head of his household and put everything he had under Josephs supervision.
local: eve Yosef nu, eye wòtsɔe wɔ eƒe subɔlae, eye wòtsɔe ɖo eƒe me nu wo dzikpɔlae hetsɔ eƒe nuwo katã ɖe esi.

eng: He said, What kind of deposit should I give you? And she said, Your seal, its cord, and the staff in your hand. He gave these to her, slept with her, and she became pregnant by him.
local: Eye wòbiae bena: Nuka matsɔ aɖo awɔba na wò mahã? Eɖo ŋu be: Wò ŋkɔsigɛ, si nètsɔna ɖoa ŋkɔ nu dzi, kple eŋuti ka kpakple wò atikplɔ, si le asiwò la! Tete wotsɔ wo nɛ, eye wòmia egbɔ, eye wòfɔ lu nɛ.

eng: Judah said to his brothers, What do we gain if we kill our brother and hide his blood?.
local: Tete Yuda golɔ na nɔviawo bena: Viɖe ka wònye na mi, ne míefi mía nɔvi wu?.

eng: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan. These are the Horite tribal chiefs, listed according to their chiefs in the land of Seir.
local: amegã Dison, amegã Ezer kple amegã Disan. Esiawo nye Xoritɔwo ƒe amegãwo le Seir-nyigba la dzi le woƒe duawo nu.

eng: From the time he appointed Joseph head of his household and of everything he had, the LORD blessed the Egyptians household because of Joseph. The LORD blessed everything he had, both in the household and in the field.
local: Tso esime wòtsɔe ɖo eƒe aƒe me nuwo dzikpɔlae, eye wotsɔ eƒe nuwo katã de esi la, Yehowa yra Egiptetɔ la ƒe aƒe le Yosef ta, eye Yehowa ƒe yayra le nusiwo katã le esi le aƒe me kple agble me la dzi.

eng: Then she got up, left, and took off her veil, dressing once again in the clothing she wore as a widow.
local: Tete wòdzo yi, eye wòɖe motsyɔnu la ɖi gatsɔ eƒe ahovɔwo ta.

eng: These are the kings who ruled in the land of Edom before a king ruled over the Israelites.
local: Esiawo nye fia, siwo ɖu Edom-nyigba dzi, hafi fia va ɖo Israel-viwo si.

eng: Come on, lets sell him to the Ishmaelites. Lets not harm him because hes our brother; hes family. His brothers agreed.
local: Mina míatsɔe adzra na Ismaeltɔwo, ke mígade asi eŋu o, elabena mía nɔvi kpamkpam wònye. Eye nɔviawo lɔ̃ ɖe edzi.

eng: So he handed over everything he had to Joseph and didnt pay attention to anything except the food he ate. Now Joseph was well-built and handsome.
local: Eye wotsɔ eƒe nuwo katã de Yosef si, eye megatsɔ ɖeke le nyaa ɖeke me o, negbe eƒe nuɖuɖu ko. Eye Yosef nyo, eye wòdze ɖeka ŋutɔ.

eng: Judah sent the kid goat with his neighbor Hirah the Adullamite so he could take back the deposits from the woman, but he couldnt find her.
local: Emegbe la Yuda tsɔ gbɔ̃tsu la ɖe xɔlɔ̃a Adulamtɔ la si, be wòaxɔ awɔbaɖonu la le nyɔnu la si; gake mekpɔe o.

eng: Bela, Beors son, ruled in Edom; his citys name was Dinhabah.
local: Beor vi Bela ɖu fia le Edom, eye eƒe fiadu enye Dinhaba.

eng: When some Midianite traders passed by, they pulled Joseph up out of the cistern. They sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver, and they brought Joseph to Egypt.
local: Ke Midian-sitsalawo va to wo ŋuti yi, eye woɖe Yosef le do la me hetsɔ dzra na Ismaeltɔwo klosalo blave, eye wokplɔ Yosef yi Egipte.

eng: Some time later, his masters wife became attracted to Joseph and said, Sleep with me.
local: Le nusiawo megbe la eƒe aƒetɔ srɔ̃ nɔa ahiãmoe ɖom ɖe Yosef ŋu, eye wògblɔ bena: Va mlɔ gbɔnye.

eng: He asked the locals of that place, Wheres the consecrated worker who was at Enaim on the road? But they said, Theres no consecrated worker here.
local: Eye wòbia dua me ŋutsuwo bena: Afika gbolo, si nɔ Enaim-mɔ dzi la le? Woɖo eŋu ne bena: Gboloa ɖeke menɔ afisia kpɔ o.

eng: When Reuben returned to the cistern and found that Joseph wasnt in it, he tore his clothes.
local: Esi Ruben gatrɔ va doa to la, megakpɔ Yosef le do la me o Tete wòdze eƒe awu.

eng: After Bela died, Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah became king.
local: Esi Bela ku la, Serax vi Yobab tso Bozra ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe.

eng: He refused and said to his masters wife, With me here, my master doesnt pay attention to anything in his household; hes put everything he has under my supervision.
local: Ke egbe, eye wògblɔ na eƒe aƒetɔ srɔ̃ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, nye aƒetɔ megatsɔ ɖeke le aƒea ƒe nya me o, eye wòtsɔ eƒe nuwo katã ɖe asi nam.

eng: So he went back to Judah and said, I couldnt find her. The locals even said, Theres no holy woman here.
local: Tete wòtrɔ yi Yuda gbɔ gblɔ bena: Nyemekpɔe o, eye dua me ŋutsuwo hã gblɔ bena, gboloa ɖeke menɔ afisia kpɔ o.

eng: After Jobab died, Husham from the land of the Temanites became king.
local: Eye esi Yobab ku la, Xusam tso Temanitɔwo ƒe anyigba dzi ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe.

eng: Then he returned to his brothers and said, The boys gone! And I—where can I go now.
local: eye esi wògatrɔ va nɔviawo gbɔ la, egblɔ bena: Ɖevia megali o; azɔ afika nye la mayi?.

eng: No one is greater than I am in this household, and he hasnt denied me anything except you, since you are his wife. How could I do this terrible thing and sin against God.
local: Amea ɖeke meɖe nunye le aƒe sia me o, eye womeɖe naneke le eme nam o, negbe wò ko, elabena srɔ̃a nènye. Aleke gɔ̃ mawɔ vɔ̃ gã sia, eye mada vo ɖe Mawu ŋuti mahã?.

eng: Judah said, Let her keep everything so we arent laughed at. I did send this kid goat, but you couldnt find her.
local: Yuda gblɔ bena: Nenɔ esi ko! Ne menya ɖu fewu le mía ŋuti o ko. Meɖo gbɔ̃tsu la ɖee, ke mèkpɔe o.

eng: After Husham died, Hadad, Bedads son who defeated Midian in the countryside of Moab, became king; his citys name was Avith.
local: Esi Xusam ku la, Bedad vi Hadad ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe; eya nye amesi ɖu Midiantɔwo dzi le Moab ƒe gbadzaƒe, eye eƒe fiadu enye Awit.

eng: His brothers took Josephs robe, slaughtered a male goat, and dipped the robe in the blood.
local: Emegbe la wowu gbɔ̃tsua ɖe, eye wotso Yosef ƒe awu la nyrɔ ɖe ʋua me.

eng: Every single day she tried to convince him, but he wouldnt agree to sleep with her or even to be with her.
local: Eye wonɔa nya sia gblɔm na Yosef ŋkeke sia ŋkeke, ke melɔ̃ be, yeamlɔ egbɔ, eye yeade asi eŋu o.

eng: About three months later, Judah was told, Your daughter-in-law Tamar has become a prostitute and is now pregnant because of it. And Judah said, Bring her out so that she may be burned.
local: Anɔ ɣleti etɔ̃ megbe la wogblɔ na Yuda bena: Lɔ̃xowò Tamar wɔ ahasi, eye wòfɔ fu ɖe eme. Tete Yuda gblɔ bena: Mikplɔe ɖo goe, ne míatɔ dzoe!.

eng: After Hadad died, Samlah from Masrekah became king.
local: Eye esi Hadad ku la, Samla tso Masreka ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe.

eng: They took the long robe, brought it to their father, and said, We found this. See if its your sons robe or not.
local: Eye wotso awu ʋlaya la ɖo ɖe wo fofo gblɔ bena: Nusia míefɔ, le ŋku ɖe ŋu nyuie, be viwò ƒe awue loo, alo menye eyae o!.

eng: One day when Joseph arrived at the house to do his work, none of the households men were there.
local: Gbe ɖeka esi wòva aƒea me, be wòawɔ eƒe dɔwo, eye aƒeametɔa ɖeke meli o la.

eng: When she was brought out, she sent this message to her father-in-law, Im pregnant by the man who owns these things. See if you recognize whose seal, cord, and staff these are.
local: Ke esi wokplɔe do goe la, edɔ ame ɖo ɖe toa gbɔ gblɔ bena: Ŋutsu, si ƒe nu enye esiawo la, eyae ɖo fum. Eye wògblɔ bena: Lé ŋku ɖe eŋu nyuie be, ameka tɔ enye ŋkɔsigɛ la kple eŋuti ka kpakple alikplɔ la mahã?.

eng: After Samlah died, Shaul from Rehoboth on the river became king.
local: Esi Samla ku la, Saul tso Rexobot, si le tɔsisia nu, ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe.

eng: He recognized it and said, Its my sons robe! A wild animal has devoured him. Joseph must have been torn to pieces.
local: Esi wòɖe ŋku ɖee nyuie la, edo ɣli bena: Vinye ƒe awu enye esia; lã wodaa ɖe vuvui, oo, evuvu Yosef vavã!.

eng: She grabbed his garment, saying, Lie down with me. But he left his garment in her hands and ran outside.
local: ele eƒe avɔ, eye wògblɔ bena: Va mlɔ anyi kplim! Ke egblẽ eƒe avɔ la ɖe esi me, eye wòsi do go le aƒea me.

eng: Judah recognized them and said, Shes more righteous than I am, because I didnt allow her to marry my son Shelah. Judah never knew her intimately again.
local: Esi Yuda ɖe ŋku ɖee nyuie la, egblɔ bena: Etɔ dzɔ wu tanye, elabena nyematsɔe na vinye Sela wòɖe o. Tso ema dzi la megade asi eŋu kpɔ o.

eng: After Shaul died, Baal-hanan, Achbors son, became king.
local: Eye esi Saul ku la, Axbor vi Baal-Xanan ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe.

eng: Then Jacob tore his clothes, put a simple mourning cloth around his waist, and mourned for his son for many days.
local: Tete Yakob dze eƒe awu, sa papahũ ɖe ali, eye wofa konyi na via ŋkeke geɖewo.

eng: When she gave birth, she discovered she had twins in her womb.
local: Ke esi eƒe vidziɣi ɖo la, wokpɔ be, venɔviwo le eƒe dɔ me.

eng: After Baal-hanan, Achbors son, died, Hadar became king; his citys name was Pau and his wifes name was Mehetabel the daughter of Matred and granddaughter of Me-zahab.
local: Esi Baal-Xanan, Axbor vi ku la, Hadar ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe; eƒe fiadu enye Pagu, eye srɔ̃a ŋkɔ enye Mehetabeel. Matred ƒe vinyɔnu kple Mesahab ƒe tɔgbuiyɔvi.

eng: When she realized that he had left his garment in her hands and run outside.
local: Esi wòkpɔ be, egblẽ eƒe avɔ ɖe ye si hesi do go la.

eng: All of his sons and daughters got up to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted, telling them, Ill go to my grave mourning for my son. And Josephs father wept for him.
local: Eye viaŋutsuwo kple vianyɔnuwo katã la akɔ nɛ ʋuu do kpoe, ke egblɔ bena: Matsɔ konyifafa ayi vinye gbɔe le tsieƒe boŋ. Ale fofoa fa nɛ ŋutɔ.

eng: At birth, one boy put out his hand, and the midwife took it and tied a red thread on his hand, saying, This one came out first.
local: Esime wòle vi dzim la, ɖeka do asi ɖa. Tete vixela tsɔ ka dzia ɖe sa ɖe asi ŋu nɛ gblɔ bena: Esiae tre dodo.

eng: These are the names of Esaus tribal chiefs according to their families, their locations, and their names: Chief Timna, Chief Alvah, Chief Jetheth.
local: Esiawo nye amegã, siwo dzɔ tso Esau me la, ƒe ŋkɔwo le woƒe ƒomewo, nɔƒewo kple ŋkɔwo nu. Amegã Timna, amegã Alwa, amegã Yetet.

eng: she summoned the men of her house and said to them, Look, my husband brought us a Hebrew to ridicule us. He came to me to lie down with me, but I screamed.
local: eya aƒeametɔwo vɛ, eye wògblɔ na wo bena: Mikpɔ ɖa, ekplɔ Hebritɔ sia vɛ na mi, amesi ɖi be, yeado ŋukpe mí. Eva gbɔnye, be yeamlɔ anyi kplim, ke medo ɣli sesĩe!.

eng: Meanwhile the Midianites had sold Joseph to the Egyptians, to Potiphar, Pharaohs chief officer, commander of the royal guard.
local: Ke Midiantɔwo ɖadzrae ɖe Egipte na Potifar, Farao ƒe aƒedzikpɔla kple eŋuti mewo ƒe amegã.

eng: As soon as he pulled his hand back, his brother came out, and she said, Youve burst out on your own. So he was named Perez.
local: Ke esi wògahe asi ɖe eme la, nɔvia do, eye vixela gblɔ bena: Nukaŋuti nèdze le nɔviwò ŋu ale? Eyata wòna ŋkɔe bena: Perez.

eng: Chief Oholibamah, Chief Elah, Chief Pinon.
local: amegã Oholibama, amegã Ela, amegã Pinon.

eng: When he heard me raise my voice and scream, he left his garment with me and ran outside.
local: Esi wòkpɔ be, medo ɣli sesĩe la, egblẽ eƒe avɔ ɖe gbɔnye, eye wòsi do go le aƒea me.

eng: Afterward, his brother with the red thread on his hand came out, and he was named Zerah.
local: Emegbe la nɔvia, si ƒe asi ŋu ka dzi le la, hã do; eyata wona ŋkɔe bena Serax.

eng: Chief Kenaz, Chief Teman, Chief Mibzar.
local: amegã Kenas, amegã Teman, amegã Mibzar.

eng: She kept his garment with her until Josephs master came home.
local: Eye wotsɔ eƒe avɔ ta da ɖe eɖokui gbɔ, vaseɖe esime eƒe aƒetɔ gbɔ.

eng: Chief Magdiel, and Chief Iram. These are Edoms tribal chiefs according to their settlements in the land they possessed. This is Esau, the ancestor of the Edomites.
local: amegã Magdiel kple amegã Iram. Esiawo nye Edom-megãwo le woƒe nɔƒewo kple woawo ŋutɔwo ƒe anyigbawonu. Eya nye Esau, amesi nye Edomtɔwo ƒe fofo.

eng: and she told him the same thing: The Hebrew slave whom you brought to us, to ridicule me, came to me.
local: Eye wògblɔ nya mawo ke na eya hã bena: Kpɔ ɖa, Hebri-kluvi, si nekplɔ vɛ na mi la, va gbɔnye, be yeado ŋukpem.

eng: but when I raised my voice and screamed, he left his garment with me and ran outside.
local: Ke esi medo ɣli sesĩe la, egblẽ eƒe avɔ ɖe gbɔnye, eye wòsi do go le aƒea me.

eng: When Josephs master heard the thing that his wife told him, This is what your servant did to me, he was incensed.
local: Esi eƒe aƒetɔ se nya, siwo srɔ̃a gblɔ nɛ bena: ale kple ale wò kluvi wɔ ɖe ŋunye la, dɔme vee ŋutɔ.

eng: Some time later, both the wine steward and the baker for Egypts king offended their master, the king of Egypt.
local: Le nya siawo megbe la Egipte-fia ƒe ahakula kple aboloƒola woda vo ɖe woƒe aƒetɔ, Egipte-fia ŋuti.

eng: Josephs master took him and threw him in jail, the place where the kings prisoners were held. While he was in jail.
local: Eye Yosef ƒe aƒetɔ lée de gaxɔ, si me fia ƒe gatɔwo nɔna la me, eye wònɔ afima le gaxɔa me.

eng: Pharaoh was angry with his two officers, the chief wine steward and the chief baker.
local: Eye Farao do dɔmedzoe ɖe eŋuti me eveawo, ahakulawo ƒe amegã kple aboloƒola wo ƒe amegã ŋuti.

eng: the LORD was with Joseph and remained loyal to him. He caused the jails commander to think highly of Joseph.
local: Ke Yehowa nɔ kple Yosef, eye wòna gaxɔdzikpɔla ƒe dzime kɔ ɖe eŋu, eye wòve enu.

eng: and he put them under arrest with the commander of the royal guard in the same jail where Joseph was imprisoned.
local: Eye wotsɔ wo de gaxɔ, si le fia ŋuti mewo ƒe amegã ƒe xɔ, si me wode Yosef hãe la me.

eng: The jails commander put all of the prisoners in the jail under Josephs supervision, and he was the one who determined everything that happened there.
local: Eye gaxɔdzikpɔla tsɔ gatɔ, siwo katã le gaxɔa me la, de Yosef si, eye nusianu, si wowɔna le afima la, eyae ɖonɛ na wo.

eng: The commander of the royal guard assigned Joseph to assist them. After they had been under arrest for some time.
local: Fia ŋuti mewo ƒe amegã la tsɔ wo de Yosef si, bena wòakpɔ wo dzi, eye wonɔ gaxɔ me ɣeyiɣi didia ɖe.

eng: The jails commander paid no attention to anything under Josephs supervision, because the LORD was with him and made everything he did successful.
local: Eye gaxɔdzikpɔla metsɔ ɖeke le nusi wòwɔna la me o, elabena Yehowa li kplii, eye nusianu, si wòwɔ na la Yehowa na wòdzea edzi nɛ.

eng: both of them—the wine steward and the baker for Egypts king who were imprisoned in the jail—had dreams one night, and each mans dream had its own meaning.
local: Gbe ɖeka ƒe zã Egipte-fia ƒe ahakula kple aboloƒola, siwo le gaxɔ me la, woku drɔ̃e wo tɔ wo tɔwoe, eye drɔ̃eawo gɔme le vovovo.

eng: When Joseph met them in the morning, he saw that they were upset.
local: Esi Yosef va wo gbɔ le ŋdi la, ekpɔ wo bena, woli kpoo.

eng: He asked the officers of Pharaoh who were under arrest with him in his masters house, Why do you look so distressed today.
local: Tete wòbia Farao ŋuti me, siwo le gaxɔ me kplii le eƒe aƒetɔ ƒe xɔ me la, bena: Nuka wɔ mieɖo adã egbea ale?.

eng: They answered, Weve both had dreams, but theres no one to interpret them. Joseph said to them, Dont interpretations belong to God? Describe your dreams to me.
local: Woɖo eŋu nɛ bena: Míeku drɔ̃e, gake amesi aɖe egɔme, meli o. Tete Yosef gblɔ na wo bena: Alo drɔ̃e gɔmeɖeɖe la menye Mawu fɔe oa? Gake milii nam ko!.

eng: The chief wine steward described his dream to Joseph: In my dream there was a vine right in front of me.
local: Tete ahakulawo ƒe amegã lĩ eƒe drɔ̃e la na Yosef, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Meku drɔ̃e kpɔ weinkaa ɖe le ŋkunye me.

eng: and on the vine were three branches. When it budded, its blossoms appeared, and its clusters ripened into grapes.
local: Alɔ etɔ̃ le weinka sia ŋu, eye esi wode asi dzedze me la, seƒoƒo ɖo wo dzi, eye woƒe tsetsewo de asi ɖiɖi me.

eng: Pharaohs cup was in my hand, so I took the grapes, crushed them into Pharaohs cup, and put the cup in Pharaohs hand.
local: Ke Farao ƒe kplu la le asinye; tete megbe weinkpoawo, eye mefiã wo ɖe Farao ƒe kplu la me, emegbe metsɔ kplu la de asi na Farao.

eng: Joseph said to him, This is the dreams interpretation: The three branches are three days.
local: Yosef ɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Drɔ̃e la gɔme enye si: Alɔ etɔ̃awo nye ŋkeke etɔ̃.

eng: After three days, Pharaoh will give you an audience and return you to your position. You will put Pharaohs cup in his hand, just the way things were before when you were his wine steward.
local: Le ŋkeke etɔ̃agbe la Farao ado wò ɖe dzi, eye wòagatsɔ wò aɖo wò dɔ nu. Eye nagatsɔ kplu ade Farao si, abe alesi nèwɔna tsã, esi nenye eƒe ahakula ene.

eng: Two years later, Pharaoh dreamed that he was standing near the Nile.
local: Le ƒe eve megbe la Farao ku drɔ̃e. Ele tsitre ɖe Nil to.

eng: But please, remember me when you are doing well and be loyal to me. Put in a good word for me to Pharaoh, so he sets me free from this prison.
local: Ke ɖo ŋku dzinye, ne eva nyo na wò! Meɖe kuku, ve nunye, eye naɖo ŋku dzinye na Farao, bena wòaɖem le xɔ sia me.

eng: In front of him, seven healthy-looking, fattened cows climbed up out of the Nile and grazed on the reeds.
local: Eye wòkpɔ nyinɔ adre, siwo nyo, eye woda ami la, do go tso tɔsisi la me, eye wole gbe ɖum le tagba.

eng: I was stolen from the land of the Hebrews, and here too Ive done nothing to be thrown into this dungeon.
local: Elabena yakatsyɔe wofim le Hebritɔwo ƒe anyigba dzi, eye afi sia hã nyemewɔ naneke, hafi wolém de ga o.

eng: Just then, seven other cows, terrible-looking and scrawny, climbed up out of the Nile after them and stood beside them on the bank of the Nile.
local: Wo yome la ekpɔ nyinɔ bubu adre, siwo vlo, eye woɖi ku nyatsã la, do tso tɔsisi la me, eye wova tsitre ɖe nyinɔ kemeawo gbɔ le tɔsisi la to.

eng: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was favorable, he said to Joseph, It was the same for me. In my dream, there were three baskets of white bread on my head.
local: Esi aboloƒolawo ƒe amegã kpɔ bena, drɔ̃e la gɔme nyo la, egblɔ na Yosef bena: Nye hã meku drɔ̃e, eye metsɔ abolo kusi etɔ̃ ɖe ta.

eng: When Jacob learned that there was grain in Egypt, he said to his sons, Why are you staring blankly at each other?.
local: Esi Yakob se bena, bli le Egipte la, Yakob gblɔna viawo bena: Nukaŋuti miele mia nɔewo ƒe mo kpɔm ale?.

eng: The terrible-looking, scrawny cows devoured the seven healthy-looking, fattened cows. Then Pharaoh woke up.
local: Eye nyinɔ, siwo vlo, eye woɖi ku nyatsã la, lé nyinɔ adre, siwo nyo, eye woda ami la mi. Tete Farao nyo.

eng: In the basket on top there were baked goods for Pharaohs food, but birds were eating them out of the basket on my head.
local: Ke kusi etametɔ me la abolo vivi vovovowo li na Farao, eye xeviwo va le wo ɖum de kusia me le tanye.

eng: The famine was severe in the land.
local: Ke dɔwuame la nu sẽ ŋutɔ le anyigba la dzi.

eng: Ive just heard that theres grain in Egypt. Go down there and buy some for us so that we can survive and not starve to death.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Mesee vavã bena, bli le Egipte; miyi ne míaƒle bli le afima na mí, bena míatsi agbe, eye miagaku o!.

eng: He went back to sleep and had a second dream, in which seven ears of grain, full and healthy, grew on a single stalk.
local: Esi wògayi alo me la, egaku drɔ̃e, eye wòkpɔ liŋɔ adre, siwo ʋã, eye wodo ku la, do ɖe luti ɖeka ŋu.

eng: Joseph responded, This is the dreams interpretation: The three baskets are three days.
local: Yosef ɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Drɔ̃e la gɔme enye si: Kusi etɔ̃awo nye ŋkeke etɔ̃.

eng: and when they had eaten all the grain that they brought from Egypt, their father said to them, Go back and buy us a little food.
local: Eye esi woɖu bli, siwo wotsɔ tso Egipte vɛ, vɔ la, wo fofo gblɔ na wo bena: Miyi, ne miagaƒle bli vɛ nam!.

eng: So Josephs ten brothers went down to buy grain in Egypt.
local: Tete Yosef nɔvi ewo dze mɔ, be woayi aɖaƒle bli le Egipte.

eng: Judah said to him, The man was absolutely serious when he said, You may not see me again without your brother with you.
local: Yuda gblɔ nɛ bena: Ŋutsu la ka ɖe dzi na mí bena: ne miekplɔ mia nɔvi vɛ o la, ekema migava ye ŋku me o.

eng: Just then, seven ears of grain, scrawny and scorched by the east wind, sprouted after them.
local: Wo yome la liŋɔ adre, siwo pepi lé, eye wòlé bu la do.

eng: However, Jacob didnt send Josephs brother Benjamin along with his brothers because he thought something bad might happen to him.
local: Ke Yakob meɖe mɔ na Yosef nɔvi Benyamin, be wòayi kple nɔviawo o, elabena ele vɔvɔ̃m be, dzɔgbevɔ̃ea ɖe ava dzɔ ɖe edzi.

eng: After three days, Pharaoh will give you an audience and will hang you from a tree where birds will peck your flesh from you.
local: Le ŋkeke etɔ̃agbe la Farao atso ta le nuwo, eye woahe wò ɖe ati ŋu, eye xeviwo ava ɖu wò ŋutilã.

eng: and the scrawny ears swallowed up the full and well-formed ears. Then Pharaoh woke up and realized it was a dream.
local: Eye liŋɔ, siwo lé bu la, mi liŋɔ adre, siwo ʋã, eye wodo ku la. Esi Farao nyo la, edze sii bena, drɔ̃e yeku.

eng: If you agree to send our brother with us, then we will go down and buy you food.
local: Ne èlɔ̃ ɖe asi le mía nɔvia ŋuti na mí la, ekema míayi aɖaƒle blia vɛ na wò.

eng: Israels sons came to buy grain with others who also came since the famine had spread to the land of Canaan.
local: Tete Israel ƒe viwo kple ame bubuwo va bli ƒle ge, elabena dɔ to le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

eng: The third day was Pharaohs birthday, and he gave a party for all of his servants. Before all of his servants, he gave an audience to the chief wine steward and the chief baker.
local: Le ŋkeke etɔ̃agbe la Farao ɖu eƒe dzigbe, eye wòɖo kplɔ̃ na eƒe dɔlawo katã, eye wòɖe ahakulawo ƒe amegã kple aboloƒolawo ƒe amegã le gaxɔa me le eƒe dɔlawo katã ƒe ŋku me.

eng: But if you dont agree to send him, then we cant go down because the man said to us, You may not see me again without your brother with you.
local: Ke ne mèlɔ̃ ɖe asi le eŋu na mí o la, ekema míayi o. Elabena ŋutsu la gblɔ na mí bena: ne miekplɔ mia nɔvi ɖe asi vɛ o la, ekema migava ye ŋku me o.

eng: In the morning, he was disturbed and summoned all of Egypts religious experts and all of its advisors. Pharaoh described his dreams to them, but they couldnt interpret them for Pharaoh.
local: Le ŋdi la Farao mekpɔ gbɔdzɔea ɖeke o, eye wòdo woyi ɖayɔ Egipte-fakalawo kple nunyalawo va ƒo ƒu, eye Farao li drɔ̃eawo na wo; ke wo dometɔa ɖeke mete ŋu ɖe egɔme na Farao o.

eng: As for Joseph, he was the lands governor, and he was the one selling grain to all the lands people. When Josephs brothers arrived, they bowed down to him, their faces to the ground.
local: Eye Yosef, si enye anyigba dzi ɖula la, dzra bli na anyigbadzitɔwo katã. Esi Yosef nɔviwo va, eye wodze klo de ta agu nɛ la.

eng: He returned the chief wine steward to his position, and he placed the cup in Pharaohs hand.
local: Egatsɔ ahakulawo ƒe amegã la ɖo eƒe ahakudɔ nu, bena wòagatsɔ kplu anɔ asi ɖem na Farao.

eng: Israel said, Why have you caused me such pain by telling the man you had another brother.
local: Israel ɖo ŋu bena: Nukaŋuti miewɔ vɔ̃ ɖe ŋunye ale, eye mieʋu me na ŋutsu la be, nɔvi gale yewo si?.

eng: Then the chief wine steward spoke to Pharaoh: Today Ive just remembered my mistake.
local: Tete ahakulawo ƒe amegã ƒo nu kple Farao gblɔ bena: Egbe la meɖo ŋku nye vodada dzi.

eng: When Joseph saw his brothers, he recognized them, but he acted like he didnt know them. He spoke to them with a harsh tone and said, Where have you come from? And they said, From the land of Canaan to buy food.
local: Yosef lé ŋku ɖe wo ŋu, eye wòdze si wo. Ke etrɔ eɖokui ame bubui ɖe wo, eye wòhe nya sesẽ kple wo bia wo bena: Afika mietso? Woɖo ŋu bena: Kanaan-nyigba dzi mietso va bli ƒle ge.

eng: But the chief baker he hanged, just as Joseph had said would happen when he interpreted their dreams for them.
local: Ke ena wohe aboloƒolawo ƒe amegã ya ɖe ati ŋu, abe alesi Yosef ɖe egɔme na wo ene.

eng: They said, The man asked us pointedly about our family: Is your father still alive? Do you have a brother? So we told him just what weve said. How were we to know hed say, Bring your brother down here.
local: Woɖo ŋu bena: Ŋutsu la bia mía ŋutɔwo kple míaƒe ƒome ta se kolokolo bena: Ɖe mia fofo gale agbea? Ɖe nɔvi gale mia sia? Eye mieʋu me ne, abe alesi wòle ene. Ɖe míenyae bena, agblɔ na mí be, míkplɔ mía nɔvi vɛ mahã?.

eng: Pharaoh was angry with his servants and put me and the chief baker under arrest with the commander of the royal guard.
local: Esi Farao bi dzi ɖe eƒe dɔlawo ŋuti, eye wòna wòlé mía kple aboloƒolawo ƒe amegã de gaxɔ me le fia ŋuti mewo ƒe amegã ƒe me la.

eng: Joseph recognized his brothers, but they didnt recognize him.
local: Eye Yosef dze si nɔviawo, ke woawo medze sii o.

eng: But the chief wine steward didnt remember Joseph; he forgot all about him.
local: Ahakulawo ƒe amegã megaɖo ŋku Yosef dzi o, ke boŋ eŋlɔe be.

eng: Judah said to his father Israel, Send the young man with me. Lets get ready to leave so that we can stay alive and not die—we, you, and our children.
local: Yuda gblɔ na fofoa Israel bena: Ɖe asi le ɖevi la ŋuti nam, bene míadze mɔ ayi, ne míatsi agbe, eye mía ŋutɔwo, wò ŋutɔ kple mía nɔviwo míagaku o.

eng: Joseph remembered the dreams he had dreamed about them, and said to them, You are spies. Youve come to look for the countrys weaknesses.
local: Tete Yosef ɖo ŋku drɔ̃e, si wòku le wo ŋu la dzi, eye wògblɔ na wo bena: Ŋkutsalawo míenye! Ɖe mieva, be miadi mɔnu le anyigba la ŋu.

eng: We both dreamed one night, he and I, and each of our dreams had its own interpretation.
local: mía kple eve míeku drɔ̃e le zã ɖeka me mia ta míatɔe, eye drɔ̃eawo gɔme hã le vovovo.

eng: They said to him, No, Master. Your servants have just come to buy food.
local: Woɖo ŋu ne bena: Gbeɖe, amegã, wò dɔlawo va nuɖuɖu ƒle ge boŋ.

eng: A young Hebrew man, a servant of the commander of the royal guard, was with us. We described our dreams to him, and he interpreted our dreams for us, giving us an interpretation for each dream.
local: Eye Hebri-ɖekakpuia ɖe, si enye fia ŋuti mewo ƒe amegã ƒe kluvi la, nɔ mía gbɔ le afima. Mieli míaƒe drɔ̃eawo nɛ, eye wòɖe wo gɔme na mi mia to mía tɔe pɛpɛpɛ.

eng: I will guarantee his safety; you can hold me responsible. If I dont bring him back to you and place him here in front of you, it will be my fault forever.
local: Manɔ enu, eye nabiae atso asinye. Ne nyemegakplɔe va ɖo ŋkuwò me o la, ekema nabum vodalae le nye agbe me ŋkekewo katã me.

eng: We are all sons of one man. We are honest men. Your servants arent spies.
local: Mí katã ŋutsu ɖeka viwo míenye, eye nyateƒetɔwo míenye: wò dɔlawo menye ŋkutsalawo o.

eng: His interpretations came true exactly: Pharaoh restored me to my position but hanged him.
local: Alesi wòɖe egɔme na mi la, nenema wòva mee; nye la wogatsɔm ɖo nye ɖa nu, ke eya la woɖee ɖe ati ŋu.

eng: If we hadnt waited so long, we wouldve returned twice by now.
local: Ɖe menye hehem míele ɖe megbe o la, anye ne miede zi eve gbɔ xoxo.

eng: He said to them, No. Youve come to look for the countrys weaknesses.
local: Eɖo ŋu na wo bena: Kpao, mɔnu ɖi ge mieva le anyigba la ŋu!.

eng: So Pharaoh summoned Joseph, and they quickly brought him from the dungeon. He shaved, changed clothes, and appeared before Pharaoh.
local: Tete Farao do woyi ɖayɔ Yosef vɛ. Enumake woɖee le gaxɔa me, eye wòko ta ɖɔ li eƒe awuwo hafi yi Farao gbɔ.

eng: Their father Israel said to them, If it has to be, then do this. Take in your bags some of the lands choice produce, and bring it down to the man as a gift: a little medicinal resin, a little honey, gum, resin, pistachios, and almonds.
local: Tete wo fofo Israel gblɔ na wo bena: Ne ele alea la, ekema miwɔ ale: Mitsɔ anyigba dzi nu vevia ɖewo de miaƒe kotokuwo me yi ɖana ŋutsu la: lifi kple anyitsi via ɖe, ami ʋeʋẽ, kotoklobo, ayedee kple newo.

eng: They said, We, your servants, are twelve brothers, sons of one man in the land of Canaan. The youngest is now with our father, but one is gone.
local: Woɖo ŋu bena: Nɔvi wuieve wò dɔlawo nye, eye ŋutsu ɖeka viwo míenye le Kanaan-nyigba dzi. Ɖevitɔ la tsi fofoa gbɔ, ke ɖeka megali o.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, I had a dream, but no one could interpret it. Then I heard that when you hear a dream, you can interpret it.
local: Eye Farao gblɔ na Yosef bena: Meku drɔ̃e, eye amea ɖeke mete ŋu ɖe egɔme nam o. Ke mese le ŋuwò bena, ne ese drɔ̃e ko la, eɖea egɔme.

eng: Take twice as much silver with you, and take back the silver returned in the top of your sacks. It might have been a mistake.
local: Migatsɔ ga bubu ɖe asi hekpe ɖe ga, siwo miekpɔ le blia dzi le kotokuawo me la ŋuti. Ɖewohi ɖe woɖe ŋu ɖi.

eng: Joseph said to them, Its just as Ive said to you. You are spies!.
local: Eye Yosef xɔ dzi na wo bena: Ele me kokoko bena, ŋkutsalawo míenye!.

eng: Joseph answered Pharaoh, Its not me. God will give Pharaoh a favorable response.
local: Tete Yosef ɖo ŋu na Farao bena: Gbeɖe, menye nyee o; ke Mawu agblɔ nusi enyo la na Farao.

eng: And take your brother, get ready, and go back to the man.
local: Emegbe miakplɔ mia nɔvi la, eye miadze mɔ ayi ŋutsu la gbɔ.

eng: But here is how to prove yourselves: As Pharaoh lives, you wont leave here until your youngest brother arrives.
local: Esia matsɔ ado mi akpɔe: Mefa Farao be, miadzo le afisia akpɔ o, negbe mia nɔvi ɖevitɔ la koe ava hafi!.

eng: So Pharaoh said to Joseph, In my dream I was standing on the bank of the Nile.
local: Eye Farao gblɔ na Yosef bena: Meku drɔ̃e, eye mele tsitre ɖe Nil to.

eng: May God Almighty make the man compassionate toward you so that he may send back your other brother and Benjamin with you. But me, if Im left childless, then Im left childless.
local: Mawu ŋusẽkatãtɔ la ana bena, ŋutsu la nave mia nu, eye woaɖe asi le mia nɔvi la kple Benyamin ŋuti na mi. Ke ne vinyewo le bubum hã, ne wònɔ bubum ko dzro.

eng: Send one of you to get your brother, but the rest of you will stay in prison. We will find out if your words are true. If not, as Pharaoh lives, you are certainly spies.
local: Mido mia dometɔ ɖeka ɖa, be wòakplɔ mia nɔvi la vɛ, ke malé mi ame mamleawo ade ga. Esia me mado mi akpɔ le be, ɖe mietoa nyateƒe loo; ke ne mele nenem o la, meta Farao bena, ŋkutsalawo míenye.

eng: In front of me, seven fattened, stout cows climbed up out of the Nile and grazed on the reeds.
local: Kasiaa mekpɔ nyinɔ adre, siwo da ami, eye wonyo la, do tso tɔsisi la me, eye wole gbe ɖum le tagba.

eng: So the men took this gift. They took twice as much silver with them, together with Benjamin. They left, traveled down to Egypt, and received an audience with Joseph.
local: Tete ŋutsuawo tsɔ nunanaawo kple ga bubu ɖe asi, eye wokplɔ Benyamin hã hedze mo yi Egipte, eye wova Yosef gbɔ.

eng: Just then, seven other cows, weak and frail and thin, climbed up after them. Ive never seen such awful cows in all the land of Egypt.
local: Wo yome la nyinɔ kudɔe adre, siwo vlo ŋuta, eye woɖi ku nyatsã la, hã do, nyemekpɔ nyinɔ vlo siawo tɔgbe le Egipte-nyigba blibo la katã dzi kpɔ o!.

eng: He put them all in prison for three days.
local: Tete wòlé wo de ga ŋkeke etɔ sɔŋ.

eng: When Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the manager of his household, Bring the men to the house and slaughter an animal and prepare it because the men will have dinner with me at noon.
local: Esi Yosef kpɔ bena, Benyamin le wo dome la, egblɔ na eƒe aƒedzikpɔla bena: Kplɔ ŋutsu siawo yi aƒe mee, eye nawu lã aɖa nu, elabena ŋutsuawo le nu ɖu ge kplim egbe ŋdɔ.

eng: On the third day, Joseph said to them, Do this and you will live, for Im a God-fearing man.
local: Le ŋkeke etɔ̃agbe la Yosef gblɔ na wo bena: Miwɔ esia, ne míatsi agbe, elabena mevɔ̃a Mawu.

eng: Then the thin, frail cows devoured the first seven, fattened cows.
local: Enumake nyinɔ, siwo ɖi ku, eye wovlo la, lé nyinɔ gbãtɔ adre, siwo da ami la mi.

eng: The man did as Joseph told him and brought the men to Josephs house.
local: Eye ŋutsu la wo, abe alesi Yosef gblɔ nɛ ene, eye wòkplɔ ŋutsuawo yi Yosef ƒe mee.

eng: But after they swallowed them whole, no one would have known it. They looked just as bad as they had before. Then I woke up.
local: Gake esi womi wo hã la, ɖeko wole abe ɖe womemi wo o ene, eye woƒe nɔnɔme gavlo abe tsã ene. Tete menya.

eng: If you are honest men, let one of your brothers stay in prison, and the rest of you, go, take grain back to those in your households who are hungry.
local: Ne mietoa nyateƒe la, ekema mina mia nɔvi ɖeka natsi ga me, ke mi mamleawo mitsɔ blia yi na mia ƒometɔwo, bena woatsɔ atsi dɔwuamea nui.

eng: When they were brought to Josephs house, the men were frightened and said, Weve been brought here because of the silver put back in our sacks on our first trip so he can overpower us, capture us, make slaves of us, and take our donkeys.
local: Tete ŋutsuawo vɔ̃, le esi wokplɔ wo yi Yosef ƒe mee la ta, eye wogblɔ bena: Ga, si miekpɔ le míaƒe kotokuawo me le míaƒe vava gbãtɔ me, tae wokplɔ mi vɛ ɖo, bena woanye zi atsyɔ mía dzi, eye woalé mí kple míaƒe tedziwo awɔ kluviwoe.

eng: I went to sleep again and saw in my dream seven full and healthy ears of grain growing on one stalk.
local: Emegbe megaku drɔ̃e kpɔ liŋɔ adre, siwo ʋã, eye wodo ku la, le luti ɖeka ŋu.

eng: But bring your youngest brother back to me so that your words will prove true and you wont die. So they prepared to do this.
local: Mikplɔ mia nɔvi ɖevitɔ vɛ nam, ekema miaƒe nyawo anye nyateƒe, eye miagaku o. Tete wowɔ nenema.

eng: They approached the man who was Josephs household manager and spoke to him at the houses entrance.
local: Eye wote ɖe Yosef ƒe aƒedzikpɔla ŋu le xɔa ƒe mo nu, eye woƒo nu kplii gblɔ bena:.

eng: The brothers said to each other, We are clearly guilty for what we did to our brother when we saw his life in danger and when he begged us for mercy, but we didnt listen. Thats why were in this danger now.
local: Eye wogblɔ na wo nɔewo bena: Nyateƒe, mia nɔvi lae nu sia va mía dzi ɖo, elabena esi miekpɔ, alesi dzidzi ƒoe, eye wòƒo koko nam: la, mieɖo tokui! Eyata mieɖo xaxa sia me ɖo.

eng: Just then, seven hard and thin ears of grain, scorched by the east wind, sprouted after them.
local: Wo yome la liŋɔ adre, siwo pepi lé wo wòlé bu, eye wotsi to nu la do.

eng: Joseph gave commands to his household manager: Fill the mens sacks with as much food as theyll hold, and put each mans silver at the top of his sack.
local: Emegbe la ede se na eƒe aƒe dzikpɔla bena: De bli ŋutsuawo ƒe kotokuwo me taŋ, alesi nu anya tsɔ na wo, eye natsɔ amesiame ƒe ga ada ɖe blia dzi le kotokua me.

eng: Please, Master, we came down the first time just to buy food.
local: Taflatse, mia aƒetɔ, mieva afi sia zi gbãtɔ va ƒle bli.

eng: Reuben responded to them, Didnt I tell you, Dont do anything wrong to the boy? But you wouldnt listen. So now this is payback for his death.
local: Ruben xɔ dzi na wo bena: Ɖe nyemegblɔ na mi bena, migada vo ɖe ɖevi la ŋu oa? Ke miesee o, azɔ wole eƒe ʋuhlɔ̃ biam mí.

eng: and the thin ears swallowed up the healthy ears. I told the religious experts, but they couldnt explain it to me.
local: Eye liŋɔ, siwo lé bu la, mi esiwo do ku la. Esia meli na afakalawo, ke wo dometɔa ɖeke mete ŋu ɖe egɔme nam o.

eng: Put my cup, the silver cup, on top of the youngest brothers sack, together with the silver for his grain. So he did just as Joseph told him to do.
local: Eye natsɔ nye klosalokplu ade ɖevitɔ ƒe kotoku me hekpe ɖe ga, si wòtsɔ va blia ƒle gee la ŋu. Tete wòwɔe, abe alesi Yosef gblɔ nɛ ene.

eng: but when we stopped to spend the night and opened our sacks, there was the exact amount of each mans silver at the top of his sack. Weve brought it back with us.
local: Ke esi mieɖo dzeƒe, eye mietu míaƒe kotokuawo la, amesiame gakpɔ eƒe ga blibo la le eƒe bli dzi le kotokua me; kpɔ ɖa, míegatsɔe ɖe asi vɛ.

eng: Joseph could no longer control himself in front of all his attendants, so he declared, Everyone, leave now! So no one stayed with him when he revealed his identity to his brothers.
local: Ke Yosef megate ŋu zi eɖokui dzi, abe alesi wòwɔ vaseɖe fifi, esime amewo le egbɔ la ene o; eye wògblɔ bena: Amesiame nado go le gbɔnye! Eye amea ɖeke menɔ Yosef gbɔ, hafi wòɖe eɖokui fia nɔviawo o.

eng: They didnt know that Joseph was listening to them because they were using an interpreter.
local: Ke womenyae bena, Yosef le esem o, elabena gbesevi le wo dome.

eng: Joseph said to Pharaoh, Pharaoh has actually had one dream. God has announced to Pharaoh what he is about to do.
local: Tete Yosef gblɔ na Farao bena: Farao ƒe drɔ̃eawo gɔme le ɖeka. Nusi wɔ ge Mawu gbɔna la, eyae wòɖe fia Farao.

eng: At dawn, the men and their donkeys were sent off.
local: Le ŋdi kanya la wodo mɔ ŋutsuawo woyi kple woƒe tedziwo.

eng: and weve brought down with us additional silver to buy food. We dont know who put our silver in our sacks.
local: Míegatsɔ ga bubu ɖe asi vɛ, be míaƒle bli lae, ke mienyã amesi tso ga la ɖe míaƒe kotokuawo me o.

eng: Israel packed up everything he owned and traveled to Beer-sheba. There he offered sacrifices to his father Isaacs God.
local: Eye Israel dze mo kple eƒemetɔwo katã, eye esi wòɖo Berseba la, esa vɔ na fofoa Isak ƒe Mawu la.

eng: He wept so loudly that the Egyptians and Pharaohs household heard him.
local: Tete wògbã avi doo, ale bena Egiptetɔwo kple Farao-ƒemetɔwo hã see.

eng: He stepped away from them and wept. When he returned, he spoke with them again. Then he took Simeon from them and tied him up in front of them.
local: Tete wòtrɔ dzo le wo gbɔ ɖade asi avifafa me, emegbe egatrɔ va wo gbɔ heƒo nu kple wo, eye wòlé Simeon le wo dome hebla le wo katã ŋku me.

eng: The seven healthy cows are seven years, and the seven healthy ears of grain are seven years. Its actually one dream.
local: Nyinɔ dami adreawo nye ƒe adre, eye liŋɔ ʋaʋã adreawo hã nye ƒe adre, drɔ̃eawo kple eve gɔme le ɖeka.

eng: They had left the city but hadnt gone far when Joseph said to his household manager, Get ready, go after the men and catch up with them! Ask them, Why have you repaid hospitality with ingratitude?.
local: Eye esi wodo go le dua me, eye wo yome mebu haɖe o la, Yosef gblɔ na eƒe aƒedzikpɔla bena: Tso, nati ŋutsuawo yome, eye ne etu wo la, nagblɔ na wo bena. Nukaŋuti mietsɔ vɔ̃ ɖo nyui teƒe mahã?.

eng: He said, You are fine. Dont be afraid. Your God and your fathers God must have hidden a treasure in your sacks. I received your money. Then he brought Simeon out to them.
local: Eɖo ŋu na wo bena: Miaƒe dzi nedze eme, migavɔ̃ o. Miaƒe Mawu kple mia fofo ƒe Mawu la tsɔ kesinɔnu ɖe miaƒe kotokuawo mele adza me. Miaƒe ga la su asinye. Tete wòkplɔ Simeon do go va wo gbɔ.

eng: God said to Israel in a vision at night, Jacob! Jacob! and he said, Im here.
local: Tete Mawu ɖe eɖokui fia Israel le drɔ̃eƒe gblɔ bena: Yakob, Yakob! Eɖo ŋu bena: Nye enye si!.

eng: Joseph said to his brothers, Im Joseph! Is my father really still alive? His brothers couldnt respond because they were terrified before him.
local: Eye Yosef gblɔ na nɔviawo bena: Nye enye Yosef! Fofonye gale agbea? Ke nɔviawo lulu le eƒe ŋku me, eye womete ŋu ɖo nyaa ɖeke ŋu nɛ o.

eng: Then Joseph gave orders to fill their bags with grain, to put back each mans silver into his own sack, and to give them provisions for their trip, and it was done.
local: Eyome Yosef de se bena, woade bli woƒe kotokuawo me taŋ, atsɔ amesiame ƒe ga ade eƒe kotokua me, eye woana mɔ dzi nuɖuɖu hã wo. Eye wowɔ na wo nenema.

eng: The seven thin and frail cows, climbing up after them, are seven years. The seven thin ears of grain, scorched by the east wind, are seven years of famine.
local: Nyinɔ kudɔe adre, siwo vlo, eye wodo ɖe wo yome la, wonye ƒe adre, eye liŋɔ adre, siwo pepi lé wotsi to nu la, wonye ɖɔŋɔliƒe adre.

eng: Isnt this the cup my master drinks from and uses to discover Gods plans? What youve done is despicable.
local: Alo menye eya me nye aƒetɔ noa nu lena, eye eya me wokpɔa nu lena dzea sii o mahã? Miewɔ busunu!.

eng: The manager brought the men into Josephs house and gave them water to wash their feet and feed for their donkeys.
local: Emegbe la ekplɔ ŋutsuawo va Yosef ƒe me, ku tsi na wo woklɔ afɔe, eye wòtsɔ gbe na woƒe tedziwo.

eng: He said, I am El, your fathers God. Dont be afraid to go down to Egypt because I will make a great nation of you there.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Nye enye Mawu, fofowò ƒe Mawu la. Megavɔ̃ Egipte yiyi o, elabena mana nazu dukɔ gãa ɖe le afima.

eng: Joseph said to his brothers, Come closer to me, and they moved closer. He said, Im your brother Joseph! The one you sold to Egypt.
local: Eye Yosef gblɔ na nɔviawo bena: Mite ɖe ŋunye dzro! Esi wole ɖe eŋu la, egblɔ bena: Nye enye mia nɔvi Yosef, si mietsɔ dzra ɖe Egipte la.

eng: They loaded their grain onto their donkeys, and they set out.
local: Tete wotsɔ bliawo ɖo woƒe tedziwo dzi, eye wodze mɔ tso afima.

eng: When he caught up to them, he repeated these words.
local: Esi wòva tu wo la, eƒo nu kpli wo alea tututu.

eng: Its just as I told Pharaoh: God has shown Pharaoh what he is about to do.
local: Eyatae megblɔ na Farao gbã bena: Nusi wɔ ge Mawu ala la, eya wòɖe fia Farao.

eng: They prepared the gift, anticipating Josephs arrival at noon, since they had heard that they would have a meal there.
local: Eye wotsa nunanaawo da ɖi, vaseɖe esime Yosef gbɔ le ŋdɔ me, elabena wose bena, yewole nu ɖu ge le afima.

eng: I will go down to Egypt with you, and I promise to bring you out again. Joseph will close your eyes when you die.
local: Nye ŋutɔ mayi Egipte kple wò, eye ye ŋutɔ magakplɔ wò agbɔe; ke Yosef ahe wò ŋkuyiwo akpe.

eng: Now, dont be upset and dont be angry with yourselves that you sold me here. Actually, God sent me before you to save lives.
local: Migana woazu nuxaxa na mi o, eye megave mi be, mietsɔm ƒle ɖe afisia o; elabena Mawue dɔm ɖe mia ŋgɔ, bena maɖe miaƒe agbe.

eng: When they stopped to spend the night, one of them opened his sack to feed his donkey, and he saw his silver at the top of his sack.
local: Ke esi woɖo dɔƒe, eye wo dometɔ ɖeka tu eƒe kotoku, be yeana nu yeƒe tedzi la, tete wòkpɔ be, yeƒe ga le blia dzi le kotokua me.

eng: Seven years of great abundance are now coming throughout the entire land of Egypt.
local: Fe adre gbɔna, si me nuɖuɖu ado agbogbo ɖe Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi.

eng: They replied, Why does my master talk to us like this? Your servants would never do such a thing.
local: Wodo ŋu nɛ bena: Ao, aƒetɔ, nukaŋuti nèle nu ƒom ale? Nu sia wɔwɔ nede megbe xaa tso wò dɔlawo gbɔ!.

eng: When Joseph came into the house, they presented him the gift they had brought with them into the house, and they bowed low in front of him.
local: Esi Yosef va aƒea me la, wotsɔ nunana, si wotsɔ vɛ la, yi ne, eye wodze klo de ta agu nɛ.

eng: Then Jacob left Beer-sheba. Israels sons put their father Jacob, their children, and their wives on the wagons Pharaoh had sent to carry him.
local: Tete Yakob dze mɔ tso Berseba, eye Israel ƒe viŋutsuwo tsɔ wo fofo Yakob kple wo viwo kple wo srɔ̃wo de tasiaɖam, siwo Farao ɖo ɖa la me, be woakplɔ wo ayii.

eng: He said to his brothers, My silvers been returned. Its right here in my sack. Their hearts stopped. Terrified, they said to each other, What has God done to us.
local: Eye wògblɔ na nɔviawo bena: Kpɔ ɖa, wogagbugbɔ nye ga nam, eya enye si le nye kotokua me! Tete wolulu, eye wodzo nyanyanya kpo wo nɔewo hegblɔ bena: Nuka enye si Mawu wo ɖe mia ŋu?.

eng: Weve already had two years of famine in the land, and there are five years left without planting or harvesting.
local: Fe eve enye si dɔwuame le anyigba dzi, eye wògasusɔ ƒe atɔ̃, siwo me womaŋlɔ nu, eye womaŋe nu o.

eng: The silver that we found at the top of our sacks, weve just brought back to you from the land of Canaan. We didnt steal silver or gold from your masters house.
local: Ga, si miekpɔ le miaƒe kotokuawo mele blia dzi la, míegatsɔ wo tso Kanaan-nyigba dzi vɛ na wò, aleke awɔ míafi klosalo alo sika le wò aƒetɔ ƒe me mahã?.

eng: After them, seven years of famine will appear, and all of the abundance in the land of Egypt will be forgotten. The famine will devastate the land.
local: Eyome la dɔŋɔliƒe adre ava, eye woaŋlɔ alesi nuɖuɖu do agbogbo ɖe Egipte-nyigba dzi la be; eve dɔwuame agblẽ anyigba la.

eng: He asked them how they were and said, How is your elderly father, about whom you spoke? Is he still alive.
local: Eye wòdo gbe na wo nyuie bia wo bena: Mia fofo tsitsi, si ŋu mieƒo nu le nam la, li sesĩe, eye ele agbe mahã?.

eng: They took their livestock and their possessions that they had acquired in the land of Canaan, and arrived in Egypt, Jacob and all of his children with him.
local: Eye wokplɔ woƒe lãwo, eye wotsɔ woƒe kesinɔnu, siwo katã wokpɔ le Kanaan-nyigba dzi la, yi Egipte, Yakob kple viawo katã.

eng: When they got back to their father Jacob in the land of Canaan, they described to him everything that had happened to them.
local: Esi wogbɔ va wo fofo Yakob gbã le Kanaan-nyigba dzi la, wogblɔ nya, siwo katã dzo ɖe wo dzi la, nɛ bena:.

eng: God sent me before you to make sure youd survive and to rescue your lives in this amazing way.
local: Ke Mawu dɔm ɖe mia ŋgɔ, bena mia ŋutɔwo kple mia viwo míatsi agbe, eye ame geɖewo nakpɔ ɖeɖe.

eng: Whoever of your servants is found with it will be put to death, and well be my masters slaves.
local: Wo dɔla, si gbɔ nakpɔe le la, naku; ke mí ame mamleawo miazu mia aƒetɔ ƒe kluviwo.

eng: No one will remember the abundance in the land because the famine that follows will be so very severe.
local: Ale bena womaganye naneke le alesi nuɖuɖu do agbogbo ɖe anyigba dzi la ŋuti o, le dɔ, si ava to la ta: elabena enu asẽ ŋutɔ.

eng: They said, Your servant our father is fine. Hes still alive. And they bowed down again with deep respect.
local: Woɖo ŋu bena: Wò dɔla, mía fofo le sesĩe, eye wogale agbe, Tete wodze klo tsyo mo anyi nɛ.

eng: His sons and grandsons, his daughters and his granddaughters—all of his descendants he brought with him to Egypt.
local: Eye wòkplɔ viaŋutsuwo kple tɔgbuiayɔviŋutsuwo, vianyɔnuwo kple tɔgbuiayɔvinyɔnuwo kple eƒe ƒome la katã va Egipte.

eng: Joseph went to inform Pharaoh and said, My father and brothers with their flocks, herds, and everything they own have come from the land of Canaan and are now in the land of Goshen.
local: Tete Yosef yi ɖagblɔ na Farao bena: Fofonye kple nɔvinyewo tso Kanaan-nyigba dzi va kple woƒe alẽwo, nyiwo kpakple nusianu, si le wo si la, eye wole Gosen-nyigba dzi.

eng: The man, the countrys governor, spoke to us with a harsh tone and accused us of being spies in the country.
local: Ŋutsu, si enye anyigba dzi ɖula la, he nya sesẽ kple mí, eye wobu mí ŋkutsalawoe.

eng: You didnt send me here; it was God who made me a father to Pharaoh, master of his entire household, and ruler of the whole land of Egypt.
local: Menye miawoe dɔm ɖe afisia o, ke boŋ Mawue, eye wòtsɔm wɔ adaŋudela vevi na Farao, eye wòɖom amegãe ɖe eƒe aƒe dzi kple dziɖulae ɖe Egipte-nyigba blibo la katã dzi.

eng: He said, Fine. Well do just as youve said. Whoever is found with it will be my slave, and the rest of you will go free.
local: Eɖo ŋu bena: Enyo, woawɔ nenema, abe alesi miegblɔ ene. Amesi gbɔ woakpɔe ale la, nazu nye kluvi: ke mi ame mamleawo miadzo ayi faa.

eng: The dream occurred to Pharaoh twice because God has determined to do it, and God will make it happen soon.
local: Ke nusi ta Farao ku drɔ̃ea zi eve ɖo la enye: Mawu ɖoe kplikpa, eye Mawu le ewo ge kaba.

eng: Joseph looked up and saw his brother Benjamin, his own mothers son, and he said, Is this your youngest brother whom you told me about? God be gracious to you, my son.
local: Esi wòfɔ kɔ dzi kpɔ dadavia Benyamin la, ebia bena: Esia nye mia nɔvi ɖevitɔ, si ŋu mieƒo nu le nam mahã? Eye wõgblɔ be: Mawu nayra wò, vinye!.

eng: These are the names of the Israelites who went to Egypt, including Jacob and his sons. Jacobs oldest son was Reuben.
local: Esiawo nye Israel ƒe vi, siwo va Egipte la, ƒe ŋkɔwo, Yakob kple viaŋutsuwo. Yakob ƒe ŋgɔgbevi enye Ruben.

eng: From all of his brothers, he selected five men and presented them before Pharaoh.
local: Eye wòkplɔ nɔvia atɔ̃ ɖe asi yi ɖatsɔ fia Farao.

eng: We told him, Were honest men, not spies.
local: Ke míegblɔ nɛ bena, nyateƒetɔwo míenye, eye míenye ŋkutsalawo o.

eng: Hurry! Go back to your father. Tell him this is what your son Joseph says: God has made me master of all of Egypt. Come down to me. Dont delay.
local: Eyata netsɔ, ne miayi fofonye gbɔ, eye miagblɔ nɛ bena: Ale viwò Yosef gblɔ esi: Mawu ɖom amegãe ɖe Egipte-nyigba blibo la katã dzi; va gbɔnye, megahe ɖe megbe o!.

eng: Everyone quickly lowered their sacks down to the ground and each opened his sack.
local: Enumake wo dome ɖesiaɖe ɖiɖi eƒe kotokua ɖe anyigba, eye amesiame tu eƒe kotoku la.

eng: Now Pharaoh should find an intelligent, wise man and give him authority over the land of Egypt.
local: Azɔ Farao nedi ame, si dze aɖaŋu, eye tagbɔ li nɛ ta, ɖo Egipte-nyigba la nu.

eng: Josephs feelings for his brother were so strong he was about to weep, so he rushed to another room and wept there.
local: Enumake Yosef tso nuƒoƒo la, eye eƒe dɔme trɔ ɖe nɔvia ŋu, eye wòdi be, yeafa avi; eyata wòyi ɖe eƒe xɔgã me ɖafa avi le afima.

eng: Reubens sons were Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
local: Ruben viŋutsuwo nye: Henox, Palku, Xezron kple Karmi.

eng: Pharaoh said to Josephs brothers, What do you do? They said to Pharaoh, Your servants are shepherds, both we and our ancestors.
local: Eye Farao bia nɔviawo bena: Dɔ ka wɔlawo míenye? Woɖo ŋu na Farao bena: Lãnyilawo wò dɔlawo nye abe mia tɔgbuiwo ke ene.

eng: We are twelve brothers, all our fathers sons. One of us is gone, but the youngest is right now with our father in the land of Canaan.
local: Nɔvi wuieve míenye, eye fofo ɖekae dzi mí. Ke ame ɖeka megali o, eye miaƒe ɖevitɔ tsi fofoa gbɔ le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

eng: You may live in the land of Goshen, so you will be near me, your children, your grandchildren, your flocks, your herds, and everyone with you.
local: Eye nanɔ Gosen-nyigba dzi, ate ɖe ŋunye, wò ŋutɔ, viwòŋutsuwo kple wò tɔgbuiyɔviwo, wò alẽwo kple nyiwo kpakple nusiwo katã le asiwò.

eng: He searched the oldest first and the youngest last, and the cup was found in Benjamins sack.
local: Eye wòde asi edidi me, dze egɔme tso xoxoetɔ dzi va wu enu ɖe ɖevitɔ dzi, eye wòkpɔ kplu la le Benyamin ƒe kotoku me.

eng: Then Pharaoh should appoint administrators over the land and take one-fifth of all the produce of the land of Egypt during the seven years of abundance.
local: Eyata Farao nadi dzikpɔlawo aɖo anyigba la dzi kaba, bena ne nuɖuɖu do agbogbo ɖe anyigba dzi le ƒe adreawo me la, woadzɔ nuawo ƒe atɔ̃lia.

eng: He washed his face, came back, pulled himself together, and said, Set out the dinner.
local: Emegbe etutu mo, eye wògado go hezi eɖokui dzi gblɔ bena: Mido kplɔ̃!.

eng: Simeons sons were Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, whose mother was a Canaanite.
local: Simeon viŋutsuwo nye: Yemuel, Yamin, Ohad. Yaxin, Zoxar kple Saul, si enye Kanaan-nyɔnu ƒe vi.

eng: They continued, Weve come to the land as immigrants because the famine is so severe in the land of Canaan that there are no more pastures for your servants flocks. Please allow your servants to settle in the land of Goshen.
local: Eye wogblɔ na Farao bena: Amedzrowo míenye va afisia na ge; elabena gbe megali na wò dɔlawo ƒe lãwo o, eye dɔwuame nu sẽ ŋutɔ le Kanaan-nyigba dzi. Eyata miede kuku, na wò dolawo nano Gosen-nyigba dzi.

eng: The man, the countrys governor, told us, This is how I will know you are honest men: Leave one of your brothers with me, take grain for those in your households who are hungry, and go.
local: Tete ŋutsu, si enye anyigba dzi ɖula la, gblɔ na mí bena: Esia me madze sii ale bena, nyateƒetɔwo míenye: Migblẽ mia nɔvi ɖeka ɖe gbɔnye, ke mi ame mamleawo mitso bli yi na mia ƒomeawo, bena woatsi dɔwuame nui.

eng: I will support you there, so you, your household, and everyone with you wont starve, since the famine will still last five years.
local: Makpɔ dziwò le afima, elabena esusɔ ƒe atɔ̃ dɔwuame naganɔ anyi, bena hiã nagatu wò ŋutɔ, viwòwo kple aƒewòmetɔwo katã o.

eng: At this, they tore their clothing. Then everyone loaded their donkeys, and they returned to the city.
local: Tete wodze woƒe awuwo, eye wo dometɔ ɖesiaɖe gado agba na eƒe tedzi, eye wogatrɔ va du la me.

eng: During the good years that are coming, they should collect all such food and store the grain under Pharaohs control, protecting the food in the cities.
local: eye woaƒo nuɖuɖuwo nu ƒu le ƒe nyui, siwo gbɔna la me, eye woadzra bliawo ɖo ɖe avawo me le duwo me na Farao.

eng: So they set out his food by himself, their food by themselves, and the Egyptians who ate with him by themselves because Egyptians dont allow themselves to eat with Hebrews; the Egyptians think it beneath their dignity.
local: Tete woɖo kplɔ nɛ ɖe vovo, eye woɖoe na woawo hã ɖe vovo, nenema ke woɖoe ɖe vovo na Egiptetɔ, siwo ɖua nu kplii la hã. Egiptetɔwo meɖua nu kple Hebritɔwo o, elabena enye ŋunyɔnuwɔwɔ le woƒe ŋku me.

eng: Levis sons were Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
local: Lewi ƒe viŋutsuwo nye: Gerson, Kehat kple Merari.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, Since your father and brothers have arrived.
local: Tete Farao gblɔ na Yosef bena: Fofowò kple nɔviwòwo va gbɔwò.

eng: But bring back your youngest brother to me. Then I will know that you are not spies but honest men. I will give your brother back to you, and you may travel throughout the country.
local: Mikplɔ mia nɔvi ɖevitɔ la vɛ nam, ne manyae bena, menye ŋkutsalawo mienye o, ke boŋ nyateƒetɔwo míenye. Emegbe maɖe asi le mia nɔvi la ŋu na mi, eye miatsa le anyigba la dzi faa.

eng: You and my brother Benjamin have seen with your own eyes that Im speaking to you.
local: Mia ŋutɔwo kple nɔvinye Benyamin hã miekpɔe kple miaƒe ŋkuwo bena, nye ŋutɔe ƒo nu kpli mi.

eng: When Judah and his brothers arrived at Josephs house, he was still there, and they fell to the ground in front of him.
local: Eye esi Yuda kple nɔviawo va Yosef ƒe me la, Yosef gale afima ko — tete wodze klo ɖe ta agu nɛ.

eng: This food will be reserved for the seven years of famine to follow in the land of Egypt so that the land wont be ravaged by the famine.
local: Eye nuɖuɖuawo anye nusi wotu ɖo na anyigba la ɖe dɔŋɔliƒe adre, siwo ava Egipte-nyigba dzi la ŋu, bena ne dɔwuame nagagblẽ anyigba la o.

eng: They were seated in front of him from the oldest to the youngest in their exact birth order, and the men looked at each other with amazement.
local: Eye wobɔbɔ nɔ anyi dze ŋgɔe le kplɔ̃a ŋu, eye wòɖo wo tso tsitsitsɔ dzi vaseɖe ɖevitɔ dzi ɖe woƒe tsitsime nu; ke ŋutsuawo ƒe nu ku, eye wode asi wo nɔewo kpɔkpɔ me.

eng: Judahs sons were Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez, and Zerah. Er and Onan both died in the land of Canaan. Perezs sons were Hezron and Hamul.
local: Yuda viŋutsuwo nye: Ger, Onan, Sela, Perez kple Serax: ke Ger kple Onan ku le Kanaan-nyigba dzi. Eye Perez viŋutsuwo nye: Xezron kple Xamul.

eng: the land of Egypt is available to you. Settle your father and brothers in the lands best location. Let them live in the land of Goshen. And if you know capable men among them, put them in charge of my own livestock.
local: Egipte-nyigba la enye si, na bena woano teƒe nyuitɔ, na fofowò kple nɔviwòwo nanɔ Gosen-nyigba la dzi Eye ne ekpa bena, ame zãzɛwo le wo dome la, ekema natsɔ nye lãhawo ade wo si.

eng: When they opened their sacks, each man found a pouch of his silver in his sack. When they and their father saw their pouches of silver, they were afraid.
local: Ke esi wotrɔ blia le kotokuawo me la, wo dome ɖesiaɖe gakpɔ eƒe gale kotokua me. Eye esi woa kple wo fofo wokpɔ ga la la, wovɔ̃ ŋutɔ.

eng: Tell my father about my power in Egypt and about everything youve seen. Hurry and bring my father down here.
local: Migblɔ bubu, si katã mekpɔ le Egipte-nyigba dzi, kple nusiwo katã miekpɔ la, fia fofonye, eye netsɔ, miakplɔ fofonye ava afisiae.

eng: Joseph said to them, Whats this youve done? Didnt you know someone like me can discover Gods plans.
local: Yosef gblɔ na wo bena: Nuka enye si miewɔ? Ɖe mienyae bena, amegã menye, eye mekaa nu o mahã?.

eng: This advice seemed wise to Pharaoh and all his servants.
local: Nya sia dze Farao kple eŋuti mewo katã ŋu.

eng: Portions of food from Josephs table were brought to them, but Benjamins portion was five times as large as theirs. So they drank together and were at ease.
local: Eye wòna womã nuɖuɖu, si le egbɔ la, na wo, ke Benyamin tɔ sɔ gbɔ wu wo katã tɔ zi atɔ̃. Emegbe la wono nu kplii, eye wokpɔ dzidzɔ.

eng: Joseph brought his father Jacob and gave him an audience with Pharaoh. Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
local: Emegbe la Yosef kplɔ fofoa Yakob va fia Farao gbɔ, tete Yakob yra Farao.

eng: Issachars sons were Tola, Puvah, Iob, and Shimron.
local: Isaxar viŋutsuwo nye: Tola, Pura, Yob kple Simron.

eng: Their father Jacob said to them, Youve taken my children from me. Josephs gone. Simeons gone. And you are taking Benjamin. All this cant really be happening to me.
local: Tete wo fofo Yakob gblɔ na wo bena: Vinyewo ko ɖem miele le ŋunye! Yosef meli o, eye Simeon hã meli o, eye miele didim be, miagaxɔ Benyamin hã! Dzinye koe nu siawo katã le dzɔdzɔm ɖo!.

eng: He threw his arms around his brother Benjamins neck and wept, and Benjamin wept on his shoulder.
local: Emegbe la ekpla asi kɔ na Benyamin, eye wòfa avi, eye Benyamin hã fa avi ɖe eƒe kɔ dzi.

eng: Judah replied, What can we say to my master? What words can we use? How can we prove we are innocent? God has found your servants guilty. We are now your slaves, all of us, including the one found with the cup.
local: Eye Yuda ɖo ŋu bena: Nuka miagblɔ na nye aƒetɔ, eye nu ka míaƒo, alo aleke miaɖe mia ɖokui nuí, esi Mawu kpɔ wò dɔlawo ƒe vodada la? Kpɔ ɖa, miawo kple amesi gbɔ wokpɔ kplua le la, miazu nye aƒetɔ ƒe kluviwo.

eng: and Pharaoh said to his servants, Can we find a man with more God-given gifts than this one.
local: Tete Farao gblɔ na eŋuti meawo bena: Ɖe míate ŋu akpɔ amea ɖe, si me Mawu ƒe gbɔgbɔ la le abe eya ene mahã?.

eng: and Pharaoh said to Jacob, How old are you.
local: Eye Farao bia Yakob bena: Ƒe nenie nèxɔ?.

eng: Zebuluns sons were Sered, Elon, and Jahleel.
local: Zebulon viŋutsuwo nye: Sered, Elon kple Yaxleel.

eng: Reuben said to his father, You may put both of my sons to death if I dont bring him back to you. Make him my responsibility, and I will make sure he returns to you.
local: Ruben gblɔ na fofoa bena. Ne nyemegakplɔe vɛ na wò o la, ekema nawu nye ŋutsuvi eveawo! Tsɔe de asi nam ko, eye magakplɔe vɛ na wò!.

eng: He kissed all of his brothers and wept, embracing them. After that, his brothers were finally able to talk to him.
local: Eyome la eɖuɖɔ nu na nɔviawo katã, eye wòfa avi ɖe woƒe kɔ dzi, emegbe la nɔviawo ƒo nu kplii.

eng: Joseph said, Id never do such a thing. Only the man found with the cup will be my slave. As for the rest of you, you are free to go back to your father.
local: Eɖo ŋu bena: Esia wɔwɔ neɖe megbe xaa tso gbɔnye! Amesi gbɔ wokpɔ kplua le la, eya anye nye kluvi: ke miawo miagbugbɔ ayi mia fofo gbɔ faa.

eng: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, Since God has made all this known to you, no one is as intelligent and wise as you are.
local: Eye Farao gblɔ na Yosef bena: Esi Mawu ɖe nu, siawo katã fia wò la, amea ɖeke meli, si dze aɖaŋu, eye wònya nu abe wò ene o.

eng: Jacob said to Pharaoh, Ive been a traveler for years. My years have been few and difficult. They dont come close to the years my ancestors lived during their travels.
local: Yakob ɖo ŋu na Farao bena: Ƒe, siwo katã metsɔ zɔ mɔe la, wonye ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blaetɔ̃. Nye agbenɔƒewo mesɔ gbɔ o, eye mekpe hiã geɖe le wo me, eye mede tɔgbuinyewo ƒe ƒe, siwo wotsɔ zɔ mɔe la nu o.

eng: These are the sons Leah bore to Jacob in Paddan-aram. Her daughter was Dinah. All of these persons, including his sons and daughters, totaled .
local: Esiawo nye Lea ƒe viŋutsu, siwo wòdzi na Yakob le Mesopotamia hekpe ɖe vianyɔnu Dina ŋu: viaŋutsuwo kple vianyɔnuwo katã le ame blaetɔ̃ vɔ etɔ̃.

eng: But Jacob said to him, My son wont go down with you because his brothers dead and hes been left all alone. If anything were to happen to him on the trip you are taking, you would send me—old as I am—to my grave in grief.
local: Eɖo ŋu bena: Vinye la mayi kple mi o, elabena nɔvia ku, eye eya ɖeka koe susɔ ɖi. Ne dzɔgbevɔ̃ea ɖe adzɔ ɖe edzi le mɔ dzi la, ekema miawoe na nye amegã wɔtamee sia metsɔ konyifafa yi tsiẽƒee.

eng: When Pharaohs household heard the message Josephs brothers have arrived, both Pharaoh and his servants were pleased.
local: Eye Farao kple eƒemetɔwo se bena, Yosef nɔviwo va. Eye wòdze Farao ŋuti mewo katã ŋu.

eng: Judah approached him and said, Please, my master, allow your servant to say something to my master without getting angry with your servant since you are like Pharaoh himself.
local: Tete Yuda te ɖe eŋu gblɔ be: Taflatse, nye aƒetɔ! Na wò dɔla naƒo nu kple nye aƒetɔ, eye megabi dzi ɖe wò dɔla ŋu o; elabena èle abe Farao ene.

eng: You will be in charge of my kingdom, and all my people will obey your command. Only as the enthroned king will I be greater than you.
local: Eyata wòe anɔ nye aƒe nu, eye nye dukɔ la katã aɖo to wò nyawo; negbe fiazikpui la ko matsɔ awu wò.

eng: Gads sons were Ziphion, Haggi, Shuni, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi, and Areli.
local: Gad viŋutsuwo nye: Zifyon, Hagi, Lumi, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi kple Areli.

eng: Jacob blessed Pharaoh and left Pharaohs presence.
local: Emegbe la Yakob yayra Farao, eye wòdzo le Farao gbɔ.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, Give your brothers these instructions: Load your pack animals and go back to the land of Canaan.
local: Tete Farao gblɔ na Yosef bena: Gblɔ na nɔviwòawo be, mido agba na miaƒe lãwo, eye míayi Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

eng: My master asked his servants, Do you have a father or brother.
local: Nye aƒetɔ bia eƒe dɔlawo bena: Ɖe fofo alo nɔviŋutsu gale mia siá?.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, Know this: Ive given you authority over the entire land of Egypt.
local: Eye Farao gblɔ na Yosef bena: Kpɔ ɖa, meɖo wò Egipte-nyigba blibo la nu.

eng: Joseph settled his father and brothers and gave them property in the land of Egypt, in the best location in the land of Rameses, just as Pharaoh had ordered.
local: Eye Yosef fia nɔƒe fofoa kple nɔviawo, eye wòna anyigba wo le Egipte le Ramses, afisi anyigba nyuitɔ le, abe alesi Farao de se nɛ ene.

eng: Ashers sons were Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, Beriah, and their sister Serah. Beriahs sons were Heber and Malchiel.
local: Aser viŋutsuwo nye: Yimna, Yiswa, Yiswi, Beria kple wo nɔvinyɔnu Gerax. Beria viŋutsuwo nye: Xeber kple Malkiel.

eng: Get your father and your households and come back to me. Let me provide you with good things from the land of Egypt so that you may eat the lands best food.
local: aɖakplɔ mia fofo kple mia ƒomeawo ava gbɔnye, eye mana anyigba nyuitɔ mi le Egipte, ne miaɖu nu ʋaʋãawo le anyigba la dzi.

eng: And we said to my master, Yes, we have an elderly father and a young brother, born when he was old. His brother is dead and hes his mothers only child. But his father loves him.
