local: Míegblɔ na nye aƒetɔ bena: fofo gale mia si, si ku amegã ɖeɖi, eye wodzi dɔmlɔea nɛ le eƒe amegãkuku me. Ke dadavia ku, eye eya ɖeka koe susɔ na dada, eye fofoa lɔ̃nɛ.

eng: Pharaoh took his signet ring from his hand and put it on Josephs hand, he dressed him in linen clothes, and he put a gold necklace around his neck.
local: Eye Farao ɖe eƒe ŋkɔsigɛ le asi hetsɔ do na Yosef, eye wòdo aklalabiɖibiɖiwu nɛ, eyome wòtsɔ sikakɔsɔkɔsɔ de kɔ nɛ.

eng: These are the sons of Zilpah, whom Laban gave to his daughter Leah. She bore these to Jacob, a total of persons.
local: Esiawo nye Silpa, amesi Laban tsɔ na vianyɔnu Lea la ƒe viŋutsu, siwo wòdzi na Yakob; wo katã le ame wuiade.

eng: Joseph provided food for his father, his brothers, and his fathers entire household, in proportion to the number of children.
local: Eye Yosef tsɔ nuɖuɖu kpɔ fofoa, nɔviawo kple fofoa ƒemetɔwo katã dzi, amesiame kple evia.

eng: You told your servants, Bring him down to me so I can see him.
local: Eye nègblɔ na wò dɔlawo bena: mikplɔe vɛ nam, ne makpɔ.

eng: Give them these instructions too: Take wagons from the land of Egypt for your children and wives, and pick up your father and come back.
local: Ke wò la, gblɔ nusi woawɔ la na wo! Mitsɔ tasiaɖamwo tso Egipte-nyigba dzi ɖe asi hena mia viwo kple mia srɔ̃wo, eye mina mia fofo hã naɖoe ava.

eng: He put Joseph on the chariot of his second-in-command, and everyone in front of him cried out, Attention! So Pharaoh installed him over the entire land of Egypt.
local: Emegbe la ena wòɖo tasiaɖam evelia, si kplɔ fia tɔ ɖo la, eye kpodolawo le eŋgɔ le gbɔgblɔm bena: Midze klo, midze klo! Ale wòɖoe Egipte-nyigba la katã nu.

eng: The sons of Jacobs wife Rachel were Joseph and Benjamin.
local: Yakob srɔ̃ Raxel ƒe viŋutsuwo nye Yosef kple Benyamin.

eng: There was no food in the land because the famine was so severe. The land of Egypt and the land of Canaan dried up from the famine.
local: Ke nuɖuɖua ɖeke megale anyigba blibo la dzi o, elabena dɔ la nu sẽ ŋutɔ, eye dɔwuame na Egipte kple Kanaan-nyigba dzi ƒu.

eng: And we said to my master, The young man cant leave his father. If he leaves, his father will die.
local: Mieɖo ŋu na nye aƒetɔ bena: Ɖevi la mate ŋu agblẽ fofoa ɖi o, ne egblẽe ɖi la, ekema fofoa aku.

eng: Dont worry about your possessions because you will have good things from the entire land of Egypt.
local: Eye migana miaƒe ŋkuwo nanɔ miaƒe xɔmenuwo ŋu, eye woƒe nu nave mi o, elabena Egipte-nyigba dzi nu nyuiwo katã lanye mia tɔ.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, I am Pharaoh; no one will do anything or go anywhere in all the land of Egypt without your permission.
local: Eye Farao gblɔ na Yosef bena: Nye enye Farao, ke ne wòa melɔ̃ o la, amea ɖeke mate ŋu akɔ eƒe asi alo afɔ dzi le Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi o.

eng: To Joseph, in the land of Egypt, were born Manasseh and Ephraim. Asenath daughter of Potiphera, priest of Heliopolis, bore them to him.
local: Viŋutsu, siwo On-nunɔla Potifera ƒe vinyɔnu Asnat dzi na Yosef le Egipte-nyigba dzi wonye Manase kple Efrayim.

eng: Joseph collected all of the silver to be found in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan for the grain, which people came to buy, and he deposited it in Pharaohs treasury.
local: Mlɔeba la Yosef ƒo ga, siwo Egiptetɔwo kple Kanaantɔwo tsɔ ƒle bli le esi la, nu ƒu, eye wòtsɔ ga la yi Farao ƒe mee.

eng: You said to your servants, If your youngest brother doesnt come down with you, youll never see my face again.
local: Tete nègblɔ na wò dɔlawo bena: ne miekplɔ mia nɔvi ɖevitɔ vɛ o la, ekema migava do ɖe nye ŋku me o.

eng: So Israels sons did that. Joseph gave them wagons as Pharaoh instructed, and he gave them provisions for the road.
local: Eye Israel viwo wɔ nenema, eye Yosef tsɔ tasiaɖamwo na wo le Farao ƒe nya la nu, eye wòna mɔ dzi nuɖuɖu hã wo.

eng: Pharaoh renamed Joseph, Zaphenath-paneah, and married him to Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera the priest of Heliopolis. Then Joseph assumed control of the land of Egypt.
local: Eye Farao na ŋkɔ Yosef bena Zafnat Paneax , eye wòtsɔ On-nunɔla Potifera ƒe vinyɔnu Asnat nɛ wòɖe. Emegbe la Yosef tsa le Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi.

eng: Benjamins sons were Bela, Becher, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim, and Ard.
local: Benyamin viŋutsuwo nye Bela, Bexer, Asbel, Gera, Naaman, Exi, Ros, Mupim, Xupim kple Ard.

eng: The silver from the land of Egypt and from the land of Canaan had been spent, and all of the Egyptians came to Joseph and said, Give us food. Why should we die before your eyes, just because the silver is gone.
local: Ke esi ga megale Egipte kple Kanaan-nyigba dzi o la, Egiptetɔwo katã va Yosef gbɔ, eye wogblɔ bena. Na nuɖuɖu mi, nukaŋuti míaku ɖe gbɔwò? Elabena ga vɔ!.

eng: When we went back to my father your servant, we told him what you said.
local: Esi mietrɔ va wò dɔla, fofonye gbɔ la, miegblɔ nye aƒetɔ ƒe nyawo nɛ.

eng: To all of them he gave a change of clothing, but to Benjamin he gave three hundred pieces of silver and five changes of clothing.
local: Eye wòna atsyɔɖowu wo dome amesiame: ke ena klosalo sekel alafa etɔ kple atsyɔɖowu atɔ Benyamin ya.

eng: These are Rachels sons who were born to Jacob, a total of persons.
local: Esiawo nye Raxel ƒe viŋutsu, siwo wòdzi na Yakob; wo katã le ame wuiene.

eng: Joseph was years old when he began to serve Pharaoh, Egypts king, when he left Pharaohs court and traveled through the entire land of Egypt.
local: Yosef xɔ ƒe blaetɔ̃, esime wòtsi tre ɖe Egipte-fia Farao ƒe ŋku me. Eye Yosef dzo le Farao gbɔ yi ɖatsa le Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi.

eng: Joseph said, Give me your livestock, and I will give you food for your livestock if the silver is gone.
local: Yosef ɖo ŋu bena: Ne ga vɔ le mia si la, ekema mikplɔ miaƒe lãhawo ve, eye mana nuɖuɖu mi ɖe wo teƒe!.

eng: Our father told us, Go back and buy for us a little food.
local: Emegbe mía fofo gblɔ bena: Migayi ɖa ƒle bli vɛ.

eng: To his father he sent ten male donkeys carrying goods from Egypt, ten female donkeys carrying grain and bread, and rations for his father for the road.
local: Eye wòɖo tedzi ewo, siwo tsɔ Egipte-nu veviwo kple tedzinɔ ewo, siwo tsɔ bli kple abolo kple mɔ dzi nuɖuɖu, ɖe fofoa.

eng: Dans son was Hushim.
local: Dan ƒe viŋutsu enye Xusim.

eng: During the seven years of abundance, the land produced plentifully.
local: Eye anyigba la wɔ nu ŋutɔ le ƒe adreawo me.

eng: So they brought their livestock to Joseph, and Joseph gave them food for the horses, flocks, cattle, and donkeys. He got them through that year with food in exchange for all of their livestock.
local: Tete wokplɔ woƒe lãhawo ve na Yosef, eye Yosef na bli wo ɖe woƒe sɔwo, alẽhawo kple nyihawo kple tedziwo teƒe, eye wòtsɔ bli kpo wo dzi le ƒe ma me ɖe woƒe lãhawo teƒe.

eng: But we said, We cant go down. We will go down only if our youngest brother is with us. We wont be able to gain an audience with the man without our youngest brother with us.
local: Ke mieɖo ŋu bena: Míate ŋui o! Ne mia nɔvi ɖevitɔ le eme la, eya ko míayi. Elabena miate ŋu ado ɖe ŋutsu la ƒe ŋku me o, ne mia nɔvi ɖevitɔ mele mía me o.

eng: He sent his brothers off; and as they were leaving, he told them, Dont be worried about the trip.
local: Emegbe edo mɔ nɔviawo, eye hafi wòadze mɔ la, exlɔ̃ nu wo bena: Migawɔ dzre le mɔ dzi o!.

eng: He collected all of the food during the seven years of abundance in the land of Egypt, and stored the food in cities. In each city, he stored the food from the fields surrounding it.
local: Eye wòƒo agblemenu siwo katã wɔ le ƒe adreawo me, le esime nuɖuɖu do agbogbo ɖe Egipte-nyigba dzi la, nu ƒu, eye wòdzra du sia du ƒe agblemetiwo ɖo ɖe ɖu la ŋutɔ me.

eng: Naphtalis sons were Jahzeel, Guni, Jezer, and Shillem.
local: Naftali viŋutsuwo nye: Yaxzeel, Guni, Yezer kple Silem.

eng: When that year was over, they came to him the next year and said to him, We cant hide from my master that the silver is spent and that weve given the livestock to my master. All thats left for my master is our corpses and our farmland.
local: Ƒe sia va yi. Le ƒe bubu me la wogava egbɔ, eye wogblɔ nɛ bena: Aƒetɔ, míate ŋu aɣla nyaa ɖeke ɖe wò o. Míaƒe ga vɔ, eye míaƒe lãhawo ge ɖe mía aƒetɔ si me. Naneke megali miatsɔ na mía aƒetɔ o, negbe mía ŋutɔwo kple miaƒe agblenyigbawo koe susɔ.

eng: Your servant my father said to us, You know that my wife gave birth to two sons for me.
local: Tete wò dɔla, fofonye gblɔ na mi bena: Mienyae bena, vi eve siawo ko srɔ̃nye dzi nam.

eng: So they left Egypt and returned to their father Jacob in the land of Canaan.
local: Ale wotso le Egipte, eye wova ɖo wo fofo Yakob gbɔ le Kanɔan-nyigba dzi.

eng: These are the sons of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to his daughter Rachel. She bore these to Jacob, a total of persons.
local: Esiawo nye Bilha, amesi Laban tso na vianyɔnu Raxel la, ƒe viŋutsu, siwo wòdzi na: Yakob; wo katã le ame adre.

eng: Joseph amassed grain like the sand of the sea. There was so much that he stopped trying to measure it because it was beyond measuring.
local: Eye Yosef tu bliawo ɖo wòsɔ gbɔ ŋutɔ abe ƒutake ene, ale bena womagate ŋu adzidzee o, eye wodzudzɔ edzidzedzidze.

eng: Why should we die before your eyes, we and our farmland too? Buy us and our farms for food, and we and our farms will be under Pharaohs control. Give us seed so that we can stay alive and not die, and so that our farmland wont become unproductive.
local: Nukaŋuti miaku ɖe gbɔwò? Tsɔ bli ƒle mia kple míaƒe agblenyigbawo, bena mía ŋutɔwo kple miaƒe agblenyigbawo nanye Farao tɔ! Na bli mí, bena míatsi agbe, eye miagaku, ne míaƒe agblenyigbawo nagazu afuti o.

eng: One disappeared and I said, He must have been torn up by a wild animal, and I havent seen him since.
local: Ɖeka gblẽm ɖi, eye mesusu be, lãa ɖe vuvui, eye va ɖo egbee nyemegakpɔe kpɔ o.

eng: They announced to him, Josephs still alive! Hes actually ruler of all the land of Egypt! Jacobs heart nearly failed, and he didnt believe them.
local: Eye wogblɔ nɛ bena: Yosef gale agbe, eye wònye dziɖula le Egipte-nyigba blibo la katã dzi. Ke nya la ka ɖe Yakob, eye mexɔe se o.

eng: All of the persons going to Egypt with Jacob—his own children, excluding Jacobs sons wives—totaled persons.
local: Yakob vi, siwo katã nu kplii yi Egipte, ne woɖe viaŋutsuwo srɔ̃wo le eme la, wole ame blaade vɔ ade.

eng: Before the years of famine arrived, Asenath the daughter of Potiphera, priest of Heliopolis, gave birth to two sons for Joseph.
local: Hafi dɔŋɔlia nava la, On-nunɔla Potifera ƒe vinyɔnu Asnat dzi ŋutsuvi eve na Yosef.

eng: So Joseph bought all of Egypts farmland for Pharaoh because every Egyptian sold his field when the famine worsened. So the land became Pharaohs.
local: Tete Yosef ƒle Egiptetɔwo ƒe agblewo katã na Farao. Eye Egiptetɔwo dome amesiame tsɔ eƒe agblenyigba dzra, elabena dɔwuamea nu sẽ ŋutɔ; ale anyigba la katã zu Farao tɔ.

eng: And if you take this one from me too, something terrible will happen to him, and you will send me—old as I am—to my grave in despair.
local: Ne miekplɔ eya hã le gbɔnye yii, eye dzɔgbevɔ̃ea ɖe adzɔ ɖe edzi la, ekema etso mia gbɔ, ne nye amegã wɔtamee sia matsɔ konyifafa ayi tsiẽƒee.

eng: When they told him everything Joseph had said to them, and when he saw the wagons Joseph had sent to carry him, Jacob recovered.
local: Ke esi wogblɔ nya, siwo katã Yosef gblɔ na wo la, nɛ, eye wòkpɔ tasiaɖam, siwo Yosef ɖo ɖee, be woaɖo ava la, wo fofo Yakob ƒe dzi gaɖo me.

eng: Josephs sons born to him in Egypt were persons. Thus, all of the persons in Jacobs household going to Egypt totaled .
local: Ŋutsuvi, siwo wodzi na Yosef le Egipte la, wole ame eve. Yakob vi, siwo katã va Egipte la, wole ame blaadre.

eng: Joseph named the oldest son Manasseh, because, he said, God has helped me forget all of my troubles and everyone in my fathers household.
local: Eye Yosef na ŋkɔ eƒe ŋgɔgbevia bena Manase, elabena egblɔ be Mawu na meŋlɔ nye fukpekpewo kple nye ƒometɔwo be.

eng: He moved the people to the cities from one end of Egypt to the other.
local: Eye eƒe dukɔ la katã zu awɔbamewo tso Egipte ƒe go ɖeka me yi go bubu me.

eng: After this happened, Joseph was told, Your father is getting weaker, so he took his two sons Manasseh and Ephraim with him.
local: Le nusiawo megbe la wogblɔ na Yosef bena: Fofowò le dɔ lém. Tete wòkplɔ viaŋutsu eveawo, Efrayim kple Manase, ɖe asi.

eng: When I now go back to your servant my father without the young man—whose life is so bound up with his.
local: Azɔ ne mava ɖo wò dɔla fofonye gbɔ, eye ɖevi, si gbɔ melɔ̃a nu le o la, megale mia dome o la.

eng: Then Israel said, This is too much! My son Joseph is still alive! Let me go and see him before I die.
local: Eye Israel gblɔ bena: Esɔ gbɔ be, vinye Yosef gale agbe! Mayi aɖakpɔe ɖa hafi aku!.

eng: Israel had sent Judah ahead to Joseph so that Joseph could explain the way to Goshen. Then they arrived in the land of Goshen.
local: Ke edɔ Yuda ɖe eŋgɔ ɖo ɖe Yosef gbɔ, bena wòaɖe ame aɖo ŋgɔ nɛ, ne wòayi Gosen. Eye esi woɖo Gosen-nyigba dzi la.

eng: He named the second Ephraim, because, he said, God has given me children in the land where Ive been treated harshly.
local: Eye wòna ŋkɔ evelia be Efrayim; elabena egblɔ be: Mawu na medzi ɖe dzi le afisi mekpe fu le la.

eng: However, he didnt buy the farmland of the priests because Pharaoh allowed the priests a subsidy, and they were able to eat from the subsidy Pharaoh gave them. Therefore, they didnt have to sell their farmland.
local: Ke meƒle nunɔlawo ƒe agblenyigbawo o, elabena nunɔlawo kpɔ wo tɔ gome le Farao gbɔ, eye woɖu gome, si wòxɔ tso Farao gbɔ la; eyata womedzra woƒe anyigbawo o.

eng: When Jacob was informed, Your son Joseph is here now, he pulled himself together and sat up in bed.
local: Eye wogblɔ na Yakob bena: Kpɔ ɖa, viwò Yosef va gbɔwò. Tete Israel do ŋusẽ eɖokui hefɔ ɖe ali nu.

eng: and when he sees that the young man isnt with us, he will die, and your servants will have sent our father your servant—old as he is—to his grave in grief.
local: ekema aku, ne ekpɔ be, ɖevi la megale mía dome o la. Eye wò dɔla aɖi fɔ, ne wò ɖɔla mía fofo wɔtamee la atsɔ konyifafa ayi tsiẽƒee.

eng: Joseph hitched up his chariot and went to meet his father Israel in Goshen. When he arrived, he threw his arms around his neck and wept, embracing him for a long time.
local: Yosef na woɖo eƒe tasiaɖam ɖo, eye wòɖoe yi fofoa Israel kpe ge le Gosen. Esi wòkpɔe la, ekpla asi kɔ ne, eye wòfa avi ɖe eƒe ko dzi ʋuu.

eng: The seven years of abundance in the land of Egypt came to an end.
local: Esi nuʋãƒe adre, siwo va Egipte-nyigba dzi nu yi vɔ la.

eng: Joseph said to the people, Since Ive now purchased you and your farmland for Pharaoh, heres seed for you. Plant the seed on the land.
local: Tete Yosef gblɔ na dukɔ la bena: Kpɔ ɖa, egbe meƒle mia kple miaƒe anyigbawo na Farao. Mixɔ nuku, ne miaƒã ɖe miaƒe agblewo me.

eng: Jacob said to Joseph, God Almighty appeared to me in Luz in the land of Canaan. He blessed me.
local: Eye Yakob gblɔ na Yosef bena: Mawu ŋusẽkatãtɔ la ɖe eɖokui fiãm le Lus le Kanaan-nyigba dzi, eye wòyram.

eng: I, your servant, guaranteed the young mans safety to my father, telling him, If I dont bring him back to you, it will be my fault forever.
local: Elabena wò dɔlae nɔ ɖevia nu le fofonye gbɔ, eye medo ŋugbe ne be: ne nyemegakplɔ ɖevi la vɛ na wo o la, ekema manye fɔɖila le fofonye gbɔ le nye agbe me ŋkekewo katã me.

eng: Israel said to Joseph, I can die now after seeing your face. You are really still alive.
local: Eye Israel gblɔ na Yosef bena: Azɔ maku faa, elabena megakpɔ wò agbagbee.

eng: and the seven years of famine began, just as Joseph had said. The famine struck every country, but the entire land of Egypt had bread.
local: tete dɔŋɔliƒe adreawo dze gɔme, abe alesi Yosef gblɔ ene. Eye dɔ to ɖe anyigbawo katã dzi, ke nuɖuɖu le Egipte-nyigba ya dzi.

eng: When the crop comes in, you must give one-fifth to Pharaoh. You may keep four-fifths for yourselves, for planting fields, and for feeding yourselves, those in your households, and your children.
local: Eye ne nuŋeɣi ɖo la, ekema miatsɔ agblemenuawo ƒe atɔ̃liawo ɖeka na Farao; ke atɔ̃liawo ene nanye mia ŋutɔwo mia tɔ, bena miatsɔ ɖe aƒa ɖe agbleawo me, eye ɖe nanye nuɖuɖu na mia ŋutɔwo, mia ƒemetɔwo kple mia viwo.

eng: and said to me, I am about to give you many children, to increase your numbers, and to make you a large group of peoples. I will give this land to your descendants following you as an enduring possession.
local: gblɔ nam bena: Mana nadzi, eye wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ ɖe dzi; mana nazu dukɔha, eye matsɔ anyigba sia ana megbewò viwo, be wòanye wo tɔ tegbee.

eng: Now, please let your servant stay as your slave instead of the young man so that he can go back with his brothers.
local: Eyata meɖe kuku na wò, wò dɔla nazu kluvi na nye aƒetɔ ɖe ɖevi la teƒe, ne ɖevia nayi aƒe kple nɔviawo.

eng: Joseph said to his brothers and to his fathers household, Let me go up and inform Pharaoh and tell him, My brothers and my fathers household who were in the land of Canaan have arrived.
local: Eye Yosef gblɔ na nɔvia wo kple fofoa ƒemetɔwo bena: Meyi maɖagblɔe na Farao bena: nɔvinyewo kple fofonyeƒemetɔ, siwo le Kanaan-nyigba dzi la, wova gbɔnye.

eng: When the famine ravaged the entire land of Egypt and the people pleaded to Pharaoh for bread, Pharaoh said to all of the Egyptians, Go to Joseph. Do whatever he tells you.
local: Ke esi dɔ va Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi la, dukɔ la ƒo zi va bia nuɖuɖu Farao. Eye Farao gblɔ na Egiptetɔwo bena: Miyi Yosef gbɔ, eye nusi wòagblɔ na mi la, miawɔe.

eng: The people said, Youve saved our lives. If you wish, we will be Pharaohs slaves.
local: Tete wogblɔ bena: Wòe xɔ mí ɖe agbe! Aƒetɔ, ne ànya ve mia nu ko, ekema mianye Farao ƒe awɔbamewo faa.

eng: Now, your two sons born to you in the land of Egypt before I arrived in Egypt are my own. Ephraim and Manasseh are just like Reuben and Simeon to me.
local: Ke viwòŋutsu eveawo, Efrayim kple Manase, siwo wodzi na wò le Egipte, hafi meva gbɔwò le Egipte la, nazu vinyewo abe Ruben kple Simeon ene.

eng: How can I go back to my father without the young man? I couldnt bear to see how badly my father would be hurt.
local: Aleke wɔ mayi fofonye gbɔ, ne ɖevi la mele ŋunye oa? Nyemagate ŋu akpɔ fofonye ƒe konyifaƒe o.

eng: The men are shepherds, because they own livestock. Theyve brought with them their flocks and herds and everything they own.
local: Lãhawo le ŋutsuawo si, elabena lãnyilawo wonye, eye wova kple woƒe alẽwo, nyiwo kple woƒe kesinɔnuwo katã.

eng: The famine covered every part of the land, and Joseph opened all of the granaries and sold grain to the Egyptians. In the land of Egypt, the famine became more and more severe.
local: Eye esi ɖɔ to le afiawo katã la, Yosef ʋu blivawo katã, eye wòdzra blia na Egiptetɔwo. Eye dɔ la nu le sesẽm ɖe dzi le Egipte-nyigba la dzi.

eng: So Joseph made a law that still exists today: Pharaoh receives one-fifth from Egypts farmland. Only the priests farmland didnt become Pharaohs.
local: Eye Yosef tsɔe wɔ see na Egiptetɔwo vaseɖe egbegbe bena, woatsɔ woƒe agblemenuwo ƒe atɔ̃liawo ɖeka ana Farao; negbe nunɔlawo ƒe anyigbawo koe menye Farao tɔ o.

eng: Your family who is born to you after them are yours, but their inheritance will be determined under their brothers names.
local: Ke vi, siwo nèdzi ɖe wo yome la, nanye tɔwo; eye woayɔ woƒe ŋkɔwo ɖe wo nɔviwo ƒe domenyinyi ŋu.

eng: When Pharaoh summons you and says, What do you do.
local: Ne Farao ayɔ mi, eye woabia mi bena, dɔ ka wɔlawo míenye mahã la.

eng: Every country came to Egypt to buy grain from Joseph, because in every country the famine had also become more severe.
local: Eye amewo tso afiawo katã va Egipte, be woaƒle bli le Yosef gbɔ, elabena dɔ la nu sẽ ŋutɔ le afisiafi.

eng: Israel lived in the land of Egypt, in the land of Goshen. They settled in it, had many children, and became numerous.
local: Ke Israel nɔ Egipte le Gosen-nyigba dzi, eye wotsi edzi, eye wodzi viwo hedzi ɖe dzi ŋutɔ.

eng: When I came back from Paddan-aram, Rachel died, to my sorrow, on the road in the land of Canaan, with some distance yet to go to Ephrathah, so I buried her there near the road to Ephrathah, which is Bethlehem.
local: Esi metrɔ tso Mesopotamia gbɔna va Kanaan la, Raxel ku le gbɔnye le mɔ dzi, esi mele tu Efrat, eye meɖii ɖe afima le Efrat, si enye Betlexem, mɔ to.

eng: say, Your servants have owned livestock since we were young, both we and our ancestors, so that you will be able to settle in the land of Goshen, since Egyptians think all shepherds are beneath their dignity.
local: ekema miaɖo ŋu bena: lãnyilawo wò dɔlawo nye tso ɖevime vaseɖe fifi abe mía tɔgbuiwo ke ene, bena wòaɖe mɔ na mi mianɔ Gosen-nyigba la dzi. Elabena Egiptetɔwo nyoa ŋu lãnyilawo.

eng: After Jacob had lived in the land of Egypt for seventeen years, and after he had lived a total of years.
local: Yakob nɔ Egipte-nyigba dzi ƒe wuiadre, eye Yakob ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo nye ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blane vɔ adre.

eng: When Israel saw Josephs sons, he said, Who are these.
local: Ke esi Israel kpɔ Yosef viwo la, ebia bena: Amekawoe nye esiawo?.

eng: Israels death approached. He summoned his son Joseph and said to him, If you would be so kind, lay your hand under my thigh, and be loyal and true to me. Dont bury me in Egypt.
local: Esi Israel kpɔ be, yeƒe kuɣi le ɖoɖom la, ena woyɔ via Yosef vɛ, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Ne mele vevie na wò la, ekema tsɔ wò asi ɖo nye ali me, eye nave nunye, aɖi anukware nam bena, màɖim ɖe Egipte o;.

eng: Joseph told his father, Theyre my sons, whom God gave me here. Israel said, Bring them to me and I will bless them.
local: Yosef ɖo ŋu na fofoa bena: Woawo nye vi, siwo Mawu nam le afisia! Eye wògblɔ bena: Kplɔ wo va gbɔnyee, ne mayra wo.

eng: When I lie down with my fathers, carry me from Egypt and bury me in their grave. Joseph said, I will do just as you say.
local: ke boŋ naɖim ɖe tɔgbuinyewo gbɔ, eye natsɔm atso Egipte aɖaɖi ɖe woƒe yɔdo me. Tete wòɖo ŋu bena: Mawɔe, abe alesi nèdi ene.

eng: Because Israels eyesight had failed from old age and he wasnt able to see, Joseph brought them close to him, and he kissed and embraced them.
local: Ke Israel tsi, eye eƒe ŋkuwo dzi tsyɔ, ale bena megate ŋu le nu kpɔm o. Eye esi wòkplɔ wo te ɖe eŋu la, ekpla asi kɔ na wo heɖuɖɔ nu na wo.

eng: Israel said, Give me your word! and Joseph gave his word. Then Israel slumped down at the head of the bed.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Ka atam! Eye wòka atam nɛ. Tete Yakob gadze ŋe ɖe eƒe abati la dzi.

eng: Israel said to Joseph, I didnt expect Id see your face, but now God has shown me your children too.
local: Tete Israel gblɔ na Yosef bena: Nyemesusu be, magakpɔ wo ŋku me o, ke azɔ Mawu na mekpɔ viwò wo gɔ̃ hã.

eng: Then Joseph took them from Israels knees, and he bowed low with his face to the ground.
local: Emegbe Yosef ɖe wo ɖa le eƒe ata dzi, eye wòdze klo de ta agu.

eng: Joseph took both of them, Ephraim in his right hand at Israels left hand, and Manasseh in his left hand at Israels right hand, and brought them close to him.
local: Eyome la Yosef le via eveawo ɖe asi Efrayim ɖe ɖusi, be woatrɔ ɖe Israel ƒe miã me, eye Manase ɖe miã, be wòatrɔ ɖe Israel ƒe ɖusi me, eye wokplɔ wo te ɖe eŋu.

eng: But Israel put out his right hand and placed it on the head of Ephraim, the younger one, and his left hand on Manassehs head, crossing his hands because Manasseh was the oldest son.
local: Ke Israel da eƒe nuɖusi ɖe Efrayim ƒe ta dzi, wònye eyae nye ɖevia hafi, eye wòda eƒe mia ɖe Manase ƒe ta dzi, ale wotso ga eƒe asiwò, evɔ wonye Manase enye tsitsia hafi.

eng: He blessed them and said, May the God before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked, may the God who was my shepherd from the beginning until this day.
local: Eye woyra Yosef gblɔ bena: Mawu, si ƒe ŋku me fofonye Abraham kple Isak zɔ le, Mawu, si nye kplɔlanye le nye agbe me ŋkekewo katã me va do egbee.

eng: may the divine messenger who protected me from all harm, bless the young men. Through them may my name be kept alive and the names of my fathers Abraham and Isaac. May they grow into a great multitude throughout the land.
local: mawudɔla, si ɖem tso nye hiãwo katã me la, ayra ɖeviawo eye woayɔ nye ŋkɔ kple fofonye Abraham kple Isak ƒe ŋkɔ ɖe wo ŋu, wòadzi ɖe dzi, eye woasɔ gbɔ le anyigba la dzi.

eng: When Joseph saw that his father had placed his right hand on Ephraims head, he was upset and grasped his fathers hand to move it from Ephraims head to Manassehs head.
local: Esi Yosef kpɔ bena, fofoa da eƒe nuɖusi ɖe Efrayim ƒe ta dzi la, menyo eŋu o; eyata wòlé fofoa ƒe asi, be yeaɖee ɖa le Efrayim ƒe ta dzi ahada ɖe Manase ƒe ta dzi.

eng: Joseph said to his father, No, my father! This is the oldest son. Put your right hand on his head.
local: Eye Yosef gblɔ na fofoa bena: Fofonye, menye nenema o; esia nye ŋgɔgbetɔ, tsɔ wò nuɖusi da ɖe eƒe ta dzi!.

eng: But his father refused and said, I know, my son, I know. Hell become a people too, and hell also be great. But his younger brother will be greater than he will, and his descendants will become many nations.
local: Ke fofoa gbe, eye wògblɔ bena: Menyae, vinye, menyae! Eya hã ava nye dukɔ, eye wòasẽ ŋu. Gake nɔvia ɖevitɔa asẽ ŋu awui, eye eƒe dzidzimeviwo anye dukɔ gã.

eng: Israel blessed them that day, saying, Through you, Israel will pronounce blessings, saying, May God make you like Ephraim and Manasseh. So Israel put Ephraim before Manasseh.
local: Eye wòyra wo gbemagbe gblɔ bena: Wò ŋkɔ Israel-viwo atsɔ ayra amewo agblɔ bena: Mawu naɖi na wò abe Efrayim kple Manase ene! Ale wotsɔ Efrayim ɖo tsitsii na Manase.

eng: Then Israel said to Joseph, Im about to die. God will be with you and return you to the land of your fathers.
local: Emegbe Israel gblɔ na Yosef bena: Mele kuku ge kaba, ke Mawu anɔ anyi kpli mi, eye woakplɔ mi ayi mia togbɛwo ƒe anyigba la dzi.

eng: Im giving you one portion more than to your brothers, a portion that I took from the Amorites with my sword and my bow.
local: Ke metsɔ anyigbaa ɖe, si mexɔ le Amoritɔwo si kple nye yi kple da la na wò, si natsɔ awu nɔviwòwo.

eng: Jacob summoned his sons and said, Gather around so that I can tell you what will happen to you in the coming days.
local: Eye Yakob ya viawo gblɔ bena: Miƒo ƒu, ne magblɔ nusi le dzɔdzɔ ge ɖe mia dzi le ɣeyiɣi, siwo gbɔna me la afia mi!.

eng: Assemble yourselves and listen, sons of Jacob; listen to Israel your father.
local: Miƒo ƒu, eye mike to, nu Yakob viwo, miɖo to mia fofo Israel!.

eng: Reuben, you are my oldest son, my strength and my first contender, superior in status and superior in might.
local: Ruben! wòe nye nye ŋgɔgbevi, nye ŋusẽ kple nye ali me vi gbãtɔ, gbãtɔ le bubu me kple gãtɔ le ŋusẽ me.

eng: Joseph fell across his fathers body, wept over him, and kissed him.
local: Tete Yosef dze fofoa dzi, fa avi ɖe edzi, eye wòɖuɖɔ nu nɛ.

eng: As wild as the waters, you wont endure, for you went up to your fathers bed, you went up and violated my couch.
local: Ele fiefiẽm abe tsi ene, maganye gbãtɔ akpɔ o, elabena ede fofowò ƒe aba dzi, eye nedo ŋunyɔe gbemagbe, ẽ, nye aba dzie nede!.

eng: Joseph then ordered the physicians in his service to embalm his father, and the physicians embalmed Israel.
local: Emegbe Yosef de se na eƒe dɔla, siwo nye atikewɔlawo, bena woasi atike na fofoa. Tete atikewɔlawo si atike na Israel.

eng: Simeon and Levi are brothers, weapons of violence their stock in trade.
local: Simeon kple Lewi la, nɔviwoe, wotsɔ woƒe lãnuwo do biae.

eng: They mourned for him forty days because that is the period required for embalming. Then the Egyptians mourned him for seventy days.
local: Esi ŋkeke blane, si wosia atike na ame kuku, de vɔ la, Egiptetɔwo fa nɛ ŋkeke blaadre sɔŋ.

eng: May I myself never enter their council. May my honor never be linked to their group; for when they were angry, they killed men, and whenever they wished, they maimed oxen.
local: Nye luʋɔ made woƒe adaŋu me o, eye nye dzi mabɔ ha kple wo o, elabena de dziku me wowu ŋutsuwo eye le dzidzɔ teƒe wòŋe ata na nyitsuwo.

eng: After the period of mourning had passed, Joseph spoke to Pharaohs household: If you approve my request, give Pharaoh this message.
local: Ke esi konyifaɣiawo va yi vɔ la, Yosef gblɔ na Farao ŋuti mewo bena: Meɖe kuku, ne miave nunye la, ekema migblɔ na Farao bena:.

eng: Cursed be their anger; it is violent, their rage; it is relentless. Ill divide them up within Jacob and disperse them within Israel.
local: Aɖi woƒe nyanyrã ŋu, elabena enu sẽ, kple woƒe dziku ŋu, elabena eteƒe meka o! Mamã wo de Yakob viwo dome, eye makaka wo ɖe Israel.

eng: My father made me promise, telling me, Im about to die. You must bury me in the tomb I dug for myself in the land of Canaan. Now, let me leave and let me bury my father, and then I will return.
local: Fofonye na meka atam ne bena, ne yeku la, ekema maɖi ye ɖe yɔdo, si yeɖe ɖe Kanaan-nyigba dzi la me. Eyata meɖe kuku, mayi aɖaɖi fofonye, emegbe magatrɔ agbɔ.

eng: Judah, you are the one your brothers will honor; your hand will be on the neck of your enemies; your fathers sons will bow down to you.
local: Yuda! wòe nɔviwòwo akatu, wò asi axe wò funɔwo ƒe ve, eye fofowoviwo adze klo na wò.

eng: Pharaoh replied, Go, bury your father as you promised.
local: Farao ɖo ŋu nɛ bena; Yi naɖaɖi fofowò, abe alesi nèka atam nɛ ene!.

eng: Judah is a lions cub; from the prey, my son, you rise up. He lies down and crouches like a lion; like a lioness—who dares disturb him?.
local: Dzatavi enye Yuda. Le nuléle ta, vinye, anɔ to dzi. Emlɔ anyi tsyɔ akɔ abe dzatatsu ene, eye abe dzatanɔ ene; ameka ate ŋu aʋuʋui?.

eng: So Joseph left to bury his father. All of Pharaohs servants went with him, together with the elder statesmen in his household and all of the elder statesmen in the land of Egypt.
local: Tete Yosef dze mɔ, be wòaɖaɖi fofoa. Eye Farao ƒe subɔlawo katã kple eŋuti mewo kpakple Egipte-nyigba dzi megãwo katã yi kplii.

eng: The scepter wont depart from Judah, nor the rulers staff from among his banners. Gifts will be brought to him; people will obey him.
local: Atamkayi magbe Yuda si nanɔ o, eye womede fiatikplɔ da le ekɔme o, vaseɖe esime amesi tɔ wònye nava; amesi dukɔwo ana tae.

eng: Josephs entire household, his brothers, and his fathers household. Only the children, flocks, and cattle remained in the land of Goshen.
local: Eye Yosef ƒemetɔwo katã, nɔviawo, kple fofoa ƒemetɔwo katã yi kplii; negbe wo viwo, woƒe alẽwo kple nyiwo ko wogblẽ ɖe Gosen-nyigba la dzi.

eng: He ties his male donkey to the vine, the colt of his female donkey to the vines branches. He washes his clothes in wine, his garments in the blood of grapes.
local: Ede ka eƒe tedzivi de weinka ŋuti, kple eƒe tedzinɔvi ɖe weinka nyui ƒe abɔwo ŋui! Enyã eƒe avɔ le wein me, kple eƒe avu le weintsetse me tsi me.

eng: Even chariots and horsemen went with him; it was a huge collection of people.
local: Eye tasiaɖamwo kple sɔɖolawo kplɔe yii ale bena, wonye ameha gãa ɖe.

eng: His eyes are darker than wine, and his teeth whiter than milk.
local: Wein na eƒe ŋku bia, eye notsi na eƒe aƒu fu.

eng: When they arrived at the threshing floor of Atad on the other side of the Jordan River, they observed a solemn, deeply sorrowful period of mourning. He grieved seven days for his father.
local: Esi wova ɖo Gorenhatad, si le Yordan godo la, wofa konyi ŋutɔŋutɔ le afima, eye wowɔ yɔƒe na wo fofoa ŋkeke adre.

eng: Zebulun will live at the seashore; hell live at the harbor of ships, his border will be at Sidon.
local: Zebulon anɔ ƒutanyigbawo dzi le ʋudzeƒewo, eye eƒe liƒo ade Zidon ke.

eng: When the Canaanites who lived in the land saw the observance of grief on Atads threshing floor, they said, This is a solemn observance of grief by the Egyptians. Therefore, its name is Abel-mizraim. It is on the other side of the Jordan River.
local: Esi Kanaantɔ, siwo nye anyigba dzi nɔlawo, kpɔ yɔƒewɔwɔ la le Gorenhatad la, wogblɔ bena: Afisia Egiptetɔwo wɔ yɔƒe gãa ɖe le. Eyata woyɔa teƒe ma bena Abel-Mizraim, si le Yordan godo.

eng: Issachar is a sturdy donkey, bedding down beside the village hearths.
local: Isaxar enye tedzi, si ƒu tsi de lãme na, eye wotsyɔ akɔ de lãkpɔwo domee.

eng: Israels sons did for him just as he had ordered.
local: Eye viaŋutsuwo wo nɛ, abe alesi wòde se na wo ene.

eng: He saw that a resting place was good and that the land was pleasant. He lowered his shoulder to haul loads and joined the work gangs.
local: Esi wòkpɔ be, dzudzɔ vivina, eye eƒe anyigba dze ani la, ebi eƒe abɔ ta, be wòatsɔ agba, eye wòzu agbatetsɔla.

eng: These are the names of the Israelites who came to Egypt with Jacob along with their households.
local: Israel viŋutsu, siwo kpe ɖe Yakob ŋuti va Egipte la, ƒe ŋkɔwo nye si, amesiame va kple eƒemetɔwo:.

eng: His sons carried him to the land of Canaan and buried him in the cave in the field of Machpelah near Mamre, which Abraham had purchased as burial property from Ephron the Hittite.
local: Eye viawo tsoe yi Kanaan, eye woɖii ɖe kpeto, si le Maxpela gble me, le Mamre kasa la me; agble, si Abraham ƒle le Efron Xititɔa si, be wòanye ameɖiƒe nɛ.

eng: Dan will settle disputes for his people, as one of Israels tribes.
local: Dan aʋli eƒe dukɔ ta abe Israel to bubuwo ke ene.

eng: Then Joseph returned to Egypt, he, his brothers, and everyone who left with him to bury his father.
local: Esi wòɖi fofoa va la, Yosef kple nɔviawo kple amesiwo katã kpe ɖe eŋu woɖaɖi fofoa la, wogatrɔ va Egipte.

eng: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah.
local: Ruben, Simeon, Lewi kple Yuda.

eng: Dan will be a snake on the road, a serpent on the path, biting a horses heels, so its rider falls backward.
local: Dan enye da tsomɔ, dakpui le afɔmɔe dzi; eɖu sɔ ƒe afɔʋume, eye sɔdola dze anyi megbe megbee.

eng: When Josephs brothers realized that their father was now dead, they said, What if Joseph bears a grudge against us, and wants to pay us back seriously for all of the terrible things we did to him.
local: Le wo fofo ƒe ku megbe la Yosef nɔviwo le vɔvɔ̃m gblɔ bena: Ɖewohĩ Yosef alé fu mi, eye wòaɖo vɔ̃, siwo katã miewɔ ɖe eŋu la, teƒe na mí.

eng: Issachar, Zebulun, and Benjamin.
local: Isaxar, Zebulon kple Benyamin.

eng: I long for your victory, LORD.
local: Yehowa, wò ɖeɖe la ko lalam mele!.

eng: So they approached Joseph and said, Your father gave orders before he died, telling us.
local: Eye wodɔ ame ɖo ɖe Yosef gbɔ gblɔ bena: Hafi fofowò naku la, ede se gblɔ bena:.

eng: Dan and Naphtali, Gad and Asher.
local: Dan, Naftali, Gad kple Aser.

eng: Gad will be attacked by attackers, but hell attack their back.
local: Gad la, adzohawo ava dze edzi; ke eya ayɔ wo de nu.

eng: Now a man from Levis household married a Levite woman.
local: Ŋutsua ɖe tso Lewito la me ɖe Lewi-nyɔnua ɖe.

eng: The total number in Jacobs family was seventy. Joseph was already in Egypt.
local: Yakob ƒe dzidzimeviwo katã le ame blaadre: ke Yosef ya tre Egipte vava.

eng: Asher grows fine foods, and he will supply the kings delicacies.
local: Aser la, nu ado agbogbo de esi, eye woadzra nuɖuɖu veviwo na fiawo.

eng: This is what you should say to Joseph. Please, forgive your brothers sins and misdeeds, for they did terrible things to you. Now, please forgive the sins of the servants of your fathers God. Joseph wept when they spoke to him.
local: Ale miagblɔ na Yosef enye si: Meɖe kuku, tsɔ nɔviwòwo ƒe nuvɔ̃ kple woƒe vodada ke wo; elabena wowɔ vɔ ɖe ŋuwò. Eyata míeɖe kuku, tsɔ míaƒe nuvɔ̃wo ke mí, elabena miawo hã míesubɔa fofowò ƒe Mawu la. Tete Yosef fa avi le nya sia ŋuti.

eng: The woman became pregnant and gave birth to a son. She saw that the baby was healthy and beautiful, so she hid him for three months.
local: Eye nyɔnu la fɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi. Esi wòkpɔ bena, edze ɖeka la, eɣlae ɣleti etɔ̃.

eng: Eventually, Joseph, his brothers, and everyone in his generation died.
local: Emegbe Yosef kple nɔviawo kple wo viwo katã ku.

eng: Naphtali is a wild doe that gives birth to beautiful fawns.
local: Naftali enye zinɔ, si ʋlãna, eye wògblɔa nu me vivi nyawo.

eng: His brothers wept too, fell down in front of him, and said, Were here as your slaves.
local: Emegbe la nɔviawo ŋutɔ yi, eye wodze klo de ta agu nɛ gblɔ bena: Miawo nye si, tsɔ mi wɔ wò kluviwoe!.

eng: When she couldnt hide him any longer, she took a reed basket and sealed it up with black tar. She put the child in the basket and set the basket among the reeds at the riverbank.
local: Ke esi megate ŋu le eɣlam o la, etsɔ kusi, si wotso aɖa lɔ̃e, eye wòtree kple aŋɔ keŋ, emegbe la etsɔ ɖevi la mlɔ me, eye wòkɔe ɖada ɖe Nil-tɔsisi to le aƒlawo me.

eng: Moses was taking care of the flock for his father-in-law Jethro, Midians priest. He led his flock out to the edge of the desert, and he came to Gods mountain called Horeb.
local: Eye Mose kpɔ toa Midian-nunɔla Yetro ƒe alẽwo dzi. Gbe ɖeka esi wòkolɔ alẽawo yi gbedzi la, eva Mawu ƒe to Xoreb gbɔ.

eng: Then Moses replied, But what if they dont believe me or pay attention to me? They might say to me, The LORD didnt appear to you.
local: Mose do ŋu gblɔ bena: Ne womexɔ nye nya dzi se o, eye womeɖo to nye gbe o, ke boŋ wogblɔ bena: Yehowa mede eɖokui fia wò o ɖe?.

eng: But the Israelites were fertile and became populous. They multiplied and grew dramatically, filling the whole land.
local: Eye Israel-viwo dzi hele agbɔ sɔm ɖe dzi ŋutɔŋutɔ, wokeke ta, eye woxɔ anyigba la dzi.

eng: Joseph is a young bull, a young bull by a spring, who strides with oxen.
local: Ati fẽ enye Yosef, ali fe le vudo to, eye wòɖe ala ɖe gli dzi.

eng: But Joseph said to them, Dont be afraid. Am I God?.
local: Eye Yosef ɖo ŋu na wo bena: Migavɔ̃ o! Alo Mawu teƒe melea?.

eng: The babys older sister stood watch nearby to see what would happen to him.
local: Eye nɔvianyɔnu le adzɔge, bena yeakpɔ nusi ava dzo ɖe edzi.

eng: The LORDs messenger appeared to him in a flame of fire in the middle of a bush. Moses saw that the bush was in flames, but it didnt burn up.
local: Eye Yehowa ƒe dɔla ɖe eɖokui fiae le dzobibi me le ŋuvea ɖe me. Esi wòlé ŋku ɖe eŋu la, ekpɔ bena, ŋuve la le bibim, gake mele fiafiãm o.

eng: The LORD said to him, Whats that in your hand? Moses replied, A shepherds rod.
local: Yehowa gblɔ nɛ bena: Nukae ma le asiwò? Eɖo ŋu bena: Atizɔtie.

eng: Now a new king came to power in Egypt who didnt know Joseph.
local: Eye fia yeyea ɖe ɖu fia le! Egipte, amesi menya naneke le Yosef ŋuti o.

eng: They attacked him fiercely and fired arrows; the archers attacked him furiously.
local: Wole fui, eye wotee kple ɖa, eye aŋutrɔdalawo ɖee ɖe nu.

eng: You planned something bad for me, but God produced something good from it, in order to save the lives of many people, just as hes doing today.
local: Miawo miesusu vɔ̃ ɖe ŋunye, ke Mawu trae wòzu nyuie, be yeawɔe axɔ ame geɖe ɖe agbe, abe alesi miele ekpɔmi egbegbe ene.

eng: Pharaohs daughter came down to bathe in the river, while her women servants walked along beside the river. She saw the basket among the reeds, and she sent one of her servants to bring it to her.
local: Tete Farao vinyɔnu va tsi le ge le Nil me. Ke egbɔ nyɔnuviwo le tsa ɖim le tɔa to. Kasiaa wòkpɔ kusia ɖe le aƒlawo dome ɖa, eye wòdo eƒe dɔlanyɔnu ɖa, be wòaɖatsɔe vɛ.

eng: Then Moses said to himself, Let me check out this amazing sight and find out why the bush isnt burning up.
local: Tete Mose gblɔ bena: Mate ɖe eŋu, ne makpɔ nukpekpe gã sia ɖa, nusi ŋuti ŋuve la mele fiefiẽm o ɖo.

eng: The LORD said, Throw it down on the ground. So Moses threw it on the ground, and it turned into a snake. Moses jumped back from it.
local: Tete wògblɔ bena: Tsoe ƒu gbe ɖe anyigba! Esi wòtsɔe ƒu gbe ɖe anyigba la, etrɔ zu da, eye Mose si le enu.

eng: He said to his people, The Israelite people are now larger in number and stronger than we are.
local: Eye wògblɔ na eƒe dukɔ la bena: Mikpɔ ɖa, Israel-dukɔ la sɔ gbɔ, eye wosẽ ŋu wu mi.

eng: But his bow stayed strong, and his forearms were nimble, by the hands of the strong one of Jacob, by the name of the shepherd, the rock of Israel.
local: Ke eva eƒe dati goŋgoŋ, eye eƒe asiwò le tsatsam; elabena exɔ ŋusẽ tso Yakob ƒe ŋusẽtɔ kple Israel ƒe kplɔla kple kpe la gbɔ.

eng: Now, dont be afraid. I will take care of you and your children. So he put them at ease and spoke reassuringly to them.
local: Eyata migavɔ̃ o, manyi mia kple mia viwo siaa! Ale wòfa ako na wo, eye wòde dzi ƒo na wo.

eng: When she opened it, she saw the child. The boy was crying, and she felt sorry for him. She said, This must be one of the Hebrews children.
local: Esi wòʋui la, ekpa ŋutsuvia ɖe le avi fam le ame. Eye eƒe nu wo nublanui ne wògblɔ bena: Ɖewo Hebri-ŋutsuviwo dometɔa ɖee!.

eng: When the LORD saw that he was coming to look, God called to him out of the bush, Moses, Moses! Moses said, Im here.
local: Ke esi Yehowa kpɔ bena, ele tetem ta, be yeakpɔe ɖa la, Mawu yɔe tso ŋuve la me bena: Mose, Mose! Eɖo ŋu bena: Nyee nye si!.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Reach out and grab the snake by the tail. So Moses reached out and grabbed it, and it turned back into a rod in his hand.
local: Yehowa gblɔ na Mose bena: Do wò asi ɖa nalé eƒe asike! Eye wodo eƒe asi ɖa lee; tete wògatrɔ zu atizɔti de esi.

eng: Come on, lets be smart and deal with them. Otherwise, they will only grow in number. And if war breaks out, they will join our enemies, fight against us, and then escape from the land.
local: Enyo, mina miadze aye ɖe wo ŋuti, bena womagasɔ gbɔ ɖe edzi o, eye ne aʋa dzɔ la, womagade míaƒe futɔwo dzi awɔ aʋa kpli mí ahaʋu le anyigba la dzi o.

eng: by God, your father, who supports you, by the Almighty who blesses you with blessings from the skies above and blessings from the deep sea below, blessings from breasts and womb.
local: tso fofowò ƒe Mawu la gbɔ, eya akpe ɖe ŋuwò! tso ŋusẽkatãtɔ la gbɔ, eya ayra wò! Atsɔ dziƒo nu nyuiwo ayra wò, atsɔ tɔ gɔme nu nyuiwo ayra wò, eye atsɔ notsi kple vidzidɔ geɖe ayra wò!.

eng: Thus Joseph lived in Egypt, he and his fathers household. Joseph lived years.
local: Eye Yosef kple fofoa ƒemetɔwo tsi Egipte-nyigba dzi, eye Yosef xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka kple ewo.

eng: Afterward, Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and said, This is what the LORD, Israels God, says: Let my people go so that they can hold a festival for me in the desert.
local: Emegbe Mose kple Aron woyi dagblɔ na Farao bena: Ale Yehowa. Israel ƒe Mawu la, gblɔ esi. Ɖe asi le nye dukɔ la ŋuti, bena wòayi aɖaɖu ŋkekenyuie nam le gbedzi!.

eng: Then the babys sister said to Pharaohs daughter, Would you like me to go and find one of the Hebrew women to nurse the child for you.
local: Tete nɔvianyɔnu gblɔ na Farao ƒe vinyɔnua bena: Nyemayi aɖayɔ Hebri-nyɔnua ɖe vɛ, bena wòana no ɖevi la na wò oa?.

eng: Then the LORD said, Dont come any closer! Take off your sandals, because you are standing on holy ground.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Megale va afisia o, ɖe wò atokotawo le afɔ; elabena anyigba, si dzi nèle la, teƒe kɔkɔee!.

eng: Do this so that they will believe that the LORD, the God of their ancestors, Abrahams God, Isaacs God, and Jacobs God has in fact appeared to you.
local: Ekema woaxɔe ase bena, Yehowa, wo fofowo ƒe Mawu, Abraham ƒe Mawu, Isak ƒe Mawu kple Yakob fe Mawu lae de eɖokui fia wò.

eng: As a result, the Egyptians put foremen of forced work gangs over the Israelites to harass them with hard work. They had to build storage cities named Pithom and Rameses for Pharaoh.
local: Tete wotsɔ dzikpɔlawo ɖo wo nu, bena woate wo ɖe anyi, ne woawɔ dɔ sesẽwo; eye wotu nudzraɖoƒewo na Farao, siwo nye Pitom kple Ramses.

eng: The blessings of your father exceed the blessings of the eternal mountains, the wealth of the everlasting hills. May they all rest on Josephs head, on the forehead of the one set apart from his brothers.
local: Fofowò ƒe yayra, si gbɔ tonyigba xoxo wɔnuwo kple togbe dzeani, siwo li tegbee ŋu la, ava Yosef ƒe ta dzi kple nɔviawo ƒe amegã ƒe dzodome.

eng: and saw Ephraims grandchildren. The children of Machir, Manassehs son, were also born on Josephs knees.
local: Eye Yosef nɔ agbe tso Efrayim ƒe tɔgbuiyɔviwo ɖo, eye Maxir Manase vi hã dzi viwo le Yosef ƒe agbenɔɣi me.

eng: But Pharaoh said, Who is this LORD whom Im supposed to obey by letting Israel go? I dont know this LORD, and I certainly wont let Israel go.
local: Farao ɖo ŋu bena: Ameka nye Yehowa, ne maɖo to eƒe gbe aɖe asi le Israel-viwo ŋuti wòayi? Nyemenya Yehowa la o, eye nyemaɖe asi le Israel ŋuti wòayi hã o!.

eng: Pharaohs daughter agreed, Yes, do that. So the girl went and called the childs mother.
local: Farao vinyɔnu la ɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Heyi! Tete nyɔnuvi la yi ɖayɔ ɖevi la ɖada vɛ.

eng: He continued, I am the God of your father, Abrahams God, Isaacs God, and Jacobs God. Moses hid his face because he was afraid to look at God.
local: Eyome egblɔ be: Nyee nye fofowò ƒe Mawu, Abraham ƒe Mawu, Isak ƒe Mawu kple Yakob ƒe Mawu. Tete Mose tsɔ nu tsyɔ mo, elabena ele Mawu kpɔkpɔ yɔm.

eng: Again, the LORD said to Moses, Put your hand inside your coat. So Moses put his hand inside his coat. When he took his hand out, his hand had a skin disease flaky like snow.
local: Eye Yehowa gagblɔ nɛ bena: Tsa wò asi ƒo ɖe wò axatome! Eye wòtsɔ eƒe asi ƒo de eƒe axatome. Esi wògaɖee ɖa la, anyi ƒo ɖe eƒe asi ŋuti fu tii.

eng: But the more they were oppressed, the more they grew and spread, so much so that the Egyptians started to look at the Israelites with disgust and dread.
local: Gake esi wole wo tem ɖe anyi hã la, dzidzim ko wole ɖe dzi, eye wole ta kekem, ale be wòde asi Israel-viwo vɔvɔ̃ me.

eng: Benjamin is a wolf who hunts: in the morning he devours the prey; in the evening he divides the plunder.
local: Benyamin enye amegãxi lénu. Ŋdi la eɖua eƒe nuléle, eye fiẽ la emãa eƒe adzonu la.

eng: Joseph said to his brothers, Im about to die. God will certainly take care of you and bring you out of this land to the land he promised to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.
local: Eye Yosef gblɔ na nɔviawo bena: Nye la mele kuku ge; ke Mawu aɖo ŋku mia dzi, eye wòakplɔ mi atso anyigba sia dzi ayi ɖe anyigba, si ŋugbe wodo na Abraham, Isak kple Yakob la dzi.

eng: Then they said, The Hebrews God has appeared to us. Let us go on a three-day journey into the desert so we can offer sacrifices to the LORD our God. Otherwise, the LORD will give us a deadly disease or violence.
local: Wogblɔ bena: Hebritɔwo ƒe Mawu la ɖe eɖokui fia mí. Na míazɔ ŋkeke etɔ̃ ƒe azɔli ayi gbeɖzi, ne miasa vɔ na Yehowa, mía Mawu la: ne menye nenem o la, adze mia dzi kple dɔvɔ̃ alo yi.

eng: Pharaohs daughter said to her, Take this child and nurse it for me, and Ill pay you for your work. So the woman took the child and nursed it.
local: Eye Farao vinyɔnu la gblɔ nɛ bena: Xɔ ɖevi sia, eye nana noe nam, ekema mana adzɔ wò! Tete nyɔnu la xɔ ɖevi la, eye wòna noe.

eng: Then the LORD said, Ive clearly seen my people oppressed in Egypt. Ive heard their cry of injustice because of their slave masters. I know about their pain.
local: Eye Yehowa gblɔ bena: Mekpɔ alesi wole nye dukɔ tem ɖe anyi le Egipte keŋ, eye mese woƒe ɣli, si wodo, be yewoavo atso yewo dzikpɔlawo si me la; ẽ, menya woƒe fukpekpewo.

eng: Then God said, Put your hand back inside your coat. So Moses put his hand back inside his coat. When he took it back out again, the skin of his hand had returned to normal.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Gagbugbɔ wò asi ƒo de wò axatome! Eye esi wògagbugbɔ eƒe asi ƒo de axatome, eye wògaɖee da la, kpɔ ɖa, eƒe asi gazu abe eƒe ŋutilã susɔea ene.

eng: So the Egyptians enslaved the Israelites.
local: Eye Egipte-tɔwo zi Israel-viwo dzi nublanuitɔe, be woawɔ dɔ sesẽ.

eng: These are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is what their father said to them. He blessed them by giving each man his own particular blessing.
local: Esiawo nye Israel ƒe to wuieveawo; eye esiawo nye nya, siwo wo fofo gblɔ na wo. Eyra wo dometɔ ɖesiaɖe wo tɔ wo tɔe.

eng: The king of Egypt said to them, Moses and Aaron, why are you making the people slack off from their work? Do the hard work yourselves.
local: Tete Egipte-fia ɖo ŋu na wo bena: Mose kple Aron, nukaŋuti miele didim be, miade dukɔ la le woƒe dɔa nu? Miheyi miaƒe dɔ sesẽ la dzi!.

eng: Joseph made Israels sons promise, When God takes care of you, you must bring up my bones out of here.
local: Tete Yosef na Israel ƒe viwo ka atam, eye wògblɔ bena: Ne Mawu aɖo ŋku mia dzi la, ekema mitso nye ƒuwo hã tso afisia yii!.

eng: After the child had grown up, she brought him back to Pharaohs daughter, who adopted him as her son. She named him Moses, because, she said, I pulled him out of the water.
local: Esi ŋutsuvia tsi la, ekplɔe ve na Farao vinyɔnu la, eye wòxɔe wɔ viae hetsɔ ŋkɔ nɛ bena Mose: elabena egblɔ be: To mee meɖee tsoe.

eng: Ive come down to rescue them from the Egyptians in order to take them out of that land and bring them to a good and broad land, a land thats full of milk and honey, a place where the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites all live.
local: Eye meɖi va, be maɖe wo le Egiptetɔwo si me, eye makplɔ wo atso anyigba sia dzi ayi ɖe, anyigba, si nyo, eye wòlólo la dzi, anyigba, si dzi notsi kple anyitsi bɔ do, afisi Kanaanitɔwo. Xititɔwo, Amoritɔwo, Perizitɔwo, Xiwitɔwo kple Yebusitɔwo le.

eng: If they wont believe you or pay attention to the first sign, they may believe the second sign.
local: Ne womexɔ wò nya dzi se o, eye womebu dzesi gbãtɔ o la, ekema woaxɔ dzesi evelia dzi ase.

eng: They made their lives miserable with hard labor, making mortar and bricks, doing field work, and by forcing them to do all kinds of other cruel work.
local: Eye dɔ sesẽ wowɔ na agbenɔnɔ ti wo; wonyã anyi, me anyikpe, eye wowɔ agbledɔ ƒomevi vovo vo, siwo wozi wo dzi nublanuitɔe de asi na wo la.

eng: Jacob ordered them, I am soon to join my people. Bury me with my ancestors in the cave thats in the field of Ephron the Hittite.
local: Eye wòde se na wo gblɔ na wo bena: Ne meyi ɖe tɔgbuinyewo gbɔ la, ekema miaɖim ɖe fofonyewo gbɔ, ɖe kpeto, si le Efron Xititɔ ƒe agble me la me.

eng: Joseph died when he was years old. They embalmed him and placed him in a coffin in Egypt.
local: Esi Yosef xa ƒe alafa ɖeka kple ewo la, eku, eye wòsi atike nɛ hetsɔe de aɖaka me le Egipte.

eng: Pharaoh continued, The lands people are now numerous. Yet you want them to stop their hard work.
local: Eye Farao gagblɔ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, dukɔ la so gbɔ le anyigba la dzi tso, eye miedi be, wòadzudzɔ woƒe dɔ la mahã?.

eng: One day after Moses had become an adult, he went out among his people and he saw their forced labor. He saw an Egyptian beating a Hebrew, one of his own people.
local: Esi Mose va tsi la, gbe ɖeka eyi nɔviawo gbɔ, eye wòkpɔ, alesi wole woƒe dɔ sesẽ la wɔmi. Tete wòkpɔ Egiptetɔa ɖe le nɔvia Hebritɔa ɖe ƒom.

eng: Now the Israelites cries of injustice have reached me. Ive seen just how much the Egyptians have oppressed them.
local: Eye esi Israel-viwo ƒe ɣli ɖi mese, eye mekpɔ, alesi Egiptetɔwo le fu wɔm wo la.

eng: If they wont believe even these two signs or pay attention to you, then take some water from the Nile River and pour it out on dry ground. The water that you take from the Nile will turn into blood on the dry ground.
local: Ke ne womexɔ dzesi eve siawo hã dzi se o, eye womese wo gbe o la, ekema naku tsi le Nil-tɔsisi me akɔ de anyigba, eye tsi, si nèku le Nil-tɔsisia me la, atrɔ zu ʋu ɖe anyigba.

eng: The king of Egypt spoke to two Hebrew midwives named Shiphrah and Puah.
local: Eye Egipte-fia de se na Hebritɔwo ƒe vixela, siwo dometɔ ɖeka ŋkɔ nye Sifra, eye evelia ŋkɔ nye Pua la bena;.

eng: in the cave thats in the field of Machpelah near Mamre in the land of Canaan that Abraham bought from Ephron the Hittite as a burial property.
local: ɖe kpeto, si le Maxpela gble me le Mamre kasa le Kanaan-nyigba dzi, agble, si Abraham ƒle le Efron Xititɔ si, be wòanye ameɖiƒe nɛ la me.

eng: On the very same day Pharaoh commanded the peoples slave masters and supervisors.
local: Gbemagbe Farao de se na dukɔ la ƒe dɔdzikpɔlawo kple eƒe dɔnunɔlawo bena:.

eng: He looked around to make sure no one else was there. Then he killed the Egyptian and hid him in the sand.
local: Eye esi wotsa ŋu mlamlamla mekpɔ amea ɖeke o la, eƒo Egiptetɔ la wu, eye wòlɔ ke ɖe edzi.

eng: So get going. Im sending you to Pharaoh to bring my people, the Israelites, out of Egypt.
local: eyata mele dɔwòm ɖe Farao gbɔ, be nakplɔ nye dukɔ Israel-viwo adzoe tso Egipte.

eng: But Moses said to the LORD, My Lord, Ive never been able to speak well, not yesterday, not the day before, and certainly not now since youve been talking to your servant. I have a slow mouth and a thick tongue.
local: Tete Mose gblɔ na Yehowa bena: Taflatse, Aƒetɔ, nyemenya nuƒoƒo o, eye esia metso etsɔ kple nyitsɔ alo tso esime nède asi nuƒoƒo me kple wò dɔla o, elabena nye aɖe metrɔna ɖe nya ŋuti o.

eng: When you are helping the Hebrew women give birth and you see the baby being born, if its a boy, kill him. But if its a girl, you can let her live.
local: Ne miele vi xem na Hebri-nyɔnua ɖe, eye miekpɔ le viadziƒe bena, ŋutsuvie la, ekema miawui, ke nenye nyɔnuvi la, ekema mina wòanɔ agbe!.

eng: That is where Abraham and his wife Sarah are buried, and where Isaac and his wife Rebekah are buried, and where I buried Leah.
local: Afima woɖi Abraham kple srɔ̃a Sara ɖo, afima wodi Isak kple srɔ̃a Rebeka ɖo, eye afima meɖi Lea hã ɖo.

eng: Dont supply the people with the straw they need to make bricks like you did before. Let them go out and gather the straw for themselves.
local: Migana gbe dukɔ la, bena woade anyi me, amè anyikpewoe, abe alesi miewɔe vaseɖe fifi ene o; ke boŋ woawo ŋuta nayi aɖadi gbe na wo ɖokui!.

eng: When Moses went out the next day, he saw two Hebrew men fighting with each other. Moses said to the one who had started the fight, Why are you abusing your fellow Hebrew.
local: Esi ŋu ke la, egayi, eye wòkpɔ Hebritɔ ɖeɖe eve le avu wɔm. Tete wògblɔ na amesi le ge dim la bena: Nukaŋuti nele nɔviwò ƒom?.

eng: But Moses said to God, Who am I to go to Pharaoh and to bring the Israelites out of Egypt.
local: Ke Mose gblɔ na Mawu bena: Ameka menye, be mayi Farao gbɔ, eye makplɔ Israel-viwo adzoe atso Egipte?.

eng: Then the LORD said to him, Who gives people the ability to speak? Whos responsible for making them unable to speak or hard of hearing, sighted or blind? Isnt it I, the LORD?.
local: Yehowa ɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Ameka wo nu na ame? Ameka wo aɖetututɔ alo tokunɔ alo nukpɔla alo ŋkuagbãtɔ? Alo menye nye Yehowa oa?.

eng: It is the field and the cave in it that belonged to the Hittites.
local: ɖe kpeto, si le agble la me, si wòƒle le Xitiviwo si la me.

eng: Now the two midwives respected God so they didnt obey the Egyptian kings order. Instead, they let the baby boys live.
local: Ke vixelawo vɔ̃ Mawu, eye womewɔ se, si Egipte-fia de na wo la dzi o, boŋ wona ŋutsuviawo nɔ agbe.

eng: But still make sure that they produce the same number of bricks as they made before. Dont reduce the number! They are weak and lazy, and thats why they cry, Lets go and offer sacrifices to our God.
local: Gake kpe alesi nu womèna vaseɖe fifi la, mina woamèe, eye migaɖe ɖeke le edzi na wo o! Elabena kuvia wɔm wole; eyata wole ɣli dom le gbɔgblɔm bena: Míayi aɖasa vɔ na miaƒe Mawu.

eng: He replied, Who made you a boss or judge over us? Are you planning to kill me like you killed the Egyptian? Then Moses was afraid when he realized: They obviously know what I did.
local: Ke eya ɖo ŋu bena: Amekae ɖo wò amegã kple ʋɔnudrɔ̃la ɖe mía nu? Ɖe nèbe, yeawu nye hã, abe alesi nèwu Egiptetɔ la enea? Tete Mose ɖe asi vɔvɔ̃ me, elabena ekpɔ bena, nya la ɖi wose.

eng: God said, Ill be with you. And this will show you that Im the one who sent you. After you bring the people out of Egypt, you will come back here and worship God on this mountain.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Manɔ anyi kpli wò! Eye esia anye dzesi na wo bena, nyee dɔ wò: Ne èkplɔ dukɔ la dzoe tso Egipte la, ekema miasubɔ Mawu le to sia dzi.

eng: Now go! Ill help you speak, and Ill teach you what you should say.
local: Heyi ko, makpe ɖe ŋuwò naƒo nu, eye mafia nusi nagblɔ la wò.

eng: After he finished giving orders to his sons, he put his feet up on the bed, took his last breath, and joined his people.
local: Esi Yakob wu nyawo nu na viawo vɔ la, eƒo ata ƒu ɖe aba dzi, eye wòmia nu, eye wòyi ɖe tɔgbuiawo gbɔ.

eng: So the king of Egypt called the two midwives and said to them, Why are you doing this? Why are you letting the baby boys live.
local: Tete Egipte-fia ya vixelawo vɛ, eye wòbia wo bena: Nukaŋuti miewɔ alea, eye miena ŋutsuviawo tsi agbe?.

eng: Make the mens work so hard that its all they can do, and they cant focus on these empty lies.
local: Dɔ la nu nasẽ na wo, ale be dɔ geɖe nanɔ wo si, ne womagaɖo to ameblenyawo o!.

eng: When Pharaoh heard about it, he tried to kill Moses. But Moses ran away from Pharaoh and settled down in the land of Midian. One day Moses was sitting by a well.
local: Farao hã se nya la, eye wòle didim be, yeawu Mose. Ke Mose si le Farao nu heyi ɖe Midian-tɔwo ƒe anyigba dzi, eye wòva bɔbɔ no vudoa ɖe to.

eng: But Moses said to God, If I now come to the Israelites and say to them, The God of your ancestors has sent me to you, they are going to ask me, Whats this Gods name? What am I supposed to say to them.
local: Eye Mose gblɔ na Mawu bena: Ne meva Israel-viwo gbɔ, eve megblɔ na wo be: mia fofowo ƒe Mawu dɔm do de mia gbɔ, eve woabiam bena eŋkɔ ɖee hã la, aleke maɖo ŋu na wòe?.

eng: But Moses said, Please, my Lord, just send someone else.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Taflatse, Aƒetɔ, ɖi amea ɖe do boŋ.

eng: The two midwives said to Pharaoh, Because Hebrew women arent like Egyptian women. Theyre much stronger and give birth before any midwives can get to them.
local: Vixelawo ɖo ŋu na Farao bena: Hebri-nyɔnuwo mele abe Egipte-nyɔnuwo ene o, ke boŋ wosesẽ; kasiaa vixela nava wo gbɔ la, woawɔ ŋutɔ dzia vi la xoxo.

eng: So the peoples slave masters and supervisors came out and spoke to the people, This is what Pharaoh says, Im not giving you straw anymore.
local: Tete nuka la ƒe dɔdzikpɔlawo kple dɔnunɔlawo yi, eve wogblɔ na dukɔ, la bena: Ale Farao ɖo enye si. Nyemegale gbe na ge mi azɔ o.

eng: God said to Moses, I Am Who I Am. So say to the Israelites, I Am has sent me to you.
local: Mawu gblɔ na Mose be: „Amesi menye, eyae menye,“ eye wògblɔ bena: Ale nagblɔ na Israel-viwo enye si: „Menye“ ye dɔm do ɖe mia gbɔ;.

eng: Now there was a Midianite priest who had seven daughters. The daughters came to draw water and fill the troughs so that their fathers flock could drink.
local: Eye vinyɔnu adre le Midian-nunɔla si, amesiwo va tsi du ge ɖe lãwo ƒe towo me, bena woana tsi wo fofo ƒe alẽwo.

eng: Then the LORD got angry at Moses and said, What about your brother Aaron the Levite? I know he can speak very well. Hes on his way out to meet you now, and hes looking forward to seeing you.
local: Tete Yehowa, do dɔmedzoe ɖe Mose ŋu gblɔ bena: Alo nyemenyae bena, nɔ viwò Aron, Lewitɔ la, nya nuƒoƒo nyuie oa? Kpɔ ɖa, egbɔna kpe; wò ge, eye ne ekpɔ wò la, dzi adzoe ŋutɔ.

eng: So God treated the midwives well, and the people kept on multiplying and became very strong.
local: Ke Mawu wo nyuie na vixelawo. Eye dukɔ la dzi ɖe dzi, eye wosẽ ŋu.

eng: Go and get the straw on your own, wherever you can find it. But your work wont be reduced at all.
local: Mia ŋutɔwo miyi, ne miadi gbe na mia ɖokui, afisi míakpɔe ale, ke womaɖe miaƒe dɔ la dzi na mi o.

eng: God continued, Say to the Israelites, The LORD, the God of your ancestors, Abrahams God, Isaacs God, and Jacobs God, has sent me to you. This is my name forever; this is how all generations will remember me.
local: esia nye nye ŋkɔ tegbee, eye nye ŋkɔ sia dzidzimewo katã woayɔ.

eng: But some shepherds came along and rudely chased them away. Moses got up, rescued the women, and gave their flock water to drink.
local: Eye alẽkplɔlawo va tutu wo ɖe megbe. Ke Mose tsi tre xɔ na wo, eye wòna woƒe alẽwo no tsi.

eng: Speak to him and tell him what hes supposed to say. Ill help both of you speak, and Ill teach both of you what to do.
local: Ekema naƒo nu kplii, eye natsɔ nyawo ade enu, eye nye la makpe ɖe mia ŋu miaƒo nu, eye mafia nusi miawɔ la mi.

eng: And because the midwives respected God, God gave them households of their own.
local: Esi vixelawo vɔ̃ Mawu ŋuti la, eyata wòna vi geɖe wo.

eng: So the people spread out all through the land of Egypt to gather stubble for straw.
local: Tete dukɔ la kaka ɖe Egipte-nyigba blibo la katã dzi, bena woadi gbe.

eng: The LORD replied to Moses, Now you will see what Ill do to Pharaoh. In fact, hell be so eager to let them go that hell drive them out of his land by force.
local: Yehowa gblɔ na Mose bena: Azɔ akpɔ nusi mele Farao wɔ ge. Alɔ sesẽ ana wòaɖe asi le wo ŋu, eye alɔ sesẽ ana wòanya wo atso eƒe anyigba dzi.

eng: Go and get Israels elders together and say to them, The LORD, the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob, has appeared to me. The LORD said, Ive been paying close attention to you and to what has been done to you in Egypt.
local: Heyi, ne naƒo Israel-viwo ƒe ame tsitsiwo nu fu, eye nagblɔ na wo bena: Yehowa, mia fofowo ƒe Mawu, Abraham ƒe Mawu, Isak ƒe Mawu kple Yakob ƒe Mawu la, de eɖokui liam gblɔ bena: Medo ŋku mia dzi, eye mekpɔ nusi dzɔ de mia dzi le Egipte.

eng: When they went back home to their father Reuel, he asked, How were you able to come back home so soon today.
local: Esi wogbɔ va wo fofo Reguel gbɔ la, ebia wo bena: Nukaŋuti miegbã egbe kaba ale?.

eng: Then Pharaoh gave an order to all his people: Throw every baby boy born to the Hebrews into the Nile River, but you can let all the girls live.
local: Emegbe Farao de se na eƒe dukɔ la katã bena: Ne Hebritɔwo dzi ŋutsuvi la, ekema mitsɔe ƒu gbe ɖe Nil-tɔsisi la me, ke mina nyɔnuviwo katã nanɔ agbe.

eng: The slave masters drove them hard and said, Make sure you make the same daily quota as when you had the straw.
local: Eye dɔdzikpɔlawo ƒo du de wo nu gblɔ bena: Ele na mi be, mia wu miaƒe goesiagbe dɔ nu blibo, abe alesi miewɔna tsã, esime gbe no mia si la ene.

eng: God also said to Moses: I am the LORD.
local: Eye Mawu ƒo nu kple Mose gblɔ nɛ bena: Nyee nye Yehowa!.

eng: Aaron will speak for you to the people. Hell be a spokesperson for you, and you will be like God for him.
local: Eya naƒo nu kple dukɔ la ɖe tewòƒe, eye wòanye wò nu; ke wò la nanɔ Mawu teƒe nɛ.

eng: Ive decided to take you away from the harassment in Egypt to the land of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, a land full of milk and honey.
local: Eyata medo bena, made mi atso Egiptetɔwo ƒe teteɖanyi la me akplɔ mi ayi Kanaanitɔwo, Xititɔwo, Amoritɔwo, Perizitɔwo, Xiwitɔwo kple Yebusitɔwo ƒe anyigba dzi, afisi notsi kple anyitsi bɔ ɖo.

eng: They replied, An Egyptian man rescued us from a bunch of shepherds. Afterward, he even helped us draw water to let the flock drink.
local: Woɖo ŋu bena: Egipte-ŋutsua ɖe xɔ mí le alẽkplɔlawo si, eye wòdu tsi gɔ̃ hã na miaƒe alẽwo wono.

eng: The Israelite supervisors, whom Pharaohs slave masters had set over them, were also beaten and asked, Why didnt you produce the same number of bricks yesterday and today as you did before.
local: Eye woƒo Israel-vi, siwo Farao ƒe dɔdzikpɔlawo tsa do dɔnunɔlawoe la, gblɔ na wo bena: Nukaŋuti mieme miaƒe anyikpe, siwo woɖo na mi la, wu nu blibo etsɔ kple egbe, abe tsã ene o mana?.

eng: I appeared to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob as God Almighty, but I didnt reveal myself to them by my name The LORD.
local: Nyee ɖe ɖokuinye fia Abraham, Isak kple Yakob abe Mawu ŋusẽkatãtɔ ene, ke nyemeɖe nye ŋkɔ Yehowa fia wo o.

eng: Take this shepherds rod with you too so that you can do the signs.
local: Eye natsɔ atizɔti sia ɖe asi, eya natsɔ awɔ dzesiawoe!.

eng: They will accept what you say to them. Then you and Israels elders will go to Egypts king and say to him, The LORD, the Hebrews God, has met with us. So now let us go on a three-day journey into the desert so that we can offer sacrifices to the LORD our God.
local: Eye ne woɖo to wò gbe la, ekema mia kple Israel-viwo ƒe ame tsitsiwo miayi Egipte-fia gbɔ, eye miagblɔ nɛ bena: Yehowa, Hebritɔwo ƒe Mawu la, ɖe eɖokui fia mí; eyata na miazɔ ŋkeke etɔ̃ ƒe mɔ ayi gbedzi, aɖamè nu na Yehowa mia Mawu la.

eng: Reuel said to his daughters, So where is he? Why did you leave this man? Invite him to eat a meal with us.
local: Egblɔ na vianyɔnuawo bena: Afika wòle? Nukaŋuti miegblẽ ŋutsu la ɖe afima? Miyi ɖayɔe vɛ, ne woaɖu nu!.

eng: Then the Israelite supervisors came and pleaded to Pharaoh, Why do you treat your servants like this?.
local: Tete Israel-viwo ƒe dɔnunɔlawo yi ɖafa konyi na Farao gblɔ bena: Nukaŋuti nele wò dɔlawo wɔm alea?.

eng: I also set up my covenant with them to give them the land of Canaan where they lived as immigrants.
local: Mebia nu kpli wo be, matsɔ Kanaan-nyigba, si dzi wonye amedzrowo le la, ana wo wòanye wo tɔ.

eng: Moses went back to his father-in-law Jethro and said to him, Please let me go back to my family in Egypt and see whether or not they are still living. Jethro said to Moses, Go in peace.
local: Eye Mose trɔ gbã va toa Yetro gbɔ, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Mele didim be, magbugbɔ aɖakpɔ nɔvinyewo ɖa le Egipte be, wogale agbe mahã? Yetro ɖo ŋu; na Mose bena. Heyi faa!.

eng: However, I know that Egypts king wont let you go unless hes forced to do it.
local: Gake menyae bena, Egipte-fia maɖe mɔ na mi miadzo o, negbe ɖeko woazi edzi hafi.

eng: Moses agreed to come and live with the man, who gave his daughter Zipporah to Moses as his wife.
local: Eye Mose lɔ̃ bena, yeatsi ŋutsu la gbɔ, eye wotsɔ vianyɔnu Zipora na Mose wòɖe.

eng: No straw is supplied to your servants, yet they say to us, Make bricks! Look at how your servants are being beaten! Your own people are to blame.
local: Womegale gbe nam wò dɔlawo azɔ o, gake wole gbɔgblɔm na mí bena: mimè anyikpe! Azɔ wole mia ƒom gɔ̃ hã, eye wobu wò dukɔ la vodalawoe.

eng: Ive also heard the cry of grief of the Israelites, whom the Egyptians have turned into slaves, and Ive remembered my covenant.
local: Eye azɔ mese Israel-viwo ƒe konyi, si wòta le Egiptetɔwo ƒe wo teteɖanyi la ŋuti, eye meɖo ŋku nye nubabla la dzi.

eng: The LORD said to Moses in Midian, Go back to Egypt because everyone there who wanted to kill you has died.
local: Eye Yehowa gblɔ na Mose le Midian bena: Tso, gbugbɔ yi ɖe Egipte, elabena amesiwo katã le didim be, yewoatsrɔ̃ wò agbe la, woku.

eng: So Ill use my strength and hit Egypt with dramatic displays of my power. After that, hell let you go.
local: Mado nye asi da, eye matsɔ nye nukunuwo katã, siwo mawɔ le wo dome la, aɖee le mo na Egiptetɔwo: emegbe hafi woana miayi.

eng: She gave birth to a son, and Moses named him Gershom, because, he said, Ive been an immigrant living in a foreign land.
local: Eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi ne, eye wòyɔ eƒe ŋkɔ bena Gersom; elabena egblɔ bena: Amedzro menye le dzronyigba dzi.

eng: Pharaoh replied, You are lazy bums, nothing but lazy bums. Thats why you say, Let us go and offer sacrifices to the LORD.
local: Ke eya ɖo ŋu gblɔ bena: Kuviatɔwo, kuviatɔwo míenye! Eyata miele gbɔgblɔm bena: Míayi aɖasa vo na Yehowa.

eng: Therefore, say to the Israelites, I am the LORD. Ill bring you out from Egyptian forced labor. Ill rescue you from your slavery to them. Ill set you free with great power and with momentous events of justice.
local: Eyata gblɔ na Israel-viwo bena: Nyee nye Yehowa! Makplɔ mí le dɔ sesẽ, si wɔm miele na Egiptetɔwo la nu, eye mana miavo atso miaƒe kluvidɔ la me, eye mado nye alɔ ɖa atsɔ ŋusẽ gã aɖe mi.

eng: So Moses took his wife and his children, put them on a donkey, and went back to the land of Egypt. Moses also carried the shepherds rod from God in his hand.
local: Tete Mose tsɔ srɔ̃a kple viawo ɖo tedzi dzi, eye wòdze mɔ heyi Egipte-nyigba dzi; eye Mose tso Mawu ƒe atikplɔ la ɖe asi.

eng: Ill make it so that when you leave Egypt, the Egyptians will be kind to you and you wont go away empty-handed.
local: Eye mana Egipte-tɔwo nave dukɔ sia nu, bena ne miele dzodzo ge la, miayi asi gbɔloe o;.

eng: A long time passed, and the Egyptian king died. The Israelites were still groaning because of their hard work. They cried out, and their cry to be rescued from the hard work rose up to God.
local: Esi eteƒe va didi la, Egipte-fia ku Eye Israel-viwo fa konyi le woƒe dɔ sesẽ la ŋu, eye wodo ɣli. Eye woƒe ɣli, si wodo, be woavo tso woƒe dɔ sesẽa me la, de Mawu gbɔ.

eng: Go and get back to work! No straw will be given to you, but you still need to make the same number of bricks.
local: Miheyi miaƒe dɔ dzi; womana gbe mi o, gake ele na mi be, miame anyikpe, abe alesi wodo na mi ene.

eng: Ill take you as my people, and Ill be your God. You will know that I, the LORD, am your God, who has freed you from Egyptian forced labor.
local: Mawɔ mi mianye nye dukɔ, eye manye miaƒe Mawu, eye miadze sii be, nyee nye Yehowa, miaƒe Mawu, amesi kplɔ mi le dɔ sesẽ la nu tso Egipte.

eng: The LORD said to Moses, When you go back to Egypt, make sure that you appear before Pharaoh and do all the amazing acts that Ive given you the power to do. But Ill make him stubborn so that he wont let the people go.
local: Eye Yehowa gblɔ na Mose bena: Ne ede Egipte la, ekema naɖo ŋku nukunu, siwo katã mede asi na wò la dzi awɔ le Farao ƒe ŋku me; ke nye la mana eƒe dzime nasẽ, bena maɖe asi le dukɔ la ŋu wòayi o.

eng: Every woman will ask her neighbor along with the immigrant in her household for their silver and their gold jewelry as well as their clothing. Then you will put it on your sons and daughters, and you will rob the Egyptians.
local: ke nyɔnu sia nyɔnu nabia klosalo kple sikanuwo kpakple avɔwo le eƒe aƒelika kple eƒe me medzrowo si, eye miatsɔ wo aɖo atsyɔ̃ na mia viŋutsuwo kple mia vinyɔnuwo. Ale miaha Egiptetɔwo enye si!.

eng: God heard their cry of grief, and God remembered his covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
local: Eye Mawu se woƒe konyifafa, eye wòɖo ŋku nu, si wòbia kple Abraham, Isak kple Yakob la dzi.

eng: The Israelite supervisors saw how impossible their situation was when they were commanded, Dont reduce your daily quota of bricks.
local: Eye wosesẽ na Israel-viwo ƒe dɔnunɔlawo be, woaɖagblɔ be, womeɖe woƒe gbesiagbe kpemème la dzi na wo o.

eng: Ill bring you into the land that I promised to give to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Ill give it to you as your possession. I am the LORD.
local: Eye makplɔ mi ayi anyigba, si ŋu meka atam ɖo be, matsɔ na Abraham, Isak kple Yakob la dzi. Nye Yehowa matsɔe na mi wòanye mia tɔ.

eng: Then say to Pharaoh, This is what the LORD says: Israel is my oldest son.
local: Gblɔ na Farao bena: Ale Yehowa gblɔ esi: Israel la nye ŋgɔgbevie!.

eng: God looked at the Israelites, and God understood.
local: Eye Mawu kpɔ Israel-viwo ƒe nublanui, eye wòve wo nu.

eng: When they left Pharaoh, they met Moses and Aaron, who were waiting for them.
local: Esi wodzo le Farao gbɔ la, wokpe Mose kple Aron, amesiwo tsi tre ɖi, be woaɖo go wo.

eng: Moses told this to the Israelites. But they didnt listen to Moses, because of their complete exhaustion and their hard labor.
local: Eye Mose va gblɔ nyawo na Israel-viwo, ke woawo meɖo to Mose o, elabena womegale dzi ɖom ɖe amea ɖeke ŋu o, le dɔ sesẽ la ta.

eng: I said to you, Let my son go so he could worship me. But you refused to let him go. As a result, now Im going to kill your oldest son.
local: Eyata mele gbɔgblɔm na wò bena: Ɖe asi le vinye ŋuti, ne wòava subɔm, ke ne ègbe asiɖeɖe le eŋu la, ekema mawu wò ŋgɔgbevi!.

eng: The supervisors said to them, Let the LORD see and judge what youve done! Youve made us stink in the opinion of Pharaoh and his servants. Youve given them a reason to kill us.
local: Eye wogblɔ na wo bena: Yehowa adrɔ̃ ʋɔnu mi, eye wòakpɔ me na mi. Elabena miena miede asi ʋeʋẽ me ɖe Farao kple eŋuti mewo, gɔ̃ la miede yi asi na wo, be woatso awu mí.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses.
local: Emegbe Yehowa gblɔ na Mose bena:.

eng: During their journey, as they camped overnight, the LORD met Moses and tried to kill him.
local: Le ma dzi, esi wova ɖo dzeƒe la, Yehowa va, eye wòdi be, yeawui.

eng: Then Moses turned to the LORD and said, My Lord, why have you abused this people? Why did you send me for this?.
local: Tete Mose trɔ ɖe Yehowa; ŋu gblɔ bena: Aƒetɔ! Nukaŋuti nèhe vɔ̃ sia va dukɔ la dzi? Nukata gɔ̃ nedɔm ɖo?.

eng: Go and tell Pharaoh, Egypts king, to let the Israelites out of his land.
local: Yi ɖaƒo nu kple Farao, Egipte-fia bena: Ɖe asi le Israel-viwo ŋuti woadzo le wo anyigba dzi.

eng: But Zipporah took a sharp-edged flint stone and cut off her sons foreskin. Then she touched Moses genitals with it, and she said, You are my bridegroom because of bloodshed.
local: Tete Zipora tsɔ kpe ɖaɖɛa ɖe lã viaŋutsu ƒe aʋanuyi ɖa, eye wòtsɔe da ɖe eƒe afɔ nu gblɔ bena: Ʋuŋugbetɔsrɔ̃ nènye nam.

eng: Ever since I first came to Pharaoh to speak in your name, he has abused this people. And youve done absolutely nothing to rescue your people.
local: Elabena tso esime meva Farao gbɔ, eye meƒo nu le wo ŋkɔa me la, azɔ gɔ̃ wòle ŋuta sẽm le dukɔ la ŋu; ke wò la meɖe dukɔ la kpɔ o.

eng: But Moses said to the LORD, The Israelites havent even listened to me. How can I expect Pharaoh to listen to me, especially since Im not a very good speaker.
local: Eye Mose ɖo ŋu na Yehowa bena: Esi Israel-viwo mele to ɖomem o la, aleke Farao aɖo to nye amesi nu menya ƒona na o la!.

eng: So the LORD let him alone. At that time, she announced, A bridegroom because of bloodshed by circumcision.
local: Tete wòɖe asi le eŋu. Gbemagbe wògblɔ bena ʋuŋugbetɔsrɔ̃ le aʋatsotso la ta.

eng: The LORD said to Aaron, Go into the desert to meet Moses. So he went, and Aaron met him at Gods mountain and greeted him with a kiss.
local: Eye Yehowa gblɔ na Aron bena: Yi ɖakpe Mose le gbea dzi! Tete wòyi ɖakpee le Mawu ƒe to la gbɔ, eye wògbugbɔ nu nɛ.

eng: Moses told Aaron what the LORD had said about his mission and all the signs that the LORD had told him to do.
local: Eye Mose gblɔ Yehowa ƒe nya, siwo katã ta wòdɔe ɖo kple dzesi, siwo katã wòde esi la nɛ.

eng: Then Moses and Aaron called together all the Israelite elders.
local: Esia megbe Mose kple Aron woyi, eye woyɔ Israel-viwo ƒe ame tsitsiwo katã ƒo ƒu.

eng: Aaron told them everything that the LORD had told to Moses, and he performed the signs in front of the people.
local: Eye Aron gblɔ nya, siwo katã Yehowa de asi na Mose la; ke eya wo dzesiawo le dukɔ la ƒe ŋku me.

eng: The people believed. When they heard that the LORD had paid attention to the Israelites and had seen their oppression, they bowed down and worshipped.
local: Esi dukɔ la se bena: Yehowa ɖo ŋku Israel-viwo dzi, eye wòkpɔ woƒe teteɖanyi la, woxɔe se, eye wode ta agu hedo gbe ɖa.

eng: Nevertheless, the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron about the Israelites and Pharaoh, Egypts king, giving them orders to let the Israelites go from the land of Egypt.
local: Tete Yehowa ƒo nu kple Mose kple Aron, eye wòdo wo de Israel-viwo kple Farao, Egipte-fia gbɔ, bena woakplɔ Israel-viwo atso Egipte-nyigba dzi.

eng: These were the leaders of their households. The descendants of Reuben, Israels oldest son: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi. These were Reubens clans.
local: Esiawo nye aƒehanuawo le woƒe ƒome la me. Ruben, Israel ƒe ŋgɔgbevi, ƒe viwo nye: Henox, Palu, Xezron kple Karmi; esiawo nye Ruben-hawo.

eng: The Simeonites: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, a Canaanite womans son. These were Simeons clans.
local: Simeon viwo nye: Yemuel, Yamin, Ohad, Yaxin kple Zoxar kpakple Saul, si nye Kanaan-nyɔnu ƒe vi; esiawo nye Simeon-hawo.

eng: These were the Levites names by their generations: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari. Levi lived years.
local: Esiawo nye Lewi viwo ƒe ŋkɔwo le woƒe dzidzime nu: Gerson, Kahat kple Merari; eye Lewi tsi xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blaetɔ̃ vɔ adre.

eng: The Gershonites: Libni and Shimei and their clans.
local: Gerson viwo nye Libni kple Simei le woƒe dzidzime nu.

eng: The Kohathites: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel. Kohath lived years.
local: Kahat viwo nye: Amram, Yizhar, Xebron kple Uziel; eye Kahat tsi xa ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blaetɔ̃ vɔ etɔ̃.

eng: The Merarites: Mahli and Mushi. These were the Levite clans by their generations.
local: Merari viwo nye: Maxeli kple Musi; esiawo nye Lewi-hawo le woƒe dzidzime nu.

eng: The LORD said to Moses, See, Ive made you like God to Pharaoh, and your brother Aaron will be your prophet.
local: Yehowa ɖo ŋu na Mose bena: Mana nanye Mawu ɖe Farao nu, eye nɔviwò Aron nanye wò nyanuɖela.

eng: Amram married Jochebed, his fathers sister. She gave birth to Aaron and Moses. Amram lived years.
local: Eye, Amram ɖe tsa Yoxebet, amesi dzi Aron kple Mose nɛ. Eye Amram tsi xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blaetɔ̃ vɔ adre.

eng: You will say everything that I command you, and your brother Aaron will tell Pharaoh to let the Israelites out of his land.
local: Gblɔ nya, siwo katã magblɔ na wò la, eye nɔviwò Aron nagblɔe na Farao be, wòaɖe asi le Israel-viwo ŋu woadzo le eƒe anyigba dzi.

eng: The Izharites: Korah, Nepheg, and Zichri.
local: Yizhar viwo nye: Korax, Nefeg kple Sixri.

eng: But Ill make Pharaoh stubborn, and Ill perform many of my signs and amazing acts in the land of Egypt.
local: Ke nye la mana Farao ƒe dzime nasẽ, bena mawo dzesi kple nukunu geɖewo le Egipte-nyigba dzi.

eng: The Uzzielites: Mishael, Elzaphan, and Sithri.
local: Uziel viwo nye: Misael, Elzafan kple Sitri.

eng: When Pharaoh refuses to listen to you, then Ill act against Egypt and Ill bring my people the Israelites out of the land of Egypt in military formation by momentous events of justice.
local: Ke Farao maɖo to mi o, eye mado nye asi ɖe Egiptetɔwo gbɔ, eye matsɔ ŋusẽ gã akplɔ nye amehawo, nye dukɔ Israel-viwo atso Egipte.

eng: Aaron married Elisheba, Amminadabs daughter and Nahshons sister. She gave birth to Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
local: Eye Aron ɖe Eliseba, Aminadab vinyɔnu, amesi nye Naxason nɔvinyɔnu, eye wòdzi Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar kple Itamar nɛ.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Go to Pharaoh and tell him: This is what the LORD says: Let my people go so that they can worship me.
local: Eye Yehowa gblɔ na Mose bena: Gblɔ na Aron be: Do wo asi kple atikplɔ ɖe tɔsisiwo, tɔʋuwo kple tawo dzi, eye nahe akpɔkplɔwo ava Egipte-nyigba la dzi!.

eng: The Egyptians will come to know that I am the LORD, when I act against Egypt and bring the Israelites out from among them.
local: Ekema Egiptetɔwo adze sii bena, nyee nye Yehowa, ne medo nye asi ɖe Egipte dzi, eye mekplɔ Israel-viwo dzoe le wo dome.

eng: The Korahites: Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph. These were the Korahite clans.
local: Korax viwo nye: Asir, Elkana kple Abiasaf; esiawo nye Korax-hawo.

eng: If you refuse to let them go, then Ill send a plague of frogs over your whole country.
local: Tete Aron do eƒe asi ɖe Egiptetɔwo ƒe tɔwo dzi, eye akpɔkplɔwo do hexɔ Egipte-nyigba la dzi.

eng: Moses and Aaron did just as the LORD commanded them.
local: Alesi Yehowa ɖe se na Mose kple Aron la, nenema wowɔ.

eng: Aarons son Eleazar married one of Putiels daughters. She gave birth to Phinehas. These were the leaders of Levite households by their clans.
local: Eye Eleazar, Aron vi ɖe Putiel vinyɔnuwo dometɔ ɖeka, amesi dzi Pinexas nɛ. Ame siawo nye Lewi viwo ƒe aƒehánuawo le woƒe ƒome la nu.

eng: The Nile will overflow with frogs. Theyll get into your palace, into your bedroom and onto your bed, into your officials houses, and among all your people, and even into your ovens and bread pans.
local: Ke afakalawo hã tso woƒe aye me nuwɔwɔwo wòe nenema, eye wohe akpɔkplɔwo va Egipte-nyigba la dzi.

eng: Moses was years old and Aaron was when they spoke to Pharaoh.
local: Mose xɔ ƒe blanyi, eye Aron xɔ ƒe blanyi vɔ etɔ̃, esime woƒo nu kple Farao.

eng: It was this same Aaron and Moses whom the LORD commanded, Bring the Israelites out of the land of Egypt in military formation.
