eng: When a famine struck the land, Abram went down toward Egypt to live as an immigrant since the famine was so severe in the land.
local: Eye do to le anyigba la dzi. Eye Abram yi ɖe Egipte, be wòa nye amedzro le afima; elabena dɔa nu sẽ ŋutɔ le anyigba la dzi?.

eng: I have placed my bow in the clouds; it will be the symbol of the covenant between me and the earth.
local: Meɖo nye anyieʋɔ ɖe alilikpowo ŋu, bena wòanye nubabla, si le mía kple anyigba dome la ƒe dzesi.

eng: After Abram had lived ten years in the land of Canaan, Abrams wife Sarai took her Egyptian servant Hagar and gave her to her husband Abram as his wife.
local: Le Abram ƒe Kanaan-nyigba dzi nɔnɔ ƒe ewo megbe la, Sarai, Abram srɔ̃, tsɔ eƒe kosi, Egipte-nyɔnu Hagar, na srɔ̃a Abram wòɖe.

eng: After Arpachshad was born, Shem lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Esi Sem dzi Arpaxsad megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa atɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: Then the kings of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, Zeboiim, and Bera took up battle positions in the Siddim Valley.
local: Tete Sodom-fia, Gomora-fia, Adma-fia, Zeboyim-fia kple Bela-alo Zoar-fia woho, eye wodze aʋa ɖe Sidim-gbadzaƒe.

eng: and Resen, the great city between Nineveh and Calah.
local: kple Resen le Niniwe kple Kalax dome, esia enye du gãtɔ.

eng: Lot looked up and saw the entire Jordan Valley. All of it was well irrigated, like the garden of the LORD, like the land of Egypt, as far as Zoar .
local: Tete Lot tsa ŋku Yordan-nutowo katã me, eye wòkpɔ bena, tsi li ŋutɔ, elabena Yehowa metsrɔ̃ Sodom kple Gomora haɖe o, ele abe Yehowa ƒe abɔ me ene, eɖi Egipte-nyigba vaseɖe Zoar ke.

eng: Then he brought Abram outside and said, Look up at the sky and count the stars if you think you can count them. He continued, This is how many children you will have.
local: Eye wòkplɔe yi ɖe xexe gblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ dziƒo ɖa, eye naxlẽ ɣletiviwo, ne ate ŋu axlẽ wo la. Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Nenema wò ƒome anɔ.

eng: Just before he arrived in Egypt, he said to his wife Sarai, I know you are a good-looking woman.
local: Ke esi wògogo Egipte la, egblɔ na srɔ̃a Sarai bena: Kpɔ ɖa, menya bena, nyɔnu dzetugbe nènye.

eng: When I bring clouds over the earth and the bow appears in the clouds.
local: Eye ava me bena, ne mele ƒu ƒom alilikpowo le dziƒo, eye anyieʋɔ ɖi ɖe alilikpoawo ŋu lã.

eng: When Arpachshad was years old, he became the father of Shelah.
local: Esi Arpaxsad xɔ ƒe blaetɔ̃ vɔ atɔ̃ la, edzi Selax.

eng: He slept with Hagar, and she became pregnant. But when she realized that she was pregnant, she no longer respected her mistress.
local: Eye wòdɔ kple Hagar, eye wòtɔ fu. Ke esi wòkpɔ bena, yefɔ fu la, eva eƒe aƒenɔ kpɔ ge ɖe ƒomevii.

eng: against King Chedorlaomer of Elam, King Tidal of Goiim, King Amraphel of Shinar, and King Arioch of Ellasar, four kings against five.
local: ɖe Kedorlaomer, Elam-fia, Tideal, Goyim-fia, Amrafel, Sinear-fia, kple Ariok, Elasar-fia ŋuti; fia ene tsitre ɖe fia atɔ̃ ŋuti.

eng: Egypt fathered Ludim, Anamim, Lehabim, Naphtuhim.
local: Eye Mizrayim dzi Luditɔwo, Anamitɔwo, Lehabitɔwo, Naftuxitɔwo.

eng: So Lot chose for himself the entire Jordan Valley. Lot set out toward the east, and they separated from each other.
local: Eye Lot tia Yordan-nutowo me katã na eɖokui, eye Lot ʋu yi ɖe ɣedzeƒe gome; ale nɔvi kple nɔvi dome klã.

eng: Abram trusted the LORD, and the LORD recognized Abrams high moral character.
local: Eye wòxɔ Yehowa dzi se, eye wòbui nɛ dzɔdzɔenyenyee.

eng: I will remember the covenant between me and you and every living being among all the creatures. Floodwaters will never again destroy all creatures.
local: ekema maɖo ŋku nye nubabla, si mebia kpli mi kpakple ŋutilã sia ŋutilã, siwo me agbegbɔgbɔ le la dzi, eye tsi magaɖɔ aɖe anyigba la bena, nu gbagbewo katã natsrɔ̃ o.

eng: When the Egyptians see you, they will say, This is his wife, and they will kill me but let you live.
local: Ne Egiptetɔwo akpɔ wò la, ekema woagblɔ bena, srɔ̃e sia, eye woawum; ke woana wòa natsi agbe.

eng: After Shelah was born, Arpachshad lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eye esi Arpaxsad dzi Selax megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa ene kple etɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: Now the Siddim Valley was filled with tar pits. When the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah retreated, they fell into them; and the rest fled to the mountains.
local: Ke aŋɔʋe geɖewo le Sidim-gbadzaƒe la. Esi Sodom-kple Gomora-fia wòsi la, woge ɖe wo me; ke ame mamleawo si yi to dzi.

eng: Sarai said to Abram, This harassment is your fault. I allowed you to embrace my servant, but when she realized she was pregnant, I lost her respect. Let the LORD decide who is right, you or me.
local: Eye Sarai gblɔ na Abram bena: Nu manyomanyo, si teƒe kpɔm mele la, etso gbɔwò. Nye ŋutɔ metsɔ nye kosi na wò; ke azɔ esi wòkpɔ bena, yefɔ fu ko la, eva kpɔ ye ge ɖe ƒomevii. Yehowa nadrɔ̃ ʋɔnu le mía kpli wò dome!.

eng: Pathrusim, Casluhim, and Caphtorim, from which the Philistines came.
local: Patrusitɔwo, Kasluxitɔwo, amesiwo dome Filistitɔwo dzɔ tso, kple Kaftoritɔwo.

eng: Abram settled in the land of Canaan, and Lot settled near the cities of the valley and pitched his tent close to Sodom.
local: Abram nɔ Kanaan-nyigba dzi; ke Lot nɔ nuto ma ƒe duwo me le eƒe agbadɔ hom le tutum, vaseɖe esime wòɖo Sodom.

eng: He said to Abram, I am the LORD, who brought you out of Ur of the Chaldeans to give you this land as your possession.
local: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Nye enye Yehowa, si kplɔ wò tso Ur le Kaldea, bena matsɔ anyigba sia na wò, ne wòanye wò domenyinu.

eng: The bow will be in the clouds, and upon seeing it I will remember the enduring covenant between God and every living being of all the earths creatures.
local: Eyata ne anyieʋɔ do ɖe alilikpowo ŋu la, makpɔe ahaɖo ŋku nubabla, si le Mawu kple ŋutilã, siwo katã me agbegbɔgbɔ le le anyigba dzi dome la dzi tegbee.

eng: So tell them you are my sister so that they will treat me well for your sake, and I will survive because of you.
local: Gblɔ ko dzro bena, nɔvinyenyɔnu nènye, ne manɔ dedie le tawò, eye matsi agbe le tawò!.

eng: When Shelah was years old, he became the father of Eber.
local: Esi Selax xɔ ƒe blaetɔ̃ la, edzi Eber.

eng: They took everything from Sodom and Gomorrah, including its food supplies, and left.
local: Eye woha Sodom kple Gomoratɔwo ƒe kesinɔnuwo kple nuɖuɖuwo katã hedzoe.

eng: Abram said to Sarai, Since shes your servant, do whatever you wish to her. So Sarai treated her harshly, and she ran away from Sarai.
local: Tete Abram gblɔ na Sarai bena: Kpɔ ɖa, wò kosi la le asiwò me, wɔ nusianu, si nyo ŋuwò la, ɖo eŋu faa! Tete Sarai bɔbɔe ɖe anyi; eyata wòsi le egbɔ.

eng: Canaan fathered Sidon his oldest son, and Heth.
local: Eye Kanaan dzi Zidon, eƒe ŋgɔgbevi, kple Xet.

eng: The citizens of Sodom were very evil and sinful against the LORD.
local: Ke Sodomtɔwo nye ame baɖawo, eye woda vo geɖe ɖe Yehowa ŋuti.

eng: But Abram said, LORD God, how do I know that I will actually possess it.
local: Ke egblɔ nɛ bena: Aƒetɔ Yehowa, nuka me madze sii le bena, màtsɔe anyi domee mahã?.

eng: God said to Noah, This is the symbol of the covenant that I have set up between me and all creatures on earth.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ na Noa bena: Esia enye nubabla, si meɖo anyi le mia kple ŋutilã, siwo katã le anyigba dzi dome la ƒe dzesi.

eng: They also took Lot, Abrams nephew who lived in Sodom, and everything he owned, and took off.
local: Eye wole Lot, Abram ƒe tɔgãyovi, amesi nɔ Sodom hã, eye woha eƒe kesinɔnuwo dzoe.

eng: After Eber was born, Shelah lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eye esi Selax dzi Eber megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa ene kple etɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: When Abram entered Egypt, the Egyptians saw how beautiful his wife was.
local: Eye esi Abram va Egipte la, Egiptetɔwo kpɔ nyɔnu la bena, edze tugbe ŋutɔ.

eng: The LORDs messenger found Hagar at a spring in the desert, the spring on the road to Shur.
local: Ke Yehowa ƒe dɔla kpɔe le tsivudoa ɖe, si le gbe dzi le Sur-mɔ dzi la to.

eng: After Lot separated from him, the LORD said to Abram, From the place where you are standing, look up and gaze to the north, south, east, and west.
local: Esi Lot klã le Abram gbɔ megbe la, Yehowa gblɔ na Abram bena: Tsa ŋku tso afisi nele, eye nakpɔ anyiehe, dziehe, ɣedzeƒe kple ɣetoɖoƒe ɖa.

eng: the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites.
local: Yebusitɔwo, Amoritɔwo kple Girgasitɔwo.

eng: He said, Bring me a three-year-old female calf, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.
local: Eye wòɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Lé nyinɔ, si xɔ ƒe etɔ̃, gbɔnɔ, si xɔ ƒe etɔ, agbo, si xɔ ƒe etɔ̃, akpakpa kple ahɔne ve nam!.

eng: Noahs sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth came out of the ark. Now Ham was Canaans father.
local: Noa ƒe viŋutsu, siwo do go le aɖakaʋua me la, woawo nye: Sem, Xam kple Yafet; Xam sia enye Kanaan fofo.

eng: When a survivor arrived, he told Abram the Hebrew, who lived near the oaks of the Amorite Mamre, who was the brother of Eshcol and Aner, Abrams treaty partners.
local: Tete amea ɖe si va gblɔe na Hebritɔ Abram, si le Amoritɔ Mamre, Eskol kple Aner nɔvi ƒe ali gãwo te la. Ame siawoe bia nu kple Abram.

eng: When Pharaohs princes saw her, they praised her to Pharaoh; and the woman was taken into Pharaohs household.
local: Eye esi Farao ŋumewo kpɔe la, wokafui na Farao, eye wokplɔ nyɔnu la va Farao ƒe me.

eng: and said, Hagar! Sarais servant! Where did you come from and where are you going? She said, From Sarai my mistress. Im running away.
local: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena; Hagar, Sarai ƒe kosi, afika nètso, eye afika néyina? Eɖo ŋu bena: Ɖe mesi le nye aƒenɔ Sarai gbɔ.

eng: When Eber was years old, he became the father of Peleg.
local: Esi Eber xɔ ƒe blaetɔ̃ vɔ ene la, edzi Peleg.

eng: the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites.
local: Xiwitɔwo, Arkitɔwo kple Sinitɔwo.

eng: because all the land that you see I give you and your descendants forever.
local: Elabena anyigba blibo la katã, si kpam nèle la, matsɔe ana wò kple wò dzidzimeviwo tegbetegbe.

eng: He took all of these animals, split them in half, and laid the halves facing each other, but he didnt split the birds.
local: Tete wotsɔ nu siawo katã vɛ nɛ hefe wo me, eye wòɖo akpa afafãawo ɖe agbaka eve me ɖewo nɔewo gbɔ; ke mefe xeawo ya me o.

eng: These were Noahs three sons, and from them the whole earth was populated.
local: Noa viŋutsu etɔ̃ siawo me amegbetɔwo katã dzo tso hekaka ɖe anyigba blibo la katã dzi.

eng: When Abram heard that his relative had been captured, he took all of the loyal men born in his household, three hundred eighteen, and went after them as far as Dan.
local: Azɔ esi Abram se bena, woɖe aboyo nɔvia la, eɖe eƒemevi alafa etɔ̃ kple wuienyi, amesiwo nya aʋawɔwɔ la, eye wòti wo yome vaseɖe Dan.

eng: Things went well for Abram because of her: he acquired flocks, cattle, male donkeys, men servants, women servants, female donkeys, and camels.
local: Eye wòwɔ Abram nyuie le eta, eye wotso alẽwo, nyiwo, tedzitsuwo, kluviwo, kosiwo, tedzinɔwo kple kposɔwo nɛ.

eng: The LORDs messenger said to her, Go back to your mistress. Put up with her harsh treatment of you.
local: Eye Yehowa ƒe ɖɔla la gblɔ nɛ bena: Trɔ yi wò aƒenɔ gbɔ, eye nabɔbɔ ɖokuiwò ɖe ete!.

eng: I will make your descendants like the dust of the earth. If someone could count the bits of dust on the earth, then they could also count your descendants.
local: Eye mana wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ abe ʋuʋudedi ene ale be, ne amea ɖe ate ŋu axlẽ ʋuʋudedi la, eya koe ate ŋu axlẽ wo dzidzimeviwo hã.

eng: the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites. After this the Canaanite clans were dispersed.
local: Arwatitɔwo, Zemaritɔwo kple Xamatitɔwo; emegbe Kanaan ƒe ƒome va keke ta ɖe dzi.

eng: After Peleg was born, Eber lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eye esi Eber dzi Peleg megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa ene kple blaetɔ̃, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: When vultures swooped down on the carcasses, Abram waved them off.
local: Tete xe ƒonuwo va dze ɖe lãwo dzi, ke Abram nyã wo ɖa.

eng: Noah, a farmer, made a new start and planted a vineyard.
local: Eye Noa de asi agbledede me, eye wòde weingblea ɖe.

eng: During the night, he and his servants divided themselves up against them, attacked, and chased them to Hobah, north of Damascus.
local: Eye eya ŋutɔ kple eƒe ameawo dze aʋa va fiti wo le zã me hesi wo, eye wonyã wo vaseɖe Xoba le Damasko ƒe anyiehe.

eng: Then the LORD struck Pharaoh and his household with severe plagues because of Abrams wife Sarai.
local: Ke Yehowa ɖe fu geɖe na Farao kple eƒe aƒe katã le Sarai, Abram srɔ̃ ŋuti.

eng: The LORDs messenger also said to her, I will give you many children, so many they cant be counted.
local: Eye Yehowa ƒe dɔla la gblɔ nɛ bena: Mawɔ wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ ŋutɔ, ale bena le woƒe agbɔsɔsɔ ta womate ŋu axlẽ wo o.

eng: The Canaanite boundary extends from Sidon by way of Gerar to Gaza and by way of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim to Lasha.
local: Eye Kanaan ƒe anyigba keke tso Zidon ɖo ta Gerar kple Gaza, ɖo ta Sodom, Gomora, Adma kple Zeboyim vaseɖe Lasa.

eng: Stand up and walk around through the length and breadth of the land because I am giving it to you.
local: Tsi tre natsa, eye nakpɔ anyigba la ƒe didime kple eƒe kekeme, elabena wòe màtsɔe ana!.

eng: When Peleg was years old, he became the father of Reu.
local: Esi Peleg xɔ ƒe blaetɔ̃ la, edzi Regu.

eng: After the sun set, Abram slept deeply. A terrifying and deep darkness settled over him.
local: Esi ɣe le to ɖom la, Abram dɔ alɔ̃ yi me ʋĩ, eye kpɔ ɖa, vɔvɔ̃ lée, eye tsizi do ɖee.

eng: He drank some of the wine, became drunk, and took off his clothes in his tent.
local: Ke esi wòno wein mu la, eɖe ama ɖe eƒe agbadɔ me.

eng: He brought back all of the looted property, together with his relative Lot and Lots property, wives, and people.
local: Eye wògaxɔ kesinɔnuawo katã kple nɔvia Lot hã le wo si hekpe ɖe eƒe kesinɔnuawo, nyɔnuawo kple ameha la ŋuti.

eng: So Pharaoh summoned Abram and said, Whats this youve done to me? Why didnt you tell me she was your wife?.
local: Eye Farao yɔ Abram ve gblɔ ne bena; Nuka enye si nèwɔ ɖe ŋunye; nukaŋuti megblɔe nam bena, ye srɔ̃e wònye o mahã?.

eng: The LORDs messenger said to her, You are now pregnant and will give birth to a son. You will name him Ishmael because the LORD has heard about your harsh treatment.
local: Yehowa ƒe dɔla la gagblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, èfɔ fu, eye àdzi ŋutsuvi, eye nayɔ eƒe ŋkɔ bena Ismael; elabena Yehowa se wò konyi, si nèfa le wò fukpekpe la ŋuti.

eng: These are Hams sons according to their clans, their languages, their lands, and their nations.
local: Esiawo nye Xam-viwo le woƒe ƒomewo, gbewo, anyigbawo kple woƒe dukɔwo nu.

eng: So Abram packed his tent and went and settled by the oaks of Mamre in Hebron. There he built an altar to the LORD.
local: Eye Abram le eƒe agbadɔ hom le tutum, le ŋgɔgbe yimii, eye wòva nɔ Mamre ƒe ati gã gbɔ le Xebron-nutowo me, eye wòɖi vɔsamlekpui ɖe afima na Yehowa.

eng: After Reu was born, Peleg lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eye esi Peleg dzi Regu megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa eve kple asieke, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: Then the LORD said to Abram, Have no doubt that your descendants will live as immigrants in a land that isnt their own, where they will be oppressed slaves for four hundred years.
local: Eye wògblɔ na Abram bena: Nyae, naganyae bena, wò dzidzimeviwo anye amedzrowo le anyigbaa ɖe, si menye wo tɔ o la dzi, eye woasubɔ anyigba ma dzi tɔwo, eye woamawo ate wo ɖe anyi ƒe alafa ene sɔŋ.

eng: Ham, Canaans father, saw his father naked and told his two brothers who were outside.
local: Eye Xam, Kanaan fofo, kpɔ fofoa ƒe ama, eye wòɖagblɔe na nɔvia eveawo le xexe.

eng: Why did you say, Shes my sister, so that I made her my wife? Now, heres your wife. Take her and go.
local: Nukaŋuti nègblɔ bena, ye nɔvinyɔnue, eye mena wòzu srɔ̃nye mahã? Ke azɔ kpɔ ɖa, meɖe asi le srɔ̃wò ŋuti na wò, kplɔe yii!.

eng: After Abram returned from his attack on Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, the king of Sodom came out to the Shaveh Valley to meet him.
local: Azɔ esi woɖu Kedorlaomer kple fia, siwo kpe ɖe eŋuti dzi hetrɔ gbɔna va aƒe la, Sodom-fia va kpee le Sawebali me, si woyɔna be fiabali me la me.

eng: He will be a wild mule of a man; he will fight everyone, and they will fight him. He will live at odds with all his relatives.
local: Awɔ nu abe lu ene, akɔ asi dzi ɖe amesiame ŋu, eye amesiame akɔ asi dzi ɖe eya hã ŋu, eye wòatigi nɔvia wo katã ɖo.

eng: Children were also born to Shem the father of all Ebers children and Japheths older brother.
local: Eye wodzi ŋutsuwo na Sem, amesi nye Eber-viwo katã tɔgbui kple Yafet nɔvi xoxoetɔ.

eng: When Reu was years old, he became the father of Serug.
local: Esi Regu xɔ ƒe blaetɔ̃ vɔ eve la, edzi Serug.

eng: Shem and Japheth took a robe, threw it over their shoulders, walked backward, and covered their naked father without looking at him because they turned away.
local: Tete Sem kple Yafet tsɔ avɔ lé ɖe abɔta hezɔ megbemegbee, eye wotsyɔe na wo fofo, eye wometrɔ kɔ kpɔ wo fofo ƒe ama o.

eng: But after I punish the nation they serve, they will leave it with great wealth.
local: Gake hã mava wɔ fu dukɔ, si subɔm wole la, eye le esia megbe la woatsɔ kesinɔnu geɖewo adzoe.

eng: Pharaoh gave his men orders concerning Abram, and they expelled him with his wife and everything he had.
local: Eye Farao do amewo, bena woakplɔ eya kple srɔ̃a kple etɔwo katã aɖo ɖa.

eng: Now Melchizedek the king of Salem and the priest of El Elyon had brought bread and wine.
local: Ke Melxizedek, Salem-fia, tsɔ abolo kple wein vɛ; ame sia enye Mawu dziƒoʋitɔ ƒe nunɔla.

eng: Hagar named the LORD who spoke to her, You are El Roi because she said, Can I still see after he saw me.
local: Tete wòyɔ Yehowa, amesi ƒo nu kplii la ƒe ŋkɔ bena: Wòe nye Mawu nusianu kpɔla! Elabena egblɔ bena: Alo menye amesi le kpɔyem la yome mekpɔ ɖa vava oa?.

eng: Shems sons: Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud, and Aram.
local: Sem viŋutsuwo nye: Elam, Asur, Arpaxsad, Lud kple Aram.

eng: After Serug was born, Reu lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eye esi Regu dzi Serug megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa eve kple adre, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: When Noah woke up from his wine, he discovered what his youngest son had done to him.
local: Ke esi aha la kɔ le mo na Noa, eye wòse nusi via ɖevitɔ wɔ ɖe eŋu la.

eng: As for you, you will join your ancestors in peace and be buried after a good long life.
local: Ke wò la ayi ɖe tɔgbuiwòwo gbɔ le ŋutifafa me, eye àku amegã belibeli hafi àku.

eng: and he blessed him, Bless Abram by El Elyon, creator of heaven and earth.
local: Eye wòyrae gblɔ bena Mawu dziƒoʋitɔ, si wɔ dziƒo kple anyigba, nayra Abram!.

eng: Therefore, that well is called Beer-lahai-roi; its the well between Kadesh and Bered.
local: Eyata woyɔ vudo ma bena: Agbetɔ, si le kpɔyem la ƒe vudo; ele Kades kple Bared dome.

eng: Arams sons: Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.
local: Aram viŋutsuwo nye: Uz, Xul, Geter kple Mas.

eng: He said, Cursed be Canaan: the lowest servant he will be for his brothers.
local: egblɔ bena: Woaƒo fi ade Kanaan, kluviwo ƒe kluvi wòanye na nɔviawo!.

eng: The fourth generation will return here since the Amorites wrongdoing wont have reached its peak until then.
local: Hafi wò dzidzime enelia agatrɔ ava afisia, elabena Amoritɔwo ƒe vodada mesɔ gbɔ vɔ haɖe o.

eng: When Serug was years old, he became the father of Nahor.
local: Esi Serug xɔ ƒe blaetɔ̃ la, edzi Naxor.

eng: bless El Elyon, who gave you the victory over your enemies. Abram gave Melchizedek one-tenth of everything.
local: Eye woakafu Mawu dziƒoʋĩtɔ, si tso wò futɔwo ɖe asi na wò la! Tete Abram tso nuawo katã ƒe ewolia ne.

eng: Hagar gave birth to a son for Abram, and Abram named him Ishmael.
local: Eye Hagar dzi ŋutsuvi na Abram, eye Abram tsɔ ŋkɔ na ŋutsuvi, si Hagar dzi nɛ la, be Ismael.

eng: Arpachshad fathered Shelah, and Shelah fathered Eber.
local: Arpaxsad dzi Selax, eye Selax dzi Eber.

eng: He also said, Bless the LORD, the God of Shem; Canaan will be his servant.
local: Eye wògagblɔbena: Woakafu Yehowa, Sem ƒe Mawu; eye Kanaan anye kluvi na wo!.

eng: After the sun had set and darkness had deepened, a smoking vessel with a fiery flame passed between the split-open animals.
local: Eye wòva ve me bena, esime ɣe ɖo to, eye viviti do la, kpɔ ɖa, dzudzɔ le dodom le kpɔa ɖe me kɔlikɔli, eye dzo ƒe ade le dzi dem le lãwo domee.

eng: After Nahor was born, Serug lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eye esi Serug dzi Naxor megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa eve, eye wòdzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: Then the king of Sodom said to Abram, Give me the people and take the property for yourself.
local: Eye Sodom-fia gblɔ na Abram bena: Tsɔ ameawo nam, ne kesinɔnuawo nasusɔ na wò!.

eng: Abram was years old when Hagar gave birth to Ishmael for Abram.
local: Abram xɔ ƒe blanyi vɔ ade, esime Hagar dzi Ismael nɛ.

eng: May God give space to Japheth; he will live in Shems tents, and Canaan will be his servant.
local: Mawu ana be, Yafet ƒe nɔƒe nakeke ɖe nu, eye wòano Sem ƒe agbadɔwo me; eye Kanaan anye kluvi na wo!.

eng: That day the LORD cut a covenant with Abram: To your descendants I give this land, from Egypts river to the great Euphrates.
local: Le ŋkeke ma dzi Yehowa bla nu kple Abram gblɔ bena: Wò dzidzimeviwo matsɔ anyigba sia, si tso Egipte-tɔsisi la nu vaseɖe Frat-tɔsisi gã la nu ana:.

eng: To Eber were born two sons: The first was named Peleg, because during his lifetime the earth was divided. His brothers name was Joktan.
local: Eye wodzi ŋutsuvi eve na Eber. Gbãtɔ ŋkɔ nye Peleg; elabena amegbetɔwo mã le eƒe ŋɔli me, eye nɔvia ŋkɔ nye Yoktan.

eng: When Nahor was years old, he became the father of Terah.
local: Esi Naxor xɔ ƒe blave vo asieke la, edzi Tarax.

eng: But Abram said to the king of Sodom, I promised the LORD, El Elyon, creator of heaven and earth.
local: Ke Abram gblɔ na Sodom-fia bena. Meta Yehowa, Mawu dziƒoʋitɔ, si wɔ dziƒo kple anyigba la.

eng: After the flood, Noah lived years.
local: Eye le tsiɖɔɖɔ la megbe la Noa ganɔ agbe ƒe alafa etɔ̃ kple blaatɔ̃, eye Noa ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo katã nye ƒe alafa asieke kple blaatɔ̃, eye wòku.

eng: together with the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites.
local: Kenitɔwo, Kinizitɔwo, Kadmonitɔwo.

eng: Joktan fathered Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah.
local: Yoktan dzi Almodad, Salep, Xazarmawet, Yarax.

eng: After Terah was born, Nahor lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Eye esi Naxor dzi Tarax megbe la, eganɔ agbe ƒe alafa ɖeka kple wuiasieke, eye wodzi ŋutsuwo kple nyɔnuwo.

eng: that I wouldnt take even a thread or a sandal strap from anything that was yours so that you couldnt say, Im the one who made Abram rich.
local: bena, nyemaxɔ naneke atso nusiwo katã nye tɔwo la me o, ka loo, alo atokotaka gɔ̃ hã o, bena nagagblɔ bena, yee wɔ Abram wòzu kesinɔtɔ o!.

eng: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaim.
local: Xititɔwo, Perizitɔwo, Refaitɔwo.

eng: Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah.
local: Hadoram, Uzal, Dikla.

eng: When Terah was years old, he became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
local: Eye esi Tarax xɔ ƒe blaadre la, edzi Abram, Naxor kple Haran.

eng: The only exception is that the young men may keep whatever they have taken to eat, and the men who went with me—Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre—may keep their share.
local: Nyemele naneke dim o, negbe nusi ɖekakpuiawo ɖu, kple ŋutsu, siwo yi kplim, Aner, Eskol kple Mamre ƒe gome la ko woawo naxɔ wo tɔ gome.

eng: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
local: Amoritɔwo, Kanaanitɔwo, Girgasitɔwo kple Yebusitɔwo.

eng: Obal, Abimael, Sheba.
local: Obal, Abimael, Seba.

eng: These are Terahs descendants. Terah became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran. Haran became the father of Lot.
local: Tarax ƒe dzidzimeviwo ŋutinya enye si: Tarax dzi Abram. Naxor kple Haran; eye Haran dzi Lot.

eng: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All of these were Joktans sons.
local: Ofir, Xawila kple Yobab. Ame siawo katã nye Yoktan viŋutsuwo.

eng: Haran died while with his father Terah in his native land, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
local: Ke Haran tre kuku na fofoa Tarax le Kaldea-nyigba dzi le wo de Ur.

eng: Their settlements extended from Mesha by way of Sephar, the eastern mountains.
local: Eye woƒe nɔƒe keke tso Mesa ɖo ta Safar vaseɖe ɣedzeƒetowo gbɔ.

eng: Abram and Nahor both married; Abrams wife was Sarai, and Nahors wife was Milcah the daughter of Haran, father of both Milcah and Iscah.
local: Eye Abram kple Naxor woɖe srɔ̃; Abram srɔ̃ ŋkɔ nye Sarai, eye Naxor srɔ̃ ŋkɔ nye Milka, amesi nye Haran, Milka kple Yiska fofo ƒe vinyɔnu.

eng: These are Shems sons according to their clans, their languages, their lands, and their nations.
local: Esiawo nye Sem viŋutsuwo le woƒe ƒomewo, gbewo, anyigbawo kple woƒe dukɔwo nu.

eng: Sarai was unable to have children.
local: Ke Sarai tsi ko, vi mele esi o.

eng: Terah took his son Abram, his grandson Lot , and his son Abrams wife, Sarai his daughter-in-law. They left Ur of the Chaldeans for the land of Canaan, and arriving at Haran, they settled there.
local: Eye Tarax ʋu kple via Abram kple tɔgbuiyɔvia Lot, si nye Haran vi, hekpe ɖe Sarai, via Abram srɔ̃ ŋuti tso Ur le Kaldea, bene wòayi Kanaan-nyigba dzi Ke esi wova Xaran la, wònɔ afima.

eng: These are the clans of Noahs sons according to their generations and their nations. From them the earths nations branched out after the flood.
local: Esia nye Noa viŋutsuwo ƒe ƒomewo le woƒe dzidzimewo kple woƒe dukɔwo nu, eye woawo me anyigba dzi dukɔwo dzɔ tso le tsiɖɔɖɔ la megbe.

eng: Terah lived years, and he died in Haran.
local: Eye Tarax ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo katã nye ƒe alafa eve kple atɔ̃, eye Tarax ku le Xaran.

eng: When Abram was years old, the LORD appeared to Abram and said to him, I am El Shaddai. Walk with me and be trustworthy.
local: Esi Abram xɔ ƒe blaasieke vɔ asieke la, Yehowa ɖe eɖokui fia Abram, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Nye enye Mawu ŋusẽkatãtɔ; zɔ le ŋkunye me, eye nazu blibo!.

eng: I will make a covenant between us and I will give you many, many descendants.
local: Eye nye nubabla nanɔ mia kpli wò dome, eye mana nasɔ gbɔ ɖe edzi.

eng: The LORD appeared to Abraham at the oaks of Mamre while he sat at the entrance of his tent in the days heat.
local: Eye Yehowa ɖe eɖokui fiae le Mamre ƒe ati gãwo gbɔ, le esime wòbɔbɔ nɔ eƒe agbadɔ nu le ŋdɔ me.

eng: Abram fell on his face, and God said to him.
local: Tete Abram tsyɔ mo anyi, eye Mawu ƒo nu kplii bena:.

eng: He looked up and suddenly saw three men standing near him. As soon as he saw them, he ran from his tent entrance to greet them and bowed deeply.
local: Esi wòfɔ mo te la, kasia ekpɔ ŋutsu etɔ̃ le tsitre le eŋgɔ ɖa; eye esi wòkpɔ wo ko la, eƒu du tso eƒe agbadɔ nu yi ɖakpe wo, eye wobɔbɔ tsyɔ mo anyi.

eng: But me, my covenant is with you; you will be the ancestor of many nations.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, mebia nu kpli wò, eye nazu dukɔ geɖewo fofo.

eng: He said, Sirs, if you would be so kind, dont just pass by your servant.
local: Tete wògblɔ bena; Aƒetɔ, ne eve nunye la, ekema megato wò dɔla ŋu yi o.

eng: And because I have made you the ancestor of many nations, your name will no longer be Abram but Abraham.
local: Eyata womagayɔ wò bena Abram akpɔ o, ke boŋ Abraham anye wò ŋkɔ. Elabena meɖo wò dukɔ geɖewo fofoe.

eng: Let a little water be brought so you may wash your feet and refresh yourselves under the tree.
local: Woaku tsi via ɖe ve, ne miaklɔ miaƒe afɔwo ŋuti; emegbe la miagbɔ ɖe eme vie.

eng: The two messengers entered Sodom in the evening. Lot, who was sitting at the gate of Sodom, saw them, got up to greet them, and bowed low.
local: Mawudɔla eveawo va ɖo Sodom le fiẽ me, eye Lot bɔbɔ nɔ Sodom gbɔ nu. Ke esi Lot kpɔ wo la, etsi tre yi ɖakpe wo, eye wobɔbɔ tsyɔ mo anyi.

eng: I will make you very fertile. I will produce nations from you, and kings will come from you.
local: Eye mana nadzi ɖe dzi ŋutɔ, eye dukɔwo kple fiawo adzɔ tso mewo.

eng: Let me offer you a little bread so you will feel stronger, and after that you may leave your servant and go on your way—since you have visited your servant. They responded, Fine. Do just as you have said.
local: eye matsɔ abolo via ɖe vɛ, ne míado ŋusẽ mia ɖokui hafi adzo; elabena esia ta mieva to miaƒe dɔla gbɔ ɖo. Eye woxɔ dzi bena: Wɔ, abe alesi negblɔ ene!.

eng: He said, Come to your servants house, spend the night, and wash your feet. Then you can get up early and go on your way. But they said, No, we will spend the night in the town square.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Oo, nye aƒetɔwo, mitrɔ va dze miaƒe dɔla gbɔ, ne míatsi adɔ, eye miaklɔ miaƒe afɔwo ŋu; etsɔ ŋdi kanya hafi miagadze miaƒe mɔ dzi. Ke woɖo ŋu ne bena: O, míatsi dugbɔdzi le ablɔ me adɔ boŋ.

eng: I will set up my covenant with you and your descendants after you in every generation as an enduring covenant. I will be your God and your descendants God after you.
local: Eye maɖo nye nubabla anyi le mía kpli wò kple megbewòviwo katã dome, eye wòanye nubabla mavɔ, bena manye wò ŋutɔ kple megbewòviwo ƒe Mawu tegbee.

eng: Abraham traveled from there toward the land of the arid southern plain, and he settled as an immigrant in Gerar, between Kadesh and Shur.
local: Eye Abraham ʋu le afima yi ɖe dziehenyigba dzi heva nɔ Kades kple Sur dome, eye wònɔ Gerar vie?.

eng: So Abraham hurried to Sarah at his tent and said, Hurry! Knead three seahs of the finest flour and make some baked goods.
local: Tete Abraham ƒu ɖu yi ɖe Sara gbɔ le agbadɔ me, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Wɔ kaba, ku wɔ memee asiʋlo etɔ̃a ɖe, nablui, eye natsɔe atɔ tatalii.

eng: He pleaded earnestly with them, so they went with him and entered his house. He made a big meal for them, even baking unleavened bread, and they ate.
local: Eye esi wòƒoe ɖe wo nu ʋuu la, wòtrɔ va dze eƒe me. Eye wòto tatali heɖo kplɔ̃ na wo, eye woɖu nu.

eng: I will give you and your descendants the land in which you are immigrants, the whole land of Canaan, as an enduring possession. And I will be their God.
local: Eye matsɔ Kanaan-nyigba blibo la katã, si dzi nènye amedzro le la, na wò, be wòanye wò ŋutɔ kple megbewòviwo tɔ tegbee, eye manye Mawu na wo.

eng: Abraham said of his wife Sarah, Shes my sister. So King Abimelech of Gerar took her into his household.
local: Eye Abraham gblɔ le srɔ̃a Sara ŋuti bena, ye nɔvinyɔnue; eyata Abimelex, Gerar-fia, na wokplɔ Sara vɛ na ye.

eng: Abraham ran to the cattle, took a healthy young calf, and gave it to a young servant, who prepared it quickly.
local: Eye Abraham ƒu ɖu yi ɖe nyiwo dome, eye wòlé nyivi nyuia ɖe tsɔ na eƒe dɔla wòɖawu enumake, eye wòmee wòbi nyuie.

eng: The LORD was attentive to Sarah just as he had said, and the LORD carried out just what he had promised her.
local: Ke Yehowa ɖo ŋku ŋugbe, si wòɖo na Sara la dzi, eye Yehowa wɔ na Sara, abe alesi wògblɔ ene.

eng: Before they went to bed, the men of the city of Sodom—everyone from the youngest to the oldest—surrounded the house.
local: Ke esi womekpɔ mia anyi haɖe o la, Sodom ŋutsuwo, ɖekakpuiwo kple ame tsitsiwo, keke dua ƒe mlɔenutɔwo ke hã va, eye woɖe to ɖe aƒe la.

eng: God said to Abraham, As for you, you must keep my covenant, you and your descendants in every generation.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ na Abraham bena: Ke wò la lé ye nubabla la me ɖe asi; wò ŋutɔ kple megbewòviwo katã milé ye nubabla la me ɖe asi!.

eng: But God appeared to Abimelech that night in a dream and said to him, You are as good as dead because of this woman you have taken. She is a married woman.
local: Ke le zã me la Mawu va Abimelex gbɔ le ɖrɔ̃eƒe gblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, àku kokoko le nyɔnu, si nèxɔ la ta, elabena ame srɔ̃e.

eng: After these events, God tested Abraham and said to him, Abraham! Abraham answered, Im here.
local: Le nya siawo megbe la Mawu do Abraham kpɔ, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Abraham! Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Nye enye si!.

eng: Then Abraham took butter, milk, and the calf that had been prepared, put the food in front of them, and stood under the tree near them as they ate.
local: Eye wòtsɔ notsi kpekẽtɔ kple notsi tsrolotɔ hekpe ɖe nyilã memee la ŋu va ɖo wo kɔme; ke eya ŋutɔ tsitre ɖe wo gbɔ le atia te, esime wole nua ɖum.

eng: She became pregnant and gave birth to a son for Abraham when he was old, at the very time God had told him.
local: Ale Sara fɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi na Abraham le eƒe amegãkuku me le ɣeyiɣi, si Mawu gblɔ ɖi xoxo la dzi.

eng: and called to Lot, Where are the men who arrived tonight? Bring them out to us so that we may have sex with them.
local: Tete wodo ɣli yɔ Lot gblɔ bena: Afika ŋutsu, siwo va gbɔwò egbe fiẽ la le? Kplɔ wo do go va mía gbɔe, ne míanya wo.

eng: This is my covenant that you and your descendants must keep: Circumcise every male.
local: Nye nubabla, si le mia kpli mi kple megbewòviwo dome, si me dim mele be, mialé ɖe asi la, enye esi: Woatso aʋa na ŋutsu sia ŋutsu le mia dome!.

eng: Now Abimelech hadnt gone near her, and he said, Lord, will you really put an innocent nation to death?.
local: Ke Abimelex meɖe asi eŋu haɖe o, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Aƒetɔ, ame maɖifɔwo hãe nawua?.

eng: They said to him, Wheres your wife Sarah? And he said, Right here in the tent.
local: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Afika srɔ̃wò Sara le? Eɖo ŋu bena: Ele agbadɔ me.

eng: God said, Take your son, your only son whom you love, Isaac, and go to the land of Moriah. Offer him up as an entirely burned offering there on one of the mountains that I will show you.
local: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Kplɔ Isak, wò tenuvi, si gbɔ mèlɔ̃a nu lena o la, yi ɖe Moria-nyigba dzi, eye natsɔe amè nui le afima le vɔsamlekpui dzi le toa ɖe, si mafia wò la dzi!.

eng: Abraham named his son—the one Sarah bore him—Isaac.
local: Eye Abraham yɔ ŋutsuvi, si Sara dzi nɛ la ƒe ŋkɔ bena Isak.

eng: Lot went out toward the entrance, closed the door behind him.
local: Eye Lot do go yi wo gbɔ le agbo la godo, eye wòtu ʋɔ ɖewo nu.

eng: You must circumcise the flesh of your foreskins, and it will be a symbol of the covenant between us.
local: Woaɖe miaƒe aʋanuyiwo ɖa, eye esia anye mia kple mia dome nubabla la ƒe dzesi.

eng: Didnt he say to me, Shes my sister, and didnt she—even she—say, Hes my brother? My intentions were pure, and I acted innocently when I did this.
local: Alo menye eya ŋutɔ gblɔ nam bena, ye nɔvinyɔnue, eye nyɔnua hã gblɔ nam bena, ye nɔviŋutsue oa? Nye dzime kɔ, eye nye asi dza le ekpɔkplɔme.

eng: Then one of the men said, I will definitely return to you about this time next year. Then your wife Sarah will have a son! Sarah was listening at the tent door behind him.
local: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Le ƒe, si gbɔna ƒe woalewoɣi la, magatrɔ ava gbɔwò, eye ŋutsuvi anɔ srɔ̃wò Sara si. Ke Sara le agbadɔ nu le emegbe le esem.

eng: Abraham got up early in the morning, harnessed his donkey, and took two of his young men with him, together with his son Isaac. He split the wood for the entirely burned offering, set out, and went to the place God had described to him.
local: Tete Abraham tɔ ŋdi kanya, bia akpa na eƒe tedzi, eye wòkplɔ dɔla eve kple via Isak ɖe asi, eye esi wòdze nake ɖe numemea ŋu la, edze mɔ yi teƒe, si Mawu gblɔ nɛ la.

eng: Abraham circumcised his son Isaac when he was eight days old just as God had commanded him.
local: Esi via Isak xɔ ŋkeke enyi la, Abraham tso aʋa ne, abe alesi Yehowa de se nɛ ene.

eng: and said, My brothers, dont do such an evil thing.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Nɔvinyewo, migawɔ nu baɖa o!.

eng: On the eighth day after birth, every male in every generation must be circumcised, including those who are not your own children: those born in your household and those purchased with silver from foreigners.
local: Ne ŋutsuvia ɖe xɔ ŋkeke enyi le miaƒe ƒome sia ƒome me la, woatso aʋa nɛ; eɖanye aƒevi alo ame ƒeƒle, si menye tɔwò dzidzia o.

eng: God said to him in the dream, I know that your intentions were pure when you did this. In fact, I kept you from sinning against me. Thats why I didnt allow you to touch her.
local: Tete Mawu gblɔ nɛ le drɔ̃eƒe bena: Nye hã menyae bena, wò dzime kɔ le ewɔwɔ me; eyata metso wò xɔ ɖo, be nagade asi eŋu ahawɔ nuvɔ̃ ɖe ŋunye o.

eng: Now Abraham and Sarah were both very old. Sarah was no longer menstruating.
local: Eye Abraham kple Sara wotsi xɔ ƒe geɖe ŋutɔ, ale bena Sara megaléa gbe gɔ̃ hã o.

eng: On the third day, Abraham looked up and saw the place at a distance.
local: Le ŋkeke etɔ̃agbe la Abraham wu mo dzi kpɔ teƒe la le adzɔge ɖa ʋĩ.

eng: Abraham was years old when his son Isaac was born.
local: Ke Abraham xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka, esime wodzi via Isak nɛ.

eng: Ive got two daughters who are virgins. Let me bring them out to you, and you may do to them whatever you wish. But dont do anything to these men because they are now under the protection of my roof.
local: Míkpɔ ɖa, vinyenyɔnu eve li, amesiwo medo ŋutsu gbɔ kpɔ o, makplɔ wo vɛ na mi, ne miawɔ nusi mielɔ̃ la, ɖe wo ŋu faa; ɖeko migawɔ nu baɖa ɖe ŋutsu siawo ŋu o, elabena esiata wova dze gbɔnye ɖo.

eng: Be sure you circumcise those born in your household and those purchased with your silver. Your flesh will embody my covenant as an enduring covenant.
local: Woatso aʋa na aƒevi kple kluvi siaa, eye wòanye nye nubabla ƒe dzesi le miaƒe ŋutilã ŋu tegbee.

eng: Now return the mans wife. Hes a prophet; he will pray for you so you may live. But if you dont return her, know that you and everyone with you will die.
local: Azɔ gagbugbɔ nyɔnu la na srɔ̃a, elabena nyagblɔɖila wònye, eye wòaɖe kuku ɖe tawo, be natsi agbe. Ke ne meɖe asi le eŋu o la, ekema nyae bena, mia kple aƒewòmetɔwo katã miaku.

eng: So Sarah laughed to herself, thinking, Im no longer able to have children and my husbands old.
local: Tete Sara ko nu ɖe dɔ me gblɔ bena: Esi metsi lé ayi ale la, ɖe nu agavivi nunyea? Eye ye aƒetɔ hã ku amegã ɖeɖi!.

eng: Abraham said to his servants, Stay here with the donkey. The boy and I will walk up there, worship, and then come back to you.
local: Tete Abraham gblɔ na dɔlawo bena: Mitsi afisia kple tedzi la; ke mía kple vinye la míayi afime aɖado gbe ɖa, emegbe míagatrɔ ava mia gbɔ.

eng: Sarah said, God has given me laughter. Everyone who hears about it will laugh with me.
local: Eye Sara gblɔ bena: Mawu do nukokoe nam, eye amesiame, si asee la, ako nu le ŋutinye.

eng: Sarah lived to be years old; this was how long she lived.
local: Sara xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blave vɔ adre; esia nye Sara ƒe agbe me ƒewo.

eng: They said, Get out of the way! And they continued, Does this immigrant want to judge us? Now we will hurt you more than we will hurt them. They pushed Lot back and came close to breaking down the door.
local: Eye wodo ɣli ɖe eta gblɔ bena: Te ɖa afia! Wò amedzro ɖeka hã sia, si va dze mía gbã la, nanɔ ɖokuiwò wum amegãe alea? Azɔ la miawo vo ɖe wòa ŋuwò wu woawo gɔ̃. Eye wonyẽ zi ɖe Lot dzi gbidigbidi, be yewoagbã ʋɔa.

eng: Any uncircumcised male whose flesh of his foreskin remains uncircumcised will be cut off from his people. He has broken my covenant.
local: Eye ne ŋutsua ɖe ti, si womeɖe aʋanuyi ɖa nɛ o la, woaɖe nenem bolobolotɔ sia ɖa le eƒe dukɔ la dome, elabena etu nye nubabla la.

eng: Abimelech got up early in the morning and summoned all of his servants. When he told them everything that had happened, the men were terrified.
local: Esi Abimelex fɔ ŋdi la, eya eƒe dɔlawo katã vɛ heto nya siawo katã na wo, eye ŋutsuawo vɔ ŋutɔ.

eng: The LORD said to Abraham, Why did Sarah laugh and say, Me give birth? At my age.
local: Tete Yehowa gblɔ na Abraham bena: Nukaŋuti Sara le nu kom le susum bena: Nye amesi ku nyagã la, magate ŋu adzi via?.

eng: Abraham took the wood for the entirely burned offering and laid it on his son Isaac. He took the fire and the knife in his hand, and the two of them walked on together.
local: Eye Abraham tsɔ numènake la ɖo la na via Isak, ke eya ŋutɔ ɖe dzo, eye wòɖo hɛ asi; ale wo kpakple eve woyi.

eng: As the days went by and Abraham became older, the LORD blessed Abraham in every way.
local: Eye Abraham tsi xɔ ƒe geɖe, eye Yehowa yra Abraham le nuwo katã me.

eng: She said, Who could have told Abraham that Sarah would nurse sons? But now Ive given birth to a son when he was old.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Ameka susu le Abraham ŋuti bena, Sara ana no viwò mahã? Elabena medzi ŋutsuvi nɛ le eƒe amegãkuku me.

eng: She died in Kiriath-arba, that is, in Hebron, in the land of Canaan; and Abraham cried out in grief and wept for Sarah.
local: Eye Sara ku le Kiryat-Arba, eya nye Xebron le Kanaan-nyigba dzi. Eye Abraham va, be wòafa konyi, eye wòafa avi na Sara.

eng: The men inside reached out and pulled Lot back into the house with them and slammed the door.
local: Ke ŋutsuawo do abɔ ɖa he Lot va wo ɖokui gbee le aƒea me, eye wotu ʋɔa.

eng: God said to Abraham, As for your wife Sarai, you will no longer call her Sarai. Her name will now be Sarah.
local: Eye Mawu gagblɔ na Abraham bena: Megayɔ srɔ̃wò Sarai bena Sarai azɔ o, ke boŋ Sara anye eƒe ŋkɔ.

eng: Then Abimelech summoned Abraham and said to him, What have you done to us? What sin did I commit against you that you have brought this terrible sin to me and my kingdom, by doing to me something that simply isnt done.
local: Tete Abimelex yɔ Abraham, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Nuka nye esi nèwɔ ɖe mía ŋu? Nuvɔ̃ ka gɔ̃ mewɔ de ŋuwò bena, nèhe dzɔgbevɔ̃e gã sia va nye ŋutɔ kple nye fiaɖuƒe la dzi mahã? Nusi womewɔna o la, eya nye esia nèwɔ ɖe ŋutinye.

eng: Is anything too difficult for the LORD? When I return to you about this time next year, Sarah will have a son.
local: Ɖe nane sesẽ na Yehowa? Le ƒe, si gbɔna ƒe woleawoɣi la, magato ava gbɔwò, eye ŋutsuvi anɔ Sara si.

eng: Isaac said to his father Abraham, My father? Abraham said, Im here, my son. Isaac said, Here is the fire and the wood, but where is the lamb for the entirely burned offering.
local: Tete Isak gblɔ na fofoa Abraham bena: Fofonye! Eɖo ŋu ne bena: Nye enye si, vinye! Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, dzo kple nake enye si, ke afika alẽ le miatsɔ ame nui?.

eng: The boy grew and stopped nursing. On the day he stopped nursing, Abraham prepared a huge banquet.
local: Eye ɖevi la tsi, eye wòɖi no, eye gbesigbe Isak ɖi no la, Abraham ɖo kplɔ̃ gãa ɖe.

eng: Abraham said to the oldest servant of his household, who was in charge of everything he owned, Put your hand under my thigh.
local: Eye Abraham gblɔ na eƒe dɔla tsitsitɔ, si kpɔa eƒe nuwo katã dzi bena. Tsɔ wò asi ɖo nye ali me.

eng: After he got up from embracing his deceased wife, he spoke with the Hittites.
local: Eye Abraham tso le ame kuku la gbɔ, eye wòƒo nu kple Xitiviwo bena:.

eng: I will bless her and even give you a son from her. I will bless her so that she will become nations, and kings of peoples will come from her.
local: Mayrae, eye mana wòadzi ŋutsuvi hã na wò. Mayrae ale bena, wòava nye dukɔwo kple dukɔwo dziɖulawo dada.

eng: Then the messengers blinded the men near the entrance of the house, from the youngest to the oldest, so that they groped around trying to find the entrance.
local: Eye wotsɔ ŋkuagbã ƒo ŋutsu, siwo le xexe le ʋɔtrua nu, ɖeviwo kple ame tsitsiwo siaa, ale bena ɖeɖi koe te wo ŋu dzodzro le ʋɔtrua nu didi me.

eng: Abimelech said to Abraham, What were you thinking when you did this thing.
local: Eye Abimelex gagblɔ na Abraham bena: Nukae nèɖo ɖe ŋunye, hafi wɔ nu sia?.

eng: Sarah lied and said, I didnt laugh, because she was frightened. But he said, No, you laughed.
local: Tete Sara sẽ nu gblɔ bena: Nyemeko nu o; elabena ele vɔvɔ̃m. Ke egblɔ ne bena: O, èko nu tso!.

eng: Abraham said, The lamb for the entirely burned offering? God will see to it, my son. The two of them walked on together.
local: Abraham xɔ edzi bena: Vinye, Mawu ŋutɔ aɖi alẽ miatsɔ amè nui. Eye wo kpakple eve wogayi ŋgɔ.

eng: By the LORD, God of heaven and earth, give me your word that you wont choose a wife for my son from the Canaanite women among whom I live.
local: eye nata Yehowa, dziƒo kple anyigba ƒe Mawu la nam be, maɖe srɔ̃ na vinye atso Kanaantɔ, siwo dome mele la ƒe nyɔnuviwo dome o;.

eng: Sarah saw Hagars son laughing, the one Hagar the Egyptian had borne to Abraham.
local: Ke esi Sara kpo bena, Egipte-nyɔnua Hagar ƒe vi, si wòdzi na Abraham, lɔ̃a gaflumuwɔwɔ la.

eng: I am an immigrant and a temporary resident with you. Give me some property for a burial plot among you so that I can bury my deceased wife near me.
local: Amedzro menye le mia dome, mina ameɖiƒem, bena matsɔ nye ame kuku la aɖi ɖe afima!.

eng: Abraham fell on his face and laughed. He said to himself, Can a -year-old man become a father, or Sarah, a -year-old woman, have a child?.
local: Tete Abraham tsyɔ mo anyi, eye wòko nu, elabena esusu le eƒe ta me bena: Alo ɖe wòadzi vi na ŋutsu, si xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka laa? Alo ɖe Sara, si xɔ ƒe blaasieke la, agate ŋu adzi vi mahã?.

eng: The men said to Lot, Whos still with you here? Take away from this place your sons-in-law, your sons, your daughters, and everyone else you have in the city.
local: Eye ŋutsuawo gblɔ na Lot bena: Amea ɖewo gale asiwò le afisia? Kplɔ towòwo kple viwònyɔnuwo kple tɔwo to, siwo katã le dua me la, do go tso teƒe sia.

eng: Abraham said, I thought to myself, No one reveres God here and they will kill me to get my wife.
local: Abraham gblɔ bena: Mebu be, afisiatɔwo manya vɔ̃a Mawu o, eye woawum le srɔ̃nye ta mahi.

eng: The men got up from there and went over to look down on Sodom. Abraham was walking along with them to send them off.
local: Esia megbe la ŋutsuawo dze mɔ tso afima, eye wokpɔ Sodom le abu me ɖa; ke Abraham yi kpli wo, be yeaɖo wo ɖa.

eng: Go to my land and my family and find a wife for my son Isaac there.
local: ke boŋ nayi mía denyigba dzi kple ƒonyemeawo gbɔ, eye naɖe srɔ̃ na vinye Isak!.

eng: They arrived at the place God had described to him. Abraham built an altar there and arranged the wood on it. He tied up his son Isaac and laid him on the altar on top of the wood.
local: Esi woɖo teƒe, si Mawu gblɔ nɛ la, Abraham ɖi vɔsamlekpui, eye wòtɔ nakeawo ɖo edzi; emegbe la ebla via Isak, eye wòkɔe mlɔ nake, siwo le vɔsamlekpuia dzi la dzi.

eng: So she said to Abraham, Send this servant away with her son! This servants son wont share the inheritance with my son Isaac.
local: egblɔ na Abraham bena: Nyã kosi sia kple via ɖa; elabena kosi sia ƒe vi mate ŋu anyi dome kple vinye Isak o.

eng: The Hittites responded to Abraham.
local: Eye Xitiviwo ɖo ŋu na Abraham bena:.

eng: because we are about to destroy this place. The LORD has found the cries of injustice so serious that the LORD sent us to destroy it.
local: Miele teƒe sia tsrɔ̃ ge, elabena dodo ɖe wo ta ɖi ɖe Yehowa ƒe to me; eyata Yehowa ɖo mí ɖa, bena míatsrɔ̃e.

eng: To God Abraham said, If only you would accept Ishmael.
local: Eye Abraham gblɔ na Mawu bena: Ismael netsi agbe na wò ko!.

eng: She is, truthfully, my sister—my fathers daughter but not my mothers daughter—and shes now my wife.
local: Evɔ, nyateƒe, nɔvinyenyɔnue; elabena fofonye vie, gake menye danye vie o, eye meɖee.

eng: when the LORD said, Will I keep from Abraham what Im about to do?.
local: Eye Yehowa gblɔ bena: Ɖe maɣla nusi wɔ ge mala la, ɖe Abraham.

eng: The servant said to him, What if the woman doesnt agree to come back with me to this land? Shouldnt I take your son back to the land you left.
local: Dɔla la ɖo ŋu ne bena: Evɔ, ne nyɔnua mehelɔ̃ be, yeakplɔm ɖo ava anyigba sia dzi o la, ɖe magbugbɔ viwò la ayi ɖe anyigba, si dzi neʋu tso la dzii mahã?.

eng: Then Abraham stretched out his hand and took the knife to kill his son as a sacrifice.
local: Eye Abraham do asi ɖa tsɔ hɛ, be yeawu via.

eng: This upset Abraham terribly because the boy was his son.
local: Ke nya la medze Abraham ŋu o le viaŋutsu la ta.

eng: Listen to us, sir. You are an eminent man of God among us. Bury your dead in one of our own select burial sites. None of us will keep our own burial plots from you to bury your dead.
local: Amegã, se mía gbe! Mawu ƒe ame ɖoɖoa nenye le mia dome; ɖi wò ame kuku la ɖe míaƒe ame veviwo ɖiƒe. Mía dometɔa ɖeke magbe bena, megaɖi wò ame kukua ɖe eƒe ameɖiƒe o.

eng: Lot went to speak to his sons-in-law, married to his daughters, and said, Get up and get out of this place because the LORD is about to destroy the city. But his sons-in-law thought he was joking.
local: Tete Lot do go yi ɖagblɔe na toa, siwo le vianyɔnuwo ɖe ge la bena: Mitso, ne míado le ɖu la me, elabena Yehowa le etsrɔ̃ ge! Ke toawo bui nukonyae.

eng: But God said, No, your wife Sarah will give birth to a son for you, and you will name him Isaac. I will set up my covenant with him and with his descendants after him as an enduring covenant.
local: Tete Mawu gblɔ bena: Nyateƒe! Srɔ̃wò Sara adzi ŋutsuvi na wò, eye nayɔ eƒe ŋkɔ bena Isak, eye mabia nu kple eya kpakple emegbeviwo tegbee.

eng: When God led me away from my fathers household, I said to her, This is the loyalty I expect from you: in each place we visit, tell them, He is my brother.
local: Esi Mawu gblɔ nam bena, maʋu le fofonye ƒe me ayi dzronyigba dzi la, eya me kee megblɔ nɛ bena: Ve nunye, eye afisiafi, si míava la, nagblɔ bena, ye nɔvi ŋutsu menye.

eng: Abraham will certainly become a great populous nation, and all the earths nations will be blessed because of him.
local: esi wònye Abraham ava nye dukɔ gã sẽŋua ɖe, eye woayra anyigba dzi dukɔwo katã le eme laa?.

eng: Abraham said to him, Be sure you dont take my son back there.
local: Abraham gblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ nyuie, bena nagagbugbɔ vinye la ayi afimae o!.

eng: God said to Abraham, Dont be upset about the boy and your servant. Do everything Sarah tells you to do because your descendants will be traced through Isaac.
local: Eye Mawu gblɔ na Abraham bena: Megana nya sia nave wò le ŋutsuvi la kple wò kosi la ta o; ke boŋ ɖo to Sara le nusianu, si wodi tso gbɔwò la me, elabena Isak me wò dzidzime-viwo adzo tso.

eng: But the LORDs messenger called out to Abraham from heaven, Abraham? Abraham? Abraham said, Im here.
local: Tete Yehowa ƒe dɔla yɔe tso dziƒo gblɔ bena: Abraham, Abraham! Eye wòtɔ bena: Nye enye si!.

eng: Abraham rose, bowed to the local citizens the Hittites.
local: Tete Abraham tsi tre, eye wobɔbɔ na anyigbadzitɔ, siwo nye Xitiviwo.

eng: When dawn broke, the messengers urged Lot, Get up and take your wife and your two daughters who are here so that you are not swept away because of the evil in this city.
local: Esi dzite tso la, mawudɔlawo ƒo du ɖe Lot te gblɔ bena: Tso nakplɔ srɔ̃wò kple viwònyɔnu eve, siwo le gbɔwò la, ne màgatsrɔ̃ le ɖu la ƒe vodada ta o!.

eng: As for Ishmael, I’ve heard your request. I will bless him and make him fertile and give him many, many descendants. He will be the ancestor of twelve tribal leaders, and I will make a great nation of him.
local: Ke le Ismael to gome la, mese wò nyawo. Kpɔ ɖa, mayrae, eye mana wòadzi, eye wòasɔ gbɔ ɖe dzi. Adzi fia wuieve, eye mawɔe wòazu dukɔ gãa ɖe.

eng: Abimelech took flocks, cattle, male servants, and female servants, and gave them to Abraham; and Abimelech returned his wife Sarah.
local: Tete Abimelex tso alẽwo, nyiwo, kluviwo kple kosiviwo na Abraham, eye wògagbugbɔ srɔ̃a Sara hã nɛ.

eng: I have formed a relationship with him so that he will instruct his children and his household after him. And they will keep to the LORDs path, being moral and just so that the LORD can do for Abraham everything he said he would.
local: Elabena metiae, bena wòade se na emegbeviwo kple eƒe me katã bena, woalé ŋku ɖe Yehowa ƒe mɔ ŋuti, eye woawɔ nu dzɔdzɔe kple nu nyui, bena ne nusi ŋugbe Yehowa do la, nava me na Abraham.

eng: The LORD, God of heaven—who took me from my fathers household and from my familys land, who spoke with me and who gave me his word, saying, I will give this land to your descendants—he will send his messenger in front of you, and you will find a wife for my son there.
local: Yehowa, dziƒo Mawu, si ɖem tso fofonye ƒe me kple mia denyigba dzi, amesi ƒo nu kplim, eye wòka atam nam bena: Wò dzidzimeviwo matsɔ anyigba sia ana la, adɔ eƒe ɖɔla ɖe ŋgɔwò, be naɖe srɔ̃ na vinye la tso afima.

eng: But I will make of your servants son a great nation too, because he is also your descendant.
local: Ke mana dukɔ nadzo tso kosi la ƒe vi hã me, elabena viwoe.

eng: The messenger said, Dont stretch out your hand against the young man, and dont do anything to him. I now know that you revere God and didnt hold back your son, your only son, from me.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Megaka asi ɖevi la ŋu o, eye megawɔ nanekee o! Menyae azɔ bena, èvɔ̃a Mawu, elabena mete wò tenuvi lam o.

eng: and spoke with them: If you yourselves allow me to bury my dead near me, listen to me and ask Ephron, Zohars son.
local: eye wòƒo nu kpli wo bena: Ne mielɔ̃ bena, maɖi nye ame kukua la, ekema miɖo tom, eye miɖe kuku na Efron Zoxar-vi nam.

eng: But I will set up my covenant with Isaac, who will be born to Sarah at this time next year.
local: Ke mabia nu kple Isak, amesi Sara adzi na wò le ƒe, si gbɔna ƒe woalèwoɣi la.

eng: He hesitated, but because the LORD intended to save him, the men grabbed him, his wife, and two daughters by the hand, took him out, and left him outside the city.
local: Ke esi wòle tɔtrɔm la, ŋutsuawo lé eya kple srɔ̃a kpakple vianyɔnu eveawo ƒe alɔnu hekplɔ wo do goe, eye woɖe asi le wo ŋu ɖe du la godo; elabena Yehowa ve woawo nu.

eng: Abimelech said, My land is here available to you. Live wherever you wish.
local: Eye Abimelex gblɔ bena: Nye anyigba enye si, no afisiafi, si nyo ŋuwò.

eng: Then the LORD said, The cries of injustice from Sodom and Gomorrah are countless, and their sin is very serious!.
local: Tete Yehowa gblɔ bena: Dodo ɖe Sodom kple Gomora ta sɔ gbɔ akpa, eye woƒe dzidadawo tso me akpa.

eng: If the woman wont agree to come back with you, you will be free from this obligation to me. Only dont take my son back there.
local: Eye ne nyɔnua melɔ̃ be, yeakpɔ wò ɖo o la, ekema menye wòe tu nye atam sia o; ɖeko megakpɔ vinye la yi afimae gbeɖe o!.

eng: Abraham got up early in the morning, took some bread and a flask of water, and gave it to Hagar. He put the boy in her shoulder sling and sent her away. She left and wandered through the desert near Beer-sheba.
local: Eye Abraham fɔ ŋdi kanya tso abolo, eye woku tsi ɖe lãgbalẽgolo me taŋ, eye wotsɔ ɖevi la kple nu mawo ɖo abɔta na Hagar hedo mɔ wo. Ke esi wole yiyim la, wova tra mɔ le Beer-Seba gbe dzi.

eng: Abraham looked up and saw a single ram caught by its horns in the dense underbrush. Abraham went over, took the ram, and offered it as an entirely burned offering instead of his son.
local: Tete Abraham fɔ kɔ dzi, eye kpɔ ɖa, agboa ɖe nye ekem, si ƒe dzowo ku aveka le emegbe, eye Abraham yi ɖalé agbo la hetsɔ mè nui ɖe via teƒe.

eng: to give me his own cave in Machpelah at the edge of his field. Let him give it to me for the full price, to be witnessed by you, as my own burial property.
local: be, wòatsɔ eƒe kpeto Maxpela, si le eƒe agble ƒe nuwuƒe la nam, eye maxe fe ɖe eta blibo, ne wòanye nye ameɖiƒe koŋ le mia dome.

eng: When God finished speaking to him, God ascended, leaving Abraham alone.
local: Esi Mawu wu eƒe nuƒoƒo nu kplii la, edzo yi dzi le Abraham gbɔ.

eng: After getting them out, the men said, Save your lives! Dont look back! And dont stay in the valley. Escape to the mountains so that you are not swept away.
local: Eye esi wokplɔ wo do goe vo la, wogblɔ bena: Ekpo ɖe asiwò azɔ, eyata ɖe ɖokuiwò! Megakpɔ megbe o, eye megatɔ ɖe afia ɖeke hã o; ke boŋ si yi ɖe to la dzi, bena màgatsrɔ̃ o!.

eng: To Sarah, he said, Ive given your brother one thousand pieces of silver. It means that neither you nor anyone with you has done anything wrong. Everything has been set right.
local: Eye wògblɔ na Sara bena: Kpɔ ɖa, metso afia na wò, eye metsɔ klosalo akpe ɖeka na nɔviwò heɖe ŋukpe le mo na wò le gbɔwò viwo katã ŋku me.

eng: I will go down now to examine the cries of injustice that have reached me. Have they really done all this? If not, I want to know.
local: Eyata maɖi ayi, ne makpɔ bena, nya, siwo katã sem mele le wo ŋuti la, le eme loo, alo mele eme o, ne manya.

eng: So the servant put his hand under his master Abrahams thigh and gave him his word about this mission.
local: Eye dɔla la tsɔ eƒe asi ɖo eƒe aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe ali me, eye wòka atam nɛ ɖe nya sia dzi.

eng: Finally the water in the flask ran out, and she put the boy down under one of the desert shrubs.
local: Esi lãgbalẽgolo me tsi la vɔ la, etsɔ ɖevi la yi ɖamlɔ ati dodoea ɖe te.

eng: Abraham named that place the LORD sees. That is the reason people today say, On this mountain the LORD is seen.
local: Eye Abraham yɔ teƒe ma bena: Afisi Yehowa ɖe eɖokui fia le. Eyata wogblɔna vaseɖe egbegbe bena: Le to dzi, afisi Yehowa ɖea eɖokui fiana le.

eng: Now Ephron was a native Hittite. So Ephron the Hittite responded to Abraham publicly in order that the Hittites and everyone at his citys gate could hear.
local: Ke Efron le Xitiviawo dome Eye Efron Xititɔ la ɖo ŋu na Abraham le Xitiviwo kple amesiwo katã va dua me la ƒe ŋku me bena:.

eng: Abraham took his son Ishmael, all those born in his household, and all those purchased with his silver—that is, every male in Abrahams household—and he circumcised the flesh of their foreskins that same day, just as God had told him to do.
local: Gbemagbe tututu Abraham tso via Ismael kple amesiwo katã wodzi le eƒe me, kple eƒe kluviwo, ŋutsu sia ŋutsu, si le Abraham ƒe me la ƒe aʋanuyiwo ɖa, abe alesi Mawu gblɔ nɛ ene.

eng: But Lot said to them, No, my lords, please.
local: Ke Lot gblɔ bena: O, Aƒetɔ, meganɔ nenema o!.

eng: Abraham prayed to God; and God restored Abimelech, his wife, and his women servants to health, and they were able to have children.
local: Eye Abraham ɖe kuku na Mawu ɖe Abimelex kple srɔ̃a kple atsusiawo katã ta, eye wòyɔ dɔ wo, bena woate ŋu adzi vi.

eng: The men turned away and walked toward Sodom, but Abraham remained standing in front of the LORD.
local: Esia megbe la ŋutsuawo trɔ yi Sodom; ke Abraham gatɔ ɖe Yehowa gbɔ ko.

eng: She walked away from him about as far as a bow shot and sat down, telling herself, I cant bear to see the boy die. She sat at a distance, cried out in grief, and wept.
local: eye wòyi ɖabɔbɔ na ɖevi la kasa le adzɔge boo, wo dome didi abe aŋutrɔ ƒe duɖiɖi me ene; elabena egblɔ bena: Nyemate ŋu akpa ɖevi la ƒe kuƒe o. Eyata wòyi ɖabɔbɔ no adzɔge boo ɖo; tete ɖevi la de asi avifafa me sesĩe.

eng: The servant took ten of his masters camels and all of his masters best provisions, set out, and traveled to Nahors city in Aram-naharaim.
local: Tete dɔla la ɖe kposɔ ewo tso eƒe aƒetɔ ƒe kposɔwo dome, eye wotsɔ eƒe aƒetɔ ƒe nu vevi vovovowo ɖe asi, eye wòdze mɔ heyi Mesopotamia, ɖe Naxor ƒe ɖu la me.

eng: No, sir. Listen, I will give you the field, and I will give you the cave in it. In front of my peoples witnesses, I will give it to you. Bury your dead.
local: Nye amegã, ɖo tom! Metsɔ agble lã kple kpeto, si le eme la, ke wò. Le nye dumetɔwo katã ŋku me metsɔe ke wò, ɖi wò ame kuku la ko!.

eng: The LORDs messenger called out to Abraham from heaven a second time.
local: Eye Yehowa ƒe dɔla gayɔ Abraham zi evelia tso dziƒo.

eng: Abraham was years old when he circumcised the flesh of his foreskin.
local: Abraham xɔ ƒe blaasieke vɔ asieke, hafi wotso aʋa nɛ.

eng: Youve done me a favor and have been so kind to save my life. But I cant escape to the mountains since the catastrophe might overtake me there and Id die.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, ève wò ɖɔla nu xoxo, eye wò amenuveve, si nètsɔ ve nunye be, megale agbe la, esɔ gbã. Gake ɖewo nyemate ŋu asi ade toa dzi, hafi gbegblẽ la nava tum, eye maku o mahi.

eng: Because of the incident with Abrahams wife Sarah, the LORD had kept all of the women in Abimelechs household from having children.
local: Elabena Yehowa xe nyɔnu, siwo katã le Abimelex ƒe me la ƒe vidzidɔ le Sara, Abraham srɔ̃ la ta.

eng: Abraham approached and said, Will you really sweep away the innocent with the guilty?.
local: Eye Abraham te ɖe eŋu gblɔ bena: Ɖe atsrɔ̃ ame dzɔdzɔewo kple ame vɔ̃ɖiwo siaa mahã?.

eng: He had the camels kneel down outside the city at the well in the evening, when women come out to draw water.
local: Le fiẽ, le nyɔnuwo ƒe tɔmedeɣi la, ena kposɔawo tsyɔ akɔ anyi ɖe dua xa le ʋudoa ɖe to.

eng: God heard the boys cries, and Gods messenger called to Hagar from heaven and said to her, Hagar! Whats wrong? Dont be afraid. God has heard the boys cries over there.
local: Ke Mawu se ɖevia ƒe avifafa, eye mawudɔla yɔ Hagar tso dziƒo gblɔ nɛ bena: Hagar, nuka le wɔwòm? Megavɔ̃ o, elabena Mawu se ɖevi la ƒe gbe tso afisi wòle.

eng: Abraham bowed before the local citizens.
local: Tete Abraham bɔbɔ na duametɔwo.

eng: and said, I give my word as the LORD that because you did this and didnt hold back your son, your only son.
local: hegblɔ bena: Mefa ɖokuinye, elabena esi nèwɔ nu sia, eye mete wò tenuvi ɖeka lam o la.

eng: This city here is close enough to flee to, and its small. Its small, right? Let me escape there, and my life will be saved.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, dua ɖe nye kem tsɔ ɖe mía gbɔ, ne masi ayi ɖe afima. Du suea ɖe ko wònye; eyata na mɔm, nemasi ayi ɖe eme, bena matsi agbe! Alo menye du suea ɖe ko wònye oa?.

eng: and his son Ishmael was years old when the flesh of his foreskin was circumcised.
local: eye via Ismael xɔ ƒe wuietɔ̃ hafi wotso aʋa nɛ.

eng: What if there are fifty innocent people in the city? Will you really sweep it away and not save the place for the sake of the fifty innocent people in it?.
local: Ɖewohi ame dzɔdzɔe blaatɔ̃ anɔ dua me; ɖe àtsrɔ̃e, eye màtsɔe ake teƒe la le ame dzɔdzɔe blaatɔ̃, siwo le eme la ta o mahã?.

eng: He said, LORD, God of my master Abraham, make something good happen for me today and be loyal to my master Abraham.
local: Eye wòdo gbe ɖa gblɔ bena: Yehowa, ye aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe Mawu! Meɖe kuku, na wòadze edzi nam egbea, eye nave nye aƒetɔ Abraham nu!.

eng: Get up, pick up the boy, and take him by the hand because I will make of him a great nation.
local: Tsi tre, yi ɖakɔ ɖevi la, eye nalée ɖe asi, elabena mana woazu dukɔ gãa ɖe.

eng: and spoke to Ephron publicly in the presence of the local citizens: If only you would accept my offer. I will give you the price of the field. Take it from me so that I can bury my dead there.
local: eye wòɖo ŋu na Efron bena: Mi duameviwo misee! Maxe fe ɖe agble la ta; meɖe kuku na wò, xɔ ga le asinye, be mate ŋu aɖi nye ame kukua ɖe afima!.

eng: I will bless you richly and I will give you countless descendants, as many as the stars in the sky and as the grains of sand on the seashore. They will conquer their enemies cities.
local: eyata mayra wò geɖe ŋutɔ, eye viwòwo asɔ gbɔ fũ abe dziƒoɣletiviwo kple ƒutake ene, eye wò dzidzimeviwo atsɔ woƒe futɔwo ƒe duwo anyi domee.

eng: He said to Lot, Ill do this for you as well; I wont overthrow the city that you have described.
local: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Le nya sia me hã meɖo to wò, eye nyemagblẽ du, si nẽwɔnɛ la o.

eng: That same day Abraham and his son Ishmael were circumcised.
local: Gbe ɖeka ma ke wotso aʋa na Abraham kple via Ismael.

eng: Its not like you to do this, killing the innocent with the guilty as if there were no difference. Its not like you! Will the judge of all the earth not act justly.
local: Esia nede megbe xaa tso gbɔwò be, nagawu ame dzɔdzɔe kple ame vɔ̃ɖi siaa zi ɖeka, eye ame dzɔdzɔe nanɔ abe ame vɔ̃ɖi ene o! Esia nede megbe xaa tso gbɔwò! Ɖe xexeame ƒe ʋɔnudrɔ̃la madrɔ̃ ʋɔnu dzɔdzɔe oa?.

eng: I will stand here by the spring while the daughters of the men of the city come out to draw water.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, mele tsitre ɖe vudoa to, eye ne dua me nyɔnuviwo va tsi du ge.

eng: Ephron responded to Abraham.
local: Efron ɖo ŋu na Abraham bena:.

eng: All the nations of the earth will be blessed because of your descendants, because you obeyed me.
local: To wò dzidzimeviwo me woayra anyigba dzi dukɔwo katã le, elabena èɖo to nye gbe.

eng: Hurry! Escape to it! I cant do anything until you get there. That is why the name of the city is Zoar.
local: Ɖe abla yi afima kaba; elabena nyemate ŋu awɔ naneke o, vaseɖe esime naɖo afima. Eyata woyɔa ɖu la be Zoar.

eng: All the men of his household, those born in his household and those purchased with silver from foreigners, were circumcised with him.
local: Eye eƒe me ŋutsuwo katã, amesiwo wodzi le eƒe me, kple dzrovi, siwo woƒle kple ga la, wotso aʋa kpe ɖe eŋu.

eng: Then God opened her eyes, and she saw a well. She went over, filled the water flask, and gave the boy a drink.
local: Tete Mawu ʋu eƒe ŋkuwo, eye wòkpɔ tsivudoa ɖe. Enumake woyi ɖaku tsi ɖe lãgbalẽgolo la me taŋ, eye wòna ɖevia wono.

eng: The LORD said, If I find fifty innocent people in the city of Sodom, I will save it because of them.
local: Yehowa ɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Ne makpɔ ame dzɔdzɔe blaatɔ̃ le Sodom-dua me la, ekema màtsɔe ake teƒe blibo la le woawo la.

eng: When I say to a young woman, Hand me your water jar so I can drink, and she says to me, Drink, and I will give your camels water too, may she be the one youve selected for your servant Isaac. In this way I will know that youve been loyal to my master.
local: eye ava me be, nyɔnuvia ɖe ava, amesi magblɔ na bena: Na tsim mano, eye woaɖo ŋu nam be: Noe, eye mana wò kposɔwo hã nano lã, ekema eyae nye nyɔnu, si nèɖo ɖi na wò ɖɔla Isak, eye eya me madze sii le be, eve nye aƒetɔ nu!.

eng: Sir, what is four hundred shekels of silver between me and you for the land so that you can bury your dead.
local: Amegã, se nye gbe! Anyigba, si ke xɔ klosalo sekel alafa ene la, nuka wònye le mía kpli wò domee? Ɖi wò ame kukua ko.

eng: After Abraham returned to the young men, they got up and went to Beer-sheba where Abraham lived.
local: Emegbe Abraham gatrɔ va eƒe dɔlawo gbɔ, eye wodze mɔ yi Beer-Seba, eye Abraham nɔ Beer-Seba.

eng: As the sun rose over the earth, Lot arrived in Zoar.
local: Eye ɣe dze, hafi Lot de Zoar.

eng: God remained with the boy; he grew up, lived in the desert, and became an expert archer.
local: Eye Mawu nɔ kple ɖevi la, eye wòtsi, eye wònɔ gbe dzi hezu aŋutrɔdala.

eng: Abraham responded, Since Ive already decided to speak with my Lord, even though Im just soil and ash.
local: Abraham xɔ dzi gblɔ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, nye amesi nye anyi kple afi ko la, meɖo dzi be, maƒo nu kpli wò, Aƒetɔ!.

eng: Even before he finished speaking, Rebekah—daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah wife of Nahor, Abrahams brother—was coming out with a water jar on her shoulder.
local: Ke esi mekpɔ ɖe nu le nya la me o la, kpɔ ɖa, Abraham nɔvi Naxor srɔ̃ Milka vi, Betuel ƒe vinyɔnuvi Rebeka lé ze ɖe abɔta va tsi du ge.

eng: Abraham accepted Ephrons offer and weighed out for Ephron the silver he requested publicly before the Hittites: four hundred shekels of silver at the current rate of exchange.
local: Abraham ɖo to Efron, eye Abraham xlẽ ho, si Efron yɔ le Xitiviawo ŋku me la ne, klosalo sekel alafa ene, ga nyui, si asitsalawo xɔna.

eng: After these events, Abraham was told: Milcah has now also given birth to sons for your brother Nahor.
local: Le nya siawo megbe la Abraham se bena, Milka hã dzi ŋutsuviwo na nɔvia Naxor.

eng: and the LORD rained down burning asphalt from the skies onto Sodom and Gomorrah.
local: Eye Yehowa na aŋɔ kple dzo dza tso dziƒo tso Yehowa gbɔ ɖe Sodom kple Gomora dzi.

eng: He lived in the Paran desert, and his mother found him an Egyptian wife.
local: Eye wònɔ Paran-gbe dzi, eye dada ɖe srɔ̃ nɛ tso Egipte-nyigba dzi.

eng: what if there are five fewer innocent people than fifty? Will you destroy the whole city over just five? The LORD said, If I find forty-five there, I wont destroy it.
local: Ɖewohi ame atɔ ato le ame dzɔdzɔe blaatɔ̃ me; le ame atɔ ta la, ɖe atsrɔ̃ ɖu blibo la katã mahã? Eɖo ŋu be: O, ne makpɔ ame blane vɔ atɔ̃ le eme la, nyematsɔe o.

eng: The young woman was very beautiful, old enough to be married, and hadnt known a man intimately. She went down to the spring, filled her water jar, and came back up.
local: Eye nyɔnuvi la dze tugbe ŋutɔ, ɖetugbi, si ŋutsua ɖeke mede asi eŋu kpɔ o la wònye, eye wòyi ɖe vudoa me ɖaku tsi ɖe eƒe ze la me, eye wògado go.

eng: So the field of Ephron in Machpelah near Mamre—the field and the cave in it, and all the trees within the fields boundaries—was officially transferred.
local: Ale Efron ƒe agble, si le Maxpela le Mamre kasa, agble la kple kpeto, si le eme, kple ati, siwo katã le agblea me godoo la.

eng: They are Uz his oldest son, Buz his brother, Kemuel the father of Aram.
local: Woƒe ŋkɔwo nye: Uz, eƒe ŋgɔgbevi, kple nɔvia Bus, Kemuel, amesi me Aramtɔwo dzɔ tso.

eng: The LORD destroyed these cities, the entire valley, everyone who lived in the cities, and all of the fertile lands vegetation.
local: Eye wotsrɔ̃ du siawo kple nuto ma me katã; dua me nɔlawo tsrɔ̃, eye nusiwo miena ɖe anyigba dzi hã gblẽ.

eng: At that time Abimelech, and Phicol commander of his forces, said to Abraham, God is with you in everything that you do.
local: Le ɣemaɣi me la Abimelex kple eƒe aʋafia Pixol wogblɔ na Abraham bena: Mawu li kpli wò le nusianu, si nèwɔna la me.

eng: Once again Abraham spoke, What if forty are there? The LORD said, For the sake of forty, I will do nothing.
local: Eye wògayi dzi ƒo nu kplii gblɔ bena: Ɖewohĩ woakpɔ ame blane le eme. Eɖo ŋu be: Le ame blane ta nyemawòe o.

eng: The servant ran to meet her and said, Give me a little sip of water from your jar.
local: Tete dɔla la ƒu du yi ɖakpee gblɔ bena: Meɖe kuku na wò, na tsi via ɖem mano!.

eng: to Abraham as his property in the presence of the Hittites and of everyone at his citys gate.
local: zu Abraham tɔ le Xitiviawo kple amesiwo katã va dua me la ŋku me.

eng: Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel.
local: Kesed, Haso, Pildas, Yidlaf kple Betuel.

eng: When Lots wife looked back, she turned into a pillar of salt.
local: Ke srɔ̃a nye kɔ kpɔ megbe, eye wòzu dzekɔ.

eng: So give me your word under God that you wont cheat me, my children, or my descendants. Just as I have treated you fairly, so you must treat me and the land in which you are an immigrant.
local: Azɔ ka atam le Mawu ƒe ŋku me le afisia be, nagable nye ŋutɔ, vinyewo kple nye dzidzimeviwo o! Abe alesi meve nu wòe ene la, nenema ke wò hã nawɔ nam kple anyigba, si dzi nènye amedzro le la!.

eng: He said, Dont be angry with me, my Lord, but let me speak. What if thirty are there? The LORD said, I wont do it if I find thirty there.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Aƒetɔ, megabi dzi be, megale nu ƒo ge o loo! Ɖewohi woakpɔ ame blaetɔ̃ le eme. Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Ne makpɔ ame blaetɔ̃ le eme la, nyemawɔe o.

eng: She said, Drink, sir. Then she quickly lowered the water jar with her hands and gave him some water to drink.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Amegã, anoe. Enumake wòdro ze la ɖi, eye wònae wono.

eng: After this, Abraham buried his wife Sarah in the cave in the field of Machpelah near Mamre, that is, Hebron, in the land of Canaan.
local: Esia megbe la Abraham ɖi srɔ̃a Sara ɖe kpeto, si le Maxpela-gblea me la me le Mamre kasa; esia enye Hebron le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

eng: Bethuel became the father of Rebekah. These are the eight Milcah bore for Nahor, Abrahams brother.
local: Eye Betuel dzi Rebeka. Ame enyi siawo Milka dzi na Naxor, Abraham nɔvi.

eng: Abraham set out early for the place where he had stood with the LORD.
local: Eye Abraham fɔ ŋdi kanya yi ɖe teƒe, si wòtsi tre ɖe Yehowa ƒe ŋku me le tsã la.

eng: Abraham said, I give you my word.
local: Eye Abraham gblɔ bena: Melɔ̃, maka atam la.

eng: Abraham said, Since Ive already decided to speak with my Lord, what if twenty are there? The LORD said, I wont do it, for the sake of twenty.
local: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, medo dzi be, maƒo nu kpli wò Aƒetɔ. Ɖewohi woakpɔ ame blave le eme. Eɖo ŋu nɛ be: Le ame blave ta nyematsrɔ̃ wo o.

eng: When she finished giving him a drink, she said, Ill draw some water for your camels too, till theyve had enough to drink.
local: Esi wòɖi kɔ la, nyɔnuvi la gblɔ nɛ bena: Madui na wò kposɔwo hã, vaseɖe esime woaɖi kɔ na wo.

eng: The field and the cave in it were officially transferred from the Hittites to Abraham as his burial property.
local: Ale Xitiviwo ɖe asi le agble la kple kpeto, si le eme la ŋuti na Abraham, be wòanye ameɖiƒe nɛ koŋ.

eng: His secondary wifes name was Reumah, and she gave birth to Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
local: Eye srɔ̃ evelia ŋkɔ nye Rehuma, eya hã dzi Tebax, Gaham, Tahas kple Maxa nɛ.

eng: and looked out over Sodom and Gomorrah and over all the land of the valley. He saw the smoke from the land rise like the smoke from a kiln.
local: Eye esi wòkpɔ Sodom kple Gomora kple afima nutowo katã me le abu me ɖa la, kpo ɖa, dzudzɔ gãa ɖe le dzi dem tso anyigba la dzi abe aŋɔɖaƒe ene.

eng: Then Abraham complained to Abimelech about a well that Abimelechs servants had seized.
local: Tete Abraham ƒo nu kple Abimelex le tsivudo, si eƒe dɔlawo xɔ sesẽe la ŋuti.

eng: Abraham said, Dont be angry with me, my Lord, but let me speak just once more. What if there are ten? And the LORD said, I will not destroy it because of those ten.
local: Eye wògblɔ be: Aƒetɔ, megabi dzi o loo, ne magaƒo nu zi ɖeka pɛ ko. Ɖewohĩ woakpɔ ame ewo le eme. Eɖo ŋu nɛ be: Le ame ewo ta nyematsrɔ̃ wo o.

eng: She emptied her water jar quickly into the watering trough, ran to the well again to draw water, and drew water for all of the camels.
local: Enumake wòkɔ tsia ɖe lãto la me, eye wògaƒu du yi vudoa me ɖadu tsi la, eye woɖu tsi na eƒe kposɔwo katã.

eng: When God destroyed the cities in the valley, God remembered Abraham and sent Lot away from the disaster that overtook the cities in which Lot had lived.
local: Esi Mawu tsrɔ̃ nuto ma me duawo la, eɖo ŋku Abraham dzi, eye wòɖe Lot hekplɔe tso ɖu, siwo me Lot nɔ la ƒe gbegblẽ me.

eng: Abimelech said, I dont know who has done this, and you didnt tell me. I didnt even hear about it until today.
local: Abimelex xɔ edzi ne bena: Nyemenya amesi wɔ nu sia o, eye wò hã mègblɔ nyaa ɖeke le eŋuti nam kpɔ o, egbe ko hafi nye hã mese nyaa ɖe le eŋu.

eng: When the LORD finished speaking with Abraham, he left; but Abraham stayed there in that place.
local: Ke esi Yehowa wu nuƒoƒo kple Abraham nu la, edzo; eye Abraham hã trɔ yi aƒe.

eng: The man stood gazing at her, wondering silently if the LORD had made his trip successful or not.
local: Ke ŋutsu la zi kpi le ekpɔm dũ, ne yeadze sii be, ɖe Yehowa na yeƒe mɔzɔzɔ dze dzi loo, alo medze edzi o.

eng: Since Lot had become fearful of living in Zoar, he and his two daughters headed up from Zoar and settled in the mountains where he and his two daughters lived in a cave.
local: Eye Lot kple vianyɔnu eveawo dzo le Zoar yi ɖanɔ to la dzi, elabena ele Zoar nɔnɔ vɔ̃m, eye woa kple vianyɔnu eveawo va no agadoa ɖe me.

eng: Abraham took flocks and cattle, gave them to Abimelech, and the two of them drew up a treaty.
local: Tete Abraham tsɔ alẽwo kple nyiwo na Abimelex, eye wo kple eve wòbia nu.

eng: As soon as the camels had finished drinking, the man took out a gold ring, weighing a half shekel, and two gold bracelets for her arms, weighing ten shekels.
local: Esi kposɔwo ɖi kɔ la, ŋutsu la tsɔ sikaŋɔtigɛ, si da sekel afã, kple alɔnugɛ eve, siwo da sekel ewo la, do nɛ.

eng: The older daughter said to the younger, Our father is old, and there are no men in the land to sleep with us as is the custom everywhere.
local: Eye tsitsitɔ gblɔ na ɖevitɔ bena: Mía fofo tsi, eye ŋutsua ɖeke megale anyigba dzi aɖe mí le amegbetɔwo ƒe kɔ la nu o.

eng: Abraham set aside, by themselves, seven female lambs from the flock.
local: Ke Abraham ɖe alẽvi adre ɖo vovo.

eng: He said, Please tell me whose daughter you are. Is there room in your fathers house for us to spend the night.
local: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Ameka vi nènyea? Meɖe kuku, gblɔe nam, ɖe dzeƒe le fofowò ƒe mea?.

eng: Come on, lets give our father wine to drink, lie down with him, and well have children from our father.
local: Va míana wein mia fofo wòano, eye míadɔ egbɔ, ne wòado fu mi míadzi vi.

eng: So Abimelech said to Abraham, What are these seven lambs youve set apart.
local: Eye Abimelex bia Abraham be: Alẽvi adre, siwo neɖo vovo la gome ɖe?.

eng: She responded, Im the daughter of Bethuel, who is the son of Milcah and Nahor.
local: Eɖo ŋu ne bena: Betuel, Milka vi, si wòdzi na Naxor la vinyɔnu menye.

eng: That night they served their father wine, and the older daughter went in and lay down with her father, without him noticing when she lay down or got up.
local: Tete wona wein wo fofo le zã ma me wono, eye tsitsitɔ yi ɖamlɔ fofoa gbɔ; ke eya menya naneke le eƒe egbɔ mɔmlɔ kple fɔfɔdzo ŋuti o.

eng: Abraham said, These seven lambs that you take from me will attest that I dug this well.
local: Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Alẽvi adre siawo naxa le asinye, ne wòanye ɖase nam bena, nyee ɖe vudo sia.

eng: She continued, We have plenty of straw and feed for the camels, and a place to spend the night.
local: Eye wògagblɔ nɛ bena: Aƒla kple gbe le mia si lu, eye dzeƒe hã li.

eng: Therefore, the name of that place is Beer-sheba because there they gave each other their word.
local: Eyata woyɔ teƒe ma bena: Beer-Seba, elabena afima wo ame eve la woka atam na wo nɔewo le.

eng: The next day the older daughter said to the younger, Since I lay down with our father last night, lets serve him wine tonight too, and you go in and lie down with him so that we will both have children from our father.
local: Esi ŋu ke la, tsitsitɔ la gblɔ na ɖevitɔ bena: Eto ƒe zã la memlɔ fofonye gbɔ. Míagana wòano wein egbe ƒe zã hã; ekema wò hã nayi aɖamlɔ egbɔ, bena mía fofo nado fu mí míadzi vi.

eng: The man bowed down and praised the LORD.
local: Eye ŋutsu la dze klo na Yehowa hetsyɔ mo anyi.

eng: After they drew up a treaty at Beer-sheba, Abimelech, and Phicol commander of his forces, returned to the land of the Philistines.
local: Eye esi wòbia nu le Beer-Seba la, Abimelex kple eƒe aʋafia Pixol wodze mɔ gatrɔ yi ɖe Filistitowo ƒe anyigba dzi.

eng: They served their father wine that night also, and the younger daughter lay down with him, without him knowing when she lay down or got up.
local: Eye le zã sia me hã wona wein wo fofo wono, eye ɖevitɔ hã yi ɖamlɔ egbɔ; ke eya menya naneke le eƒe egbɔ mɔmlɔ kple fɔfɔdzo ŋuti o.

eng: Bless the LORD, God of my master Abraham, who hasnt given up his loyalty and his faithfulness to my master. The LORD has shown me the way to the household of my masters brother.
local: gblɔ bena: Woakafu Yehowa, nye aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe Mawu, amesi meŋlɔ nye aƒetɔ be le enu veve kple nuteƒewɔwɔ nɛ me o la. Elabena Yehowa fia mɔm, eye wòkplɔm va nye aƒetɔ nɔviwo gbɔe.

eng: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beer-sheba, and he worshipped there in the name of the LORD, El Olam.
local: Eye Abraham do atiwo ɖe Beer-Seba, eye wòyɔ Yehowa Mawu, si le agbe tegbee la ƒe ŋkɔ dzi le afima.

eng: Both of Lots daughters became pregnant by their father.
local: Ale wɔ Lot ɖo fu vianyɔnu eveawo.

eng: The young woman ran and told her mothers household everything that had happened.
local: Ke nyɔnuvi la ƒu du yi aƒe me, be woaɖagblɔ nya siawo na dadaa.

eng: Abraham lived as an immigrant in the Philistines land for a long time.
local: Ke Abraham nye amedzro le Filistitowo ƒe anyigba dzi eteƒe didi.

eng: The older daughter gave birth to a son and named him Moab. He is the ancestor of todays Moabites.
local: Eye tsitsiwo dzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòtsɔ ŋkɔ nɛ bena Moab; eya nye egbegbe Moabitɔwo tɔgbui.

eng: Rebekah had a brother named Laban, and Laban ran to the man outside by the spring.
local: Ke nɔviŋutsu le Rebeka si, eŋkɔ nye Laban. Esi wòkpɔ ŋɔ tigɛ kple alɔnugɛ le nɔvia ŋu eye wose Rebeka ƒe nya bena ale kple ale ŋutsua gblɔ nam enye si la.

eng: The younger daughter also gave birth to a son and named him Ben-ammi. He is the ancestor of todays Ammonites.
local: Eye ɖevitɔ hã dzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòtsɔ ŋkɔ ne be Ben-Ami, eya nye Amonitɔwo tɔgbui.
