eng: When he had seen the ring and the bracelets on his sisters arms, and when he had heard his sister Rebekah say, This is what the man said to me, he went to the man, who was still standing by the spring with his camels.
local: Laban ƒu du yi ŋutsua, si gale kposɔwo gbɔ le vudoa to ko la gbɔ.

eng: Laban said, Come in, favored one of the LORD! Why are you standing outside? Ive prepared the house and a place for the camels.
local: Tete wògblɔ bena: Va, wò amesi Yehowa yra! Nukaŋuti nètsi dugbɔ dzi? Medzra xɔ me ɖo, eye teƒe hã li na kposɔwo.

eng: So the man entered the house. Then Laban unbridled the camels, provided straw and feed for them and water to wash his feet and the feet of the men with him.
local: Eye esi wòkplɔ ŋutsua va aƒe me la, eɖe akpa na kposɔawo, eye wòtsɔ aƒla kple gbe ve na kposɔawo; emegbe la eku tsi vɛ na ŋutsua kple amesiwo katã kpe ɖe eŋuti la, bena woaklɔ woƒe afɔwo.

eng: and set out a meal for him. But the man said, I wont eat until Ive said something. Laban replied, Say it.
local: Ke esi wotsɔ nuɖuɖu ɖo ekɔme la, egblɔ bena: Nyemaɖu nu haɖe o, negbe ɖeko magblɔ nya, si mehe vɛ la hafi! Woɖo ŋu be: Gblɔe faa!.

eng: The man said, I am Abrahams servant.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Abraham ƒe dɔla menye.

eng: The LORD has richly blessed my master, has made him a great man, and has given him flocks, cattle, silver, gold, men servants, women servants, camels, and donkeys.
local: Yehowa yra nye aƒetɔ wòkpɔ nu ŋutɔ, eye wòna alẽwo kple nyiwo, klosalo kple sika, kluviwo kple kosiviwo, kpo-sɔwo kple tedziwoe.

eng: My masters wife Sarah gave birth to a son for my master in her old age, and hes given him everything he owns.
local: Eye nye aƒetɔ srɔ̃, Sara, dzi ŋutsuvi nanye aƒetɔ, esime woku nyagã, eye nye aƒetɔ tsɔ eƒe kesinɔnuwo katã na via.

eng: My master made me give him my word: Dont choose a wife for my son from the Canaanite women, in whose land Im living.
local: Eye nye aƒetɔ gblɔ nam, eye wòna meka atam ɖe dzi nɛ bena: Megaɖe srɔ̃ na vinye tso Kanaantɔ, siwo ƒe anyigba dzi mele la ƒe nyɔnuviwo dome o;.

eng: No, instead, go to my fathers household and to my relatives and choose a wife for my son.
local: ke boŋ yi ɖe fofonye ƒe me, ɖe ƒonyemeawo gbɔ, eye naɖe srɔ̃ na vinye le afima!.

eng: I said to my master, What if the woman wont come back with me.
local: Eye megblɔ na nye aƒetɔ bena: Ne nyɔnu la melɔ̃ be, yeakplɔm ɖo o ɖe?.

eng: He said to me, The LORD, whom Ive traveled with everywhere, will send his messenger with you and make your trip successful; and you will choose a wife for my son from my relatives and from my fathers household.
local: Eɖo ŋu nam bena: Yehowa, amesi ƒe ŋku me zɔm mele la, adɔ eƒe dɔla akpe ɖe ŋuwò, bena wò mɔzɔzɔ la nadze edzi na wò, eye naɖe srɔ̃ na vinye atso ƒonyemeawo gbɔ le fofonye ƒe me.

eng: If you go to my relatives, you will be free from your obligation to me. Even if they provide no one for you, you will be free from your obligation to me.
local: Ne ava ƒonyemeawo gbɔ, eye womelɔ̃ be, woatsɔe na wò o la, ekema menye wòe tu nye atam la o.

eng: Today I arrived at the spring, and I said, LORD, God of my master Abraham, if you wish to make the trip Im taking successful.
local: Esi meva ɖo vudoa to egbea la, medo gbe ɖagblɔ bena: Yehowa, wò nye aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe Mawu! Ne ana nye azɔli, si dzi mele, nadze edzi nam la, — kpɔ ɖa, mele tsitre ɖe vudoa to —.

eng: when Im standing by the spring and the young woman who comes out to draw water and to whom I say, Please give me a little drink of water from your jar.
local: ekema na bena, nyɔnuvi, si ava tsi ɖu ge, eye magblɔ nɛ bena: Na tsi via ɖem mano.

eng: and she responds to me, Drink, and I will draw water for your camels too, may she be the woman the LORD has selected for my masters son.
local: eye woaɖo ŋu nam be: Noe, eye mana wò kposɔwo hã la, eya nanye nyɔnu, si Yehowa ɖo ɖi na nye aƒetɔ ƒe vi la!.

eng: Before I finished saying this to myself, Rebekah came out with her water jar on her shoulder and went down to the spring to draw water. And I said to her, Please give me something to drink.
local: Esi nyemekpɔ ɖe nu le nya la me o la, tete Rebeka lé ze ɖe ta vɛ, eye wòyi ɖe vudo la me ɖaku tsi. Eye megblɔ ne bena. Meɖe kuku, na tsim mano!.

eng: She immediately lowered her water jar and said, Drink, and I will give your camels something to drink too. So I drank and she also gave water to the camels.
local: Enumake wòdro eƒe ze la ɖi gblɔ bena: Noe, eye mana wò kposɔwo hã. Esi menoe vɔ la, ena kposɔwo hã wono.

eng: Then I asked her, Whose daughter are you? And she said, The daughter of Bethuel, Nahors son whom Milcah bore him. I put a ring in her nose and bracelets on her arms.
local: Emegbe mebiae bena: Ameka vi nènyea? Eɖo ŋu bena: Betuel, amesi Milka dzi na Naxor ƒe vi menye, eye metsɔ ŋɔtigɛ kple alɔnugɛ ɖo ne.

eng: I bowed and worshipped the LORD and blessed the LORD, the God of my master Abraham, who led me in the right direction to choose the granddaughter of my masters brother for his son.
local: Tete medze klo na Yehowa, eye metsyɔ mo anyi hekafu Yehowa, nye aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe Mawu, si kplɔm nyuie ale, be maɖe nye aƒetɔ nɔvi ƒe vinyɔnu na ye aƒetɔ ƒe vi la.

eng: Now if youre loyal and faithful to my master, tell me. If not, tell me so I will know where I stand either way.
local: Azɔ, ne miedi be, miave nye aƒetɔ nu, eye miaɖi anukware nɛ la, ekema migblɔe nam; ke ne mielɔ̃ o la, migblɔe nam, bena nenye ɖusi me alo mia mee hã, ne maɖo tae.

eng: Laban and Bethuel both responded, This is all the LORDs doing. We have nothing to say about it.
local: Tete Laban kple Betuel wogblɔ bena: Nya sia tso Yehowa gbɔ, eyata míate ŋu agblɔ nya vɔ̃ alo nya nyui ɖe eŋu o.

eng: Here is Rebekah, right in front of you. Take her and go. She will be the wife of your masters son, just as the LORD said.
local: Kpo ɖa, Rebeka anye si, ele asiwò me, kplɔe yii, ne wòanye wò aƒetɔ vi la srɔ̃, abe alesi Yehowa gblɔe ene!.

eng: When Abrahams servant heard what they said, he bowed low before the LORD.
local: Ke esi Abraham ƒe dɔla se nya siawo la, etsyɔ mo anyi na Yehowa.

eng: The servant brought out gold and silver jewelry and clothing and gave them to Rebekah. To her brother and to her mother he gave the finest gifts.
local: Eye dɔla la ɖe klosalo- kple sikanuwo kple avɔ veviwo na Rebeka, eye wotsɔ akpedanu xɔasiwo na nɔvia kple dadaa.

eng: He and the men with him ate and drank and spent the night. When they got up in the morning, the servant said, See me off to my master.
local: Esia megbe la eya kple ŋutsu, siwo kpe ɖe eŋu la, woɖu nu, eye wono nu, eye wotsi dɔ. Ke esi wofɔ ŋdi la, egblɔ bena: Mido mɔm, ne mayi nye aƒetɔ gbɔ!.

eng: Her brother and mother said, Let the young woman stay with us not more than ten days, and after that she may go.
local: Eye nɔvia kple dadaa gblɔ bena: Na nyɔnuvi la nanɔ mía gbɔ ʋee abe ŋkeke ewo ene, emegbe la ayi faa.

eng: But he said to them, Dont delay me. The LORD has made my trip successful. See me off so that I can go to my master.
local: Eɖo ŋu na wo bena: Migasikam ɖi o! Yehowa na nye mɔzɔzɔa dze edzi nam; eyata mido mɔm, ne mayi nye aƒetɔ gbɔ.

eng: They said, Summon the young woman, and lets ask her opinion.
local: Eye wogblɔ bena: Míayɔ nyɔnuvi la vɛ, bena míabia eya ŋutɔ ase.

eng: They called Rebekah and said to her, Will you go with this man? She said, I will go.
local: Tete woyɔ Rebeka vɛ, eye wobiae bena: ayi kple ŋutsu sia? Eɖo eŋu bena: E, mayi.

eng: So they sent off their sister Rebekah, her nurse, Abrahams servant, and his men.
local: Eye wodo mɔ wo nɔvi Rebeka kple amesi na noe le ɖevime la, kpe ɖe Abraham ƒe dɔla kple eŋumewo ŋuti.

eng: And they blessed Rebekah, saying to her, May you, our sister, become thousands of ten thousand; may your children possess their enemies cities.
local: Eye woyra Rebeka gblɔ nɛ bena: Wò, mía nɔvinyɔnu, nadzi ame akpe nanewo, eye viwòwo natsɔ woƒe futɔwo ƒe duwo anyi domee!.

eng: Rebekah and her young women got up, mounted the camels, and followed the man. So the servant took Rebekah and left.
local: Esia megbe Rebeka kple eƒe nyɔnudɔlawo ɖe kposɔwo dzi, eye wodze mɔ dze ŋutsu la yome. Ale ŋutsu la kplɔ Rebeka hedzoe.

eng: Now Isaac had come from the region of Beer-lahai-roi and had settled in the arid southern plain.
local: Ke Isak gbɔ tso Agbetɔ, si le kpɔyem la ƒe vudo me; elabena enɔ dziehenyigba dzi.

eng: One evening, Isaac went out to inspect the pasture, and while staring he saw camels approaching.
local: Gbe ɖeka Isak yi gbe me le fiẽ, be wòado gbe ɖa. Eye esi wòfɔ mo te la, ekpɔ kposɔwo gbɔna ɖa.

eng: Rebekah stared at Isaac. She got down from the camel.
local: Ke esi Rebeka fɔ kɔ dzi la, ekpɔ Isak ɖa; tete wòɖi le kposɔ la dzi.

eng: and said to the servant, Who is this man walking through the pasture to meet us? The servant said, Hes my master. So she took her headscarf and covered herself.
local: eye wòbia ɖɔla la bena: Ŋutsu ka enye kem le gbe me zɔm gbɔna mía kpe ge? Dɔla la ɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Nye aƒetɔe. Tete wòtsɔ motsyɔnu tsyɔ ŋku me.

eng: The servant told Isaac everything that had happened.
local: Esia megbe la dɔla la xlẽ nusiwo katã wòwɔ la fia Isak.

eng: Isaac brought Rebekah into his mother Sarahs tent. He received Rebekah as his wife and loved her. So Isaac found comfort after his mothers death.
local: Eye Isak kplɔe yi ɖe dada Sara ƒe agbadɔ me, eye wòɖe Rebeka wòzu srɔ̃a, eye wòlɔnɛ ŋutɔ. Ale Isak ƒe ake fa le dada ƒe ku ŋuti.

eng: Abraham married another wife, named Keturah.
local: Eye Abraham gaɖe srɔ̃, si ŋkɔ nye Ketura.

eng: The children she bore him were Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
local: Eya dzi Simran, Yoksan, Medan, Midian, Yisbak kple Suax nɛ.

eng: Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan. Dedans sons were Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
local: Yoksan dzi Seba kple Dedan; eye Asuritɔwo, Letusitɔwo kple Leumitɔwo dzɔ tso Dedan me.

eng: When a famine gripped the land, a different one from the first famine that occurred in Abrahams time, Isaac set out toward Gerar and toward King Abimelech of the Philistines.
local: Ke do to le anyigba la dzi, abe alesi wòto le Abraham ŋɔli ene; tete Isak ʋu yi ɖe Filistitowo ƒe fia Abimelex gbɔ le Gerar.

eng: Midians sons were Ephah, Epher, Enoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All of these were Keturahs sons.
local: Eye Midianviwo nye: Efa, Efer, Henox, Abida kple Edaa. Ame siawo katã dzɔ tso Ketura me.

eng: The LORD appeared to him and said, Dont go down to Egypt but settle temporarily in the land that I will show you.
local: Eye Yehowa ɖe eɖokui fiae gblɔ bena: Megayi Egipte o, ke boŋ na anyigba, si mafia wò la dzi!.

eng: Abraham gave everything he owned to Isaac.
local: Eye Abraham tsɔ eƒe nuwo katã na Isak.

eng: Stay in this land as an immigrant, and I will be with you and bless you because I will give all of these lands to you and your descendants. I will keep my word, which I gave to your father Abraham.
local: Nye amedzro le anyigba sia dzi, eye manɔ anyi kpli wò, eye mayra wò. Elabena matsɔ anyigba blibo la katã ana mia kple wò dzidzimeviwo, eye mawɔ atam, si meka na fofowò Abraham la dzi.

eng: To the sons of Abrahams secondary wives, Abraham gave gifts and, while he was still living, sent them away from his son Isaac to land in the east.
local: Ke Abraham na nu srɔ̃ bubuawo ƒe viwo heɖe wo ɖa le Isak ŋu, eye wòɖo wo ɖe ɣedzeƒenyigba dzi le eya ŋutɔ ƒe agbenɔɣi me.

eng: I will give you as many descendants as the stars in the sky, and I will give your descendants all of these lands. All of the nations of the earth will be blessed because of your descendants.
local: Mana wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ abe dziƒo ɣletiviwo ene, eye matsɔ anyigba sia katã ana wò dzidzimeviwo, eye le wò dzidzimeviwo me woayra anyigba dzi dukɔwo katã le.

eng: Abraham lived to the age of .
local: Eye ƒe, siwo katã Abraham nɔ agbe la, wonye ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blaadre vɔ atɔ̃.

eng: I will do this because Abraham obeyed me and kept my orders, my commandments, my statutes, and my instructions.
local: Elabena Abraham ɖo to nye gbe, eye wòwɔ nye se, nye ɖoɖo kple nye mɔnufiame, siwo megblɔ nɛ la dzi.

eng: Abraham took his last breath and died after a good long life, a content old man, and he was placed with his ancestors.
local: Ke Abraham ku amegã ɖeɖi, eye agbenɔnɔ tii hafi wòku, eye wòyi ɖe tɔgbuiawo gbɔ.

eng: So Isaac lived in Gerar.
local: Eye Isak nɔ Gerar.

eng: His sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him in the cave in Machpelah, which is in the field of Zohars son Ephron the Hittite, near Mamre.
local: Eye via Isak kple Ismael woɖii ɖe Maxpela-kpeto la me, le Xititɔ Efron, Zoxarvi ƒe agble, si le Mamre kasa la me.

eng: So Isaac summoned Jacob, blessed him, and gave him these orders: Dont marry a Canaanite woman.
local: Emegbe la Isak yɔ Yakob vɛ, eye wòyrae, eye wòde se nɛ gblɔ nɛ bena: Megaɖe srɔ̃ tso Kanaan-vinyɔnuwo dome o!.

eng: When Isaac had grown old and his eyesight was failing, he summoned his older son Esau and said to him, My son? And Esau said, Im here.
local: Esi Isak ku amegã ɖeɖi, eye eƒe ŋkuwo dzi tsyɔ, ale be megale nu kpɔm o la, eya via xoxoetɔ Esau gblɔ nɛ bena: Vinye! Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Nyee nye si.

eng: When the men who lived there asked about his wife, he said, Shes my sister, because he was afraid to say, my wife, thinking, The men who live there will kill me for Rebekah because shes very beautiful.
local: Ke esi afima ŋutsuwo bia srɔ̃a ta se la, egblɔ bena, ye nɔvinyɔnue. Elabena ele vɔvɔ̃m be, ne yeʋu me be, ye srɔ̃e la, ekema afima ŋutsuwo awu ye le ye srɔ̃ Rebeka ta; elabena edze tugbe ŋutɔ.

eng: Thus Abraham and his wife Sarah were both buried in the field Abraham had purchased from the Hittites.
local: Le agble, si Abraham ƒle le Xitiviwo si la me woɖi Abraham kple srɔ̃a Sara ɖo.

eng: Get up and go to Paddan-aram, to the household of Bethuel, your mothers father, and once there, marry one of the daughters of Laban, your mothers brother.
local: Yi Mesopotamia ɖe dawo fofo Betuel ƒe me, eye naɖe dawo nɔvi Laban ƒe vinyɔnua ɖe!.

eng: He said, Im old and dont know when I will die.
local: Tete wògblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, metsi, eye nyemenya nye kugbe o.

eng: After Isaac had lived there for some time, the Philistines King Abimelech looked out his window and saw Isaac laughing together with his wife Rebekah.
local: Eye esi wonɔ afima eteƒe didi la, gbe ɖeka Filistitɔwo ƒe fia Abimelex kpɔ, alesi Isak kple srɔ̃a Rebeka wole fefemi, to fesre nu.

eng: Jacob got to his feet and set out for the land of the easterners.
local: Tete Yakob dze mɔ, eye wòyi ɖe ɣetoɖoƒetɔwo ƒe anyigba dzi.

eng: After Abrahams death, God blessed his son Isaac, and Isaac lived in Beer-lahai-roi.
local: Le Abraham ƒe ku megbe la Mawu yra via Isak. Eye Isak nɔ „Agbetɔ, si le kpɔyem la“ ƒe vudo gbɔ.

eng: God Almighty will bless you, make you fertile, and give you many descendants so that you will become a large group of peoples.
local: Mawu ŋusẽkatãtɔ nayra wo, eye wòana nadzi asɔ gbɔ ɖe dzi, bena natrɔ azu dukɔ geɖe.

eng: So now, take your hunting gear, your bow and quiver of arrows, go out to the field, and hunt game for me.
local: Eyata tsɔ wò adedanuwo, ɖaku kple da, eye nayi gbe me aɖawu lãa ɖe vɛ nam!.

eng: So Abimelech summoned Isaac and said, Shes your wife, isnt she? How could you say, Shes my sister? Isaac responded, Because I thought that I might be killed because of her.
local: Tete Abimelex yɔ Isak hegblɔ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, srɔ̃wo wònye tso! Menye wòe gblɔ bena, ye nɔvinyɔnue oa? Isak ɖo ŋu nɛ bena; Elabena mele vɔvɔ̃m be, woawum le eta.

eng: He saw a well in the field in front of him, near which three flocks of sheep were lying down. That well was their source for water because the flocks drank from that well. A huge stone covered the wells opening.
local: Eye wòkpɔ vudoa de le gbea dzi, eye alẽha etɔ̃ wotsyɔ akɔ anyi ɖe afima; elabena vudo sia me wokua tsi lena na alẽhawo wonona, eye kpe gãa ɖe le vudo la nu.

eng: These are the descendants of Ishmael, Abrahams son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarahs servant, bore for Abraham.
local: Esiawo nye Abrahamvi Ismael, amesi Egipte-nyɔnu Hagar, Sara ƒe kosi, dzi na Abraham la ƒe dzidzimeviwo.

eng: He will give you and your descendants Abrahams blessing so that you will own the land in which you are now immigrants, the land God gave to Abraham.
local: Eye woatsɔ Abraham ƒe yayra ana mia kple wò dzidzimeviwo, bena nanyi anyigba, si Mawu tsɔ na Abraham, si dzi nènye amedzro le la dome.

eng: Make me the delicious food that I love and bring it to me so I can eat. Then I can bless you before I die.
local: Emegbe naɖa nu nyuia ɖe nam, abe alesi nyoa ŋunye ene, eye natsɔe vɛ nam, ne maɖu, be mayra wò hafi aku!.

eng: Abimelech said, What are you trying to do to us? Before long, one of the people would have slept with your wife; and you would have made us guilty.
local: Eye Abimelex gblɔ nɛ bena: Nuka enye si nèwɔ ɖe mía ŋu? Esusɔ vie vinyewo dometɔ ɖeka de asi srɔ̃wòa ŋu kloe, eye ahe dzɔgbevɔ̃e gãa ɖe ava mía dzi.

eng: When all of the flocks were gathered there, the shepherds would roll the stone from the wells opening, water the sheep, and return the stone to its place at the wells opening.
local: Eye ne alẽhawo katã va ƒo ƒu ɖe afima la, ekema womlia kpe la le vudoa nu, eye wonaa tsi alẽawo; emegbe wogamlia kpe la tua vudoa nu ɖe eteƒe.

eng: These are the names of Ishmaels sons, by their names and according to their birth order: Nebaioth, Ishmaels oldest son; Kedar; Adbeel; Mibsam.
local: Esiawo nye Ismaelviwo ƒe ŋkɔwo le woƒe ƒomeŋkɔwo nu. Nebayot, Ismael ƒe ŋgɔgbevi, Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam.

eng: So Isaac sent Jacob off, and he traveled to Paddan-aram, to Laban son of Bethuel the Aramean and brother of Rebekah, Jacob and Esaus mother.
local: Tete Isak do mɔ Yakob, eye wòyi ɖe Aramtɔ Betuel vi Laban, amesi enye Yakob kple Esau dada Rebeka nɔvi la gbɔ.

eng: Rebekah was listening when Isaac spoke to his son Esau. When Esau went out to the field to hunt game to bring back.
local: Eye Rebeka se nya, siwo Isak gblɔ na via Esau la. Ke esi Esau yi gbe me, be woawu lã atsɔ va aƒee la.

eng: Abimelech gave orders to all of the people, Anyone who touches this man or his wife will be put to death.
local: Tete Abimelex de se na eƒe dukɔ blibo la katã bena: Amesi ke awɔ nane ɖe ŋutsu sia kple srɔ̃a ŋu la, woawui.

eng: Jacob said to them, Where are you from, my brothers? They said, Were from Haran.
local: Yakob bia wo bena: Nɔvinyewo, afika mietso? Woɖo ŋu bena. Míetso Xaran!.

eng: Mishma; Dumah; Massa.
local: Misma, Duma, Masa.

eng: Esau understood that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him to Paddan-aram to marry a woman from there. He recognized that, when Isaac blessed Jacob, he had ordered him, Dont marry a Canaanite woman.
local: Ke esi Esau kpɔ bena, Isak yra Yakob, eye wòɖoe ɖe Mesopotamia, bena wòaɖe srɔ̃ le afima, eye wòyrae de se nɛ gblɔ bena: Megaɖe srɔ̃ tso Kanaan-vinyɔnuwo dome o.

eng: Rebekah said to her son Jacob, I just heard your father saying to your brother Esau.
local: Rebeka gblɔ na via Yakob be: Mese alesi fofowò gblɔ na noviwò Esau bena:.

eng: Isaac planted grain in that land and reaped one hundred shearim that year because the LORD had blessed him.
local: Eye Isak ƒã nu ɖe anyigba ma dzi eye wòŋe nu le ƒe ma me wòdzi ɖe dzi zi alafa ɖeka sɔŋ, elabena Yehowa yrae.

eng: Hadad; Tema; Jetur; Naphish; and Kedemah.
local: Xadad, Tema, Yetur, Nafis kple Kedma.

eng: Then he said to them, Do you know Laban, Nahors grandson? They said, We know him.
local: Egblɔ na wo bena: Mienyã Naxor vi Laban mahã? Woɖo ŋu bena: Ẽ, mienyae!.

eng: and that Jacob had listened to his father and mother and gone to Paddan-aram.
local: eye bena Yakob ɖo to fofoa kple dadaa, eye wòyi Mesopotamia la.

eng: Bring me some game and make me some delicious food so I can eat, and I will bless you in the LORDs presence before I die.
local: Di adelã, eye naɖa nu nyuia ɖe vɛ nam, ne maɖu, eye mayra wò le Yehowa ƒe ŋku me hafi aku.

eng: Isaac grew richer and richer until he was extremely wealthy.
local: Ale ŋutsu la kpɔ nu gakpɔ nu ɖe dzi, vaseɖe esime woɖo kpo le eme.

eng: He said to them, Is he well? They said, Hes fine. In fact, this is his daughter Rachel now, coming with the flock.
local: Egabia wo bena: Eli sesĩe mahã? Woɖo ŋu bena: Eli sesĩe! Kpɔ ɖa, via Raxel hã kplɔ alẽwo gbɔnae.

eng: These are Ishmaels sons. These are their names by their villages and their settlements: twelve tribal leaders according to their tribes.
local: Ame siawo nye Ismaelviwo kple woƒe ŋkɔwo. Fia wuieve le woƒe aƒetsotsowo, agbadɔwo kple woƒe dukɔwo nu.

eng: Esau realized that his father Isaac considered Canaanite women unacceptable.
local: Esau dze sii bena, Kanaan-vinyɔnuawo medze fofoa Isak ŋu o.

eng: Now, my son, listen to me, to what Im telling you to do.
local: Eyata vinye, se nye gbe, eye nawɔ nusi magblɔ na wò la!.

eng: He had livestock, both flocks and cattle, and many servants. As a result, the Philistines envied him.
local: Eye wòkpɔ alẽhawo kple nyihawo hekpe ɖe kluvi geɖewo ŋuti, ale bena Filistitowo ʋã ŋui.

eng: He said to them, Its now only the middle of the day. Its not time yet to gather the animals. Water the flock, and then go, put them out to pasture.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, ɣe gale dzi, eye womaƒo alẽwo nu ƒu haɖe o. Mina tsi alẽawo woano, emegbe miana woagaɖu gbe.

eng: Ishmael lived to the age of . He took his last breath and died, and was placed with his ancestors.
local: Ismael ƒe agbe me ŋkekewo nye ƒe alafa ɖeka kple blave vɔ adre, eye wòku, eye wòyi ɖe tɔgbuiawo gbɔ.

eng: So he went to Ishmael and married Mahalath daughter of Abrahams son Ishmael and sister of Nebaioth, in addition to his other wives.
local: Eyata Esau yi ɖe Ismael gbɔ, eye wòɖe Mahalat, Abraham vi Ismael ƒe vinyɔnu, si enye Nebayot nɔvinyɔnu, hekpe ɖe srɔ̃ bubuawo ŋu.

eng: Go to the flock and get me two healthy young goats so I can prepare them as the delicious food your father loves.
local: Yi lãhawo gbɔ, eye nalé gbɔ̃vi nyui eve vɛ nam, bena maɖa nu nyuia ɖe na fofowò, abe alesi nyoa eŋu ene.

eng: The Philistines closed up and filled with dirt all of the wells that his fathers servants had dug during his father Abrahams lifetime.
local: Vudo, siwo katã fofoa ƒe dɔlawo ɖe le fofoa Abraham ŋɔli la, Filistitɔwo yi ɖaxe wo, eye wole anyi ɖe wo me taŋ.

eng: They said to him, We cant until all the herds are gathered, and then we roll the stone away from the wells opening and water the flock.
local: Wogblɔ bena: Míale ŋui o, vaseɖe esime woaƒo alẽhawo katã nu ƒu; ekema míamli kpe la ɖa le vudoa nu, hafi miana tsi alẽawo woano.

eng: Jacob left Beer-sheba and set out for Haran.
local: Ke Yakob dze mɔ tso Beer-Seba heyina Xaran.

eng: He established camps from Havilah to Shur, which is near Egypt on the road to Assyria. He died among all of his brothers.
local: Eye woƒe nɔƒe tso Xawila vaseɖe Sur le Egipte ƒe ɣedzeƒe heɖo ta Asur ke; ale wɔ womimi wo nɔviwo katã ɖo. Esau kple Yakob.

eng: You can bring it to your father, he will eat, and then he will bless you before he dies.
local: Emegbe natsɔe ayi na fofowò, ne wòaɖu, eye wòayra wò hafi aku.

eng: When Rachel realized that she could bear Jacob no children, Rachel became jealous of her sister and said to Jacob, Give me children! If you dont, I may as well be dead.
local: Ke esi Raxel kpɔ bena, yemedzi via ɖeke na Yakob o la, Raxel ʋã ŋu nɔvianyɔnu la, eye wògblɔ na Yakob bena: Na madzi vi; ne menye nenem o la, maku!.

eng: Abimelech said to Isaac, Move away from us because you have become too powerful among us.
local: Eye Abimelex gblɔ na Isak bena: Dzo le mía gbɔ, elabena ekpɔ ŋusẽ ɖe mía dzi akpa!.

eng: While he was still talking to them, Rachel came with her fathers flock since she was its shepherd.
local: Esi wògale nu ƒom kpli wo la, tete Raxel kplɔ fofoa ƒe alẽwo vɛ, elabena alẽkplɔla wònye.

eng: He reached a certain place and spent the night there. When the sun had set, he took one of the stones at that place and put it near his head. Then he lay down there.
local: Eye wòva ɖo teƒea ɖe hetsi afima dɔ, ela bena ɣe ɖo to xoxo. Tete wòmia anyi ɖe afima, eye wòtsɔ afima kpea ɖe heziɔ tae.

eng: These are the descendants of Isaac, Abrahams son. Abraham became the father of Isaac.
local: Esia nye Abraham vi Isak ƒe dzidzimeŋutinya: Abraham ye dzi Isak.

eng: Jacob said to his mother Rebekah, My brother Esau is a hairy man, but I have smooth skin.
local: Tete Yakob gblɔ na dadaa Rebeka bena: Enyo hafi, gake nɔvinye Esau la ɖu me wònye, ke nye la fu mele ŋunye o.

eng: Jacob heard that Labans sons were saying, Jacob took everything our father owned and from it he produced all of this wealth.
local: Ke esi Laban viŋutsuwo gblɔ bena: Yakob da dzo mía fofo ƒe nuwo katã, eye nusiwo nye mía fofo tɔ la, woawoe wòxɔ wɔ eƒe kesinɔnu siawo katãe.

eng: Jacob was angry at Rachel and said, Do you think Im God? God alone has kept you from giving birth.
local: Tete Yakob do dɔmedzoe ɖe Raxel ŋuti gblɔ bena: Alo Mawu ye menye, amesi xe wò vidzidɔa?.

eng: So Isaac moved away from there, camped in the valley of Gerar, and lived there.
local: Tete Isak ʋu le afima ɖatu eƒe agbadɔ ɖe Gerar bali me, eye wònɔ afima.

eng: He dreamed and saw a raised staircase, its foundation on earth and its top touching the sky, and Gods messengers were ascending and descending on it.
local: Eye wòku drɔ̃e kpɔ atsrɔea ɖe tɔ anyigba, eye eƒe nugbɔ de dziƒo, eye wòkpɔ mawudɔlawo le eflɔm le dzi yim gale ɖiɖim.

eng: When Jacob saw Rachel the daughter of Laban his uncle, and the flock of Laban, Jacob came up, rolled the stone from the wells opening, and watered the flock of his uncle Laban.
local: Ke esi Yakob kpo Raxel, edada nɔvi Laban ƒe vinyɔnu kple edada nɔvi Laban ƒe alẽwo la, Yakob te va, eye wómli kpe la ɖa le vudoa nu, eye wòna tsi edada nɔvi Laban ƒe alẽwo wono.

eng: Isaac was years old when he married Rebekah the daughter of Bethuel the Aramean and the sister of Laban the Aramean, from Paddan-aram.
local: Isak xɔ ƒe blane, esime wòɖe Rebeka, si nye Aramtɔ Betuel tso Mesopotamia ƒe vi kple Aramtɔ Laban ƒe nɔvinyɔnu.

eng: And Jacob saw that Laban no longer liked him as much as he used to.
local: Eye Yakob kpɔ be, Laban megakɔa ŋku me ɖee abe tsã ene o.

eng: Isaac dug out again the wells that were dug during the lifetime of his father Abraham. The Philistines had closed them up after Abrahams death. Isaac gave them the same names his father had given them.
local: Eye Isak na wogaha vudo, siwo woɖe le fofoa Abraham ŋɔli, eye Filistitɔwo va xe wo, le Abraham ƒe ku megbe la me, eye wògatsɔ ŋkɔ na wo, abe alesi fofoa yɔ wòe ene.

eng: She said, Heres my servant Bilhah. Sleep with her, and she will give birth for me. Because of her, I will also have children.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Nye kosi Bilha enye si, ɖɔ kplii, bena wòadzi vi ɖe nye akɔ, eye maxɔ mamayɔvi tso egbɔ!.

eng: What if my father touches me and thinks Im making fun of him? I will be cursed instead of blessed.
local: Misela fofonye ali asi ŋunye akpɔ, ekema abum ameflulae, eye mayram o, ke aƒo fi boŋ adem.

eng: Jacob kissed Rachel and wept aloud.
local: Emegbe la Yakob ɖuɖɔ nu na Raxel, eye wòɖe asi avifafa me.

eng: Suddenly the LORD was standing on it and saying, I am the LORD, the God of your father Abraham and the God of Isaac. I will give you and your descendants the land on which you are lying.
local: Eye wòkpɔ Yehowa le tsitre ɖe eŋgɔ gblɔ bena: Nye enye Yehowa, fofowò Abraham ƒe Mawu kple Isak ƒe Mawu: Anyigba, si dzi nèmlɔ la, màtsɔe ana mia kple wò dzidzimeviwo.

eng: Isaac prayed to the LORD for his wife, since she was unable to have children. The LORD was moved by his prayer, and his wife Rebekah became pregnant.
local: Ke Isak, ƒo koko na Yehowa le srɔ̃a ta, elabena etsi ko. Eye Yehowa ɖo to eƒe kokoƒoƒo la, eye srɔ̃a Rebeka fɔ fu.

eng: Then the LORD said to Jacob, Go back to the land of your ancestors and to your relatives, and I will be with you.
local: Eye Yehowa gblɔ na Yakob bena: Trɔ yi fofowò ƒe anyigba dzi kple ƒowòmeawo gbɔ, eye manɔ anyi kpli wò!.

eng: So Rachel gave her servant Bilhah to Jacob as his wife, and he slept with her.
local: Eye wòtsɔ eƒe kosi Bilha ne wòde, eye Yakob dɔ kplii.

eng: Isaacs servants dug wells in the valley and found a well there with fresh water.
local: Eye Isak ƒe dɔlawo ɖe vudo ɖe bali la me, eye wotu tsi dzidzi le afima.

eng: His mother said to him, Your curse will be on me, my son. Just listen to me: go and get them for me.
local: Dadaa ɖo ŋu ne bena: Vinye, nye boŋ ŋue wò fiƒoɖe la laɖi; ɖo tom ko, eye nayi aɖalée vɛ!.

eng: Jacob told Rachel that he was related to her father and that he was Rebekahs son. She then ran to tell her father.
local: Eye Yakob gblɔ na Raxel bena, fofoa nɔvi yenye, eye Rebeka vi yenye; tete wòƒu ɖu yi ɖagblɔe na fofoa.

eng: But the boys pushed against each other inside of her, and she said, If this is what its like, why did it happen to me? So she went to ask the LORD.
local: Ke esi ɖeviawo le ŋu ɖom wo nɔewo le eƒe dɔ me la, egblɔ bena: Ne ele alea la, nukaŋuti megale agbe ɖo? Eye wòyi ɖabia Yehowa se.

eng: Your descendants will become like the dust of the earth; you will spread out to the west, east, north, and south. Every family of earth will be blessed because of you and your descendants.
local: Eye wò dzidzimeviwo asɔ gbɔ abe ʋuʋudedi ene, eye nakeke ta ɖe ɣetoɖoƒe, ɣedzeƒe, anyiehe kple dziehe, eye woayra anyigba dzi dukɔwo katã. Ne mia kple wò dzidzimeviwo me.

eng: So Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah and summoned them into the field where his flock was.
local: Tete Yakob dɔ woyi ɖayɔ Raxel kple Lea va eƒe lãha gbɔ le gbe me.

eng: Isaacs shepherds argued with Gerars shepherds, each claiming, This is our water. So Isaac named the well Esek because they quarreled with him.
local: Ke Gerartɔwo ƒe lãnyilawo wɔ dzre kple Isak ƒe lãnyilawo gblɔ bena: Mía tɔe nye; tsi la. Eyata wòna ŋkɔ vudo la be Esek, elabena wowɔ dzre ɖe eta.

eng: Bilhah became pregnant and gave birth to a son for Jacob.
local: Eye Bilha fɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi na Yakob.

eng: So he went and got them and brought them to his mother, and his mother made the delicious food that his father loved.
local: Tete wòyi ɖalée vɛ na dadaa, eye dadaa ɖa nu nyuia ɖe, abe alesi nyoa fofoa ŋu ene.

eng: When Laban heard about Jacob his sisters son, he ran to meet him. Laban embraced him, kissed him, and invited him into his house, where Jacob recounted to Laban everything that had happened.
local: Esi Laban se nya siawo le nɔvianyɔnu ƒe vi Yakob ŋuti la, eƒu ɖu yi ɖakpee, eye wòkpla asi kɔ nɛ heɖuɖɔ nu nɛ, eye wòkplɔe va eƒe me. Eye wòxlẽ nya, siwo katã va dzɔ la, na Laban.

eng: I am with you now, I will protect you everywhere you go, and I will bring you back to this land. I will not leave you until I have done everything that I have promised you.
local: Manɔ anyi kpli wò, eye afisiafi, si nayi la, makpɔ dziwò, eye mana nagatrɔ ava anyigba sia dzi. Elabena nyemagblẽ wò ɖi, vaseɖe esime mawɔ nya, siwo megblɔ na wò la dzi o.

eng: He said to them, I am aware that your father no longer likes me as much as he used to. But my fathers God has been with me.
local: Eye wògblɔ na wo bena: Mekpɔ be, mia fofo megakɔa ŋku me ɖem abe tsã ene o; gake fofonye ƒe Mawu la li kplim.

eng: And the LORD said to her, Two nations are in your womb; two different peoples will emerge from your body. One people will be stronger than the other; the older will serve the younger.
local: Eye Yehowa gblɔ nɛ bena; Dukɔ evee le wò dɔ me, eye dukɔ eveawo amã atso wò dɔ me ke; dukɔ ɖeka asesẽ awu ɖeka, eye tsitsitɔ asubɔ ɖevitɔ!.

eng: They dug another well and argued about it too, so he named it Sitnah.
local: Eyome la wogaɖe vudo bubu, eye wogawɔ dzre ɖe eya hã ta; eyata wòna ŋkɔe bena Sitma.

eng: Rachel said, God has judged in my favor, heard my voice, and given me a son. So she named him Dan.
local: Tete Raxel gblɔ bena: Mawu tso afia nam, ese nye gbe, eye wòna ŋutsuvim. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Dan.

eng: Rebekah took her older son Esaus favorite clothes that were in the house with her, and she put them on her younger son Jacob.
local: Emegbe la Rebeka tsɔ via xoxoetɔ Esau ƒe atsyɔɖowu, siwo le egbɔ la, hedo na via ɖevitɔ Yakob.

eng: When she reached the end of her pregnancy, she discovered that she had twins.
local: Eye esi eƒe vidziɣi ɖo la, wokpɔ bena, venɔviwoe le eƒe do me.

eng: You know that Ive worked for your father as hard as I could.
local: Mia ŋutɔwo mienyae bena, nye ŋusẽ katã metsɔ subɔ mia fofo lae.

eng: When Jacob woke from his sleep, he thought to himself, The LORD is definitely in this place, but I didnt know it.
local: Esi Yakob nyɔ tso alɔ me la, egblɔ be: Vavã, Yehowa le afisia, gake nyemenyae o!.

eng: Laban said to him, Yes, you are my flesh and blood. After Jacob had stayed with Laban for a month.
local: Tete Laban gblɔ nɛ bena: Nyateƒee, nɔvinye kpamkpam nènye! Ke esi wònɔ egbɔ ɣleti ɖeka la.

eng: He left there and dug another well, but they didnt argue about it, so he named it Rehoboth and said, Now the LORD has made an open space for us and has made us fertile in the land.
local: Eye wòʋu le afima ɖo ta ŋgɔ, eye wòɖe vudo bubu; ke womewɔ dzre ɖe eya ta o; eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Rehobot hegblɔ be: Azɔ Yehowa na nɔƒe mí, bena míakeke ta ɖe anyigba la dzi.

eng: Rachels servant Bilhah became pregnant again and gave birth to a second son for Jacob.
local: Eyome la Raxel ƒe kosi Bilha gafɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi evelia na Yakob.

eng: On his arms and smooth neck she put the hide of young goats.
local: Eye wotso gbɔ̃viawo ƒe agbalẽ bla ɖe eƒe asiwò kple eƒe ko, siwo ŋu ɖi niŋɔniŋɔ la ŋu.

eng: But your father cheated me and changed my payment ten times. Yet God didnt let him harm me.
local: Gake mia fofo blem, eye wòɖɔ li nye fetu zi ewo; ke Mawu meɖe mɔ nɛ be, wòawɔ vɔ ɖe ŋunye o.

eng: Laban said to Jacob, You shouldnt have to work for free just because you are my relative. Tell me what you would like to be paid.
local: Laban gblɔ na Yakob bena: Nɔvinye nènye! Ɖe nawɔ ɖɔ nam dzodzroa? Gblɔe nam, nuka lanye wò fetu?.

eng: The first came out red all over, clothed with hair, and she named him Esau.
local: Esi gbãtɔ do la, eŋuti bia hẽ, eye fu le eŋu kedzekedze, eyata woyɔ eƒe ŋkɔ bena Esau.

eng: Then he went up from Gerar to Beer-sheba.
local: Emegbe la eʋu le afima va Beer-Seba.

eng: He was terrified and thought, This sacred place is awesome. Its none other than Gods house and the entrance to heaven.
local: Eye wòyɔ gblɔ bena: Ŋɔdziƒe ka enye si! Afi sia menye afia ɖeke o, negbe Mawu ƒe aƒe kple dziƒogbo ko wonye.

eng: Rachel said, Ive competed fiercely with my sister, and now Ive won. So she named him Naphtali.
local: Eye Raxel gblɔ bena: Meʋli heƒo koko na Mawu le nɔvinye ta, meɖu dzi Eyata wòna ŋkɔe bena Naftali.

eng: and the delicious food and the bread she had made she put into her sons hands.
local: Eye wòtsɔ nuɖuɖu la kple abolo, si wòƒo la, ɖe asi na via Yakob.

eng: If he said, The speckled ones will be your payment, the whole flock gave birth to speckled young. And if he said, The striped ones will be your payment, the whole flock gave birth to striped young.
local: Ne egblɔ bena: lã ŋɔtaŋɔtawo nanye wò fetu la, ekema lãha blibo la katã dzia vi ŋɔtaŋɔtawo sɔŋ, eye ne egagblɔ bena: lã, siwo me fli to, nanye wò fetu la, ekema lãha blibo la dzia vi, siwo me fli to la.

eng: Immediately afterward, his brother came out gripping Esaus heel, and she named him Jacob. Isaac was years old when they were born.
local: Eyome nɔvia hã do, eye wòlé Esau ƒe afɔkpodzi ɖe asi; eyata woyɔ eƒe ŋkɔ bena Yakob. Isak xɔ ƒe blaade, eye wòdzi wo.

eng: Now Laban had two daughters: the older was named Leah and the younger Rachel.
local: Nyɔnuvi eve le Laban si, xoxoetɔ ŋkɔ nye Lea, eye ɖevitɔ ŋkɔ nye Raxel.

eng: The LORD appeared to him that night and said, I am the God of your father Abraham. Dont be afraid because I am with you. I will bless you, and I will give you many children for my servant Abrahams sake.
local: Tete Yehowa ɖe eɖokui fiae le zã ma me gblɔ bena: Nyee nye fofowò Abraham ƒe Mawu! Megavɔ̃ o, elabena nye la meli kpli wò; mayra wò, eye mana wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ ɖe dzi le nye dɔla Abraham ta.

eng: After Jacob got up early in the morning, he took the stone that he had put near his head, set it up as a sacred pillar, and poured oil on the top of it.
local: Ke esi Yakob fɔ ŋdi kanya la, etsɔ kpe, si wòziɔ la, ɖo dzesikpee, eye wòkɔ ami ɖe edzi.

eng: When Leah realized that she had stopped bearing children, she took her servant Zilpah and gave her to Jacob as his wife.
local: Ke esi Lea kpɔ bena, yemegale vi dzim o la, etsɔ eƒe kosi Silpa na Yakob wòɖe.

eng: Jacob went to his father and said, My father. And he said, Im here. Who are you, my son.
local: Tete wòyi ɖe fofoa gbɔ gblɔ bena: Fofonye! Eɖo ŋu bena: Nye enye si! Vinye, wò amekae?.

eng: When the young men grew up, Esau became an outdoorsman who knew how to hunt, and Jacob became a quiet man who stayed at home.
local: Esi ɖeviawo va tsi la, Esau zu adela, eye wòlɔ̃a gbe me nɔnɔ; ke Yakob enye ame fafa, eye wòlɔ̃a agbadɔ me nɔnɔ.

eng: Leah had delicate eyes, but Rachel had a beautiful figure and was good-looking.
local: Lea ƒe ŋkuwo dzi wɔ dɔ; ke Raxel ya nyo, eye wòdze tugbe.

eng: God took away your fathers livestock and gave them to me.
local: Ale Mawu xɔ lãha blibo le mia fofo si hetsɔ nam.

eng: So Isaac built an altar there and worshipped in the LORDs name. Isaac pitched his tent there, and his servants dug a well.
local: Tete wòɖi vɔsamlekpui heyɔ Yehowa ƒe ŋkɔ dzi le afima, eye wòtu eƒe agbadɔ ɖe afima. Emegbe Isak fe dɔlawo ɖe vudo ɖe afima hã.

eng: Leahs servant Zilpah gave birth to a son for Jacob.
local: Eye Silpa, Lea ƒe kosi la, dzi ŋutsuvi na Yakob.

eng: He named that sacred place Bethel, though Luz was the citys original name.
local: Eye wòna ŋkɔ teƒe ma bena: Betel; ke tsã la woyɔnɛ be Lus.

eng: Jacob said to his father, Im Esau your oldest son. Ive made what you asked me to. Sit up and eat some of the game so you can bless me.
local: Yakob gblɔ na fofoa bena: Nye enye Esau, wò ŋgɔgbevi, mewɔ abe alesi nègblɔ nam ene. Fɔ ɖe ali nu, ne naɖu nye adelã la, eye nayram.

eng: Isaac loved Esau because he enjoyed eating game, but Rebekah loved Jacob.
local: Eye Isak lɔ̃a Esau, elabena adelã vivia enu; ke Rebeka ya lɔ̃a Yakob.

eng: Jacob loved Rachel and said, I will work for you for seven years for Rachel, your younger daughter.
local: Eye Yakob lɔ̃a Raxel, eyata wògblɔ bena: Masubɔ wò ƒe adre le Raxel, wò nyɔnuvi ɖevitɔ la ta.

eng: But Abimelech set out toward him from Gerar, with Ahuzzath his ally and Phicol the commander of his forces.
local: Gbe ɖeka Abimelex kple xɔlɔ̃a Ahusat kple eƒe aʋafia Pixol wotso Gerar va egbɔ.

eng: When the flocks were mating, I looked up and saw in a dream that the male goats that mounted the flock were striped, speckled, and spotted.
local: Eye esi lãwo le asi yɔm la, meku drɔ̃e kpɔ bena, lãtsu, siwo me fli to, esiwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta, kple esiwo tso kpɔ sɔŋ, le lãnɔwo liam.

eng: Jacob made a solemn promise: If God is with me and protects me on this trip Im taking, and gives me bread to eat and clothes to wear.
local: Eye Yakob ɖe adzɔgbe gblɔ bena: Ne Mawu anɔ anyi kplim, eye wòakpɔ dzinye le mɔ, si dzi mele la dzi, eye wòana abolom maɖu kple awum mado.

eng: and Leah said, What good luck! So she named him Gad.
local: Tete Lea gblɔ bena: Nye Mawu ɖi! Eye wòna ŋkɔe be Gad.

eng: Isaac said to his son, How could you find this so quickly, my son? He said, The LORD your God led me right to it.
local: Tete Isak gblɔ na via bena: Aleke wɔ nèkpɔe kaba ale, vinye? Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Elabena wò Mawu la na wòva ɖo gom.

eng: Once when Jacob was boiling stew, Esau came in from the field hungry.
local: Gbe ɖeka Yakob ɖa nu, eye Esau gbɔ tso gbe me, eye dɔ wui ɖi tsi ɖe eŋu.

eng: Laban said, Id rather give her to you than to another man. Stay with me.
local: Laban gblɔ bena: Enyo be, màtsɔe na wò, wu esi màtsɔe na ŋutsu bubu; nɔ gbɔnye!.

eng: Isaac said to him, Why have you come after me? You resented me and sent me away from you.
local: Eye Isak gblɔ na wo bena: Nuka ta mieva gbɔnye ɖo, esi mielé fum, eye mienyãm le mia gbɔ?.

eng: In the dream, Gods messenger said to me, Jacob! and I said, Im here.
local: Eye mawudɔla gblɔ nam le drɔ̃e me bena: Yakob! Meɖo ŋu bena: Nye enye si!.

eng: and I return safely to my fathers household, then the LORD will be my God.
local: eye wòana maganɔ ava fofonye ƒe me dedie la, ekema Yehowa anye nye Mawu.

eng: Leahs servant Zilpah gave birth to a second son for Jacob.
local: Eyome Silpa, Lea ƒe kosi la, gadzi ŋutsuvi evelia na Yakob.

eng: Isaac said to Jacob, Come here and let me touch you, my son. Are you my son Esau or not.
local: Isak gblɔ na Yakob bena: Te ɖe ŋunye, vinye, ne mali asi ŋuwò akpɔ, be vinye Esau nènye loo, alo menye eyae o!.

eng: and said to Jacob, Im starving! Let me devour some of this red stuff. Thats why his name is Edom.
local: Tete Esau gblɔna Yakob bena: Na nu dzia ɖem maɖu, elabena ɖɔ wum ɖi tsi ɖe ŋunye. Eyata woyɔ eƒe ŋkɔ be Edom.

eng: Jacob worked for Rachel for seven years, but it seemed like a few days because he loved her.
local: Ale Yakob subɔ ƒe adre ɖe Raxel ta, eye wòle nɛ abe ŋkekea ɖewo ko ene, le alesi gbegbe wòlɔ̃ne la ta.

eng: They said, We now see that the LORD was with you. We propose that there be a formal agreement between us and that we draw up a treaty with you.
local: Wogblɔ nɛ bena: Míekpɔ dze sii keŋ bena, Yehowa li kpli wò; eyata míegblɔ be, anyo be, míaka atam na mía nɔewo, eye míabla nu kpli wò.

eng: He said, Look up and watch all the striped, speckled, and spotted male goats mounting the flock. Ive seen everything that Laban is doing to you.
local: Eye wogblɔ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, lãtsu, siwo me fli to, esiwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta, kple esiwo tso kpɔ la, le lãnɔawo liam. Elabena mekpɔ nusi Laban wɔ ɖe ŋuwò la.

eng: This stone that Ive set up as a sacred pillar will be Gods house, and of everything you give me I will give a tenth back to you.
local: Eye kpe sia, si meɖo dzesikpee la, anye Mawu ƒe aƒe, eye matsɔ nusianu, si nanam la ƒe ewolia na wò kokoko.

eng: and Leah said, Im happy now because women call me happy. So she named him Asher.
local: Tete Lea gblɔ bena: Mekpɔ dzɔgbe nyui! Azɔ vinyɔnuwo akafum bena, dzɔgbenyuitɔ menye. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Aser.

eng: So Jacob approached his father Isaac, and Isaac touched him and said, The voice is Jacobs voice, but the arms are Esaus arms.
local: Tete Yakob te ɖe fofoa Isak ŋu, eye esi wòli asi eŋu kpɔ la, egblɔ bena: Gbe la enye Yakob ƒe gbe, ke asiawo nye Esau ƒe asi.

eng: Jacob said to Laban, The time has come. Give me my wife so that I may sleep with her.
local: Emegbe la Yakob gblɔ na Laban bena: Tsɔ srɔ̃nye nam azɔ, elabena nye ŋkekewo ɖe, bena maɖee!.

eng: Jacob said, Sell me your birthright today.
local: Yakob ɖo ŋu nɛ be: Tsɔ wò ŋgɔgbevinyenye ƒle nam gbã gbɔ.

eng: you must not treat us badly since we havent harmed you and since we have treated you well at all times. Then we will send you away peacefully, for you are now blessed by the LORD.
local: be nagawɔ vɔ̃a ɖeke mi o, abe alesi miawo hã miewɔ naneke wò o, ke boŋ nyui sɔŋ ko miewɔ na wò, eye miena nedzo le ŋutifafa me la ene, elabena amesi Yehowa yra nènye.

eng: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed a sacred pillar and where you made a solemn promise to me. Now, get up and leave this country and go back to the land of your relatives.
local: Nye enye Mawu, si ɖe eɖokui fia wò le Betel, afisi nèkɔ ami ɖe kpe dzi, eye nèɖe adzɔgbe nam le. Tso, naʋu le anyigba sia dzi àgatrɔ yi ɖe mia denyigba dzi!.

eng: During the wheat harvest, Reuben found some erotic herbs in the field and brought them to his mother Leah. Rachel said to Leah, Give me your sons erotic herbs.
local: Le luŋeɣi la Ruben yi agble me, eye wòkpɔ agbitsa tsɔ vɛ na dada Lea. Tete Raxel gblɔ na Lea bena: Meɖe kuku, na viwò ƒe agbitsa la ɖem!.

eng: Isaac didnt recognize him because his arms were hairy like Esaus arms, so he blessed him.
local: Ale medze sii o, elabena eƒe asiwò to fu kedzekedze abe nɔvia Esau ƒe asi ene, eye wòyrae.

eng: So Laban invited all the people of that place and prepared a banquet.
local: Tete Laban kpe dua me ŋutsuwo katã, eye wòɖo kplɔ̃ na wo.

eng: Esau said, Since Im going to die anyway, what good is my birthright to me.
local: Tete Esau gblɔ be: Mele kuku ge tso, nuka gɔ̃ ŋgɔgbevinyenye awɔ nam?.

eng: Isaac prepared a banquet for them, and they ate and drank.
local: Emegbe eɖo kplɔ̃ na wo, woɖu nu, eye wono nu.

eng: Rachel and Leah answered him, Is there any share or inheritance left for us in our fathers household?.
local: Tete Raxel kple Lea woɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Ɖe gome kple domenyinyia ɖe gali na mi le mía fofo ƒe mea?.

eng: Leah replied, Isnt it enough that youve taken my husband? Now you want to take my sons erotic herbs too? Rachel said, For your sons erotic herbs, Jacob may sleep with you tonight.
local: Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Alo mesɔ gbɔ be, nèxɔ srɔ̃nye le asinye oa? Azɔ nèle didim be, nagaxɔ vinye ƒe agbitsa hãa? Tete Raxel gblɔ bena: Ekema nedɔ gbɔwò egbe zã ɖe viwò ƒe agbitsa la nu!.

eng: Isaac said, Are you really my son Esau? And he said, I am.
local: Eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Vinye Esau nènyea? Eɖo ŋu bena: E, nyee!.

eng: However, in the evening, he took his daughter Leah and brought her to Jacob, and he slept with her.
local: Ke esi zã ɖo la, ekplɔ vianyɔnu Lea yi nɛ, eye wòdɔ kplii.

eng: Jacob said, Give me your word today. And he did. He sold his birthright to Jacob.
local: Eye Yakob gblɔ be: Ka atam ƒu dzi nam gbɔ! Tete wòka atam nɛ, eye wòtsɔ eƒe ŋgɔgbevinyenye ƒle na Yakob.

eng: They got up early in the morning, and they gave each other their word. Isaac sent them off, and they left peacefully.
local: Esi ŋu ke la, woka atam na wo nɔewo, eye Isak do mɔ wo, eye wodzo le egbɔ le ŋutifafa me.

eng: Doesnt he think of us as foreigners since he sold us and has even used up the payment he received for us?.
local: Ɖe mele mia bum amedzrowoe oa? Elabena etsɔ mí dzra, eye wòle ga, si wòxɔ ɖe mía ta la ɖum vaseɖe mi.

eng: When Jacob came back from the field in the evening, Leah went out to meet him and said, You must sleep with me because Ive paid for you with my sons erotic herbs. So he slept with her that night.
local: Esi Yakob gbɔ tso gbe me le fiẽ la, Lea yi ɖakpee gblɔ bena: Gbɔnyee nava, elabena mexe fe ɖe tawo kple vinye ƒe agbitsa! Eye wòdɔ egbɔ le zã ma me.

eng: Isaac said, Bring some food here and let me eat some of my sons game so I can bless you. Jacob put it before him and he ate, and he brought him wine and he drank.
local: Eye wògblɔ bena: Tsɔe vɛ nam, ne maɖu vinye ƒe adelã la, eye mayra wò! Tete wòtsɔe ve nɛ woɖu, emegbe wotsɔ wein vɛ nɛ wònɔ.

eng: Laban had given his servant Zilpah to his daughter Leah as her servant.
local: Eye Laban tsɔ eƒe kosi Silpa na via Lea, bena woasubɔe.

eng: So Jacob gave Esau bread and lentil stew. He ate, drank, got up, and left, showing just how little he thought of his birthright.
local: Eye Yakob tsɔ abolo kple bɔbɔ na Esau. Tete wòɖu nu no nu, eye wòtso dzo. Ale wo, eye Esau do vlo eƒe ŋgɔgbevinyenye enye esi.

eng: That day Isaacs servants informed him about the well that they had been digging and said to him, We found water.
local: Gbemagbe ke Isak ƒe dɔlawo va gblɔ nɛ le vudo, si woɖe la ŋuti be: Mieke ɖe tsi ŋu!.

eng: God responded to Leah. She became pregnant and gave birth to a fifth son for Jacob.
local: Eye Mawu ɖo to Lea, eye wòfɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi atɔ̃lia na Yakob.

eng: All of the wealth God took from our father belongs to us and our children. Now, do everything God told you to do.
local: Elabena kesinɔnu, siwo katã Mawu xɔ le mía fofo si la, wonye mía ŋutɔwo kple mia viwo tɔ; eyata wɔ nusianu, si Mawu gblɔ ha wò la ko!.

eng: His father Isaac said to him, Come here and kiss me, my son.
local: Eyome la fofoa Isak gblɔ nɛ bena: Te ɖe ŋunye azɔ, vinye, eye naɖuɖɔ nu nam!.

eng: In the morning, there she was—Leah! Jacob said to Laban, What have you done to me? Didnt I work for you to have Rachel? Why did you betray me.
local: Ke esi ŋu ke la, ekpɔ be, Lea ye. Eye wògblɔ na Laban bena: Nuka enye si nèwɔ ɖe ŋunye? Alo menye Raxel ta mesubɔ wò ɖo oa? Nukaŋuti nèblema?.

eng: He called it Shibah; therefore, the citys name has been Beer-sheba until today.
local: Eye wòna ŋkɔe be Siba, eyata du la ƒe ŋkɔ nye Beer-Seba vaseɖe egbegbe.

eng: Leah said, God gave me what I paid for, what I deserved for giving my servant to my husband. So she named him Issachar.
local: Eye Lea gblɔ bena: Mawu xe fe nam, elabena metsɔ nye kosi na srɔ̃nye wòɖe. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Isaxar.

eng: So Jacob got up, put his sons and wives on the camels.
local: Tete Yakob tsɔ viawo kple srɔ̃awo ɖo kposɔwo dzi.

eng: So he came close and kissed him. When Isaac smelled the scent of his clothes, he blessed him, See, the scent of my son is like the scent of the field that the LORD has blessed.
local: Esi wote ɖe eŋu ɖuɖɔ nu nɛ la, eʋẽ eƒe awu me se; eye wòyrae gblɔ bena: Ehɛ̃, vinye le ʋeʋẽm abe alesi gbe, si Yehowa yra, ʋẽnae ene.

eng: Laban said, Where we live, we dont give the younger woman before the oldest.
local: Laban ɖo ŋu ne bena: Menye míaƒe kɔ be, woatre ɖevitɔ ɖeɖe na xoxoetɔ o.

eng: When Esau was years old, he married Judith daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Basemath daughter of Elon the Hittite.
local: Ke esi Esau xɔ ƒe blane la, eɖe Xititɔ Beervii ƒe vinyɔnu Yudit kple Xititɔ Elon ƒe vinyɔnu Basmat.

eng: Leah became pregnant again and gave birth to a sixth son for Jacob.
local: Eyome Lea gafɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi adelia na Yakob.

eng: and set out with all of his livestock and all of his possessions that he had acquired in Paddan-aram in order to return to his father Isaac in the land of Canaan.
local: Emegbe la ekplɔ eƒe lãwo katã, eye wotsɔ eƒe kesinɔnuwo katã, nusianu, si wòkpɔ le Mesopotamia la, eye wòdzo yina ɖe fofoa Isak gbɔ le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

eng: May God give you showers from the sky, olive oil from the earth, plenty of grain and new wine.
local: Mawu ana dziƒozãmu kple anyigba wɔnu wò, eye àkpɔ bli kple wein geɖe fũ ŋutɔ.

eng: Complete the celebratory week with this woman. Then I will give you this other woman too for your work, if you work for me seven more years.
local: Ne ewu kwasiɖa sia nu la, ekema matsɔ eya hã na wò ɖe ɖɔ, si nagawɔ nam le ƒe adre bubu me la nu.

eng: They made life very difficult for Isaac and Rebekah.
local: Woawo zu nuxaxa gãa ɖe na Isak kple Rebeka.

eng: and she said, God has given me a wonderful gift. Now my husband will honor me since Ive borne him six sons. So she named him Zebulun.
local: Tete Lea gblɔ bena Mawu na nu nyuia ɖem, azɔ srɔ̃nye abum, elabena medzi ŋutsuvi ade nɛ. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Zebulon.

eng: May the nations serve you, may peoples bow down to you. Be the most powerful man among your brothers, and may your mothers sons bow down to you. Those who curse you will be cursed, and those who bless you will be blessed.
local: Dukɔwo asubɔ wò, eye ƒomewo anɔ wò ŋusẽ te. Aɖu aƒetɔ ɖe nɔviwòwo nu, eye dawòviwo adze klo na wò. Amesi ke aƒo fi ade wò la, woaƒo fi adee, eye amesi ke ayra wò la, wõayrae!.

eng: Now, while Laban was out shearing his sheep, Rachel stole the households divine images that belonged to her father.
local: Ke esi Laban yi fu sẽ ge na lãwo la, Raxel fi fofoa ƒe aklama kpakpɛwo.

eng: So that is what Jacob did. He completed the celebratory week with this woman, and then Laban gave him his daughter Rachel as his wife.
local: Yakob wɔ nenema, eye wowu kwasiɖa la nu; emegbe wotsɔ via Raxel hã nɛ wòɖe.

eng: After this, she gave birth to a daughter and named her Dinah.
local: Emegbe la edzi nyɔnuvi, eye wòna ŋkɔe be Dina.

eng: After Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, and just as Jacob left his father Isaac, his brother Esau came back from his hunt.
local: Ke esi Isak wu Yakob yayra nu, eye Yakob ɖo go tso fofoa Isak gbɔ la, tete nɔvia Esau gbɔ tso adegbe.

eng: Moreover, Jacob deceived Laban the Aramean by not sending word to him that he was leaving.
local: Eye Yakob ble Laban Aramtɔ la, elabena megblɔe nɛ hafi dzo o.

eng: Laban had given his servant Bilhah to his daughter Rachel as her servant.
local: Eye Laban tsɔ eƒe kosi Bilha na via Raxel, bena woasubɔe.

eng: Then God remembered Rachel, responded to her, and let her conceive.
local: Tete Mawu ɖo ŋku Raxel dzi, eye Mawu ɖo toe, eye wòʋu eƒe vidzidɔ.

eng: So Jacob and his entire household left. He got up, crossed the river, and set out directly for the mountains of Gilead.
local: Ale wòsi yi kple nusiwo katã le esi la, eye wotso tɔsisi la heɖo ta Gilead-towo gbɔ.

eng: He too made some delicious food, brought it to his father, and said, Let my father sit up and eat from his sons game so that you may bless me.
local: Eye eya hã ɖa du nyuia ɖe tsɔ vɛ na fofoa, eye wògblɔ na fofoa bena: Fofonye, fɔ ɖe ali nu, eye naɖu viwò ƒe adelã la, bena nayram!.

eng: Jacob slept with Rachel, and he loved Rachel more than Leah. He worked for Laban seven more years.
local: Azɔ edɔ kple Raxel hã, eye wolɔa Raxel wua Lea. Eye wòsubɔe ƒe adre bubu la.

eng: She became pregnant and gave birth to a son and said, God has taken away my shame.
local: Eye wòfɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi, tete wògblɔ bena: Mawu ɖe nye ŋukpe ɖa!.

eng: Three days later, Laban found out that Jacob had gone.
local: Le ŋkeke etɔ̃ megbe la wova gblɔ na Laban bena, Yakob si.

eng: His father Isaac said to him, Who are you? And he said, Im your son, your oldest son, Esau.
local: Fofoa Isak gblɔ nɛ bena: Wò amekae? Eɖo ŋu bena: Nye enye Esau, wò ŋgɔgbevi.

eng: When the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, he opened her womb; but Rachel was unable to have children.
local: Ke esi Yehowa kpɔ bena, womelɔ̃a Lea ƒe nya o la, enu eƒe vidzidɔ, eye wòxe Raxel tɔ.

eng: She named him Joseph, saying to herself, May the LORD give me another son.
local: Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Yosef; elabena egblɔ be: Yehowa agatsɔ ŋutsuvi bubu akpee nam.

eng: so Laban took his brothers with him, chased Jacob for seven days, and caught up with him in the mountains of Gilead.
local: Tete wotsɔ nɔvia wo kpe ɖe eɖokui ŋu ti eyome ŋkeke adre sɔŋ, eye wòva tui le Gilead-towo dzi.

eng: Isaac was so shocked that he trembled violently. He said, Who was the hunter just here with game? He brought me food, and I ate all of it before you came. I blessed him, and he will stay blessed.
local: Tete Isak ɖi vo ŋutɔ gblɔ bena: Ameka gɔ̃ enye si tsɔ adelã vɛ nam, eye meɖu nuawo katã ƒe ɖe, hafi neva, eye meyrae mahã? Eya tɔ ko yayra la anye!.

eng: Leah became pregnant and gave birth to a son. She named him Reuben because she said, The LORD saw my harsh treatment, and now my husband will love me.
local: Eye Lea fɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Ruben; elabena egblɔ be: Yehowa kpɔ nye nublanui, azɔ srɔ̃nye alɔ̃m.

eng: After Rachel gave birth to Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, Send me off so that I can go to my own place and my own country.
local: Ke esi Raxel dzi Yosef megbe la, Yakob gblɔ na Laban bena: Do mɔm, ne mayi mía de.

eng: That night, God appeared to Laban the Aramean in a dream and said, Be careful and dont say anything hastily to Jacob one way or the other.
local: Eye Mawu va Laban, Aramtɔ la gbɔ le zã me drɔ̃eƒe, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖokuiwò dzi, be nagaƒo nu yɔa ɖeke kple Yakob o.

eng: When Esau heard what his father said, he let out a loud agonizing cry and wept bitterly. He said to his father, Bless me! Me too, my father.
local: Esi Esau se fofoa ƒe nyawo la, edo ɣli sesĩe nublanuitɔe gblɔ na fofoa bena: Fofonye, yra nye hã!.

eng: She became pregnant again and gave birth to a son. She said, The LORD heard that I was unloved, so he gave me this son too, and she named him Simeon.
local: Eyome la egafɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi. Eye wògblɔ bena: Yehowa se bena, womelɔ̃a nye nya o; eyata wotso esia hã nam. Eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Simeon.

eng: Give me my wives and children whom Ive worked for, and I will go. You know the work Ive done for you.
local: Tsɔ srɔ̃nye kple vinye, siwo ta mesubɔ wò ɖo la nam, ne madzo; elabena wò ŋutɔ ènya alesi mesubɔ wòe!.

eng: Laban reached Jacob after Jacob had pitched his tent in the mountains. So Laban and his brothers also pitched theirs in the mountains of Gilead.
local: Eye Laban va tu Yakob; eye Yakob tu eƒe agbadɔ ɖe to la dzi, ke Laban kple nɔviawo tu wo tɔ ɖe Gilead-to la dzi.

eng: Isaac said, Your brother has already come deceitfully and has taken your blessing.
local: Ke egblɔ bena: Nɔviwò to aye meva xɔwo yayra la xoxo.

eng: She became pregnant again and gave birth to a son. She said, Now, this time my husband will embrace me, since I have given birth to three sons for him. So she named him Levi.
local: Emegbe wògafɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi, eye wògblɔ bena: Azɔ ko srɔ̃nye aku ɖe ŋunye, elabena medzi ŋutsuvi etɔ nɛ. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Lewi.

eng: Laban said to him, Do me this favor. Ive discovered by a divine sign that the LORD has blessed me because of you.
local: Tete Laban gblɔ nɛ bena: Ve nunye dzro! Elabena medze sii bena, Yehowa yram le tawo.

eng: Laban said to Jacob, What have you done? You have deceived me and taken off with my daughters as if they were prisoners of war.
local: Tete Laban gblɔ na Yakob bena: Nuka nye esi nèwɔ, eye nèblem hekplɔ vinyenyɔnuwo dzoe abe aboyomewo ene?.

eng: Esau said, Isnt this why hes called Jacob? Hes taken me twice now: he took my birthright, and now hes taken my blessing. He continued, Havent you saved a blessing for me.
local: Eɖo ŋu bena: Yakob ye woyɔnɛ vavã! Zi eve enye si wòblem: Exɔ ŋgɔgbevinyenye le asinye, azɔ egaxɔ nye yayra hã! Eye wòbiae bena: Mèkpɔtɔ yayraa ɖeke ɖi nam oa?.

eng: She became pregnant again and gave birth to a son. She said, This time I will praise the LORD. So she named him Judah. Then she stopped bearing children.
local: Eye wògafɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi, eye wògblɔ bena: Azɔ la makafu Yehowa! Eyata wòna ŋkɔe bena Yuda. Emegbe la edzudzɔ vidzidzi.

eng: so name your price and I will pay it.
local: Eye wògayi edzi bena: Gblɔ fetu, si dim nèle la nam, ne mana wò!.

eng: Why did you leave secretly, deceiving me, and not letting me know? I wouldve sent you off with a celebration, with songs and tambourines and harps.
local: Nukaŋuti nèsi le adza me, eye nèblem, eye mèklãm, be maɖo wò ɖa kple aseyetsotso, hadzidzi, ʋuƒoƒo kple saŋkuƒoƒo o.

eng: Isaac replied to Esau, Ive already made him more powerful than you, and Ive made all of his brothers his servants. Ive made him strong with grain and wine. What can I do for you, my son.
local: Tete Isak gblɔ na Esau bena: Kpɔ ɖa, meɖoe aƒetɔ ɖe dziwò, metsɔ nɔviawo katã wɔ eƒe dɔlawoe, eye metsɔ bli kple wein hã nɛ: azɔ nuka gɔ̃e gali mawɔ na wò, vinye?.

eng: You didnt even let me kiss my sons and my daughters good-bye. Now youve acted like a fool.
local: eye mèna meɖuɖɔ nu na nye tɔgbuiyɔviwo kple vinyenyɔnuwo o mahã? Ewɔ bometsinu!.

eng: Jacob said to him, You know how Ive worked for you, and how well your livestock have done with me.
local: Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Wò ŋutɔ ènya alesi mesubɔ wòe, kple alesi wò lãha va zu le gbɔnye.

eng: Esau said to his father, Do you really have only one blessing, Father? Bless me too, my father! And Esau wept loudly.
local: Esau xɔ edzi na fofoa bena: Alo yayra sia ɖeka koe le asiwòa, fofonye? Yra nye hã, fofonye! Eye Esau gbã avi doo.

eng: While in my care, what little you had has multiplied a great deal. The LORD blessed you wherever I took your livestock. Now, when will I be able to work for my own household too.
local: Nusi le asiwò hafi meva la, ele sue ko; ke azɔ la edzi ɖe dzi ŋutɔ, eye afisiafi, si meto la, Yehowa yraa wò; ke ɣekaɣi gɔ̃e nye hã mawɔ dɔ na ɖokuinye?.

eng: and I have the power to punish you. However, your fathers God told me yesterday, Be careful and dont say anything hastily to Jacob one way or the other.
local: Ŋusẽ le asinye be, mawɔ vɔ̃ mi hafi, gake le etsɔ ƒe zã, si va yi me la, mia fofo ƒe Mawu la gblɔ nam bena: Kpɔ ɖokuiwò dzi, be nagaƒonu vɔ̃a ɖeke kple Yakob o.

eng: His father Isaac responded and said to him, Now, you will make a home far away from the olive groves of the earth, far away from the showers of the sky above.
local: Tete fofoa Isak gblɔ nɛ bena: Manɔ anyigba wɔnu dzi akpɔ o, eye dziƒozãmua ɖeke madza na wò o.

eng: Laban said, What will I pay you? Jacob said, Dont pay me anything. If you will do this for me, I will take care of your flock again, and keep a portion.
local: Tete wògblɔ bena: Nuka mana wò? Yakob ɖo ŋu bena: Nyemaxɔ agaga femee hã le asiwò o, negbe ne alɔ nusi magblɔ na wò la dzi ko; na maganyi wò alẽwo, eye makpɔ wo dzi.

eng: Youve rushed off now because you missed your fathers household so much, but why did you steal my gods.
local: Ke esi nèdzo, elabena aƒe le dzrowòm vevie la, nukaŋuti nefi nye mawuwo?.

eng: You will live by your sword; you will serve your brother. But when you grow restless, you will tear away his harness from your neck.
local: Wò yi anyi wò, eye àsubɔ nɔviwò. Ke ne èle ʋiʋlim la, aɖe eƒe kɔkuti ɖa le wò kɔ dzi.

eng: I will go through the entire flock today, taking out all of the speckled and spotted sheep, all of the black male lambs, and all of the spotted and speckled female goats. That will be my price.
local: Egbe la matsa le wò lãha la katã me, eye maɖe lã, siwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta, kple esiwo tso kpɔ la, ɖe vovo: Alẽvi, siwo le yibɔ, kple gbɔ̃, siwo tso kpɔ, kple esiwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta la, nanye nye fetu!.

eng: Jacob responded to Laban, I was afraid and convinced myself that you would take your daughters away from me.
local: Tete Yakob ɖo ŋu na Laban bena: Mele vɔvɔ̃m, elabena mesusu be, ɖewohĩ axa viwònyɔnuawo le asinye.

eng: Esau was furious at Jacob because his father had blessed him, and Esau said to himself, When the period of mourning for the death of my father is over, I will kill my brother.
local: Ke Esau lé Yakob ɖe fu me le yayra, si fofoa tsɔ nɛ la ta, eye Esau ɖo ta me gblɔ bena: Ne fofonye aku, eye woanɔ fu nyim nɛ la, ekema mawu nɔvinye Yakob!.

eng: Whomever you find with your divine images wont live. Identify whatever I have that is yours, in front of your brothers, and take it. Jacob didnt know that Rachel had stolen them.
local: Amesi ke gbɔ nakpɔ wò mawuawo le la, magano agbe o! Le mía nɔviawo katã ƒe ŋku me ka nye nuwo me, eye natsɔe! Ke Yakob menyae bena. Raxel ye fii o.

eng: I will be completely honest with you: when you come to check on our agreement, every female goat with me that isnt speckled or spotted and every male lamb with me that isnt black will be considered stolen.
local: Gbesigbe nava kpɔ nye fetua ɖa la, ekema nye anukwareɖiɖi adze ƒã; ne àkpɔ gbɔ̃, si mele ŋɔtaŋɔta o, kple esi metso kpɔ o, kpakple alẽvi, si mele yibɔ o la, ekema ɖe mefii.

eng: Rebekah was told what her older son Esau was planning, so she summoned her younger son Jacob and said to him, Esau your brother is planning revenge. He plans to kill you.
local: Esi Rebeka se via xoxoetɔ Esau ƒe nyawo la, ena woyɔ via ɖevitɔ Yakob vɛ, eye wògblɔ nɛ bena: Kpɔ ɖa, nɔviwò Esau ɖo be, yeabia hlɔ̃ awu wò.

eng: Laban said, All right; lets do it.
local: Laban ɖo ŋu bena: Nenɔ nenema, abe alesi negblɔ ene.

eng: Laban went into Jacobs tent, Leahs tent, and her two servants tent and didnt find them. So he left Leahs tent and went into Rachels.
local: Tete Laban ka Yakob kple Lea kpakple kosi eveawo ƒe agbadɔwo me, mekpɔe o; emegbe la; edze le Lea ƒe agbadɔ me heyi ɖe Raxel ƒe agbadɔ me.

eng: So now, my son, listen to me: Get up and escape to my brother Laban in Haran.
local: Eyata vinye, ɖo to nye gbe! Tso, eye nasi ayi ɖe nɔvinye Laban gbɔ le Xaran!.

eng: However, on that very day Laban took out the striped and spotted male goats and all of the speckled and spotted female goats—any with some white in it—and all of the black male lambs, and gave them to his sons.
local: Gbemagbe ke wòɖe gbɔ̃tsu, siwo me fli to, kple esiwo tso kpɔ, kple gbɔ̃nɔ, siwo katã le ŋɔtaŋɔta, kple esiwo tso kpɔ — lã sia lã, si me ɣi to — kpakple alẽvi, siwo le yibɔ la, ɖe vovo, eye wòde wo asi na viawo.

eng: Live with him for a short while until your brothers rage subsides.
local: Eye nanɔ egbɔ ŋkekea ɖewo, vaseɖe esime nɔviwò ƒe dziku la nu nata nɛ.

eng: Now Rachel had taken the divine images and put them into the camels saddlebag and sat on them. Laban felt around in the whole tent but couldnt find them.
local: Ke Raxel fi aklama kpakpɛawo, eye wòtsɔ wo ɣla ɖe kposɔ ƒe akpa le hebɔbɔ no edzi. Eye Laban ka agbadɔ la katã me, ke mekpɔ naneke o.

eng: He put a three-day trip between himself and Jacob, while Jacob was watching the rest of Labans flock.
local: Eye wòɖo be, ye kple Yakob dome mɔ nadidi ŋkeke etɔ, eye Yakob nyi Laban ƒe lã mamleawo.

eng: until your brothers anger at you goes away and he forgets what you did to him. Then I will send for you and bring you back from there. Why should I suffer the loss of both of you on one day.
local: vaseɖe esime dzi nadzudzɔ ekuku ɖe ŋuwò, eye wòaŋlɔ nusi nèwɔ ɖe eŋu la be! Ekema mado woakplɔ wò tso afima vɛ. Nukaŋuti mi ame eve miabu ɖem gbe ɖeka?.

eng: Rachel said to her father, Sir, dont be angry with me because I cant get up for you; Im having my period. He searched but couldnt find the divine images.
local: Eye wògblɔ na fofoa bena: Nye aƒetɔ, megabi dzi be, nyemate ŋu atsi tre o, elabena mekpɔ dzinu! Eye wódii gadu, ke mekpɔ aklama kpakpɛawo o.

eng: Then Jacob took new branches from poplar, almond, and plane trees; and he peeled white stripes on them, exposing the branches white color.
local: Tete Yakob tsɔ logo, xe kple nyati mumuwo, eye woŋlɔ wo ŋu ale: eɖe tsroa ɖe le wo ŋu, eye wòkpɔtɔ ɖe ɖe wo ŋu.

eng: Rebekah then said to Isaac, I really loathe these Hittite women. If Jacob marries one of the Hittite women, like the women of this land, why should I go on living.
local: Eye Rebeka gblɔ na Isak bena: Agbenɔnɔ tim le Xitivi-nyɔnuawo ta. Ne Yakob hã aɖe anyigba sia dzi tɔ, siwo nye Xitiviwo ƒe vinyɔnua ɖe la, ekema nukaŋuti megale agbe ɖo?.

eng: Jacob was angry and complained to Laban, What have I done wrong and whats my crime that youve tracked me down like this?.
local: Azɔ Yakob bi dzi hedo dɔmedzoe ɖe Laban ŋuti. Eye Yakob bia Laban bena. Vo ka meda, eye vɔ ka mewo be, nele hosuhosu dom nam ale?.

eng: He set the branches that he had peeled near the watering troughs so that they were in front of the flock when they drank, because they often mated when they came to drink.
local: Emegbe la etsɔ ati, siwo woŋlɔ la, da ɖe lãwo ƒe tsinonu me, eye ne lãwo le asi yɔm le tsinɔƒea la, etsɔa wo dana ɖe woƒe ŋku me.

eng: Youve now felt through all of my baggage, and what have you found from your households belongings? Put it in front of our relatives, and let them decide between us.
local: Eka nye nuwo katã me, aƒewo me nu ka nakpɔ mahã? Tsɔe va da ɖe mia kple nɔviwòawo katã ŋku me, bena woatso atia na mia dometɔ, si to dzo!.

eng: Jacob went on his way, and Gods messengers approached him.
local: Eye esi Laban fo ŋdi kanya la, eɖuɖɔ nu na tɔgbuiayɔviwo kple vianyɔnuwo, eye wòyra wo. Emegbe la Laban dze mɔ gatrɔ yi enɔƒe.

eng: When the flock mated in front of the branches, they gave birth to striped, speckled, and spotted young.
local: Eye ne lãwo lé ŋku ɖe atiawo ŋu hafi fɔ fu la, ekema wodzia vi, siwo me fũ to, kple esiwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta, kple esiwo tso kpɔ.
